Title: Digital Analyst
HTML:
Turn B2C data into sharper journeys, smarter trading and stronger growth.

Lijnden | B2C (Ecommerce, IT, UX & Online Merchandising) | 40 hours | Full-time

At Just Brands, we build menswear brands with character. PME Legend, Cast Iron and Vanguard each have their own voice, their own audience and their own place in the market. In B2C, growth is shaped by thousands of decisions across ecommerce, retail, CRM, digital marketing, site merchandising, site experience, IT, UX and marketplaces.

The challenge is not having more data. The challenge is knowing what matters, what it means and what to do next. That is where ecommerce performance, customer behaviour, trading insights and business intelligence come together.

But what really sets us apart is how we work together.

We’re team first. No ego, no unnecessary layers, no endless talking, no corporate theatre. We back each other, speak up, take ownership and keep pushing for better. We work hard, stay sharp and make sure there’s room to enjoy the ride too.

That’s where you jump onboard.

Why this role matters

This is where ecommerce analytics, B2C performance and digital decision-making become commercial impact. As our Ecommerce Performance Analyst, you support data-driven decision-making across all B2C channels: Retail, E-Commerce, Digital Marketing & CRM, Site Merchandising, Site Experience, IT & UX and Marketplaces.

You collect, analyse and interpret data to generate actionable insights that improve performance, customer experience and operational efficiency. You help teams understand what is happening, why it is happening and which actions will create better results. You are not here to build dashboards that quietly gather dust.

You are here to turn B2C data into decisions that teams trust, use and act on.

What you’ll do

  • Support data-driven decision-making across all B2C channels, including Retail, E-Commerce, Digital Marketing & CRM, Site Merchandising, Site Experience, IT & UX and marketplaces;
  • Collect, analyze and interpret data to generate actionable insights that improve performance, customer experience and operational efficiency;
  • Build, maintain and improve dashboards for ecommerce performance, customer behavior, campaign performance, trading, funnel performance and channel results;
  • Design forecasting tools to anticipate customer behavior, sales trends, demand patterns and commercial opportunities;
  • Provide performance analytics to senior stakeholders, supporting decision-making and P&L accountability across online channels;
  • Lead department-wide projects that improve data collection, data integration and financial insight generation across B2C operations;
  • Analyze ecommerce KPIs such as revenue, conversion rate, traffic quality, average order value, margin, retention, customer lifetime value, funnel drop-off and product/category performance;
  • Translate data into clear recommendations for Ecommerce, CRM, Digital Marketing, Site Merchandising, Retail, Marketplaces, UX and IT;
  • Monitor digital marketing and CRM performance across paid media, email, automation flows, campaign traffic and customer segments;
  • Support ecommerce trading rhythms with weekly and monthly insights on what is selling, what is slowing down and where commercial opportunities sit;
  • Analyze customer journeys and identify friction points across web shops, apps, landing pages, checkout, content and onsite experience;
  • Support A/B testing and experimentation, including test setup, measurement, analysis and learnings;
  • Improve data quality, tracking consistency, reporting definitions and governance across B2C;
  • Help teams understand the story behind the numbers by presenting insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

What success looks like in your first year

  • B2C teams use your dashboards and insights in weekly and monthly decision rhythms;
  • Ecommerce, CRM, marketing, site experience and marketplace teams make better decisions based on trusted data;
  • You identify conversion, trading or customer journey opportunities that lead to measurable business improvements;
  • Forecasting becomes sharper and helps teams anticipate sales trends, customer behavior and commercial risks;
  • Data collection and integration improve, reducing noise, manual work and reporting confusion;
  • Senior stakeholders receive clearer performance analytics that support P&L ownership and better prioritization;
  • A/B tests and experiments create learnings that change how we build campaigns, pages and customer journeys.

What makes this role exciting

  • You work across the full B2C ecosystem: ecommerce, retail, CRM, digital marketing, marketplaces, site experience, UX and IT;
  • You sit close to revenue, conversion, customer behaviour, trading performance and P&L decisions;
  • You influence both commercial performance and customer experience for three strong menswear brands;
  • You work on dashboards, forecasting, experimentation and strategic B2C projects;
  • You help shape how a growing international business becomes more data-driven;
  • You turn scattered data into clear direction, which is where the magic gets wonderfully operational.

You’ll probably love this role if you…

  • enjoy turning messy data into clear direction and practical next steps;
  • like investigating why performance changes, not just reporting that it changed;
  • understand that good analytics is part numbers, part business sense and part storytelling;
  • get energy from working with ecommerce, marketing, CRM, UX, IT and marketplace teams;
  • care about both performance and customer experience;
  • prefer impact over perfection and want your work to change decisions.

What you bring

  • Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Business Analytics, Statistics, Economics, Marketing, Ecommerce or a related field;
  • 5+ years of experience in ecommerce analytics, digital analytics, business intelligence, performance analysis or B2C data analysis;
  • Strong knowledge of ecommerce KPIs: conversion rate, revenue, AOV, traffic quality, margin, retention, CLV, funnel drop-off and product/category performance;
  • Strong experience with GA4, SQL, Excel and Power BI or Tableau;
  • Experience building dashboards, reporting structures and forecasting models;
  • Experience with digital marketing analytics, CRM analytics, campaign performance and customer journey analysis;
  • Experience with A/B testing, experimentation, CRO or UX analytics is a strong plus;
  • Strong analytical skills and the ability to connect commercial, financial and customer data;
  • Comfortable working with cross-functional teams in a fast-moving retail or ecommerce environment;
  • Strong communication skills: you can explain insights clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders;
  • Fluent English; Dutch or German is a plus.

What Just Brands is really like

We’re team first. In B2C, that means we challenge assumptions with data, keep conversations sharp and turn insight into action. No ego. Just practical collaboration, honest decisions and a team that likes improving what actually matters.

You’ll Join a Company Where

  • People help each other out;
  • Ideas and people matter more than job titles;
  • Hard work matters, and so does enjoying the ride;
  • If something can be better, we improve it.

What you get

  • Salary depending on experience and background;
  • End-of-year bonus equal to one gross monthly salary;
  • Healthy and varied lunch every day;
  • Exclusive access to our own Gym, including classes such as boxing, yoga and padel;
  • Parties and drinks that will be talked about for years to come;
  • Staff discount on our clothing.

Ready to turn ecommerce performance data into decisions that change results?

Still reading? Good sign.

If you want to work close to ecommerce, CRM, marketing, marketplaces, customer behaviour and real commercial decisions, this could be your next step.

Ready to apply?

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Help Wellis turn data into better decisions across the business. Bring data together, create key metrics, build clear dashboards, and deliver pragmatic analyses that move fast in a scale-up environment.

We're looking for a hands-on, proactive Data Analyst to join us. You will be working with data from multiple systems and translating it into insights for Operations, Finance, Marketing and other teams. You'll work in our Google stack (BigQuery, Looker, GA4) and collaborate closely with stakeholders to improve reporting, data quality, and day-to-day decision-making.

Introduction

Data is becoming a core part of how Wellis operates — and it's growing quickly in both volume and complexity. To help us make better, faster decisions across the business, we're looking for a Data Analyst who can turn data from multiple systems into clear insights that teams can act on. We're looking for someone who combines strong analytical skills with a pragmatic, business-minded approach — and who takes initiative to find answers, improve data quality, and help shape how Wellis uses data at scale.

About Wellis

Wellis is a fast-growing healthtech scale-up that is reinventing one of the most traditional sectors: healthcare. What started just three years ago has already grown into a company with tens of thousands of customers, and we are only at the beginning. By combining medical expertise with technology and data, we make healthcare smarter, more efficient, and more personal.

Our mission is to improve everyday lives by making care accessible, personal, and seamless. Through our platform, people can take control of their health journey with trusted medical support, smart digital tools, and tailored programs. We move fast, challenge conventions, and build solutions that truly make an impact, because we believe better healthcare should be within everyone's reach.

About The Role

As a Data Analyst at Wellis, you'll help teams make better, faster decisions by turning data into clear insights. You'll work across multiple systems (e.g., ecommerce, subscriptions, shipping, CRM and advertising platforms) and bring data together in our Google Cloud environment. From there, you'll build analyses and dashboards that improve visibility, sharpen priorities, and support day-to-day business choices — in a fast-moving scale-up where pragmatism matters.

You'll collaborate closely with stakeholders across the business and translate their questions into reliable reporting and actionable recommendations, while helping improve data quality and consistency as we scale.

Key Responsibilities:


Make data across key systems available in one central place and keep it usable (e.g., Firmhouse, Shopify, MyParcel, and upcoming tools like Odoo) in our Google Cloud environment.
Write and maintain SQL queries to combine datasets (e.g., inventory and sales, orders and invoices) and deliver reliable, actionable outputs.
Build and improve dashboards and reports (primarily in Looker) to track performance across teams (marketing, operations, customer care, and finance).
Turn operational and Financial data into clear insights for decision-making — balancing speed and accuracy in a fast-moving scale-up.
Investigate data quality issues and work with stakeholders and system owners to improve definitions, structures, and consistency over time.
Help set reporting standards (formats, definitions, and access/permissions) so insights scale across the organization.
Translate stakeholder questions into analyses, clarify requirements, and proactively propose next steps and deeper questions.
Partner closely with marketing and leadership to support commercial performance tracking, experimentation insights, and strategic discussions.



About The Team

You'll join our growing data team, working closely with Margit (Lead Data & Analytics) and Data Analyst. In this role, you'll collaborate directly with key stakeholders across the business, including the leadership team — translating questions into clear analyses and actionable insights. As Wellis continues to scale and add more systems and reporting needs, you'll have plenty of room to take ownership, proactively improve how we work with data, and help set standards for dashboards and decision-making.

A Week in the Life


Monday: Sync with stakeholders to align on priorities; review weekend data anomalies; update executive dashboard with key metrics.
Tuesday: Deep-dive analysis session with Marketing team on campaign performance; build new customer segmentation model.
Wednesday: Workshop with Operations on optimising patient flow based on data patterns; stakeholder interviews for new dashboard requirements.
Thursday: Build automated reporting for German market launch metrics; collaborate with Medical Operations on treatment efficacy analysis.
Friday: Present weekly insights to leadership team; document findings; plan next week's analytical priorities; knowledge sharing session with fellow analyst.



Who You Are


Minimum 3 years of experience as a Data Analyst (or in a similar role) in a fast-moving, business-driven environment.
Strong SQL skills and comfortable working with cloud data warehouses (experience with BigQuery is a plus).
Experience building and maintaining dashboards and self-serve reporting (experience with Looker is a plus).
Confident combining data from multiple systems and sources (e.g., inventory management, subscriptions, orders, shipping, invoicing and financial).
Able to work with imperfect data and still deliver clear, actionable insights — pragmatic over perfection.
Proactive and self-starter: you investigate, iterate, and improve without needing constant direction.
Strong stakeholder management skills and able to translate business questions into analyses people can act on.
Fluent in English and preferably Dutch; experience in e-commerce is a plus.



What We Offer


Salary range € 3200 - € 5500 gross per month (depending on experience)
25 holidays
Learning budget
Monthly personal budget (Alleo)
Pension plan
20 days of remote working per year
Working from home allowance (2 days a week)
Hybrid working: partly from our new office in central Rotterdam (2 days)
The opportunity to help build a fast-growing healthtech scale-up from the inside.
A hands-on data role with real ownership: from connecting new data sources to delivering analyses and dashboards stakeholders rely on.
Close collaboration with teams across the business (operations, finance, product, marketing) and direct exposure to decision-making.
The chance to help bring structure to reporting standards, dashboards, and role-based access as the organization scales.
A culture of short lines, speed, and continuous improvement — where pragmatism and impact matter.



Hiring Process


We want the application process at Wellis to be fast, transparent, and personal. Here's what to expect:
Application via our website – leave your details and we'll be in touch shortly.
Introductory chat with Georgios (Tech Recruiter) – an informal online call to get to know you and answer your questions.
Interview with Margit (Data) – a deep-dive conversation about your experience, how you approach stakeholders, and how you like to work in a fast-moving environment.
Case – a practical, business-focused data case to understand how you structure your thinking, make assumptions, and translate insights into recommendations (you don't need "perfect" data to do well here).
Final conversation with relevant stakeholders – a closing meeting to align on expectations, team fit, and the impact you'll make at Wellis.



We keep the process compact and clear: you'll always know what to expect and truly get to know our team before making your decision.

Title: Data-analist
Description:
Your mission




You’ll join a team that is in the process of further building the organization’s data capabilities. Together with your team of 4, you will work on developing and improving dashboards and data solutions used across different departments, such as logistics, finance, and HR.




In this role, you won’t just focus on building dashboards in Power BI. A big part of your work will involve understanding and preparing data. You’ll investigate where data comes from, how it is structured, and how it can be transformed into insights the business can actually use. To do this, you’ll work closely with stakeholders and the IT department to ensure the right data is available and usable.




Why you should consider this opportunity

You work at the intersection of business and data, with direct impact on domains such as logistics, finance, and HR
Plenty of room to contribute ideas on how data can be used more effectively across the organization
Close interaction with stakeholders, allowing you to see how your work directly supports the business
An environment where the data function is still developing and where you can contribute to its growth




Organization

You will join an international organization within the logistics sector. The company combines years of experience in transportation and logistics services with an increasing focus on data and digitalization.




This is what is expected from you

Experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar role (minimum 1.5 years)
Strong knowledge of SQL and Power BI
Experience with cloud environments (they are on Azure)
Strong communication skills and the ability to work with different stakeholders
A proactive mindset and willingness to think along with the business




In return for your hard work, you will receive

Salary between €3,470 and €4,500 based on full-time employment (40 hours)
25 vacation days
Solid pension scheme
Travel expense reimbursement
Hybrid working with three days per week in the office
Tailored learning and development budget for your professional growth

Title: BI Specialist
HTML:
Ben jij een BI Specialist en lijkt het je leuk om mee te werken aan het opzetten van een BI Competence Center? Wil jij je inzetten voor de Nederlandse vluchtelingensector? Lees dan snel verder!


Dit ga je doen


  • Het zorgdragen voor de juiste implementatie van het BICC;
  • Het in kaart brengen van wensen uit de business;
  • Het meedenken over datamogelijkheden en waar nodig deze vertalen naar beleid en/of actiepunten;
  • Het schakelen met diverse stakeholders;
  • Actief meedenken over verbetermogelijkheden over de data-omgeving.


Hier ga je werken


Deze organisatie in Utrecht, waar jij als BI Specialist komt te werken, vormt een onmisbaar onderdeel binnen een groot maatschappelijk thema.


De organisatie heeft in 2025 een Business Intelligence Competence Center (BICC) opgezet om als interne hub op het gebied van BI te acteren en zo meer data gedreven te gaan werken. Dit maakt het voor hen mogelijk om grote hoeveelheden data en processen op de juiste manier te centraliseren en daarmee de business optimaal te ondersteunen.

Als BI Specialist zorg jij dat het BICC op de juiste wijze verder wordt doorontwikkeld. Hiervoor werk je vanaf het moment van de aanbesteding en het selectieproces van een leverancier die het DWH en de ETL laag op zal leveren.

Je zorgt voor goede interne en externe communicatie rondom de data-ontwikkelingen. Jij vormt de brug tussen de business en het platform, waardoor je schakelt met stakeholders om wensen in kaart te brengen en deze te vertalen naar concrete BI-oplossingen. Daarnaast denk je actief mee hoe je met behulp van de beschikbare data de business optimaal kan ondersteunen. Samen met de IT Manager en Informatiemanager werk je aan het databeleid voor de komende jaren. Gezien dit een nieuwe rol is, is er veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief, verantwoordelijkheid en om te pionieren.


Dit doe je uiteraard niet alleen. Je werkt samen met een IT-team dat dagelijks verantwoordelijk is voor de strategie en operatie op IT-vlak. Je werkt nauw samen met een PowerBI Specialist die zich heeft gespecialiseerd in het ontwikkelen van rapportages.


Dit neem je mee


  • Een achtergrond in Data Analyse of BI Development;
  • Ervaring met het in kaart brengen en verbeteren van processen is een pre;
  • Hbo werk/denkniveau;
  • Affiniteit met de zorg, overheid of de vluchtelingensector;
  • Communicatief vaardig met een pro-actieve houding;
  • Ervaring met stakeholdermanagement tot aan directieniveau.


Dit krijg je


  • Salaris: 4000 - 6000 euro bruto o.b.v. een 36-urige werkweek;
  • 8% Vakantiegeld;
  • 8,3% eindejaarsuitkering;
  • 0,23 cent reiskostenvergoeding OF een volledige ov-vergoeding;
  • Extra 50 euro bruto per maand bovenop jouw salaris als tegemoetkoming voor jouw ziektekostenverzekering;
  • Goede pensioen voorziening;
  • Opleidings- en begeleidingsmogelijkheden;
  • Thuiswerkmogelijkheden;
  • Flexibele werktijden.

Title: Data Analyst II - Flights
Description:
At Booking.com, data drives our decisions. Technology is at our core. And innovation is everywhere. But our company is more than datasets, lines of code or A/B tests. We’re the thrill of the first night in a new place. The excitement of the next morning. The friends you encounter. The journeys you take. The sights you see. And the memories you make. Through our products, partners and people, we make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

From our hubs in Manchester, London, Amsterdam our Trips Business Unit helps people get where they want to go anywhere in the world. Whether customers want the freedom of a car, the convenience of a

flight, the ease of a taxi or the economy of public transport, we make it all possible

Our team is passionate about helping people travel. They see challenges as opportunities. And they’re

always ready for change.

Our Flight business is one of the cornerstones of the connected trip. We ought to offer the best and most convenient flights to every customer, those that start their trip by buying a flight or those that

complementing their existing journey with a flight. We will do this by having the best inventory available at the most competitive prices powering a site that solves all the difficulties of booking a flight today!

Role Description:

The role is for a Data Analyst II, working within the Flights Platform Data Science and Analytics team in Amsterdam.

As a successful candidate you will be collaborating closely with the Core Services Product teams. As a DA II, you will be involved in various stages of data analytics solutions, from ideation to implementation, helping steer decisions by providing relevant and actionable data insights. You will be closely working within the Core Services Product teams, ensuring our customers can successfully complete their transactions, make their payments, and receive their tickets and refunds.

You will be working independently on data consumption and preparation. This role also involves designing and maintaining dashboards, and engaging with a range of stakeholders.

Key Job Responsibilities and Duties:


Responsible for owning the design and delivery of entire Data Analytics solutions based on business requirements, bringing them from the initial idea-generation phase to implementation independently.
Responsible for working autonomously on consuming data, preparing it for analytical use, translating business problems to data stories and leveraging input from business stakeholders to determine the right technical solutions to deliver customer value.
Responsible for leveraging previous knowledge, experience and substantial skills in data analysis and data visualization to answer business questions in the most efficient way. They understand stakeholder needs and create intuitive and insightful visualizations that translate data into actionable insights to answer those needs.
Responsible for independently identifying and managing stakeholders in operational, project-based, and managerial roles. Effectively communicates, addresses stakeholder needs, and conveys complex analysis results in a clear manner. Begins to guide junior colleagues in stakeholder engagement.
Responsible for being flexible in adopting existing internal and external Data Analytics approaches, proposing and spotting opportunities to apply new approaches and expanding their technical competencies and their peers’ when a more efficient way presents itself.
Responsible for collaborating with peers in related crafts (ie.Data Engineering), anticipating data needs to ensure smooth progress of their individual projects, and for ensuring quality of their own work by validating it through peer review.
Responsible for influencing business decisions within their product team.



Qualifications & Skills:


Master degree, or equivalent experience in a quantitative field (Statistics, Mathematics, Econometrics, Computer Science, Physics, Engineering, Bioinformatics, or similar)
PhD or 3+ years of experience
Experience solving real problems using data mining techniques and with statistical rigor
Able to weave a narrative when presenting data
Strong experience of using insights from disparate data sources.
Demonstrates strong ownership, stakeholder management and prioritisation skills.
Strong analytical skills with commercial acumen, with the ability to find opportunities & threats.
Strong technical skills (Hadoop, R, SQL, Python, Excel)
Tableau skills would also be beneficial
Able to balance the need for accuracy and efficiency
Able to distil & relay complex, technical information to a non-technical audience



Benefits & Perks - Global Impact, Personal Relevance:

Booking.com’s Total Rewards Philosophy is not only about compensation but also about benefits. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package, as well unique-to-Booking.com benefits which include:


Annual paid time off and generous paid leave scheme including: parent, grandparent, bereavement, and care leave
Hybrid working including flexible working arrangements, and up to 20 days per year working from abroad (home country)
Industry leading product discounts - up to 1400 per year - for yourself, including automatic Genius Level 3 status and Booking.com wallet credit



Inclusion at Booking.com:

Inclusion has been a core part of our company culture since day one. This ongoing journey starts with our very own employees, who represent over 140 nationalities and a wide range of ethnic and social backgrounds, genders and sexual orientations.

Take it from our Chief People Officer, Paulo Pisano: “At Booking.com, the diversity of our people doesn’t just build an outstanding workplace, it also creates a better and more inclusive travel experience for everyone. At Booking.com, data drives our decisions. Technology is at our core. And innovation is everywhere. But our company is more than datasets, lines of code or A/B tests. We’re the thrill of the first night in a new place. The excitement of the next morning. The friends you encounter. The journeys you take. The sights you see. And the memories you make. Through our products, partners and people, we make it easier for everyone to experience the world.

From our hubs in Manchester, London, Amsterdam our Trips Business Unit helps people get where they want to go anywhere in the world. Whether customers want the freedom of a car, the convenience of a

flight, the ease of a taxi or the economy of public transport, we make it all possible

Our team is passionate about helping people travel. They see challenges as opportunities. And they’re

always ready for change.

Our Flight business is one of the cornerstones of the connected trip. We ought to offer the best and most convenient flights to every customer, those that start their trip by buying a flight or those that

complementing their existing journey with a flight. We will do this by having the best inventory available at the most competitive prices powering a site that solves all the difficulties of booking a flight today!

Role Description:

The role is for a Data Analyst II, working within the Flights Platform Data Science and Analytics team in Amsterdam.

As a successful candidate you will be collaborating closely with the Core Services Product teams. As a DA II, you will be involved in various stages of data analytics solutions, from ideation to implementation, helping steer decisions by providing relevant and actionable data insights. You will be closely working within the Core Services Product teams, ensuring our customers can successfully complete their transactions, make their payments, and receive their tickets and refunds.

You will be working independently on data consumption and preparation. This role also involves designing and maintaining dashboards, and engaging with a range of stakeholders.

Key Job Responsibilities and Duties:

  • Responsible for owning the design and delivery of entire Data Analytics solutions based on business requirements, bringing them from the initial idea-generation phase to implementation independently.
  • Responsible for working autonomously on consuming data, preparing it for analytical use, translating business problems to data stories and leveraging input from business stakeholders to determine the right technical solutions to deliver customer value.
  • Responsible for leveraging previous knowledge, experience and substantial skills in data analysis and data visualization to answer business questions in the most efficient way. They understand stakeholder needs and create intuitive and insightful visualizations that translate data into actionable insights to answer those needs.
  • Responsible for independently identifying and managing stakeholders in operational, project-based, and managerial roles. Effectively communicates, addresses stakeholder needs, and conveys complex analysis results in a clear manner. Begins to guide junior colleagues in stakeholder engagement.
  • Responsible for being flexible in adopting existing internal and external Data Analytics approaches, proposing and spotting opportunities to apply new approaches and expanding their technical competencies and their peers’ when a more efficient way presents itself.
  • Responsible for collaborating with peers in related crafts (ie.Data Engineering), anticipating data needs to ensure smooth progress of their individual projects, and for ensuring quality of their own work by validating it through peer review.
  • Responsible for influencing business decisions within their product team.


Qualifications & Skills:

  • Master degree, or equivalent experience in a quantitative field (Statistics, Mathematics, Econometrics, Computer Science, Physics, Engineering, Bioinformatics, or similar)
  • PhD or 3+ years of experience
  • Experience solving real problems using data mining techniques and with statistical rigor
  • Able to weave a narrative when presenting data
  • Strong experience of using insights from disparate data sources.
  • Demonstrates strong ownership, stakeholder management and prioritisation skills.
  • Strong analytical skills with commercial acumen, with the ability to find opportunities & threats.
  • Strong technical skills (Hadoop, R, SQL, Python, Excel)
  • Tableau skills would also be beneficial
  • Able to balance the need for accuracy and efficiency
  • Able to distil & relay complex, technical information to a non-technical audience


Benefits & Perks - Global Impact, Personal Relevance:

Booking.com’s Total Rewards Philosophy is not only about compensation but also about benefits. We offer a competitive compensation and benefits package, as well unique-to-Booking.com benefits which include:

  • Annual paid time off and generous paid leave scheme including: parent, grandparent, bereavement, and care leave
  • Hybrid working including flexible working arrangements, and up to 20 days per year working from abroad (home country)
  • Industry leading product discounts - up to 1400 per year - for yourself, including automatic Genius Level 3 status and Booking.com wallet credit

Title: Business Intelligence Intern
Description:
Metyis is growing! We are looking for a Business Intelligence Intern to join our Data and Analytics team in Amsterdam.

Who We Are

Metyis is a global and forward-thinking firm operating across a wide range of industries, developing and delivering AI & Data, Digital Commerce, Marketing & Design solutions and Advisory services. At Metyis, our long-term partnership model brings long-lasting impact and growth to our business partners and clients through extensive execution capabilities.

With our team, you can experience a collaborative environment with highly skilled multidisciplinary experts, where everyone has room to build bigger and bolder ideas. Being part of Metyis means you can speak your mind and be creative with your knowledge. Imagine the things you can achieve with a team that encourages you to be the best version of yourself.

We are Metyis. Partners for Impact.

What We Offer


2 months of intensive training from our super skilled BI team, on topics like data warehousing, data modelling and Power BI reporting (Data transformation, DAX, report building).
Followed by 4 months of relevant working experience on a live business case.
Interact with clients regularly to drive their business towards impactful change.
Play a key role in the team by creating new business solutions via data-driven insights.
Receive guidance by a dedicated mentor to make sure you make the most out of your time at Metyis.
Experience being part of a fast-growing international and diverse team.



What You Will Do


Assist in the design, development and maintenance of BI reporting systems.
Understand with data warehousing and data modelling activities.
Participate in the creation of data-driven solutions to support business needs.
Collaborate with data scientists, business analysts and software engineers to understand and implement reporting requirements.
Participate in report reviews and providing constructive feedback.
Stay up to date with the latest developments in all the BI tech stacks and best practices.



What You’ll Bring


Currently pursuing a Master's degree in Business Analytics, Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics or a related field.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to derive insights from complex data sets.
Basic understanding of SQL or BI is helpful.
Ability to work in a team environment.
A passion for data analysis and a desire to learn and grow, in the field of business intelligence.



At Metyis, we are driven by curiosity and collaboration. We value diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging (DEIB) in all its forms as it makes us stronger as an organisation and promotes creativity and innovation. We welcome all talents and are committed to creating a workplace where every employee can make a meaningful impact and grow.

Title: Data Analist
Description:
Let op: we nemen voor deze vacature alleen sollicitaties in behandeling van kandidaten die in Nederland wonen en hier mogen werken.




Zet data om in inzichten waar organisaties écht betere keuzes door maken.




Organisaties beschikken over steeds meer data. Over huisvesting, vastgoed, werkplekken, processen, bezetting en samenwerking. Maar data is pas waardevol als je weet welke vraag erachter zit, welke patronen zichtbaar worden en wat dit betekent voor de keuzes die een organisatie moet maken.




Bij Aestate helpen we organisaties om complexe vraagstukken te ontrafelen. We kijken naar mens, inhoud en proces. Naar wat er op papier staat, maar vooral ook naar wat er in de praktijk gebeurt. Binnen Digitale Transitie zetten we data in om opdrachtgevers meer grip, inzicht en handelingsperspectief te geven.




Voor ons datateam zoeken we een Data Analist die analytisch sterk is, zorgvuldig werkt en data weet te vertalen naar bruikbare inzichten voor opdrachtgevers en projectteams.




Wat ga je doen?

Als Data Analist verzamel, structureer en analyseer je data binnen projecten en voor onze eigen organisatie. Je zorgt ervoor dat informatie betrouwbaar, begrijpelijk en toepasbaar wordt. Denk aan data over vastgoed, huisvesting, werkplekgebruik, processen, prestaties of andere organisatievraagstukken.




Je werkt binnen multidisciplinaire projectteams, samen met data analisten, data engineers, adviseurs en projectleiders. Jij zit op het snijvlak van data, inhoud en advies. Je helpt om vraagstukken beter te begrijpen, patronen en trends zichtbaar te maken en inzichten te vertalen naar concrete adviezen.




Daarbij gaat het niet alleen om het maken van dashboards of rapportages. Je denkt ook mee over de vraag achter de vraag. Welke data is nodig? Is de data betrouwbaar? Welke aannames doen we? Wat zegt de analyse wel, en vooral ook: wat zegt deze niet? En hoe vertalen we dit naar keuzes waar een opdrachtgever echt iets aan heeft?




Waar je aan werkt

/ Het verzamelen, structureren en analyseren van data binnen projecten.

/ Het inzichtelijk maken van databronnen, definities en datakwaliteit.

/ Het signaleren van patronen, trends, afwijkingen en verbeterkansen.

/ Het maken van dashboards, rapportages en visualisaties die aansluiten bij de behoefte van stakeholders.

/ Het vertalen van analyses naar duidelijke inzichten en concrete handelingsperspectieven.

/ Het ondersteunen van projectteams en opdrachtgevers bij datagedreven besluitvorming.

/ Het samenwerken met data engineers rondom datastromen, datasets en technische haalbaarheid.

/ Het bijdragen aan de verdere ontwikkeling van onze datagedreven dienstverlening binnen Digitale Transitie.




Wie ben jij?

Je bent analytisch, nauwkeurig en nieuwsgierig. Je vindt het leuk om complexe informatie terug te brengen tot duidelijke inzichten. Je werkt systematisch, toetst aannames en laat je niet te snel tevredenstellen door een mooi dashboard alleen. Voor jou moet de inhoud kloppen.




Tegelijk ben je geen Data Analist die alleen achter het scherm wil zitten. Je vindt het belangrijk om te begrijpen wat een opdrachtgever nodig heeft en kunt complexe informatie helder uitleggen aan verschillende doelgroepen. Je schakelt makkelijk met adviseurs, engineers en projectteams en zoekt actief afstemming.




Je hoeft nog geen jarenlange werkervaring te hebben. We zoeken iemand met een sterke analytische basis, technische affiniteit en de motivatie om verder te groeien binnen data, advies en digitale transitie.




Wat je meebrengt

/ Een afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in data, business analytics, informatica, wiskunde, natuurkunde, econometrie, bedrijfskunde of een vergelijkbare richting.

/ Ervaring of affiniteit met data-analyse, business intelligence, datamodellering of datavisualisatie.

/ Kennis van tools zoals Excel, SQL en Power BI.

/ Affiniteit met Python, Azure, Fabric, Git of vergelijkbare tooling is mooi meegenomen.

/ Het vermogen om data te structureren, analyseren en begrijpelijk te presenteren.

/ Een zorgvuldige en reproduceerbare manier van werken.

/ Interesse in vraagstukken rondom vastgoed, huisvesting, onderwijs, organisaties en digitale transitie.

/ Sterke communicatieve vaardigheden en de wil om je adviesvaardigheden verder te ontwikkelen.

/ Nieuwsgierigheid naar ontwikkelingen op het gebied van data, AI en technologie.

/ Beschikbaarheid van minimaal 32 uur per week.




Mis je één onderdeel, maar herken je jezelf sterk in deze rol? Dan horen we alsnog graag van je. We zoeken vooral iemand met analytisch vermogen, leergierigheid en de drive om data praktisch toepasbaar te maken.




Hoe deze rol zich verhoudt tot het team

Binnen het datateam werk je samen met verschillende disciplines. Als Data Analist ligt jouw focus op analyse, inzicht, visualisatie en advies. De Data Engineer richt zich meer op infrastructuur, datastromen, datakwaliteit en technische ontsluiting. Business analisten en adviseurs richten zich sterker op vraagarticulatie, proces, strategie en besluitvorming.




In de praktijk werk je nauw samen. Juist die combinatie maakt onze dienstverlening sterker: betrouwbare data, scherpe analyses en adviezen die aansluiten bij de echte vraag van de opdrachtgever.




Waar je in kunt groeien

Bij Aestate krijg je ruimte om je verder te ontwikkelen. Bijvoorbeeld richting advanced analytics, AI-toepassingen, datavisualisatie, data consultancy of sectorinhoudelijke expertise binnen vastgoed, onderwijs of organisatieontwikkeling.




Je kunt groeien in technische diepgang, maar ook in adviesvaardigheid en stakeholdermanagement. Uiteindelijk ontwikkel je je tot iemand die niet alleen data analyseert, maar ook helpt richting te geven aan besluitvorming.




Wat je bij ons vindt

Bij Aestate werk je aan vraagstukken die ertoe doen. Vraagstukken waarin organisaties beter willen functioneren, mensen prettiger willen werken, leren of zorgen, en data helpt om betere keuzes te maken.




Je komt terecht in een team van ontrafelexperts. We houden van complexe situaties, omdat daar vaak de meeste waarde te creëren is. We werken scherp, creatief en zorgvuldig, met veel aandacht voor samenwerking. Verschillende invalshoeken maken ons beter.




Je krijgt vertrouwen, verantwoordelijkheid en ruimte om mee te bouwen aan een groeiend vakgebied binnen Aestate.




Daarnaast kun je rekenen op

/ Een salaris tussen € 3.250 en € 4.000 bruto per maand op basis van 40 uur, passend bij jouw opleiding, ervaring en ontwikkeling.

/ Een bonusregeling van meer dan één maandsalaris, gebaseerd op behaalde resultaten en bijdrage.

/ 30 vakantiedagen en vakantiegeld.

/ Hybride werken met goede faciliteiten voor een prettige thuiswerkplek.

/ Een thuiswerkvergoeding.

/ Een NS Business Card voor zakelijke reizen.

/ De keuze tussen een leaseauto of een mobiliteitsvergoeding met reiskostenvergoeding.

/ Een laptop van de zaak en een telefoon of telefoonvergoeding.

/ Een pensioenregeling.

/ Een onkostenvergoeding voor je dagelijkse werkzaamheden.

/ Een studiebudget tot € 2.400 per jaar, afgestemd op jouw ontwikkelbehoefte.

/ Persoonlijke coaching en ruimte voor professionele groei.

/ Interne trainingen, workshops en kennisdeling via onder andere de Aestate Academy.

/ Een open en informele cultuur met korte lijnen, vertrouwen en veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief.




Iets voor jou?

Wil jij data gebruiken om organisaties verder te helpen? En wil je je ontwikkelen op het snijvlak van data, analyse, advies en digitale transitie?




Dan maken we graag kennis met je.

Een uitgebreide motivatiebrief is niet nodig. We leren je liever persoonlijk kennen.




Het aanvragen van een Verklaring Omtrent het Gedrag en het maken van een assessment zijn onderdeel van de procedure.

Een uitgebreide motivatiebrief is niet nodig. We leren je liever persoonlijk kennen.


Het aanvragen van een Verklaring Omtrent het Gedrag en het maken van een assessment zijn onderdeel van de procedure.

Title: Data Engineer - Amsterdam, Netherlands
Description:
Role – Technology Lead

Technology – Databricks, Azure

Location – Amsterdam, NL

Compensation – Competitive (including bonus)

Job Description

Today, the corporate landscape is dynamic and the world ahead is full of possibilities! None of the amazing things we do at Infosys would be possible without an equally amazing culture, the environment where ideas can flourish and where you are empowered to move forward as far as your ideas will take you.

At Infosys, we assure that your career will never stand still, we will inspire you to build what’s next and we will navigate further together. Our journey of learnability, values and trusted relationships with our clients continue to be the cornerstones of our organization and these values are upheld only because of our people.

Your role

In the role of Technology Lead, you will interface with key stakeholders and apply your technical proficiency across different stages of the Software Development Life Cycle including Requirements Elicitation, Application Architecture definition and Design. You will play an important role in creating the high level design artifacts. You will also deliver high quality code deliverables for a module, lead validation for all types of testing and support activities related to implementation, transition and warranty. You will be part of a learning culture, where teamwork and collaboration are encouraged, excellence is rewarded, and diversity is respected and valued.

Required


Proven experience leading end-to-end delivery of technology solutions in a client-facing environment.
Strong experience in solution design, development, implementation, and production support.
Experience managing and mentoring technical teams, including work allocation, reviews, and stakeholder communication.
Strong understanding of SDLC, Agile/Scrum methodologies, release management, and delivery governance.
Ability to work directly with client business and technology teams to gather requirements, manage expectations, and drive outcomes.
Experience in project estimation, planning, risk management, issue resolution, and status reporting.
Excellent communication, presentation, and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to coordinate across onsite and offshore teams to ensure quality and timely delivery.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with an ownership mindset.
Willingness and eligibility to work from the client location and support business-critical activities across time zones.


Preferred


Experience working in an onsite-offshore delivery model for global clients.
Experience leading teams of 5–15 members across multiple workstreams.
Exposure to cloud platforms (Azure, AWS, or GCP), DevOps, CI/CD, and automation frameworks.
Domain experience in Banking, Financial Services, Insurance.
Experience in client relationship management, solution consulting, and business development support.
Experience in proposal development, effort estimation, and pre-sales activities.
Relevant certifications in Agile, Scrum, Cloud Technologies, ITIL, or Project Management.
Experience driving process improvements, automation initiatives, and operational excellence programs.
Prior onsite experience in European client engagements.
Strong leadership skills with the ability to influence stakeholders and foster high-performing teams.


Personal

Besides the professional qualifications of the candidates, we place great importance in addition to various forms personality profile. These include:


High analytical skills
A high degree of initiative and flexibility
High customer orientation
High quality awareness
Excellent verbal and written communication skills


About Infosys

Infosys is a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in 59 countries to navigate their digital transformation.

With over four decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer clients, in 59 countries, as they navigate their digital transformation powered by cloud and AI. We enable them with an AI-first core, empower the business with agile digital at scale and drive continuous improvement with always-on learning through the transfer of digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem. We are deeply committed to being a well-governed, environmentally sustainable organization where diverse talent thrives in an inclusive workplace.

All aspects of employment at Infosys are based on merit, competence and performance. We are committed to embracing diversity and creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Infosys is proud to be an equal opportunity employer

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst

Amsterdam, Netherlands

Competitive salary and benefits




This is an opportunity to join a high-impact data team at the heart of a large-scale digital platform. You will work on complex, real-world problems that directly influence product direction, commercial strategy, and customer experience. This role offers strong exposure to senior stakeholders and the chance to shape how data drives decision-making across the business.




The Company

They are a leading digital platform operating at scale across a high-traffic consumer marketplace. With millions of monthly users, they enable both individuals and businesses to connect, transact, and grow. Their product spans mobile and web, and they operate in a fast-paced, data-rich environment. Data plays a central role in how they optimise user experience, monetisation, and long-term growth.




The Role

Partner with product, commercial, and marketing stakeholders to define analytical priorities and deliver insights that drive decision-making
Translate ambiguous business questions into structured analysis and actionable outcomes
Build dashboards and reporting that focus on meaningful KPIs across revenue, growth, and customer behaviour
Analyse large-scale datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across the platform
Influence stakeholders at all levels through clear and compelling communication of insights
Support experimentation and measurement strategies to improve product and commercial performance
Collaborate with data and engineering teams to improve tracking, data quality, and metric definitions




Your Skills & Experience




Strong commercial experience in data analysis within a product-led digital environment such as e-commerce or marketplaces
Advanced SQL skills, including working with complex datasets and optimised queries
Hands-on experience with Python for analysis, modelling, or automation
Familiarity with modern cloud data platforms such as BigQuery, Databricks, or AWS
Experience using BI tools such as Tableau to create impactful dashboards
Strong understanding of key business concepts including customer lifecycle, monetisation, and growth metrics
Confident communicator with the ability to influence stakeholders and translate data into business value
Comfortable working in ambiguous environments and proactively identifying opportunities




What They Offer




Competitive salary and comprehensive benefits package
A collaborative, cross-functional working environment
Opportunity to influence business and product strategy at scale
Strong career progression and exposure to senior leadership
Access to modern data tools and technology




How to Apply

If you are a commercially minded Senior Data Analyst looking to make a tangible impact, apply now to find out more.

Title: BI & Data Management Lead
Description:
Do you want to have an excellent experience as an employee in one of the most pioneering toy companies in the market? Are you passionate about games and driven to provide exceptional products and services to customers worldwide?

This is the company you are looking for!

At Jumbo Group, we are not just an international company; we are an international playground. Our world is filled with games, puzzles, and toys that spread joy and inspire creativity across the globe. We enjoy a robust presence throughout Europe and in all corners of the world.

About The Role

BI & Data Management is a newly formed function within Jumbo Group's Digital Transformation department. Our ambition is bold: to make trusted, well-defined data the foundation of how Jumbo makes decisions, and to put clear, role-relevant insight and AI-enabled answers directly into the hands of leadership and every function.

We are looking for a BI & Data Management Lead to shape and drive this function. This is a genuine player-coach role. You will be hands-on with the data, the dashboards and our AI initiatives; you will raise the technical bar and help our BI Analyst grow; and you will sit as a full member of the Digital Transformation leadership team, setting direction alongside our IT and Digital Experience leads.

It is a rare opportunity to own and build a modern, AI-enabled data function from the ground up, with direct visibility to the Management Team and CEO, real autonomy, and a clear path to grow as the function grows.

What You'll Do

Deliver hands-on and raise the bar


Design and build KPI cockpits: focused, role- and function-specific performance views, starting with an MT/CEO cockpit, using Power BI.
Own and improve Power BI dashboards, data models and semantic layers, and drive adoption across business units.
Work directly in the data warehouse and align it with reporting, KPI and AI needs.
Coach and develop the BI Analyst on the craft: data modelling, DAX, definitions, dashboard design and delivery.



Drive AI and automation


Help lead Jumbo's AI Data Warehouse Q&A initiative, enabling the Management Team and senior leadership to ask natural-language questions about sales and finance KPIs, and taking it from pilot to trusted, everyday use.
Shape how AI is applied to data across the function: spot high-value use cases, work with agents built per use case, and help set direction on tooling and platforms (for example Azure OpenAI).
Ensure clean definitions and a well-structured warehouse so that AI answers, and the decisions built on them, are trustworthy.
Bring an experimentation mindset: stay close to what is newly possible with AI and automation, and turn it into practical wins that remove manual, repetitive effort.



Set direction for the function


Own the BI & Data Management roadmap and maturity plan for 2027 and beyond, with AI and automation as core threads.
Champion the cockpit concept: multiple focused, actionable views rather than one dashboard for everything.
Prioritise the BI backlog against business value and keep delivery pragmatic.



Establish definitions, governance and data quality


Build and maintain the KPI dictionary and data dictionary, giving the organisation shared, consistent definitions and the clean foundation that reliable AI depends on.
Improve source data quality in partnership with the teams that own it, and make quality visible and actionable.
Define standards for dashboards, datasets, ownership and certification, positioned as complementary to, not a replacement for, business ownership.



Align stakeholders


Drive the cross-functional BI/Data working group (Finance, Sales, Operations, Marketing).
Build trust across functions and navigate ownership sensitivities with a collaborative, complementary mindset.
Translate business questions into clear data, reporting and AI solutions.



What We're Looking For

Must-have


Strong hands-on Power BI: data modelling, DAX, semantic models and dashboard design.
Solid SQL and genuine comfort working with a data warehouse.
A real enthusiasm for AI and hands-on experience applying it to data, for example LLM-based Q&A over data, or AI-assisted analytics and automation.
A track record of defining KPIs and metrics and driving consistency and governance.
The ability to turn business questions into clear, trusted reporting.
Real stakeholder skills: able to align and constructively challenge across functions and up to leadership.
A coaching mindset; someone who enjoys developing others.
Pragmatic and delivery-focused, and comfortable being both hands-on and strategic.



Nice-to-have


Experience with any of: Oracle Fusion, Anaplan, Zoho CRM, or similar enterprise source systems.
Exposure to data warehouse platforms (for example Azure / Microsoft Fabric, Snowflake, Databricks, SQL Server) and to data cataloguing or data quality tooling.
Experience with Azure OpenAI or similar enterprise AI platforms, and with building or evaluating AI agents.
Retail, consumer goods, e-commerce or marketplace (Amazon, bol.com) context.
Experience building or maturing a BI function.



Who You Are


Ready to step up into a lead role and own something end to end.
Genuinely curious about AI and eager to put it to work in a real business setting.
Equally credible discussing DAX with an analyst and priorities with the CEO.
Collaborative rather than territorial about data and reporting ownership.
Motivated by building, growing, and having a real seat at the table.

Title: Sustainability Analyst - Analytics & Reporting
Description:
City : Rotterdam State : Zuid-Holland (NL-ZH) Country : Netherlands (NL) Requisition Number : 46742

At Bunge, sustainability is not a separate agenda—it is embedded in how we operate, source, process and deliver oilseed and grain products across the globe for food, feed and renewable fuel. As a Sustainability Analyst within our Global Sustainability team, you will play a key role in transforming complex sustainability data into meaningful insights that support business decisions, environmental performance management and external reporting.

This is an exciting opportunity for someone who enjoys combining analytics, sustainability and business impact. Unlike many sustainability roles that focus on a single country or region, this position offers exposure to Bunge's global operations of 150 processing plants and supply chain footprint handling 200 mln tonnes of commodities across 50 countries, enabling you to contribute to sustainability performance on a broadr scale.

What You'll Do

As part of the Sustainability Analytics & Reporting team, you will help ensure the availability, quality and integrity of sustainability data and analytics used across the organization.

Your responsibilities will include:


Developing and improving analytical tools and reporting models related to environmental performance and forecasting,
Supporting and peer-reviewing recurring and ad-hoc sustainability reporting deliverables for internal and external stakeholders.
Analyzing large sustainability datasets relating to energy, greenhouse gas emissions (Scope 1, 2 and 3), water, waste and other sustainability performance indicators.
Collaborating with stakeholders across Sustainability, Operations, Finance, Business Technology and other business functions to enhance data quality and reporting processes.
Supporting the development and continuous improvement of dashboards and reporting solutions, both through in-house Business Intelligence teams and through self-made solutions using Excel, Tableau and upcoming AI-driven analytical tools.
Contributing to the measurement, analysis and reporting of environmental sustainability performance.
Preparing reports, visualizations and presentations that translate data into actionable business insights.
Supporting internal controls and audit activities by maintaining high standards of data quality, documentation and reporting accuracy.
Identifying opportunities to improve, automate and streamline sustainability reporting processes.


What Makes This Role Unique

This is not a regional level sustainability role.

As part of our global Sustainability team, you will work across multiple regions, business units and operational environments, helping to drive sustainability insights and reporting across Bunge's worldwide footprint.

You will contribute to topics that are shaping the future of the food and agriculture industry, including environmental performance, sustainability reporting and evolving regulatory requirements. Your work will support decision-making at both operational and strategic levels, helping Bunge achieve its sustainability ambitions.

What We're Looking For

You are naturally analytical, curious and comfortable working with large and complex datasets. You enjoy understanding the story behind the numbers and can translate data into clear, actionable insights.

Additionally, you bring:


A Bachelor's or Master's degree in Sustainability, Environmental Sciences, Finance, Accounting, Data Analytics, Business Management, Agronomy, Food Process Engineering or a related field.
Minimum 2 years working experience in sustainability reporting, sustainability analytics or sustainability auditing, involving CSRD and GHG Protocol, ideally with exposure towards agriculture commodities,
Strong Excel skills and experience working with reporting, analytics or Business Intelligence tools such as Tableau, with a keen interest into using AI pragmatically
The ability to work with complex datasets while maintaining a high level of accuracy and attention to detail.
Strong problem-solving skills and a structured, analytical mindset.
Project management skills with the ability to manage multiple workflows, track deadlines and coordinate deliverables across multiple teams.
Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with stakeholders across functions and geographies.
Professional proficiency in English. Knowledge of Dutch, Portuguese and/or Spanish is welcomed
Eligible to work in the Netherlands.


What We Offer

At Bunge, you'll join a collaborative and international environment where sustainability, innovation and data-driven decision-making come together.

We offer:


A highly visible role within Bunge's Global Sustainability organization.
The opportunity to contribute to sustainability initiatives with global reach and impact.
Exposure to a wide range of stakeholders across different regions, functions and businesses.
Continuous learning and professional development opportunities.
A dynamic and purpose-driven work environment within one of the world's leading agribusiness and food companies.
Competitive compensation and benefits.
Attractive office next to Rotterdam Blaak station.


Interested?

Click the Apply button and let’s connect.

We look forward to hearing from you.

At Bunge (NYSE: BG), our purpose is to connect farmers to consumers to deliver essential food, feed and fuel to the world. As a premier agribusiness solutions provider, our team of :34,000 dedicated employees partner with farmers across the globe to move agricultural commodities from where they’re grown to where they’re needed—in faster, smarter, and more efficient ways. We are a world leader in grain origination, storage, distribution, oilseed processing and refining, offering a broad portfolio of plant-based oils, fats, and proteins. We work alongside our customers at both ends of the value chain to deliver quality products and develop tailored, innovative solutions that address evolving consumer needs. With 200+ years of experience and presence in over 50 countries, we are committed to strengthening global food security, advancing sustainability, and helping communities prosper where we operate. Bunge has its registered office in Geneva, Switzerland and its corporate headquarters in St. Louis, Missouri. Learn more at Bunge.com.

Every day our people exemplify these values, which represent Bunge at its core:


We Are One Team – Collaborative, Respectful, Inclusive
We Lead The Way – Agile, Empowered, Innovative
We Do What’s Right – Safety, Sustainability, With Integrity


If this sounds like you, join us! We value and invest in people who believe in our purpose and are excited to live it every day – people who are #ProudtoBeBunge

As a premier agribusiness solutions provider, our team of :34,000 dedicated employees partner with farmers across the globe to move agricultural commodities from where they’re grown to where they’re needed—in faster, smarter, and more efficient ways. We are a world leader in grain origination, storage, distribution, oilseed processing and refining, offering a broad portfolio of plant-based oils, fats, and proteins. We work alongside our customers at both ends of the value chain to deliver quality products and develop tailored, innovative solutions that address evolving consumer needs. With 200+ years of experience and presence in over 50 countries, we are committed to strengthening global food security, advancing sustainability, and helping communities prosper where we operate. Bunge has its registered office in Geneva, Switzerland and its corporate headquarters in St. Louis, Missouri. Learn more at Bunge.com.

Every day our people exemplify these values, which represent Bunge at its core:

  • We Are One Team – Collaborative, Respectful, Inclusive
  • We Lead The Way – Agile, Empowered, Innovative
  • We Do What’s Right – Safety, Sustainability, With Integrity

If this sounds like you, join us! We value and invest in people who believe in our purpose and are excited to live it every day – people who are #ProudtoBeBunge

Title: Data-analist
Description:
Tilburg University | Library & IT Services

is op zoek naar een




Data-analist / Procescoördinator




Afdeling: Collectiemanagement & Publieksdiensten – TiU Library

Locatie: Tilburg

Contractomvang: 0,8 fte (32 uur per week)

Fulltime bruto maandsalaris: €3057 – €3985

Contractduur: 12 maanden, mogelijk opgevolgd door een vast contract

Beoogde startdatum: 1 oktober 2026, of eerder indien mogelijk




Wil jij de impact van de universiteitsbibliotheek op onderzoek en onderwijs beter meetbaar en zichtbaar maken? En kun je die data-inzichten direct omzetten in concrete procesverbeteringen? Dan zoeken wij jou!




Jouw functie

In deze functie als Data-analist / Procescoördinator combineer je twee complementaire taken: data-analyse uit bibliotheeksystemen en wetenschappelijke informatiebronnen én het verbeteren van processen over afdelingsgrenzen heen. Een belangrijk speerpunt in het beleidsplan van de UB is het optimaliseren van de dienstverlening op basis van datagedreven keuzes en procesmanagement. Je werkt dus in een belangrijke rol waarin je direct bijdraagt aan het verbeteren van de dienstverlening aan onderzoekers, studenten en andere gebruikers van de UB.




De data uit verschillende bibliotheek- en bedrijfssystemen, repositories en wetenschappelijke informatiebronnen groeit snel en vergroot onze mogelijkheid om gerichte verbeteringen door te voeren. Een belangrijke uitdaging is om data-inzichten en procesoptimalisatie nog beter op elkaar af te stemmen. In je rol als Data-analist / Procescoördinator zorg je ervoor dat data en processen elkaar versterken en bijdragen aan de continue verbetering van de bedrijfsvoering van de bibliotheek.




Als data-analist werk je aan het beschikbaar stellen, controleren en verrijken van gegevens uit diverse databronnen, zodat deze bruikbare inzichten opleveren voor verbetering van de dienstverlening. Je zorgt voor data van hoge kwaliteit, met oog voor volledigheid en consistentie, zodat die gegevens ook bruikbaar zijn voor analyse van gebruikspatronen en contentlicenties. Je bent behendig in het opstellen van dashboards, rapportages en visualisaties die bruikbare inzichten bieden. Je presenteert je bevindingen en adviezen aan het management en relevante stakeholders. Je draagt bij aan het meetbaar en zichtbaar maken van de impact van de UB op onderzoek en onderwijs.




Als procescoördinator speel je ook een belangrijke rol bij het stroomlijnen van processen over afdelingsgrenzen heen. Je werkt hierin samen met inhoudelijk deskundige collega’s op de werkvloer, met functioneel beheerders en de LIS-procesbeheerder. Je coördineert het vastleggen en optimaliseren van UB-processen en voert regie op de PDCA-cyclus. Je fungeert als verbindende schakel tussen processen van verschillende afdelingen. Je monitort en rapporteert over de voortgang en effectiviteit van processen. Je initieert en begeleidt verbeterprojecten op het gebied van procesoptimalisatie, bewaakt kwaliteitsstandaarden en zorgt voor draagvlak voor nieuwe inzichten. Daarmee vervul je een belangrijke rol en draag je bij aan een toekomstbestendige en gebruiksgerichte informatievoorziening.




Jouw profiel

Opleiding en/of werk- en denkniveau: Afgeronde opleiding (HBO of hoger) in een richting zoals data-science, informatiekunde, bedrijfskunde, procesmanagement of een vergelijkbare richting.
Werkervaring: Minimaal 2 jaar relevante werkervaring met data-analyse en/of procescoördinatie. Ook minder ervaren kandidaten met aantoonbare affiniteit voor data-analyse en procesmanagement worden uitgenodigd te solliciteren.
Talen: Minimaal C1 Engels en C1 Nederlands.
Vaardigheden:
Data-analyse: werken met data uit verschillende systemen, gegevens combineren, rapportages opstellen.
Procesmatig werken: processen bewaken, stroomlijnen en verbeteren.
Digitale vaardigheden: Kennis van data-analyse tools als Power BI, SQL, Excel, Python en R. Kennis van protocollen en tools zoals COUNTER, Celus, MARC21/RDA is een pré. Kennis van tools als MS Visio en interesse in methodieken als LEAN en Agile.
Communicatieve vaardigheden: bevindingen presenteren, management adviseren.
Competenties:
Je bent geïnteresseerd in het vertalen van data-inzichten naar concrete procesverbeteringen.
Je bent nauwkeurig en hebt oog voor detail.
Je trekt graag de kar om kennis en vaardigheden te verbeteren.
Je werkt graag samen, bent flexibel en servicegericht.




Ons aanbod

Tilburg University biedt uitstekende arbeidsvoorwaarden in een professionele én gastvrije werkomgeving:

Dienstverband van 0,8 fte (32 uur per week).
Salaris tussen €3.057,- en €3.985,- bruto per maand bij een fulltime dienstverband (UFO-profiel Medewerker Bibliotheek Services 1, salarisschaal 7). Tilburg University hanteert een neutrale beloningssystematiek die uitgaat van relevante werkervaring.
Dit is een vacature conform artikel 2.3 lid 1 CAO NU. Je krijgt een tijdelijk contract voor 12 maanden. Verlenging is bespreekbaar.
Vakantietoeslag van 8% en een eindejaarsuitkering van 8,3%.
Ruim 8 weken vakantie.
Hybride werken met thuiswerkvergoeding (€2 p/dag) en internetvergoeding (€25 p/maand).
Vergoeding duurzaam woon-werkverkeer: wandelen, fietsen en OV.
Keuzemodel voor extra verlof, pensioen, fiets of personal training.
Verhuiskostenvergoeding (onder voorwaarden).
30%-regeling voor internationale medewerkers (indien van toepassing).
Pensioen via ABP, waarvan TiU 70% van de premie betaalt en jij slechts 30%.
Ruim opleidingsaanbod: persoonlijke ontwikkeling, leiderschap, onderwijs, onderzoek of taalcursussen.
Inclusieve werkcultuur: iedereen welkom, gelijke kansen.
Een groene campus in Tilburg, goed bereikbaar met OV.




Kijk voor meer informatie op onze website en in de CAO Nederlandse Universiteiten.




Meer weten?

Wil je meer weten voordat je solliciteert? Neem gerust contact op met Gerard Bierens via g.j.m.bierens@tilburguniversity.edu.




Solliciteren?

We nodigen je van harte uit om uiterlijk 21 augustus 2026 te solliciteren; dit kan uitsluitend online. Richt je motivatiebrief aan Gerard Bierens en voeg je CV toe. We kijken uit naar je reactie.




Je start idealiter 1 oktober 2026, of eerder indien mogelijk, met werken voor Tilburg University.




Deze vacature wordt eerst 7 dagen intern opengesteld, waarna de vacature extern zal worden gepubliceerd. Bij gelijke geschiktheid, gaat onze voorkeur uit naar een interne kandidaat.




Over Tilburg University

Tilburg University is dé universiteit voor mens- en maatschappijwetenschappen. Al sinds onze oprichting in 1927 zetten we ons in voor een rechtvaardige, inclusieve en duurzame samenleving. Met toonaangevend onderzoek en inspirerend onderwijs dragen wij bij aan duiding en waar mogelijk aan oplossingen voor actuele maatschappelijke vraagstukken, van klimaatverandering tot digitalisering

Onze gemeenschap is persoonlijk, betrokken en kleinschalig. Op onze groene, compacte en levendige campus is ruimte voor ontmoeting, samenwerking én reflectie en persoonlijke groei. Lees meer over Tilburg University.

Werken bij Tilburg University doe je tegen aantrekkelijke arbeidsvoorwaarden in een inclusief team. Hier krijg je volop kansen voor het versterken van je talenten en het ontwikkelen van een loopbaan. In een warme werksfeer waarin we hechten aan de waarden: curious, connected, caring en courageous.




Over de divisie Library & IT Services (LIS)

De divisie Library & IT Services (LIS) staat voor het realiseren van state-of-the-art informatievoorziening, IT-diensten en bibliotheekdienstverlening voor onderwijs, onderzoek en bedrijfsvoering van Tilburg University. LIS stelt zich daarbij op als Adviseur en dienstverlener. Vanuit die positie bieden wij moderne voorzieningen op het gebied van informatievoorziening en informatie – en communicatietechnologie. Daarnaast is de divisie verantwoordelijk voor de wetenschappelijke informatievoorziening en bibliotheek services. De divisie telt circa 200 medewerkers en bestaat uit 6 afdelingen; IT-dienstverlening, IT-Vernieuwing, Projecten & Informatievoorziening, CIO office, Universiteitsbibliotheek en de universiteitskrant Univers. Lees meer over de divisie Library & IT Services | Tilburg University.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Marketing Data Analyst – Marketing Mix & Audience Insights

Eindhoven, The Netherlands




I am currently seeking for a Marketing Data Analyst to join a dynamic team and drive impactful marketing decisions through analytics, audience insights, and data-driven strategy.




This is a role where you will have a big impact within a global organisation, you will be resposible for a number of different Marketing and innovative projects.




Dutch speaking is required for this role.




Key Responsibilities:

Develop and maintain Marketing Mix Models (MMM) to measure the effectiveness of campaigns and optimize media spend.
Support the design and execution of marketing strategies, using data to inform channel allocation, messaging, and creative decisions.
Conduct audience segmentation to identify high-value customer groups and uncover opportunities for growth.
Implement audience targeting strategies, ensuring campaigns reach the right people at the right time.
Analyze campaign performance and provide actionable insights to improve ROI and overall marketing effectiveness.
Collaborate closely with marketing, product, and analytics teams to deliver integrated, data-driven solutions.




Required Skills & Experience:

Proven experience in marketing analytics, preferably with exposure to Marketing Mix Modelling.
Strong knowledge of audience segmentation, targeting, and personalization techniques.
Proficiency in data analysis tools (e.g., SQL, Python, R, Excel, Tableau/Power BI).
Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders.
Excellent communication and problem-solving skills.




What We Offer:

Opportunity to shape marketing strategy with data at the core.
Collaborative environment with cross-functional exposure.
Career development in a fast-growing, innovative company.
Competitive salary and benefits package.




If you’re ready to leverage data to drive smarter marketing decisions and deliver measurable impact, we want to hear from you!

Title: BI Specialist / Data Analyst
Description:
Epassi’s purpose is to boost everyday well-being.

We are a leading provider of employee benefit solutions in Europe. We were established in Finland in 2007, and in 2008 we were the first company to launch a mobile-payable employee benefit payment solution in Europe. Since then, we have consistently grown, diversifying our products and introducing our services into new markets. Epassi has been awarded by the Financial Times as one of the fastest-growing companies in Europe on multiple occasions.

Epassi's purpose is to boost everyday wellbeing. We are a leading provider of employee benefit solutions across Europe, established in Finland in 2007. We were the first company in Europe to launch a mobile-payable employee benefit payment solution in 2008, and have consistently grown since — diversifying our products and entering new markets. Epassi has been recognised by the Financial Times as one of Europe's fastest-growing companies on multiple occasions.

We are currently hiring a BI Specialist / Data Analyst to be based in Amsterdam. In this role, you will translate complex data into actionable business insights that drive strategic decision-making across the organisation. You'll design, develop, and maintain reporting solutions and dashboards, working closely with stakeholders across departments to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and relevance. You act as the bridge between data and actionable insights — based in Amsterdam in hybrid setup (3 days in office, 2 days remote).

Responsibilities


Design, develop and maintain BI dashboards, reports and data models in Power BI, connecting to data in Databricks.
Translate business requirements into analytical solutions and data products.
Ensure data integrity, consistency and governance across all reporting layers.
Act as a trusted data advisor to business stakeholders across departments.
Collect, clean and transform data from multiple sources using SQL and ETL tools.
Build and maintain automated reports and interactive dashboards for management and operational teams.
Conduct ad-hoc data analyses to support business questions and strategic initiatives.
Collaborate with IT and data engineering on data warehouse and pipeline improvements.
Document data definitions, logic and methodologies to support data literacy across the organisation.


Requirements


Minimum 3-5 years of hands-on experience in a BI or data analyst role, ideally within a SaaS/tech organisation.
Advanced Power BI skills, including DAX, data modelling and report design.
Strong hands-on expertise in SQL - several years of experience with the tool.
Experience with ETL/ELT pipelines and data warehousing concepts.
Demonstrated ability to turn raw data into business-ready insights and communicate findings to non-technical audiences.
Proven track record of building dashboards and data models used in production.
Experience working with cross-functional stakeholders and translating business needs into data requirements.
Bachelor's or Master's degree in Data Science, Business Intelligence, Information Management, Computer Science or a related field.
English — professional working proficiency (required). Dutch — advantageous but not mandatory.


Preferred


Microsoft Power BI certification (PL-300).
Experience working with Databricks as a data platform.


Your Track Record


You have owned analytics for a business area or market.
You have delivered insights that influenced real decisions.
You have built reporting trusted by senior stakeholders.
You have worked long enough in roles to develop and improve analytics, not just deliver quick outputs.


Key competencies


Analytical mindset: You approach problems with structure and attention to detail, turning ambiguous data challenges into clear, actionable conclusions.
Communication: You present complex data findings clearly to both technical and non-technical audiences, making insights accessible and actionable.
Proactivity: You are curious and self-driven — able to spot opportunities in data without being asked, and take initiative to turn them into insights.
Collaboration: You are a team player who builds trust across departments, working seamlessly with IT, finance, operations and beyond.
Data ownership: You take responsibility for data quality, governance and documentation, ensuring the business can rely on the information you provide.


Why join us


Be part of an ambitious growth journey and join a global community of driven, bold, entrepreneurial Epassians.
Work with a clear purpose — boosting everyday wellbeing — and create a meaningful, positive impact on individuals and society.
Thrive in an open and inclusive culture with low hierarchy, built on respect, camaraderie, and shared success.
Grow personally and professionally in a supportive environment where employee wellbeing is a true priority.
Join a fast-growing tech company with a strong financial foundation, with real opportunities to make an impact.
Help shape our position as the number one player in Europe's employee benefit market.
