Title: Product Analyst
Description:
We are Sweden's leading app and marketplace for booking beauty and wellness services. As a SaaS company with industry-leading software, we empower more than 16,000 professionals across the country. Our platform brings together nearly every salon and clinic in Sweden, making it easy for consumers to book appointments with hairdressers, massage therapists, podiatrists, beauty salons, chiropractors, and many other specialists—all in one place.

On Bokadirekt.se, consumers can discover, book, and pay for treatments and services in just a few clicks. Every month, our platform is visited by 3 million consumers, and more than 2 million bookings are made through our website and app. We’re growing rapidly and are currently a team of more than 100 employees, with offices in Stockholm and Luleå.

About The Role

We are looking for a Product Analyst to join our Data & Analytics team and serve as the dedicated analytics partner to our Product organisation.

As a Product Analyst, you will help our product teams make better decisions through data. You'll work closely with product managers, designers, and engineers to uncover user insights, measure product performance, and drive experimentation across the customer journey. You'll play a key role in establishing best practices for product analytics, event tracking, and experimentation while enabling teams to make confident, data-informed decisions.

Your insights will directly influence the experience of millions of consumers and thousands of professionals using Bokadirekt every month.

Beyond product analytics, we're looking for someone with an AI-native mindset—someone who knows how to leverage AI to work smarter while applying sound judgement to validate AI-generated insights and recommendations. This is an opportunity to shape how analytics, experimentation, and AI contribute to the continued growth of one of Sweden's most widely used consumer platforms.

What You'll Do


Drive product insights across the full customer journey by identifying user behaviour, friction points, drop-offs, and growth opportunities
Own A/B testing end-to-end, from experiment design and statistical validation to analysis and post-mortems
Champion our experimentation framework by coaching product managers, designers, and engineers on experiment design and data-informed decision-making
Collaborate with Product and Engineering teams to define, implement, and continuously improve reliable event tracking standards
Leverage AI tools to accelerate analysis, improve productivity, and apply sound judgement when validating AI-generated insights


Who You Are


Degree in Business, Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field
Proven experience (2–4 years) as a Product Analyst or Data Analyst, ideally in a technology-driven environment
Strong proficiency in SQL
Hands-on experience designing, analysing, and evaluating A/B tests
Experience working with product event tracking in fast-moving environments where processes and data are continuously evolving
Comfortable bringing structure to ambiguous situations and turning insights into action
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders
Experience with GCP (BigQuery, Looker), Amplitude, Mixpanel, Confidence by Spotify or similar
Professional fluency in English; Swedish is a plus


What We Offer


The opportunity to influence product strategy and user experience through data at one of Sweden's most widely used consumer apps
The opportunity to shape how product decisions are made through experimentation, analytics, and AI
A collaborative culture built on ownership, innovation, and knowledge sharing
A flexible hybrid work model with four days in the office each week, including Wednesdays as our "Inspiration Day," when the whole company comes together for breakfast and shared activities
A modern office in the heart of Stockholm
A competitive salary and benefits package

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
You know that bit where every company says they’re “changing the world”? Yeah, it can feel a little forced. But we like to think our story actually has some realness to it.




Our story begins with Shadi Bitar and Ninos Malki, who came to Sweden from Syria as kids and discovered that books could be a bridge - helping them learn the language, understand the culture, and explore new worlds.




A thought emerged, what if everyone could have that? And just like that, Nextory was born - your all-access pass to read, listen, and discover new perspectives without limits. And now, we're on a quest to find a Data Scientist.




Let us tell you about the role 📖

As a Data Scientist at Nextory, you will be part of our centralized data team, supporting our Business value stream with an analytical mindset and tech-savvy approach to drive sustainable subscriber growth. You will work closely with stakeholders across Business, Marketing, and Product to create data-driven insights and models that improve decision-making and business performance.




Some examples of what you might be spending your time on:

Owning projections and forecasts related to activations and new paying subscribers
Supporting budgeting, forecasting, and ad hoc business analysis with actionable insights
Building and improving Nextory’s central data capabilities and ways of working
Supporting the Performance Marketing team with MMM, attribution models, and other analytical frameworks to improve marketing efficiency
Drive data-informed growth by analyzing user behavior, optimizing funnels, and running A/B tests to improve acquisition and conversion.
Build dashboards, reporting tools, and measurement frameworks in collaboration with Product and Business to provide clear performance insights and support Nextory’s growth strategy.




The main character we’re looking for 👀

We believe that you are currently working in a similar role at a fast-moving, product-focused company. You enjoy solving complex business challenges through data and are confident working with multiple stakeholders and know how to balance business needs with analytical rigor.




Furthermore, we believe you have:

Several years of experience working as a Data Scientist or Data Analyst
Strong knowledge of SQL and Python
Experience working with performance marketing, attribution modeling, Marketing Mix Modeling (MMM), or related analytical methods
Good understanding of digital marketing channels such as Google Ads, Meta, SEO, and online customer acquisition
Experience building dashboards or working with BI tools (e.g. Looker, Tableau, or similar)




The Nextory way of working 🏠

At Nextory, we believe a strong and inclusive culture is key to our success - and that culture flourishes when we meet and collaborate in person as much as possible. That’s why we spend the majority of the working time at our beautiful office!




We are based in Stockholm, Sweden, with offices in Paris and Bangalore. Our hybrid work policy includes four days in the office and Wednesdays as a work-from-home day.




Your next story starts here 💫

We look forward to your application. Applications are reviewed continuously, if you have any questions about the role, please contact Erika Thorslund, Talent Acquisition Partner, erika.thorslund@nextory.com




Please note that we do not offer relocation and that a six-month probationary period applies.

Title: Master Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Job Location: Tumba

Working Model: Hybrid

Master Data Analyst

Do you want to work in a global company where you can contribute to a more sustainable food production? DeLaval is a leading developer, manufacturer, and supplier of solutions for dairy farmers around the world. As a company built on innovation, we constantly work to find ways of helping our customers do more with less. 

Role and responsibilities

In the area of Data Management we are seeking an Analyst who will work to ensure the quality and consistency of global master data within DeLaval. In this role your task is to continuously improve and simplify our way of working with SAP and support and drive assignments/projects in the MDM area.

Purpose Of Role And Key Responsibilities


Drive / participate improvement assignments/projects in the Master Data area
Creates processes to ensure master data quality and consistency
Enhance the usage of data as a base for business development
Drive Automation and simplification of our way of working with data, cross sites, systems and functions


Qualifications

To be successful in this role we believe that you have:


Computer Science Master degree, or extensive practical experience
Experience in Master Data Management
Experience in SAP ERP (SD,MM, Query creation), and in particular SAP S/4 HANA and SAP MDG is a plus
Experience from Business Transformation or Business Development from a Value Chain perspective is seen as a plus
Project Management experience from 3-5 large scale cross functional projects
Experience from Conceptual Data Modelling is seen as a plus
Fluent in English, spoken and written


Working at DeLaval

Our core values Passion, People, Professionals and Partnerships connect individuals across entities and geographies. Success is far more than knowledge – it is strongly related to behaviour. At DeLaval we are all guided by our three behaviours: Set Direction, Make Things Happen and One DeLaval. 

 

We Offer You a Workplace Like No Other, Where State-of-the-art Technology Goes Hand In Hand With Animal Welfare. DeLaval Is a Part Of The Tetra Laval Group Together With Tetra Pak And Sidel. In Addition, We Offer


Secure employment and benefits from the collective agreement (occupational pension, work-related accident insurance).
Parental leave compensation (approx. 10 percent of the employee's monthly salary).
Wellness allowance of SEK 4 000/year.
Private healthcare insurance.
Flexible working hours and reduced working hours.
30 days paid annual leave.


 

Additional information

This is a full-time permanent position based at DeLaval’s headquarters in Tumba, south of Stockholm. We offer a hybrid workplace model, allowing flexibility to work remotely.

We review applications ongoing. We do not accept applications via email, however if you have any questions regarding the role or the recruitment process don’t hesitate to contact us.

Contact

Hiring manager: Per Guhres, per.guhres@delaval.com

Recruiter: Agnes Edbom, agnes.edbom@delaval.com

About DeLaval

Our vision is to make sustainable food production possible. As a company built on innovation, we constantly work to find ways of helping our customers, dairy farmers, do more with less by providing world-leading milking equipment and solutions.

Today, DeLaval has approximately 4,500 passionate professionals operating in more than 100 markets around the world. DeLaval is part of the Tetra Laval Group. 

See more at corporate.delaval.com

Title: Data Scientist / Data Analyst - Automotive
Description:
WeQuel is looking for a Data Scientist / Data Analyst

We are currently seeking experienced Data Scientists and Data Analysts for assignments within the automotive industry in the Stockholm/Södertälje area. You will work in a data-driven environment where your analytical models and tools directly support strategic business decisions – from pricing and asset management to lifecycle planning for next-generation vehicle platforms.

You are someone who combines strong technical skills with a genuine curiosity for how data can solve real business problems, and you are comfortable working across both analytical and engineering disciplines.

We are looking for candidates with experience in one or more of the following:


Building analytical models that support business decisions, such as forecasting, pricing, or lifecycle planning
Strong proficiency in Python and SQL as core working tools
Developing dashboards, analytical prototypes, or APIs
Working with large datasets and translating findings into actionable insights
Collaborating with data engineering teams to ensure reliable and scalable outputs
Relevant degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, or Engineering


Meritorious:


Experience from the automotive industry
Background in residual value modelling, financial analytics, or asset management
Experience working in agile, cross-functional teams
Familiarity with cloud environments or BI tools such as Power BI or Snowflake


Location: Stockholm/Södertälje

Language requirements: Fluent English required, Swedish is a merit

What We Offer

At WeQuel, you will become part of a consulting team that puts people first. We believe that success is built through engagement, collaboration, and continuous development. We offer a flat organization with short decision-making processes, a strong sense of community, and the opportunity to actively shape your own professional growth.

Title: Ecommerce Analyst
Description:
About the Digital Db Team

Db is a fast-growing brand creating innovative travel gear for creatives, athletes, and modern explorers. We're passionate about delivering outstanding customer experiences that match the quality of our products.

As we continue to scale our e-commerce business globally, we are looking for an Ecommerce Analyst to join our Digital team.




About the Job

This role sits within the Digital team and reports directly to the Chief Digital Officer to support digital analysis across our digital head functions.

Working closely with teams across Ecommerce, Marketing, Finance, and Operations, you will help us understand what's driving performance across our ecommerce business and identify growth opportunities.

You will be responsible for ensuring the business has the insights, reporting, and data quality needed to make informed decisions.

Whether it's breaking down campaign results, investigating changes in conversion rate, forecasting future performance, or identifying opportunities to improve the customer journey, you will play a key role in helping the team make better decisions and move faster.




Key Responsibilities

Data Quality & Reporting

Own the accuracy and consistency of our ecommerce data, ensuring GA4, on-site tracking, and Shopify data can be trusted.
Partner with the Finance and Operations teams, who own the commercial and transactional data foundation (including Business Central), so that ecommerce reporting ties back to a single, agreed source of truth.
Build and maintain the reports and dashboards the team relies on, ensuring key metrics are clearly defined and consistently measured.
Investigate and resolve tracking discrepancies across key platforms.
Utilize tools such as Claude and other modern technologies to improve analysis, reporting, and decision-making.

Ecommerce Analytics & Insights

Monitor and analyze ecommerce performance across key metrics such as traffic, conversion rate, revenue, average order value, customer behavior, and retention.
Identify trends, opportunities, and performance drivers across markets, categories, products, and customer segments.
Investigate unexpected changes in performance and identify root causes.
Translate data into actionable recommendations that support business growth and improve customer experience.
Present findings and recommendations in a clear and actionable way.

Commercial Performance & Planning

Analyze campaign, category, product, and market performance to identify growth opportunities.
Support forecasting, promotional planning, and commercial decision-making through data-driven insights.
Evaluate promotional activities and provide recommendations to improve future performance.
Deliver post-campaign analysis, highlight key learnings and opportunities, and share results with the Marketing team.
Monitor customer behavior throughout the purchase journey, including conversion trends and cart abandonment performance.

SEO Performance

Monitor and report on organic search performance.
Identify opportunities and measure the impact of SEO efforts.
Experimentation Support
Support Product and Development teams by providing data, analysis, and insights for experimentation and optimization initiatives.
Evaluate the impact of A/B tests and changes.




Qualifications & Skills

Experience

3+ years of experience in Ecommerce Analytics, Digital Analytics, Business Intelligence, Marketing Analytics, or a similar analytical role.
Experience working with Google Analytics 4 (GA4).
Experience working within Shopify or a similar ecommerce platform.
Experience working with reporting and visualization tools such as Power BI.
Experience working with digital marketing platforms and conversion tracking.
Experience working cross-functionally with multiple stakeholders and external partners.
Experience working within e-commerce, retail, or consumer brands.
Experience supporting experimentation, optimization, or A/B testing initiatives is a plus.
Experience analyzing ecommerce KPIs including conversion rate, customer acquisition, retention, average order value, and revenue performance.

Skills

Strong analytical skills and the ability to translate data into business recommendations.
Advanced Excel or Google Sheets skills.
Strong communication skills and the ability to present insights in a clear and actionable way.
Comfortable in a fast-moving, high-growth environment.
Comfortable working with large datasets and validating data quality; working knowledge of SQL is a plus.
Understanding of SEO principles and digital marketing measurement.
Interest in AI tools and emerging technologies.
Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
Commercial mindset with a passion for ecommerce and customer experience.




What We Offer

The opportunity to work with a passionate and international team.
A dynamic, fast-paced work environment where your input makes a real impact.
Competitive compensation and benefits package.
Employee discounts on Db products.
An inclusive culture where you can be who you are and that values work-life balance, with flexibility and understanding to help you perform your best both inside and outside of work.
A fun group of people passionate about what makes Db connected to our strong communities: Outdoor activities, Urban Fashion, Meaningful Journeys, and Connecting with people across the world.







Application deadline:

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis from mid August.

  • Experience working with Google Analytics 4 (GA4).
  • Experience working within Shopify or a similar ecommerce platform.
  • Experience working with reporting and visualization tools such as Power BI.
  • Experience working with digital marketing platforms and conversion tracking.
  • Experience working cross-functionally with multiple stakeholders and external partners.
  • Experience working within e-commerce, retail, or consumer brands.
  • Experience supporting experimentation, optimization, or A/B testing initiatives is a plus.
  • Experience analyzing ecommerce KPIs including conversion rate, customer acquisition, retention, average order value, and revenue performance.

Skills

  • Strong analytical skills and the ability to translate data into business recommendations.
  • Advanced Excel or Google Sheets skills.
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to present insights in a clear and actionable way.
  • Comfortable in a fast-moving, high-growth environment.
  • Comfortable working with large datasets and validating data quality; working knowledge of SQL is a plus.
  • Understanding of SEO principles and digital marketing measurement.
  • Interest in AI tools and emerging technologies.
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously.
  • Commercial mindset with a passion for ecommerce and customer experience.


What We Offer

  • The opportunity to work with a passionate and international team.
  • A dynamic, fast-paced work environment where your input makes a real impact.
  • Competitive compensation and benefits package.
  • Employee discounts on Db products.
  • An inclusive culture where you can be who you are and that values work-life balance, with flexibility and understanding to help you perform your best both inside and outside of work.
  • A fun group of people passionate about what makes Db connected to our strong communities: Outdoor activities, Urban Fashion, Meaningful Journeys, and Connecting with people across the world.



Application deadline:

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis from mid August.

Title: Decision Analyst
Description:
About The Role

As a Decision Analyst at Instabee, you’ll play a key role in helping us make smarter, faster decisions across the business. You’ll partner with teams across the company from product, operations, to tech and beyond - to understand challenges, gather insights, and co-create solutions. Whether it’s structuring an ambiguous problem, evaluating new data sources, or communicating a compelling recommendation, you’ll bring clarity and impact. This role is perfect for someone who loves solving problems, is comfortable navigating complexity, and sees data as a powerful tool, not an end in itself.

Some of the things you’ll do


Tackle complex and ambiguous problems by bringing structure and taking clear, decisive action
Translate complex data and information into concise, actionable insights using both qualitative and quantitative methods
Collaborate across diverse teams to co-create smart solutions and drive real change
Communicate insights clearly and confidently to stakeholders at all levels, from junior teams to senior leadership
Continuously explore and integrate new internal and external data sources to strengthen our decision-making foundation
Analyze data from multiple sources to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities
Partner with teams to understand business challenges and build tailored tools or frameworks that improve how we work
Act as a go-to person for analytical support—sharing knowledge, guiding ad-hoc analysis, and elevating decision quality across the company


Your future team

You’ll join a tight-knit team working on Instabee’s most critical challenges. We collaborate across departments, drive clarity in ambiguity, and value real-world impact over theoretical perfection. Curiosity, humility, and a strong drive to make things better are what bring us together.

What We’re Looking For


Bachelor’s degree within engineering, information technology, finance, economics or other relevant field
Experience presenting solutions to senior management
Experience working within a data heavy environment for at least 4 years
Experience working with solving business problems and improving ways of working
Experience driving real impact, leading stakeholders to materialise the change that the insight points to
Experience using AI coding tools
Experience working with data processing tools including Front End visualization tools (e.g. Tableau, PowerBI, PowerPoint, Google Slides) and generic tools (e.g. SQL, Python, Excel/Google Sheets)


The cherry on top


You bring a strong blend of business and data understanding—and know how to connect the two
You’re curious, always learning, and stay on top of evolving best practices
You care about creating real-world results and know when “good enough” is enough
You’re confident challenging the status quo and backing it up with structured thinking
You communicate clearly and effectively in English—even when things get complex


If this is you, then we can’t wait to meet you!

What You’ll Get

We can promise you that you will be challenged, develop your skills and have the opportunity to work with truly amazing and competent colleagues. If you like change and want to set new processes and ways of working—this is the place for you!

Of course, we have all the necessary and standard benefits in place, but in addition to this, we also offer:

🌇 Sky-High Office: Modern and newly renovated with a 360-degree view around Stockholm

🐶 Puppy Friendly: Bring your dog to work—we’ve got a whole floor for our furry friends!

🏓 Fun & Games: Ping pong, shuffleboard, foosball—even a karaoke room!

👶 Parental Pay: Paid leave for six months to spend time with your little one

🎂 Birthday Day Off: Celebrate your birthday with a well-earned break

⏰ Flexible Hours: Hybrid setup and hours that work with your life

Get to know us

In 2022, Instabee was founded after combining Budbee and Instabox - two leading last-mile delivery companies with three consumer-facing brands: Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy. Instabee serves millions of consumers across Europe with convenient deliveries and is on track to become the leading European e-commerce enabler. Always with the consumer at heart.

We’re fast, both in terms of parcel delivery and when it comes to growing our business. So far our journey has taken us from Sweden to the rest of Scandinavia and parts of continental Europe, but we have no plans on stopping anytime soon. As we continue to expand, the complexity of Instabee grows in parallel. With that in mind, we’re now looking for you to help us on the journey ahead.

This recruitment process consists of background checks via Svensk Bakgrundsanalys as a review of previous public criminal records. The result is public information that is handled confidentially. This is mandatory for all recruitment processes at Instabee in Sweden.

Please note that, due to the summer holiday period, we will begin reviewing applications in August. We appreciate your patience and will be in touch with all candidates once the recruitment process resumes.

Instabee Group, with consumer-facing brands Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy, brings together years of experience in delivery. Today, Instabee Group is present in five markets, serving thousands of online merchants and millions of consumers with seamless home deliveries and convenient parcel locker deliveries, using the best fuel available. With smart technology, a strong focus on sustainability, and a secure supply chain, Instabee has become a trusted partner for online merchants and a preferred choice for consumers.

Title: Analytics Engineer II
Description:
The Platform team creates the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily, enabling rapid growth in our users and our business around the globe. Spanning many disciplines, we work to make the business work; creating the infrastructure, tooling, frameworks, and capabilities needed to welcome a billion customers.

We’re looking for an Analytics Engineer II to join Spotify's Platform Central Data (PCD) squad, a cross-functional Data Engineering and Analytics Engineering team within the Platform Mission. You’ll help build and maintain trusted analytical models, metrics, and data products that power developer productivity, platform health, and leadership decision-making. Working closely with Data Engineers, Product, Engineering, and Platform partners, you’ll translate platform signals into reliable, well-modeled data assets that help Spotify ship faster and safer.

What You"ll Do


Build and maintain analytical data models using dbt (or similar SQL-based transformation frameworks) in BigQuery for a broad set of stakeholders
Build and operate reliable data pipelines using SQL, with a focus on testing, observability, and CI/CD
Help define and evolve key metrics for platform health, developer productivity, and ML/AI platform adoption
Partner with Data Engineers on upstream pipelines and collaborate with Product, Engineering, and Data Science to scope and deliver insights
Improve data quality, performance, and cost efficiency across pipelines and models, including troubleshooting and backfills
Contribute to dashboards and self-serve data products that enable better decision-making across teams
Follow and contribute to data quality, testing, and documentation practices across the analytics layer
Participate in a fair support rotation for key datasets, pipelines, and analytical products


Who You Are


You have 2+ years of experience in analytics engineering, data engineering, or a related field
You have strong SQL skills and experience with data modelling
You are experienced with dbt (or similar SQL-based transformation frameworks) and a cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, or Databricks SQL
You are familiar with workflow orchestration tools such as Airflow, Dagster, Prefect, or Flyte
You care about data quality, reliability, and testability
You are comfortable working with BI/visualisation tools such as Looker or Tableau
You communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical partners
You are able to prioritize and deliver in a fast-moving environment
You have experience with platform or developer productivity data, experimentation, or ML/AI metrics


Where You'll Be

This role is based in London or Stockholm.

We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home.

Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.

At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice

Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

Title: BI-utvecklare - Analysenheten
Description:
Vi söker en erfaren BI utvecklare med goda kunskaper i Power BI. Rollen passar dig som gillar att röra dig mellan verksamhet och teknik och som vill vara med och utveckla smarta, datadrivna arbetssätt inom flera olika verksamhetsområden.

Uppsala kommun har över 18 000 medarbetare och erbjuder goda möjligheter att påverka, bidra och utvecklas – med fokus på arbetsglädje och en hållbar balans mellan arbete och privatliv. Du blir en del av den koncerngemensamma analysenheten på kvalitet- och planeringsavdelningen, där vi arbetar med både återkommande analyser som befolkningsprognoser och omvärldsanalyser samt riktade uppdrag från organisationen.

Inom enheten finns ett datastyrningsteam med tre informationsarkitekter och en teamledare, där du ingår. Uppdraget är att utveckla datastyrning och informationsarkitektur samt att stötta verksamheterna i deras dataarbete – både strategiskt och operativt.

Beskrivning av arbetsuppgifter

Uppsala kommun står inför att upphandla ett koncerngemensamt beslutsstöd som ska bidra till ett mer automatiserat arbetssätt inom styrning, ledning och uppföljning – och samtidigt skapa bättre förutsättningar för datadriven utveckling.

Vi söker nu en erfaren BI-utvecklare med goda kunskaper i Power BI till ett spännande uppdrag. Rollen passar dig som trivs i gränslandet mellan verksamhet och teknik. I rollen får du en central funktion i arbetet med att modernisera kommunkoncernens rapporterings- och uppföljningsstöd. Du kommer att vara med och ersätta befintliga lösningar med moderna BI-rapporter, i nära samarbete med både verksamheten och IT-specialister.

Du bygger dataset, modeller och rapporter i datalager och ser till att data presenteras på ett värdeskapande sätt. Rollen kräver att du förstår verksamhetens behov, analyserar datakällor och fungerar som en länk mellan verksamheten och IT.

Dina arbetsuppgifter:


Utveckla och implementera BI-rapporter och dashboards.
Ersätta befintligt rapportstöd med moderna BI-lösningar.
Ta fram och förvalta datamodeller och dataset.
Samla in och omsätta verksamhetsbehov till datalösningar.
Integrera och bearbeta data från flera olika datakällor.
Samverka med verksamhetsrepresentanter och IT-funktioner.


Kvalifikationer

Vi söker dig som har en högskoleutbildning inom exempelvis systemvetenskap, datavetenskap, ekonomi eller Business Intelligence. Du har minst tre års dokumenterad erfarenhet av att utveckla och implementera Power BI‑lösninga, gärna inom kommunal eller regional verksamhet.

Du har mycket goda kunskaper i datamodellering i Power BI, Power Query och DAX, inklusive optimering och prestanda. Du är van vid att integrera och bearbeta data från flera källor (t.ex. SQL Server, Excel och externa system) och har god förståelse för systemintegration och informationsflöden mellan verksamhetssytem. Du har även kunskap om datalager och moderna BI‑plattformar, gärna Microsoft Fabric.

Du har erfarenhet av data från verksamhetssystem (t.ex. HR eller ekonomi) och god kunskap om GDPR och datahantering. Du dokumenterar lösningar väl och uttrycker dig flytande på svenska i tal och skrift.

Meriterande: erfarenhet av Heroma, Lifecare, migrering från äldre verksamhetssystem till BI samt andra BI‑verktyg, t.ex. Qlik Sense.

Du har tydligt förankrade värderingar och en god förmåga att resonera kring frågor på ett etiskt och begreppsmässigt plan. I ditt arbete tar du ansvar för dina uppgifter och arbetar självständigt genom att strukturera ditt arbetssätt och driva dina processer framåt. Vidare trivs du att arbeta med komplexa frågeställningar, har en analytisk förmåga och kan bryta ner problem i mindre delar för att hitta lösningar även på mer avancerade utmaningar.

Du samarbetar väl med andra och möter människor på ett lyhört och smidigt sätt. Du har en god förståelse för hur människor tar till sig kunskap och att olika individer har olika förutsättningar. Du anpassar därför ditt sätt att kommunicera efter mottagaren.

Upplysningar

Har du frågor om tjänsten eller rekryteringsprocessen, kontakta: Fredrik Tormod, analyschef, 018-726 33 39.

Fackliga företrädare:

Akavia och Sacorådet, Camilla Ahlsén Hoff, 018-727 24 34.

DIK, Erik Boman, 018-727 18 99.

Vision, Liselotte Ehn, 018-727 49 49.

Har du frågor gällande registrering av din ansökan, kontakta område rekrytering: 018-727 12 86.

Uppsala kommun tillämpar individuell och differentierad lönesättning enligt gällande löneavtal.

I din ansökan ska du ange medborgarskap. Om du inte är medborgare i Sverige, EU, EES eller Schweiz måste du också kunna styrka att du har ett giltigt arbetstillstånd eller är undantagen från skyldigheten att ha arbetstillstånd. Anställning kan ske först efter kontroll av arbetstillstånd, vilket krävs enligt utlänningsförordningen.

En anställning hos Uppsala kommun kan komma att innebära en krigsplacering.

Om vår organisation

Välkommen till kommunledningskontoret. Här jobbar du tillsammans med medarbetare på avdelningar för kommunikation, hr, it, ekonomi, upphandling och fastighet samt avdelningarna kvalitet och planering, trygghet och hållbarhet samt kansli. På kommunledningskontoret ligger även organisationerna för näringsliv och destination samt gemensam service. Kommunledningskontoret är länken mellan våra förtroendevaldas beslut och kommunens alla verksamheter. Vårt uppdrag är bland annat att samordna och driva övergripande frågor inom demokrati, samhälls- och näringslivsutveckling samt ekologisk och social hållbarhet. Mer information om hur det är att arbeta i Uppsala kommun hittar du på https://www.uppsala.se/jobb/

Anställningsvillkor

Anställningsform: Tillsvidareanställning Omfattning: Heltid Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse Lönebeskrivning: Månadslön

Vi undanber oss erbjudanden om annonserings- och rekryteringshjälp.

Hantering av personuppgifter

Dina personuppgifter kommer att användas för att administrera din ansökan inom Uppsala kommun.

För dig med skyddad identitet

Du som söker jobb och har skyddade personuppgifter ska ringa det telefonnummer som finns angivet vid "Har du frågor gällande registrering av din ansökan" så kommer du att bli guidad vidare för en säker ansökningsprocess.

Nytt system för jobbansökningar

Vi använder nu ett nytt system för att ta emot jobbansökningar. Det betyder att du inte längre kan logga in med ett gammalt konto. Du är varmt välkommen att skapa ett nytt konto eller att söka jobbet utan ett konto.

Meriterande: erfarenhet av Heroma, Lifecare, migrering från äldre verksamhetssystem till BI samt andra BI‑verktyg, t.ex. Qlik Sense.

Du har tydligt förankrade värderingar och en god förmåga att resonera kring frågor på ett etiskt och begreppsmässigt plan. I ditt arbete tar du ansvar för dina uppgifter och arbetar självständigt genom att strukturera ditt arbetssätt och driva dina processer framåt. Vidare trivs du att arbeta med komplexa frågeställningar, har en analytisk förmåga och kan bryta ner problem i mindre delar för att hitta lösningar även på mer avancerade utmaningar.

Du samarbetar väl med andra och möter människor på ett lyhört och smidigt sätt. Du har en god förståelse för hur människor tar till sig kunskap och att olika individer har olika förutsättningar. Du anpassar därför ditt sätt att kommunicera efter mottagaren.

Upplysningar

Har du frågor om tjänsten eller rekryteringsprocessen, kontakta: Fredrik Tormod, analyschef, 018-726 33 39.

Fackliga företrädare:

Akavia och Sacorådet, Camilla Ahlsén Hoff, 018-727 24 34.

DIK, Erik Boman, 018-727 18 99.

Vision, Liselotte Ehn, 018-727 49 49.

Har du frågor gällande registrering av din ansökan, kontakta område rekrytering: 018-727 12 86.

Uppsala kommun tillämpar individuell och differentierad lönesättning enligt gällande löneavtal.

I din ansökan ska du ange medborgarskap. Om du inte är medborgare i Sverige, EU, EES eller Schweiz måste du också kunna styrka att du har ett giltigt arbetstillstånd eller är undantagen från skyldigheten att ha arbetstillstånd. Anställning kan ske först efter kontroll av arbetstillstånd, vilket krävs enligt utlänningsförordningen.

En anställning hos Uppsala kommun kan komma att innebära en krigsplacering.

Om vår organisation

Välkommen till kommunledningskontoret. Här jobbar du tillsammans med medarbetare på avdelningar för kommunikation, hr, it, ekonomi, upphandling och fastighet samt avdelningarna kvalitet och planering, trygghet och hållbarhet samt kansli. På kommunledningskontoret ligger även organisationerna för näringsliv och destination samt gemensam service. Kommunledningskontoret är länken mellan våra förtroendevaldas beslut och kommunens alla verksamheter. Vårt uppdrag är bland annat att samordna och driva övergripande frågor inom demokrati, samhälls- och näringslivsutveckling samt ekologisk och social hållbarhet. Mer information om hur det är att arbeta i Uppsala kommun hittar du på https://www.uppsala.se/jobb/

Anställningsvillkor

Anställningsform: Tillsvidareanställning Omfattning: Heltid Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse Lönebeskrivning: Månadslön

Vi undanber oss erbjudanden om annonserings- och rekryteringshjälp.

Hantering av personuppgifter

Dina personuppgifter kommer att användas för att administrera din ansökan inom Uppsala kommun.

För dig med skyddad identitet

Du som söker jobb och har skyddade personuppgifter ska ringa det telefonnummer som finns angivet vid "Har du frågor gällande registrering av din ansökan" så kommer du att bli guidad vidare för en säker ansökningsprocess.

Nytt system för jobbansökningar

Vi använder nu ett nytt system för att ta emot jobbansökningar. Det betyder att du inte längre kan logga in med ett gammalt konto. Du är varmt välkommen att skapa ett nytt konto eller att söka jobbet utan ett konto.

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
TL;DR - We are looking for analytics engineers to own the semantic and modeling layer of our warehouse by transforming raw data into well-defined, trustworthy datasets and metrics. You are obsessed with analytical consistency, designing impactful metrics, and building strong foundations for self-service analytics.

Why Lovable?

Lovable lets anyone and everyone build software with any language. From solopreneurs to Fortune 100 teams, millions of people use Lovable to transform raw ideas into real products - fast. We are at the forefront of a foundational shift in software creation, which means you have an unprecedented opportunity to change the way the digital world works. Lovable-built applications and websites are visited hundreds of millions of times a month, and our enterprise footprint is compounding fast. And we’re just getting started.

We’re a small, talent-dense team building a generation-defining company from Stockholm. We value extreme ownership, high velocity, and low-ego collaboration. We seek out people who care deeply, ship fast, and are eager to make a dent in the world.

What we are looking for:


Expertise with SQL, dbt, SQLMesh or similar tools (data modeling, testing, macros, docs)
Experience with data warehousing concepts, cloud warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift, Databricks) and BI tools (Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Hex, Metabase, etc)
Understanding of dimensional modeling, data contracts, and metrics/semantical layers
Familiarity with modern ELT and orchestration workflows (Airflow, Dagster, Prefect, etc)
Strong business acumen and ability to translate domain logic into scalable data structures


What you will do:


Build and maintain data models, following modular, tested, and version-controlled practices
Partner with domain teams to understand business logic and codify it into reusable models and metrics
Define and document key metrics and data contracts across domains
Collaborate with Data Platform Engineers to optimize query performance and warehouse cost
Automate and maintain data documentation, lineage, and governance standards
Develop guidelines for analytics development, data modeling and structure conventions


Our tech stack

We're building with tools that both humans and AI love:


Frontend: React
Backend: Golang and Rust
Cloud: Cloudflare, GCP, AWS, Many LLM providers
DevOps & Tooling: Github Actions, Grafana, OTEL, infrastructure-as-code (Terraform)


And always on the lookout for what's next!

How We Hire


Fill in a short form then jump on an intro call with recruiter.
Complete the general programming exercise.
Show us how you approach problems during several technical interviews.
Tell us about your most impressive project.


About Your Application


Please submit your application in English - it’s our company language so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join
We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested please apply through our careers portal

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Angry Birds 2
Description:
Rovio is part of the SEGA family and world famous for our flagship IP Angry Birds - want to know more about Rovio as an employer? Click here.

Data is at the heart of everything we do at Rovio. It enables us to continually improve our games and provide incredible experiences for the millions of users who play our games every day. All of this data is only valuable if it can be used to guide our day to day operations and the mission of the Data and Analytics craft is to turn that raw data into actionable insights.

We are now looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our flagship Angry Birds 2 game in the Stockholm studio. Coming into this role, you will build an understanding of the game and different business levers and use that knowledge to build new game features, optimising existing ones, structure vague problem statements in collaboration with key stakeholders. You will be working as a part of a live ops pod, supporting it as a dedicated team member.

The team is located in our studio in the centre of Stockholm and works in a hybrid mode (3 days per week onsite). Currently not based in Sweden? No problem at all, we will help you with the relocation and work permit if needed!

You will have impact and fun at work by:


Working with the team members of your pod, participating in the end to end process of new feature development from planning, research and testing to launch and post launch evaluation
Bringing an objective perspective and recommendations to all phases of decision making, leveraging your technical skills to mine raw data for valuable information
Constantly designing and running in-game A/B test experiments, helping the team to find options leading to better game monetization performance and higher audience satisfaction
Creating dashboards and reports to help the game team understand your findings
Proactively working with data exploration and looking for opportunities to improve gameplay experience
Working closely with peer Data Analysts, Product Managers, Designers and Producers to continuously improve how we are interpreting game metrics and making decisions
Coaching and sharing best practices between colleagues in the data craft and whole game team


Experience and skills we are looking for:


High proficiency in SQL
Proficiency in scripting languages such as Python or R
Solid understanding of statistics (hypothesis testing, regression, analysing bias)
Experience in product analysis and experiments design including A/B testing
Excellent presentation and collaboration skills with proven ability to present data and provide actionable recommendations to senior stakeholders
Prior experience as an analyst in mobile games or apps with large audiences
Passion for playing games and understanding what makes a successful feature


You'll work in a hybrid setup with two to three days at the office per week. Kindly send us your CV in English. We look forward to speaking to you!

We welcome applicants to use the name they want to be addressed by in the application form, regardless of whether it is their legal name.

At Rovio, we are also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities or conditions in our working environment and job application procedures. We are committed to continuously improving workplace accessibility. If you have any questions before applying, please write to us at recruitment@rovio.com.

What to expect when you join us

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Role Summary

Reporting to the Analytics Manager, this is a position that requires a highly professional and organised individual.

Main objective of the role is to provide analytical support to various departments within the organisation, with a

strong focus on delivering data analysis and actionable insights to support stakeholders.

Responsibilities:


Supporting the production of management reports
Maintaining and improving current reporting structures
Creating new reports either on ad hoc or regular basis
Building automated front end tools and reports using Tableau
Data Reconciliations
Undertaking data analysis to support business initiatives
Communicating findings of analysis throughout the business
In-depth customer behaviour analytics
Using data analysis to drive profitability of customers
Interrogate large databases
Other ad hoc duties required to be undertaken by the Analytics Team


Role Requirements


Educated to degree level in a numerate/marketing subject or have extensive experience in a relevant discipline
Advanced Excel skills
Understanding of business financials, ROI & profitability
Passion for Analytics
Ability to present complex information in simple terms with clear recommendations based on data insight
Strong customer focus mentality
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment, adapt easily to changing situations and demonstrates flexibility in


juggling priorities


Problem solver with a can-do attitude


Benefits

So what can you expect from ComeOn as a place of work?


A competitive remuneration package inc. a company bonus scheme! 💰
Fantastic quarterly team events and weekly company events 😎
A one-time bonus to help you set up a comfortable workstation at home (we're truly Hybrid!)
On top of our Hybrid work arrangement, up to 40 days a year to work fully remote from anywhere 🌎


At ComeOn, we've embraced a Hybrid Work model, giving our employees the flexibility to work some days at home. Our offices are thoughtfully designed to support this model, featuring practical workspaces for focused efforts and collaborative zones for dynamic creative exchanges. We believe in the power of collaboration to drive innovation and success. While our offices are known for their vibrant atmosphere, rest assured that our primary focus is on achieving results together. We prioritize efficiency and maintain a balance between hard work and camaraderie. When you visit our offices, expect a warm welcome and professional support. Choose ComeOn for your next career move, and experience a workplace that values productivity, teamwork, and the spirit of collaboration.

At ComeOn Group we value diversity and we take concrete action to ensure fairness in our recruitment process. We are committed to assess candidates on their merit without discriminating against any applicant for whatever reason that is protected by law. We truly value you, as you are.

Want to know more about us? Just visit our website www.comeon-group.com or any of our social media channels to take a deep dive into our culture!

Title: Analytics Consultant
Description:
Vill du skapa förutsättningar för bättre datadrivet beslutsfattande? Då har du kommit rätt! Vi söker dig som vill utveckla morgondagens analysplattformar för att hjälpa organisationer att jobba mer datadrivet.




Om rollen som konsult inom Data & Analytics

Som konsult inom Data & Analytics hos VASS blir du en nyckelspelare i att hjälpa våra kunder att realisera sina ambitioner mot bättre datadrivet beslutsfattande. Ditt uppdrag är att omsätta affärsbehov till moderna och effektiva analyslösningar, oftast baserat på Microsofts molnplattform Azure. Med djup förståelse för kundens affär och processer hjälper du kunden att agera utifrån deras data, vilket skapar möjligheter att optimera verksamheten framåt. Vi bygger molnbaserade data warehouse-lösningar med hjälp av Microsoft Azure och Databricks som främsta verktyg. Vi utvecklar och implementerar lösningar från start till mål och kan även diskutera arkitektur samt för- och nackdelar med olika koncept.




Rollerna det talas mest om inom analysområdet just nu är Data Analysts, Data Engineers och Data Scientists, och vi ser behov hos våra kunder inom alla dessa kompetenser. I dessa roller kombineras teknisk skicklighet med strategisk insikt, helst arbetandes sida vid sida med dina kollegor på VASS. Vill du ta din karriär till nästa nivå? Välkommen att ansöka!




Dina kompetenser och egenskaper

Utbildning som ingenjör, ekonom eller arbetslivserfarenheter som demonstrerar en stark förmåga att sätta sig in i och lösa problem.
Tidigare erfarenhet av Microsofts produkter inom Data & Analytics, gärna inom moln och gärna i rollen som konsult.
+4 års erfarenhet.
Förstår vikten av göra det lilla extra när det behövs men kan samtidigt ge sig själv (och andra) en paus när det behövs.
Trivs i att verka i en föränderlig miljö och inser att flexibilitet är en superkraft.
Talar och skriver obehindrat på svenska såväl som engelska.
Vågar ta hjälp och även hjälpa andra.
Nyfiken.




Mer om rollen

Vi rekryterar löpande och ser gärna att du kan börja så snart som möjligt men månar om att hitta rätt person. Därför värderar vi dina personliga egenskaper och att vi trivs ihop högt.




Har du frågor eller funderingar? Kontakta rekryterande chefer Andreas Åkerblad, andreas.akerblad@vasscompany.com alternativt Elin Hellström, elin.hellstrom@vasscompany.com.




Om VASS

Med en kombinerad kraft av 250 lokala experter, och fler än 4 000 globala kollegor inom VASS, tar vi en ledande roll inom IT- och management-konsulting. Vi tar fram strategier, designar och realiserar tjänster som inte bara uppfyller kundens behov, utan också driver deras affär framåt. Vårt arbete går från idé till genomförande och vidareutveckling, med fokus på människan, tekniken och affären. Vår framgång bygger på engagerade team av drivna kollegor. Vill du vara en del av vår framgång?




Hos VASS står du alltid i fokus. Här får du utrymme att påverka din utveckling och möjligheten att växa i nya roller. Vi rustar dig med rätt kunskap och de rätta verktygen för din professionella och personliga utvecklingsresa. När du växer, växer företag.

Title: BI Developer
Description:
About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth. 

WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.

For more information, visit wppmedia.com.

Role Summary And Impact

As a BI Developer at WPP Media, you will build and maintain Power BI solutions that help our specialists and clients follow up on media performance. The role is centered around developing user-friendly dashboards, establishing robust data streams, and creating well-structured data models that enable reliable reporting at scale. You’ll translate business needs into technical BI solutions and work closely with engineers, Data Scientists, client teams, and customers - often integrating outputs from different measurement and analytics projects (e.g., MMM, attribution, and other studies) into clear, actionable reporting.

Requirements

Skills and Experience


Bachelor’s degree (or equivalent experience) in Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field
Experience building and maintaining Power BI solutions, both independently and together with others
Good knowledge of SQL
Experience working in a cloud environment, preferably Google Cloud/BigQuery
Understanding of how data flows and data models are set up to support stable reporting
Swedish: good understanding in speech and writing
English: fluent in speech and writing


Meriting


Experience with data modeling and data integration
Knowledge of DAXs and/or Python
Experience from media/marketing environments (e.g., agency, publisher, platform)
Knowledge of digital marketing and media measurement
Experience working with or visualising outputs from measurement projects (e.g., MMM, attribution)


Life At WPP Media & Benefits

Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising.

In Sweden, our benefits include a SEK 3,000 wellness allowance, a 37,5-hour workweek, half-days and “klämdagar" off, 30 days of vacation, and two paid volunteer days per year. WPP Media Sweden is a member of Almega, and all employees are covered by a collective bargaining agreement (kollektivavtal), which ensures occupational pension, insurance, and other negotiated terms. We host regular team activities, knowledge-sharing sessions, and wellness initiatives throughout the year.

WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers. We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

Application

This is a full-time permanent position with a start date as soon as possible. We apply a six-month probationary period. In addition to reviewing your CV and conducting interviews, our recruitment process includes a testing phase. Selection and interviews are ongoing, so please submit your application with your CV and cover letter as soon as possible.

We believe that diverse perspectives make us stronger and encourage applications from people of all genders, backgrounds, and experiences.

Contact

If you have questions about the position, feel free to contact Viktor Åhlén, Head of Operations & Engineering viktor.ahlen@choreograph.com. Due to GDPR, we do not accept applications via email.

Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

Title: Consumer Analyst
Description:
Are you passionate about building true consumer preference—turning data and insights into real user and business impact?

At Instabee, we are on a mission to become the undisputed #1 service in the eyes of our consumers. We are now looking for a Consumer Analyst to join our Consumer Department. In this role, you will play a fundamental part in building the analytical foundation and insights required to ensure our organization is aligned, informed, and accountable for delivering a superior consumer experience across all markets.

This role is ideal for someone who combines strong analytical capabilities with business acumen and the ability to communicate findings clearly to diverse stakeholders.

About The Role

As Consumer Analyst, you will translate complex data and consumer signals into clear, actionable insights that can drive strategic and tactical impact. Partnering with teams across Data & Analytics, Product, Design, Operations, Strategy and Market to understand challenges, synthesize insights from diverse sources, and co-create solutions grounded in consumer and business relevance.

Your goal is to build a solid foundation for consumer, brand, and experience analytics, making insights accessible and actionable company-wide. Whether you are mapping the behavior of different consumer segments, uncovering what drives long-term retention, or tracking the impact of a new product feature, you will play a key role in guiding our journey toward becoming the undisputed #1 service in all our markets.

What You'll Do


Manage the Consumer Scorecard: Define and evolve our core KPIs (NPS, User ratings, Brand preference, CSAT, MAU, Consumer base metrics, Operational metrics etc.) to ensure a trusted, single source of truth for our performance across the full consumer journey and lifecycle.
Analyze Loyalty & Retention: Conduct cohort and retention analysis to identify the key drivers of consumer satisfaction and long-term loyalty. You’ll explore how different user segments behave and evolve, and translate user and service delivery insights into cross-functional guidance for the most effective ways to win, delight, and retain our consumers.
Analyze Consumer Behavior: Dive deep into user behavior across web, app, communications and customer service channels. You will analyze funnels and identify drop-off points to provide concrete product and experience recommendations. This includes proactively identifying tracking gaps and defining what needs to be measured and how, to ensure decisions are grounded in reliable data.
Translate Data into Action: Synthesize quantitative and qualitative data into concise, actionable insights. You will provide a holistic lens to ensure we deliver a seamless and superior experience across the entire consumer journey.
Co-create Analytical Foundations: Partner with data and tech teams to ensure our data collection is high-quality, scalable, and reliable. You’ll help shape the tracking and data structures (e.g., GA4, BigQuery) required to turn digital behavior into trustworthy insights.
Drive Performance Insights: Enable data-driven decision-making through forecasting, trend analysis, and the development of automated dashboards in Tableau, Steep or similar tools.
Collaborative Problem Solving: Act as the consumer analytics partner for teams across Instabee, helping them move past the "what" to understand the "why" behind consumer behavior.
Drive Experimentation: Design, run, and evaluate A/B tests—taking ownership from the initial hypothesis to the final conclusion and recommended next steps.


Your profile

You are an analytical problem-solver with a natural drive to uncover patterns and understand exactly what drives consumer behavior and business performance. You are curious, proactive, and motivated by creating real-world results rather than theoretical perfection. You don't just provide data; you see it as your mission to provide the actionable insights that enable our teams to deliver a great consumer experience and our business to grow.

Experience & Mindset


Analytical Experience: 4-5+ years of experience in consumer analytics, digital analytics or a related field - ideally within a fast-paced service or e-commerce environment.
Commercial Curiosity: You have a strong business acumen and a natural drive to understand the "why" behind the numbers. You focus on what is relevant and actionable for the business, and proactively surface insights and opportunities.
Problem Solver: Strong analytical and numerical skills with a business-oriented mindset. You excel at bringing structure to ambiguous problems and can navigate complex datasets to find the most impactful answers.
Communication: You can translate complex analysis into sharp, actionable narratives for diverse stakeholders. You communicate insights clearly and confidently to stakeholders at all levels, from junior teams to senior leadership.
Collaboration: Team player who enjoys working across Product, Tech, and Business teams to develop insights and build solutions together.
Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, IT, Statistics, Business, or other relevant field.


Analytical toolkit


Consumer Analytics: Proven ability to synthesize various internal and external consumer data into holistic consumer analytics and insights.
Digital Analytics: Experience with tools such as Amplitude, GA4, or similar behavioral tracking platforms.
Data Foundations: Proficiency in SQL and Python or similar, and experience working with large datasets.
Visualization: Ability to build and maintain clear dashboards and funnels that give our teams an ongoing view of consumer health (e.g., using Tableau, Power BI, or Steep).
AI coding tools: Experience in using AI tools in your analytical work is an advantage.
Experimentation: Hands-on experience designing, running, and evaluating A/B tests is an advantage.
Tracking: Knowledge of digital tracking implementation and Google Tag Manager is an advantage.


What You'll Get

We can promise you that you will be challenged, develop your skills and have the opportunity to work with truly amazing and competent colleagues. If you like changes and want to set new processes and ways of working - this is the place for you!

Benefits

Of course, we have all the necessary and standard benefits in place, but in addition to this, we can also offer:


Sky-High Office: Modern and newly renovated with a 360-degree view around Stockholm.
Puppy Friendly: Bring your dog to work - we've got a whole floor for our furry friends!
Fun & Games: Ping pong, Shuffleboard, Foosball, and even a Karaoke Room for your leisure time.
Parental Pay for Six Months: Step away from work and provide the best care and love to your little one.
A Day Off on Your Birthday: We love to celebrate, so enjoy your birthday to the fullest with a day off each year!
Hybrid setup and flexible working hours


Get to know us

In 2022, Instabee was founded after combining Budbee and Instabox - two leading last-mile delivery companies with three consumer-facing brands: Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy. Instabee serves millions of consumers across Europe with convenient deliveries and is on track to become the leading European e-commerce enabler. Always with the consumer at heart.

We’re fast, both in terms of parcel delivery and when it comes to growing our business. So far our journey has taken us from Sweden to the rest of Scandinavia and parts of continental Europe, but we have no plans on stopping anytime soon. As we continue to expand, the complexity of Instabee grows in parallel. With that in mind, we’re now looking for you to help us on the journey ahead.

This recruitment process consists of background checks via Svensk Bakgrundsanalys as a review of previous public criminal records. The result is public information that is handled confidentially. This is mandatory for all recruitment processes at Instabee in Sweden.

Please note that, due to the summer holiday period, we will begin reviewing applications in August. We appreciate your patience and will be in touch with all candidates once the recruitment process resumes.

Instabee Group, with consumer-facing brands Budbee, Instabox, and Porterbuddy, brings together years of experience in delivery. Today, Instabee Group is present in five markets, serving thousands of online merchants and millions of consumers with seamless home deliveries and convenient parcel locker deliveries, using the best fuel available. With smart technology, a strong focus on sustainability, and a secure supply chain, Instabee has become a trusted partner for online merchants and a preferred choice for consumers.

Title: Analytics Consultant
Description:
Analytics Engineer - Inhouse line role

Stockholm | Hybrid | Permanent




Help shape the future of data, analytics & AI




An established Nordic organisation is investing heavily in its modern data platform and is looking for an Analytics Engineer to play a key role in that journey.




This is an opportunity to move beyond traditional reporting and help define how data is modelled, governed and consumed across the business. You'll work at the intersection of business, analytics and engineering, building the foundations for self-service analytics and future AI capabilities.




The Role

You'll be responsible for making data accessible, trusted and meaningful by developing semantic models, defining business metrics and creating scalable analytics solutions that support decision-making across the organisation.




Working closely with Data Engineers, business stakeholders and AI specialists, you'll help ensure the organisation's data is ready not only for reporting today, but for AI-driven use cases tomorrow.




Key Responsibilities

Design and develop semantic models and scalable data models
Define, document and maintain business KPIs and metrics
Build intuitive dashboards and analytical solutions in Power BI
Enable self-service analytics across the business
Ensure strong data quality, governance, security and performance
Create data models that support future AI and natural language analytics
Collaborate with technical and non-technical stakeholders to translate business requirements into data solutions




What We're Looking For

Several years' experience developing BI solutions using Power BI
Strong SQL and data modelling skills
Experience working with Microsoft Fabric, Azure or similar modern cloud data platforms
Experience implementing security models (such as Row-Level Security) and data governance best practices
Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to bridge business and technical teams




Nice to Have

Semantic Models
DAX
Lakehouse or Data Warehouse architectures
dbt
Experience working with AI-enabled analytics or modern data platforms




About You

You're someone who enjoys discussing business challenges just as much as designing data models. You understand that great analytics starts with well-structured, trusted data, and you're excited by the opportunities AI brings - but also recognise that successful AI depends on strong data foundations.




You're naturally curious, pragmatic and enjoy bringing clarity to complex information.




Why Join?

Play a key role in a major data transformation programme
Work with modern technologies including Microsoft Fabric and AI-enabled analytics
Influence the design of a next-generation analytics platform
Collaborate closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders
Join an organisation where data is becoming central to strategic decision-making




If you're an Analytics Engineer looking to have a genuine impact on how an organisation uses data and AI, we'd love to tell you more about this opportunity.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Join our global force of 400+ innovators, blending the latest in tech with the greatest in soundtracking — from our Stockholm HQ to offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris, Oslo, and Seoul. We're an industry leader with a startup mentality. We take what we do seriously, but we don't take ourselves too seriously. Come create with us, and let the world feel your work.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll join an embedded, AI-native analytics team that sits inside the commercial and product domains driving our growth. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with the CPO and product teams as we evolve from a music-library product into a broader creative-workflow platform, defining what success looks like for surfaces that don't exist yet and proving it with data.

What you'll do


Partner with the CPO and product teams to define success metrics, surface growth opportunities, and shape product strategy with evidence.
Stand up and advise on measurement for new product experiences end to end — instrumentation, KPI design, and leading-indicator tracking.
Run the full analytics stack — from descriptive analysis through experimentation — to evaluate performance and drive decisions.
Close data gaps and build scalable, self-serve measurement infrastructure so teams can monitor long-term impact without you in the loop.
Make our data trustworthy for agentic analytics by resolving gaps and enriching context and metadata so both AI tools and people can rely on it.


What you'll bring


Strong stakeholder management and business acumen. Track record of influencing senior decision-makers.
High agency. Habit of driving decisions through tailored communication and data storytelling.
Sharp analytical instincts. You scope vague questions with vetted frameworks, state hypotheses up front, and reason to a defensible answer even with imperfect data.
Sound judgement about data. A solid grasp of statistics and the discernment to know when past data predicts the future and when to override it for where the business is heading.
Technical versatility. Proficiency across SQL and Python (notebooks in Hex), dbt (on BigQuery), plus experience with AI coding tools and techniques including context engineering and evals.
Experienced experimentalist. Fluent in A/B testing and causal inference.


Music to our ears


Data modelling know-how and experience with semantic layers.
Experience in PLG and SaaS environments (B2C and/or B2B).
A track record of defining metrics for new 0→1 product surfaces.


What we'll offer


A fast-paced environment with real room for personal growth and tangible impact on the business.
The chance to help write the new playbook for running Analytics in the AI era combining timeless principles with fresh learnings from emerging tooling.
A diverse, collaborative team of Analysts and Engineers building together.


Equal opportunity employer

We believe that bringing people together from different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives makes for a healthy workplace, a more successful business and a better world. We value diversity and encourage everyone to come and soundtrack the world with us.

Application

Ready to make the world feel your work? Please apply, in English.

Title: Dataanalytiker till Svenska Röda Korset
Description:
Vill du omvandla data till insikter som stärker människors engagemang?

På Svenska Röda Korset arbetar vi varje dag för att hjälpa människor före, under och efter kriser – i Sverige och världen. För att förstå människors engagemang, utveckla våra insamlingsaktiviteter och fatta bättre beslut behöver vi bli ännu mer datadrivna. Nu söker vi en Dataanalytiker som vill vara med och utveckla hur vi använder data för att skapa större värde för våra givare, medlemmar och volontärer. Här får du kombinera analys, affärsförståelse och ny teknik i en organisation där dina insikter bidrar till att göra verklig skillnad.

Vi erbjuder dig

Hos Svenska Röda Korset blir du en del av en organisation med ett starkt humanitärt uppdrag och en tydlig ambition att utveckla våra arbetssätt genom data, analys och digitalisering.

Hos Oss Får Du Bland Annat


En nyckelroll där du får påverka hur data används för att utveckla engagemang och insamling
Möjlighet att arbeta med avancerad analys, AI och prediktiva modeller
Vara en del av ett datadrivet och agilt team med hög kompetens och stort engagemang
Arbeta nära verksamheten och bidra med analyser som leder till konkreta beslut och förbättringar
Möjlighet att utveckla organisationens analysförmåga och stärka ett datadrivet arbetssätt
En värderingsstyrd organisation där samarbete, innovation och människors lika värde står i centrum



Det här gör du hos oss

Som Dataanalytiker blir du en central del i arbetet med att förstå och utveckla relationen till våra givare, medlemmar och andra engagerade. Du arbetar både strategiskt och operativt med att identifiera beteenden, mönster och trender som hjälper verksamheten att fatta bättre beslut och skapa mer träffsäkra aktiviteter.

Du arbetar nära kollegor inom verksamheten och bidrar med analyser som utvecklar kommunikation, målgruppsarbete och insamlingsinitiativ.

I Rollen Kommer Du Bland Annat Att


Vara en sammanhållande och drivande kraft för analysarbetet inom avdelningen
Analysera beteenden, utveckling och engagemang hos givare, medlemmar och andra engagerade
Arbeta hypotesdrivet för att identifiera möjligheter, risker och förbättringsområden
Ta fram rapporter, dashboards och beslutsunderlag
Bidra med analyser som utvecklar insamlingsaktiviteter och kommunikationsinsatser tillsammans med agila team
Presentera data och insikter på ett tydligt och pedagogiskt sätt för olika målgrupper
Stötta och utveckla kollegor så att fler kan arbeta datadrivet i vardagen
Bidra till utvecklingen av AI-drivna analyser, prediktiva modeller och nya arbetssätt



Ditt team och arbetsplats

Du blir en del av avdelningen Engagemang och Insamling, inom enheten Insikt & Innehåll, och tillhör teamet DAP (Data, Analys & Plattform).

Du arbetar nära kollegor inom Engagemang och Insamling samt har många kontaktytor med IT och Innovation. Tillsammans utvecklar ni organisationens datadrivna arbetssätt och skapar bättre förutsättningar för framtidens engagemang.

Mer om dig

Vi söker dig som drivs av att förstå människor genom data och som tycker om att omsätta analyser till konkreta rekommendationer. Du är nyfiken, analytisk och trivs med att arbeta nära verksamheten där du får kombinera teknik, kommunikation och problemlösning.

För att lyckas i rollen ser vi att du har:


Flera års erfarenhet av dataanalys, gärna inom CRM, marknad, medlemsorganisation eller liknande verksamhet
Goda kunskaper i SQL
Erfarenhet av Python, R eller motsvarande programmeringsspråk, alternativt ett stort intresse av att utvecklas vidare inom området
Erfarenhet av prediktiva modeller, maskininlärning och AI-driven analys
Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska, både i tal och skrift



Meriterande


Erfarenhet av att bygga dashboards och rapporter i Power BI
Erfarenhet av att formulera och testa hypoteser samt omsätta resultat till verksamhetsnytta



Som person

Vi tror att du är en analytisk och nyfiken person som gillar att utforska data och hitta nya möjligheter. Du arbetar strukturerat och lösningsorienterat och har lätt för att samarbeta med olika delar av verksamheten. Du är kommunikativ och tycker om att göra komplex information enkel att förstå. Samtidigt är du prestigelös, engagerad och motiveras av att utveckla både arbetssätt och människor omkring dig.

Övrig Information


Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning med placering på Lindhagensgatan 126 i Stockholm.
Vi tillämpar sex månaders provanställning.
Vi värdesätter det personliga mötet och arbetar gärna från kontoret. Samtidigt erbjuder vi möjlighet till hybridarbete, med hemarbete ett par dagar i veckan när verksamheten tillåter.



Så här får vi kontakt

I den här rekryteringen har vi valt att samarbeta med Ada Digital som strävar efter en transparent, inkluderande och snabbrörlig konsult- och rekryteringsupplevelse. Vi vill veta mer om dig och din potential! Du söker tjänsten enkelt och behöver inte bifoga några dokument. Det räcker med en motivering till varför du söker tjänsten och om du saknar CV kan du bifoga din LinkedIn-profil. Följ sedan din ansökan live via vår hemsida. Urvalet sker löpande och tjänsten kan bli tillsatt före sista ansökningsdatum. Är du nyfiken och vill veta mer innan du söker? Hör av dig till ansvarig rekryterare. Vi ser fram emot att få kontakt med dig!
