Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Analytics Engineer, Stockholm

Bravida is one of the Nordic region's leading installation and service companies covering electrical, HVAC, plumbing, sprinkler, security & fire, and automation across Sweden, Norway, Denmark and Finland with over 12,000 employees and around 300 local branches. Behind that scale sits serious operational complexity: projects, procurement, and finance functions that run on data. We're hiring an experienced Analytics Engineer to own the data models that ties it all together.

The Role

Data modelling is the core of this job. You'll design and own the semantic layer that makes Bravida's operational and financial data trustworthy, consistent, and accessible for reports today and generative BI tomorrow. AI isn't a side interest here; it's central to how we expect this role to operate and where we're taking our analytics capability.


Architect and own data models in dbt on Snowflake as the primary deliverable
Define and enforce modelling standards, documentation, and data contracts across domains
Build and manage pipelines in Dagster
Work with AI tooling actively to accelerate your own output and enable smarter self-service for the business
Drive our move toward generative analytics, shaping how business users query and interpret data through natural language
Work directly with operations, procurement, and finance to model their domains properly
Lead analytics data quality and reliability improvements across the stack



The Team

The Data & Analytics team sits inside Operational Excellence, a fast-moving function with a mandate to improve how Bravida runs at every level. That means getting out into the business, building relationships with branch managers and project leads across the Nordics, and making data something people actually pull toward rather than have pushed at them. We move quickly, we're visible, and we don't accept "this is how we've always done it." If you need a perfectly quiet environment to do your best work, this probably isn't the right fit. If you thrive on direct feedback, short cycles, and seeing your work land in the real world fast, it is.

What You Bring


3+ years as an Analytics Engineer, Data Engineer, or Data Analyst
Deep dbt experience; you've built and maintained production-grade models others depend on
Strong Snowflake or comparable modern platform experience
Solid grasp of dimensional modelling, data contracts, and semantic layer design
Active user of AI tools in your workflow and a view on how they change the job
Genuine interest in generative BI and what it means for how businesses consume data beyond today
Strong stakeholder management; you can hold a room, push back constructively, and get buy-in from people who weren't looking for it
You spot problems before they're reported and propose solutions before they're requested
You make good calls with incomplete information and don't wait to be told what to build next
English required; Nordic languages a bonus



What We Offer


Ownership of a decision engine that matters at real scale
A modern stack (Snowflake, dbt, Dagster) with active investment in AI and generative BI
Direct access to senior stakeholders across operations, procurement, and finance
A fast team with a real mandate, not a support function waiting for tasks
Autonomy on your own career and continuous development



The construction and installation industry is going through fundamental change in how projects are run, how decisions get made, and how value gets measured. The civic projects Bravida works on: the hospitals, schools, infrastructure, and buildings that society depends on, they deserve to be built and then operated on better data. We take that responsibility seriously. That's what this role is part of.

Process: Intro call with hiring manager, then a practical technical case study followed by meeting the team and discussing the solution. Final step is meeting some of the Operations team and key stakeholders.

You must be eligible to work in Sweden and the EU.

Title: Management Consultant - Data & Analytics
Description:
Your role




Our Data & Analytics (D&A) team combine broad business understanding with technical expertise to help our clients make better data-driven decisions.




Expect to participate in projects ranging from advisory services to hands-on development of D&A solutions. We help our clients define a strategy, organize their D&A capabilities, and implement data platforms and analytical solutions. The analytical work ranges from business intelligence to Machine Learning and Generative AI.




Together with an ambitious and multidisciplinary team of colleagues, you will help our clients understand their current situation, risks, and opportunities and advise how they can use technology to solve their business needs. The type of projects will be varied, and you will have access to experts throughout our international community of consultants.




Your professional and personal growth will be supported by a Development Manager. As you deepen your expertise and grow as a consultant, you will take on greater responsibilities in our client engagements and our internal business activities. Such as team development, employer branding, developing new offerings, pitching to clients, and other activities.




What We Are Looking For In Our New Colleagues




You may have a background as a developer and now want to combine technical work with more business‑focused and strategic responsibilities. Alternatively, you may work in Consulting, have a strong technical interest, and want to deepen your expertise within data and AI.




We Expect Candidates To Have




Experience in data collection and analysis, along with a general understanding of business processes.
Familiarity with Business Intelligence solutions and Generative AI development, as well as programming proficiency in Python and SQL.
Some experience with Database design, ETL, and Data Visualization is also desirable.
Experience with Microsoft´s Data & Analytics tools is a plus (Fabric, PowerBI, SQL Server, Data Factory, etc.)
Ability to balance analytical rigor with practical, implementable recommendations.
Structured communication, adapting style to managers and operational teams and an ability to establish credibility quickly in new environments.
A master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or other relevant field.
Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint to support daily business activities.
Excellent communication skills in both Swedish and English, spoken and written.




What We Offer




BearingPoint is a great place to work. We can offer you the opportunity to work for a company with a global reach and a unique culture that allows everyone to reach their highest potential. We also offer team-specific competency development opportunities and employee benefits such as:

Professional and personal growth, supported by your Development Manager.
An international, dynamic, and inspiring work environment.
A steep learning curve in client-facing roles with a high impact on the business performance of our clients.
Competitive compensation and benefits package, including but not limited to an occupational pension, health insurance, wellness grants, expanded parental benefits, and more.
Competency development opportunities include our First 1000-day program and other Nordic & Firm-wide training and boot camps (Grow with BearingPoint).
An annual curriculum of various social events and sports activities.




What Now?




Send us your resume in either Swedish or English, and we´ll get back to you shortly. A cover letter is optional, and Interviews will be held continuously.




During the interview process, you will meet various colleagues from the team and conduct case interviews.




Want to find out more about the team? Meet some of our colleagues here (Meet our Sweden team), or read an article with our D&A team lead Marcus Ehrndal here.




As part of our recruitment process, candidates may be subject to background checks. These checks help us ensure a safe and trustworthy workplace and may include verification of identity, employment history, education, and any relevant criminal records, in accordance with applicable laws.

Title: Statistiker till Stockholm
Description:
På Migrationsverket arbetar vi med en av vår tids stora samhällsfrågor – migrationen. Totalt är vi cirka 6 000 personer här, och vi har ett ovanligt viktigt jobb!



Vi söker statistiker med inriktning statistikproduktion till enheten för statistik och analys. Migrationsområdet är i en period av stora skiften inom flera omvärldsfaktorer såsom EU:s migrations och asylpakt, regeringens reformpolitik och det geopolitiska säkerhetsläget. Mot bakgrund av dessa förändringar behöver myndigheten förstärka analysförmågan och forma framtidens statistikproduktion. 



Som medarbetare på Migrationsverket kan du förvänta dig ett givande arbete i en dynamisk miljö som präglas av ett fokus på ständiga förbättringar, samarbete och respekt för människan.



Du kommer att tillhöra enheten för statistik och analys som är en del av planeringsavdelningen. Enheten ansvarar för myndighetens samlade statistik och består av 18 kollegor placerade i Norrköping och Sundbyberg. På enheten finns en bred kompetens där medarbetarna bland annat arbetar med statistikförfrågningar, uppföljnings-, prognos- och prediktionsarbete samt utvecklings- och förvaltningsarbete. Enheten samarbetar mycket med andra enheter på den egna avdelningen såväl som med andra avdelningar.



Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter



Som statistiker kommer du att utveckla och förvalta ett eller flera statistikområden tillsammans med övriga medarbetare på enheten. Arbetet omfattar hela statistikprocessen, från bearbetning och kvalitetssäkring av data till publicering och kommunikation av statistik. 



I rollen ingår bland annat att: 

analysera och bearbeta registerdata som underlag för statistikproduktion
utveckla logik och kravställa nya variabler till statistikregister
säkerställa definitioner, dokumentation och kvalitet i statistik och register
utveckla och förvalta ditt statistikområde i vår statistikportal, som är under uppbyggnad
tillgängliggöra statistik genom statistikportalen samt genom fördjupningsrapporter, löpande statistikleveranser och statistikbeställningar 
besvara frågor om statistiken från såväl interna som externa statistikanvändare
bidra till utveckling och effektivisering av statistikproduktionen  

Vem är du?



Vi söker dig som är analytisk, noggrann och strukturerad. Du trivs med att fördjupa dig i data och har ett systematiskt arbetssätt där kvalitet och tillförlitlighet är självklara utgångspunkter. 



Du är servicemedveten och tycker om att ge stöd och vägledning till både interna och externa användare av statistiken. Rollen ställer också krav på god samarbetsförmåga, samtidigt som du kan planera och driva ditt arbete framåt självständigt. Du har förmåga att ta egna initiativ och bidrar aktivt till utveckling och förbättring av arbetssätt och processer inom statistikområdet. 



Vi söker dig som har

akademisk examen med inriktning statistik, eller annan utbildning som Migrationsverket bedömer som likvärdig
programmeringskunskaper i SQL, SAS eller Qliksense 
god kommunikativ förmåga och uttrycker dig väl i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska

Det är meriterande om du har

erfarenhet av statistikutveckling inom offentlig förvaltning
erfarenhet av arbete med registerbaserad statistik, att specificera logik för att ta hand om registreringar i handläggningssystem och kravställa till datalagerutvecklare 
erfarenhet av arbete med kvalitetssäkring och dokumentation av statistik 
erfarenhet och god kännedom om Migrationsverkets uppdrag och verksamhet

Anställningsform: Tillsvidareanställning

Om rekryteringsprocessen



För oss är det viktigt att all kompetens på arbetsmarknaden tillvaratas. Vi välkomnar därför alla sökande och strävar efter en jämn könsfördelning och mångfald. Som statlig myndighet rekryterar vi efter förtjänst och skicklighet. På arbetsgivarverket.se kan du läsa mer om vad det innebär.

utförligt som möjligt på urvalsfrågorna för att vi ska kunna göra en rättvis bedömning av din erfarenhet om du är kvalificerad att gå vidare i rekryteringen eller inte. HTML:
På Migrationsverket arbetar vi med en av vår tids stora samhällsfrågor – migrationen. Totalt är vi cirka 6 000 personer här, och vi har ett ovanligt viktigt jobb!

Vi söker statistiker med inriktning statistikproduktion till enheten för statistik och analys. Migrationsområdet är i en period av stora skiften inom flera omvärldsfaktorer såsom EU:s migrations och asylpakt, regeringens reformpolitik och det geopolitiska säkerhetsläget. Mot bakgrund av dessa förändringar behöver myndigheten förstärka analysförmågan och forma framtidens statistikproduktion. 

Som medarbetare på Migrationsverket kan du förvänta dig ett givande arbete i en dynamisk miljö som präglas av ett fokus på ständiga förbättringar, samarbete och respekt för människan.

Du kommer att tillhöra enheten för statistik och analys som är en del av planeringsavdelningen. Enheten ansvarar för myndighetens samlade statistik och består av 18 kollegor placerade i Norrköping och Sundbyberg. På enheten finns en bred kompetens där medarbetarna bland annat arbetar med statistikförfrågningar, uppföljnings-, prognos- och prediktionsarbete samt utvecklings- och förvaltningsarbete. Enheten samarbetar mycket med andra enheter på den egna avdelningen såväl som med andra avdelningar.

Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter

Som statistiker kommer du att utveckla och förvalta ett eller flera statistikområden tillsammans med övriga medarbetare på enheten. Arbetet omfattar hela statistikprocessen, från bearbetning och kvalitetssäkring av data till publicering och kommunikation av statistik. 

I rollen ingår bland annat att: 

  • analysera och bearbeta registerdata som underlag för statistikproduktion
  • utveckla logik och kravställa nya variabler till statistikregister
  • säkerställa definitioner, dokumentation och kvalitet i statistik och register
  • utveckla och förvalta ditt statistikområde i vår statistikportal, som är under uppbyggnad
  • tillgängliggöra statistik genom statistikportalen samt genom fördjupningsrapporter, löpande statistikleveranser och statistikbeställningar 
  • besvara frågor om statistiken från såväl interna som externa statistikanvändare
  • bidra till utveckling och effektivisering av statistikproduktionen  

Vem är du?

Vi söker dig som är analytisk, noggrann och strukturerad. Du trivs med att fördjupa dig i data och har ett systematiskt arbetssätt där kvalitet och tillförlitlighet är självklara utgångspunkter. 

Du är servicemedveten och tycker om att ge stöd och vägledning till både interna och externa användare av statistiken. Rollen ställer också krav på god samarbetsförmåga, samtidigt som du kan planera och driva ditt arbete framåt självständigt. Du har förmåga att ta egna initiativ och bidrar aktivt till utveckling och förbättring av arbetssätt och processer inom statistikområdet. 

Vi söker dig som har

  • akademisk examen med inriktning statistik, eller annan utbildning som Migrationsverket bedömer som likvärdig
  • programmeringskunskaper i SQL, SAS eller Qliksense 
  • god kommunikativ förmåga och uttrycker dig väl i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska

Det är meriterande om du har

  • erfarenhet av statistikutveckling inom offentlig förvaltning
  • erfarenhet av arbete med registerbaserad statistik, att specificera logik för att ta hand om registreringar i handläggningssystem och kravställa till datalagerutvecklare 
  • erfarenhet av arbete med kvalitetssäkring och dokumentation av statistik 
  • erfarenhet och god kännedom om Migrationsverkets uppdrag och verksamhet

Title: BI-analytiker
Description:
Techster Solutions befinner i dagsläget sig i en spännande tillväxtfas och söker nu fler kollegor, inledningsvis dig med minst 3+ års erfarenhet och som kan stärka teamet.

Du kan förvänta dig en omväxlande miljö, dagarna kan se väldigt olika ut - men en sak är säkert, här får du använda dina kompetenser och sätter själv gränsen för hur mycket du vill utvecklas. Som ett kunskapsbolag är det viktigt för oss att våra specialister delar med sig av sin kompetens och får kollegorna att växa.

Rollbeskrivning:

Vi söker nu en BI-analytiker.

Uppdragsbeskrivning:

Kund söker en driven BI analytiker till Digitaliseringsenheten. Digitaliseringsenheten ingår i avdelningen för verksamhetsstöd som har till uppgift att säkerställa en stödjande verksamhet i balans med kärnverksamhetens behov.

Digitaliseringsenheten har i uppdrag att utveckla verksamhetsnära digitala lösningar för att möta de utvecklingsbehov som finns inom kundens olika verksamhetsdelar. Enheten har ett helhetsansvar för nyutveckling, underhåll och support. Digitaliseringsenheten består av fyra agila utvecklingsteam som har hög ambition och arbetar med moderna metoder och verktyg och präglas av innovation och nytänkande.

Placering: Kundens kontor i Stockholm men med viss möjlighet till distansarbete.

Arbetsuppgifter

Som BI-analytiker i vårt datafångstteam är du central i att driva vår transformation mot en mer insiktsdriven myndighet. Du ansvarar för att omvandla verksamhetens behov till tydliga och användarvänliga rapporter i Power BI on-premise där du skapar struktur i data och lyfter fram de insikter som gör skillnad. Arbetet innefattar att samla in krav, modellera data och bygga rapporter och dashboards som inte bara visar siffror utan berättar en historia. Du kommer att samarbeta nära både tekniska specialister och verksamhetsnära användare och bidra till att våra data blir ett effektivt beslutsstöd. I rollen ingår även att säkerställa datakvalitet, förbättra befintliga lösningar och vara en drivande kraft i att utveckla vårt sätt att arbeta med datadriven analys.

Krav

Skallkrav:


Relevant akademisk utbildning inom data/IT (t.ex. systemvetenskap, datavetenskap, statistik eller liknande) eller motsvarande erfarenhet som bedöms likvärdig.
Minst 2–3 års aktuell och relevant arbetslivserfarenhet för rollen
Mycket goda kunskaper i att bygga rapporter och visualiseringar i Power BI (gärna on-premise).
Erfarenhet av att arbeta med SSAS-kuber (SQL Server Analysis Services) och ha en förståelse för hur data modelleras och struktureras för effektiv analys i Power BI.
Förmåga att kommunicera insikter på ett pedagogiskt och visuellt tilltalande sätt (“data storytelling”).
Goda kunskaper i SQL och erfarenhet av att arbeta med relationsdatabaser.
Erfarenhet av kravinsamling och nära dialog med verksamheten för att förstå behov och omsätta dem i BI-lösningar.
Strukturerat arbetssätt med fokus på datakvalitet, användbarhet och leveranssäkerhet.
Mycket god förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska
Utvärdering av de personliga egenskaperna kommer att ske vid en eventuell intervju


Börkrav:


Förståelse för grundläggande design- och UX-principer för att skapa tydliga och användarvänliga rapporter.
Erfarenhet av att arbeta med färgsättning, layout och visualisering för att förstärka budskap i data.
Förmåga att coacha och utbilda andra i BI-verktyg och datadrivet arbetssätt.
Erfarenhet av att samarbeta med andra i agila utvecklingsprojekt


Ansökan

Tror du att du är Techster Solutions nästa stjärna? Tveka då inte att skicka in din ansökan redan nu. Urval sker löpande och tillträde sker enligt överenskommelse.

Arbetsplats och kultur

Vi är ett entreprenördrivet IT företag, med både produktkunskap och försäljning (Techster AB) och konsultativa tjänster (Techster Solutions AB). Det innebär att vi är mycket öppna för nya tekniska lösningar, kunskapsutbyte och ständigt lärande. Att ha roligt och samtidigt behålla den familjära andan utan hierarkier är lika viktigt.

Vi vill skapa det konsultbolag som du alltid velat jobba för, men kanske inte hittat ännu. Redan idag har vi en blandning av seniora och yngre medarbetare, såväl som män och kvinnor. Det vill vi fortsätta att bygga på. Så välkommen med din ansökan!

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Join Truecaller – The place where innovation meets impact!




Truecaller's mission is to build trust in communication by making it safer, smarter, and more efficient. Born in Sweden, trusted by the world, and here’s why we stand out:

We are trusted by over 450 million active users every month across 190+ countries
We identify over 15 billion calls daily, helping users avoid spam and scams
We are powered by a team of 450+ employees from 45+ nationalities




We always look for people who take initiative, own their work, and keep raising the bar. An entrepreneurial mindset matters here, especially when it turns bold ideas into real actions. We stay collaborative and focused, always searching for smarter paths forward. If you want to make an impact and grow with a team that inspires millions, you’ll fit right in.




The role:

As a Senior Data analyst, you’ll be responsible for the team's quantitative insights. From understanding our data, identifying problems, to working with product, tech, and design on hypothesizing solutions. You’ll be the driver of our experimentation and learning efforts and will collaborate with different teams.




What you will do

Understanding Truecaller's data.
Taking ownership of quantitative insights for your team, playing a crucial role in product development.
Defining experiments, overseeing their setup, and analyzing their results to drive decision-making.
Developing and maintaining key performance indicators (KPIs) for the team, ensuring their tracking, reliability, and reporting.
Creating data pipelines and dashboards to measure the team's KPIs and effectively communicate insights to both the team and leadership.
Coaching and mentoring the team on experimentation and data-driven product development processes, fostering a culture of data-driven decision-making.




What you bring in

4+ years of experience in Data Analytics
Experience in using SQL to extract, transform, and analyse data
Experience in data visualisation tools like Looker (preferred), QlikSense, Tableau, or Power BI
Experience in mentoring, coaching, and evangelizing about data in product development
Experience in digging into different data sources to understand why specific customer behaviours happen
Fluent in spoken and written English




It would be great if you also have

Big data query tools (we use BigQuery).
Experience with data from mobile applications.




What we offer:

We support growth through learning resources, leadership programs, mentoring, and real hands-on work. People can move between teams and projects to build new skills and keep things interesting. We offer clear internal mobility and a transparent path for progression, with leaders who stay involved and provide guidance throughout the year. In addition, you will benefit from:




A comprehensive compensation package: We offer a competitive salary, 30 days of paid vacation, private health insurance, parental leave top-up, pension, and wellness contributions.
Modern tools to do your best work: Choose your preferred computer and phone within our budget, so you can work comfortably and efficiently.
A people-focused office culture: We value in-person collaboration and follow an office-first model, with some flexibility. Our offices offer a vibrant environment with opportunities to learn, connect, and recharge, from breakfast, lunch, and well-stocked snack stations and quiet spaces to team activities such as movie nights, tech meetups, and cultural events. There's something for everyone.
Truecaller’s “Lab Days” offer a space for imagination: 5 times per year for 3 days, where everyone steps away from their normal tasks to explore new, bold ideas and build things they’ve always wanted to. It’s a space where curiosity leads the way, and prototypes take shape. Some concepts even make it into production, and a few have grown into real features used by millions today. Lab Days allow you to be creative, learn fast, and help shape Truecaller's future.




Come as you are:

Truecaller is committed to building a diverse and inclusive team. We believe that a wide range of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences strengthens our products and our culture. No matter where you're from, what language you speak, or how you identify, we value what makes you unique and would love to get to know you.




Check out Life at Truecaller - Behind the code: https://www.instagram.com/lifeattruecaller/




Sounds like a great opportunity?




We will fill the position as soon as we find the right candidate, so please send your application as soon as possible. As part of the recruitment process, we will conduct a background check.




We only accept applications in English.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Brinner du för att arbeta med data och dataprodukter och vill samtidigt arbeta i samhällets tjänst för en stark och säker ekonomi? Då är denna tjänst för dig! Vi söker dig som vill använda din tekniska kompetens för att fortsätta utveckla hur Riksbanken arbetar med data och analys med fokus på tillgångsförvaltning och betalningar.

VI ERBJUDER DIG

Du får möjlighet att vidareutveckla din tekniska kompetens samtidigt som du fördjupar din kunskap inom Riksbankens verksamhetsområden så att vi utvecklar lösningar som är enkla att ta till sig och som gör nytta i verksamheten. Du möter utmaningar och blir en viktig del i vår förändringsresa - från att utveckla AI och automatisera dataflöden till att bygga robusta IT-lösningar, förstärka cybersäkerhet och bygga framtidens betalningssystem. Här löser du komplexa problem och utvecklas i en prestigelös miljö som präglas av samarbete, nyfikenhet, lärande och mod.

DET HÄR GÖR DU HOS OSS

Riksbankens Data Och Statistik Över Tillgångsförvaltning Och Betalningar Består Till Stor Del Av Mikro- Och Transaktionsdata. Betalningsstatistiken Från Finansiella Institut Är i En Uppbyggnadsfas Och Det Innebär Att Du Är Med Och Utformar Dataflöden Från Grunden. Du Arbetar i Ett Agilt Team Med Helhetsansvar För Sin Data Och Sina Dataprodukter. Konkret Innebär Rollen Att Du


ansvarar för design och utveckling av dataflöden för bearbetning av stora och detaljerade datamängder
skriver automatiska tester för att kvalitetssäkra din kod och underhåller CI/CD-pipelines
utvecklar API:er för att tillgängliggöra data
använder marimo notebooks för att visualisera och tillgängliggöra resultat
bidrar till att utveckla hur Riksbanken använder AI i sin verksamhet


DITT TEAM

Dina närmaste teammedlemmar utgörs av två av Riksbankens datateam där olika kompetenser arbetar sida vid sida och lär av varandra. Datateamen är en del av Riksbankens tvärfunktionella verksamhetsområde Data och Statistik och består av ett femtital medarbetare. Tillsammans med datateamen för du en nära dialog med relevanta policyavdelningar för att leverera data och dataprodukter som är användarvänliga, robusta och värdeskapande. Organisatoriskt kommer du att tillhöra avdelningen för marknader. Här kan du läsa mer om Riksbankens organisation.

MER OM DIG

Vi Ser Att Du Har


en relevant akademisk examen (exempelvis inom data science, matematik, programmering eller liknande)
erfarenhet av att utveckla dataflöden i orkestreringsverktyg som Dagster eller liknande (Airflow, dbt, etc.)
minst 2 års erfarenhet av att koda i Python eller motsvarande programmeringsspråk
erfarenhet av att hantera och bearbeta stora datamängder
goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, både skriftligt och muntligt.


Utöver ovan grundkrav är det meriterande om du även har:


erfarenhet av containermiljöer som Podman eller Kubernetes
erfarenhet av att arbeta med datalake-lagring så som S3 och LakeFS
erfarenhet av att arbeta enligt DevOps-principer med fokus på automatisering och CI/CD
erfarenhet från finans- eller fintechbranschen.
erfarenhet av att arbeta agilt


Vi söker dig som är driven, ansvarsfull, kvalitetsmedveten, har gott omdöme och är bra på att samarbeta. Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.

MER OM TJÄNSTEN

Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse

Anställningstyp: Tillsvidareanställning, provanställning kan komma att tillämpas

Placering: Kontor i centrala Stockholm

Arbetstid: Förtroendearbetstid

Möjlighet att jobba distans: 1 eller 2 dagar i veckan

Förmåner: Generösa semestervillkor, löneväxling till pension, subventionerad lunch, friskvårdsbidrag, subventionerad massage och tillgång till gym och ledarledda träningspass.

Tjänsten kan komma att placeras i säkerhetsklass och en säkerhetsprövning innefattande registerkontroll, säkerhetsprövningssamtal och särskild personutredning görs då för slutkandidaten. För vissa tjänster ställs krav på svenskt medborgarskap.

ATT JOBBA PÅ RIKSBANKEN

Riksbanken är en myndighet under riksdagen som arbetar på medborgarnas uppdrag för en stark och säker ekonomi. Som landets centralbank har vi en nyckelroll för individer, hushåll och företag i Sverige och i över 350 år har vi utforskat nya vägar för att vara i framkant – från kopparplåtmynt till AI. Oavsett inom vilket område din kompetens finns – är det med stolthet vi tar oss an våra uppdrag. Tillsammans arbetar vi för en låg och stabil inflation, bidrar till ett effektivt och stabilt finansiellt system och säkerställer att betalningar fungerar smidigt, även i kriser. Vi ger också ut Sveriges sedlar och mynt. Läs mer om hur det är att arbeta på Riksbanken här .

ANSÖK

Vi vill ha din ansökan med CV senast den 17 augusti. Vi vill att du i denna rekrytering besvarar ett antal urvalsfrågor. På så vis kan du enkelt söka tjänsten och vi ges möjligheten att utvärdera våra kandidater på lika villkor. Arbetsprov kan komma att ingå som en del av rekryteringsprocessen. Vi genomför också bakgrundskontroller på slutkandidaterna, vilket innebär att vi kontrollerar examensbevis eller annat dokument som styrker din utbildning.

Du kan läsa mer om hur det fungerar när du söker jobb på Riksbanken här . Vi ser fram emot att få kontakt med dig!

Title: Power BI Consultant
Description:
På B3 Indes förvandlar vi data till värdefulla insikter som driver förändring och innovation. Vi skapar lösningar inom AI, Business Intelligence, Data Science och Automation för att maximera värdet av data och stärka företags konkurrenskraft. Vår styrka ligger i att kombinera passion för AI och data med en stark laganda, där vi stöttar varandra och löser utmaningar tillsammans.

Rollen som konsult inom Power BI på B3:

Hos oss får du chansen att jobba med några av Sveriges mest spännande kunder och projekt. Vad innebär det? Jo, att du får:


Gräva i data och hitta insikter som inte bara hjälper våra kunder – utan kanske till och med gör världen lite bättre.
Göra företag mer datadrivna och smartare genom att guida dem i rätt riktning.
Utforma effektiva visualiseringar som våra kunder kan agera på.
Bygga och implementera avancerade lösningar – och faktiskt se dem göra skillnad!


Här handlar det inte bara om teknik – det handlar om att modellera, tillgängliggöra och visualisera data som kan skapa verklig förändring. Vi gillar att tänka stort, experimentera och lösa kluriga problem tillsammans. Och det bästa? Du får ett team av grymma kollegor att bolla idéer med, lära dig av och ha kul ihop med!

Det är din passion för data och analys som gör skillnaden. Utöver det ser vi gärna:


Har du en relevant universitetsutbildning? Toppen! Har du kanske skaffat motsvarande kunskaper genom arbetslivserfarenhet? Det funkar lika bra!
Minst 4 års relevant arbetslivserfarenhet – vi letar efter någon som har hunnit samla på sig en del erfarenhet inom området.
Flytande svenska och engelska – eftersom båda språken används dagligen i arbetet.
Tidigare konsultroller är ett plus! Har du jobbat som konsult tidigare? Då är det en extra merit, men inget krav.


Men framför allt...

Har du djup erfarenhet av Power BI och DAX? Grymt – det är viktigt! Men vi lägger också stor vikt vid din förmåga att se helheten, skapa användbara lösningar men framför allt att du är nyfiken. Om du dessutom har koll på:


Någon av de vanligaste databaserna och molntjänsterna
Kanske har du också koll på någon av de andra vanligaste BI-verktygQen (ex; Qlik eller Tableau)
Snowflake och/eller Databricks
Informationsmodellering och Dimensionsmodellering
DevOps och Visual Studio.


Är det här ett stort plus!

Varför välja B3 Indes?

💡 Utveckling & karriär – Väx i en entreprenörsdriven miljö där din kompetens får ta plats.

🌿 Balans & välmående – Vi värnar om ditt välbefinnande med stöd för wellness och en flexibel vardag.

🚀 Energi & gemenskap – Här präglas kulturen av öppenhet, nyfikenhet och en passion för att skapa något större – tillsammans.

❤️ Vi bryr oss – på riktigt – Vi engagerar oss i samhällsfrågor och samarbetar med initiativ som Suicide Zero, My Dream Now och Pink Programming för att göra skillnad även utanför arbetslivet.

En arbetsplats som gör skillnad:

Vi är stolta över att ha utsetts till ett av Sveriges fem främsta karriärföretag 2025 – en utmärkelse som speglar vår energi, nyfikenhet och vårt engagemang för att skapa en inkluderande och inspirerande arbetsplats. Här värdesätts olika perspektiv och idéer, och vi tror att det är just det som gör oss starkare.

Hos oss får du friheten att styra över dina uppdrag, din arbetstid och din utveckling. Vi erbjuder konkurrenskraftiga löner, flexibilitet att forma din vardag och en sund balans mellan jobb och fritid. Och vi vet att gemenskap är minst lika viktigt som teknik – därför satsar vi på allt från konferensresor och sommarfester till sköna AW:s där vi kan släppa jobbet för en stund.

Om B3 Consulting Group

B3 Consulting Group är ett expansivt konsultbolag med cirka 1000 medarbetare. Vi brinner för innovation och hjälper Sveriges ledande företag och organisationer att skapa framtidens möjligheter genom digital transformation. B3 finns på tolv orter i Sverige samt i Polen, med huvudkontor i Stockholm. Bolaget grundades 2003, är noterat på Nasdaq Stockholm Small Cap och omsatte 1 141 MSEK 2023.

Vi är stolta över vår prisbelönta företagskultur, där olikheter, erfarenheter och gemensam energi värdesätts. Som ett etiskt och transparent bolag strävar vi efter att ha en positiv påverkan på samhället, människor och miljön. Vi är även engagerade i Women in Tech och initiativ för att stärka AI-utbildning för kvinnor. 2024 utsågs vi till Årets Employer Branding-företag, och vår HR-chef vann titeln Årets HR-chef.

Är du redo att bli en del av ett företag där din passion för AI och data kan göra skillnad? Läs mer om oss och våra möjligheter här!

Title: Global People Analyst
Description:
We don’t just build technology. We build hope for everyone dealing with Cancer.

Elekta is shaping the standard of care, together with healthcare providers, by developing precision radiation medicine that brings advanced radiotherapy into routine clinical practice. Our solutions support consistent, efficient and personalized care, addressing evolving patient needs. Through adaptive radiotherapy, precision treatment delivery and integrated workflows, Elekta supports care teams across a range of clinical settings. Each year more than 2 million patients are treated using Elekta solutions across over 130 countries. Elekta is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, with over 4,000 employees and offices in more than 40 countries. The company is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm. For more information, visit elekta.com.

What You’ll Do At Elekta

As a Global People Analyst, you will play a key role in enabling a data-driven People function by transforming complex people data into actionable insights. You will work closely with stakeholders across Organizational Development, HR Partnering, and HR Operations to support strategic decision-making and global initiatives.

This role is part of a collaborative, global environment where you will help shape how data is used to improve employee experience, organizational performance, and business outcomes.

Responsibilities


Analyze people data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across hiring, engagement, performance, and turnover.
Translate data into clear insights and recommendations to support business decisions.
Develop, maintain, and enhance dashboards, reports, and data models for global HR reporting.
Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across HR systems and data sources.
Define and evolve key people metrics, benchmarks, and reporting standards.
Partner with HR and business stakeholders to support strategic initiatives and ad hoc analysis.
Present insights in a clear and compelling way to enable data-driven decision-making.
Leverage tools and analytics capabilities to improve accessibility and quality of people insights.
Ensure compliance with data privacy regulations and internal governance standards.
Support audit processes by providing accurate, well-documented data and analysis.


What You’ll Bring


University degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field.
Minimum 3 years of relevant experience, ideally in a global and dynamic organization.
Strong analytical skills with experience interpreting and presenting data insights.
Advanced skills in MS Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint.
Experience working with HR systems and databases; familiarity with Workday is preferred.
Experience building and managing datasets from internal and external sources is an advantage.
Ability to communicate complex data clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Fluent written and spoken English.


What You’ll Get


30 paid vacation days per year.
4 additional Elekta days off.
Defined contribution pension scheme.
Private Medical Insurance.
Lunch benefit.
Contribution to wellness activities.
A collaborative office environment, working alongside talented colleagues in a supportive and engaging workplace.


Application process

We look forward to receiving your application. Please submit your CV via the “Apply” button. We review applications on an ongoing basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. The last day to apply is 24 July 2026.

  • Advanced skills in MS Office, particularly Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Experience working with HR systems and databases; familiarity with Workday is preferred.
  • Experience building and managing datasets from internal and external sources is an advantage.
  • Ability to communicate complex data clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
  • Fluent written and spoken English.

What You’ll Get

  • 30 paid vacation days per year.
  • 4 additional Elekta days off.
  • Defined contribution pension scheme.
  • Private Medical Insurance.
  • Lunch benefit.
  • Contribution to wellness activities.
  • A collaborative office environment, working alongside talented colleagues in a supportive and engaging workplace.

Application process

We look forward to receiving your application. Please submit your CV via the “Apply” button. We review applications on an ongoing basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible. The last day to apply is 24 July 2026.

Title: Data Analyst, Controlling
Description:
We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our team in Packaging Solutions’ BU Northern Europe Controlling team. This role is part of our ambition to build a modern, AI-enabled analytics capability where data analysts act as proactive partners in business decision-making.

Hybrid role, with location in Jönköping, Stockholm, Lahti, Helsinki, or Riga.

Your role

As a Data Analyst, you focus on turning data into profitability-improving actions. You work closely with controllers and business stakeholders to analyze performance, identify improvement opportunities, and support decisions related to pricing, cost optimization, and resource allocation.

This is not a back-office reporting role. You are expected to actively engage with stakeholders, ask the right questions, and continuously improve how data and analysis are used to support profitable business outcomes.

The Data Analyst reports to the Head of Controlling, BU Northern Europe, Packaging Solutions.

Key Responsibilities


Analyst is expected to design structured datasets and models together with IT, not just use them
Analyze financial and business data to identify trends, drivers, and deviations
Explore and apply AI and advanced analytics tools to improve efficiency, quality, and insight
Ensure data quality, consistency, and “single source of truth” across reports and forward looking analyses
Build, maintain, and continuously improve reports and dashboards for decision-making
Combine and structure data from multiple systems and sources
Collaborate closely with controllers and business stakeholders to understand their needs and translate them into meaningful analysis
Support controllers with deeper analysis, scenario modelling, and ad hoc business questions
Communicate insights clearly and confidently to non-financial stakeholders
Identify opportunities to automate recurring analyses and reporting



What You Bring


Relevant academic background (e.g. finance, economics, data, engineering, or similar)
Initial experience and/or strong interest in data analysis, reporting, or finance, with practical experience or curiosity towards modern AI tools
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Power BI), Excel, and data handling
Competence in AI-assisted analytics and vibe coding, enabling fast experimentation, insight generation, and data tool development, with a strong foundation in SQL and Python
Ability to interact confidently with controllers and business stakeholders, and explain data-driven insights in a clear and structured way
Capability and proof of use-cases with AI, automation, and new analytical tools
Analytical mindset with a willingness to challenge numbers and ask “why”
Structured, proactive, and collaborative way of working
Fluency in English



What We Offer

You will be part of our diverse working culture which supports you in exploring new perspectives and developing professionally. You will contribute to a more sustainable world, as we believe that anything made from fossil-based materials today can be made from a tree tomorrow. Sustainability and innovation are at the core of our strategy. To keep you going safely, we support your physical and mental wellbeing.

How can you apply for this role?

You can only submit your application via our recruitment tool. We hope to hear from you as soon as possible, but no later than 17 of August, 2026 and please send your application in English. Depending on the role, we might invite you to a video interview and/or aptitude tests.

If you have questions about the position, please contact the hiring manager Sami Muhonen, sami.muhonen@storaenso.com and questions about the recruitment process can be directed to our Talent Acquisition Partner Veera Kaislasalo at veera.kaislasalo@storaenso.com.

As it’s the holiday season, our process may be a bit slower than usual. We’ll make sure to get back to you with your questions by August at the latest.

Welcome to the The renewable materials company!

Stora Enso is a global leader in renewable materials with a strong focus on packaging. Our purpose is to replace non-renewable materials with renewable solutions. Together with our customers, we design and deliver competitive, high-quality packaging materials and solutions, made from fresh and recycled fibers, accelerating the transition to a circular bioeconomy. Stora Enso has approximately 19,000 employees and our sales in 2025 were EUR 9.3 billion.

We believe that diversity strengthens our competitiveness and leads to better decision‑making. Diverse teams with different backgrounds help us explore new perspectives and challenge our ways of thinking. We actively work to increase diversity in the workplace and welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Stora Enso is an equal opportunity employer that follows non‑discriminatory practices.

Learn more about working in Stora Enso: Working at Stora Enso – Career for a better climate. Every day. - YouTube

Title: Senior Data Analyst - DRM & Music
Description:
Join our global force of 400+ innovators, blending the latest in tech with the greatest in soundtracking — from our Stockholm HQ to offices in London, New York, Los Angeles, Berlin, Paris, Oslo, and Seoul. We're an industry leader with a startup mentality. We take what we do seriously, but we don't take ourselves too seriously. Come create with us, and let the world feel your work.

As a Senior Data Analyst, you'll join an embedded, AI-native analytics team that sits inside the commercial and product domains driving our growth. You'll work closely with DRM and Music leadership — and the product team — as we grow our Music & DRM business. On the DRM side, you'll validate the metrics that matter, build business cases that motivate product investment, and back our position in platform negotiations. On the Music side, you'll help leadership make better decisions faster: shaping how we think about capex and acquisition tradeoffs, making our biggest financial decisions more data-driven, and building a clear picture of how music moves across our platform and out into the world. You'll have a dedicated mandate spanning both DRM and Music while drawing on the shared infrastructure, engineering support, and peers of our central Data & Analytics team.

What you'll do


Be a trusted thought partner to Music and DRM leadership. Bring structured thinking to high-stakes questions, sanity-check what we measure, and proactively raise the questions stakeholders haven't thought to ask.
Build the business cases that move decisions forward. Turn platform data into the commercial case for what we should build next, and put hard numbers behind contract terms, rate changes, and revenue-share positions where the stakes are real money.
See around corners. Bring data-driven insights around our music investments, and build a deep understanding of how different customer segments use our music — so we can anticipate where demand is heading before it shows up in the numbers.
Connect what happens on our platform with how music performs in the world. Run deep-dives that combine internal and external data to explain why a metric moved, where value is being left on the table, and where the growth opportunities are.
Make our data trustworthy for agentic analytics — enrich context and metadata, and use AI coding tools to ship instrumentation and data-quality fixes directly so both people and AI tools can rely on it.
Uphold analytical rigour as self-serve expands. Push back on false precision in revenue and accrual estimates, set the bar for what good analysis looks like, and resist unnecessary complexity where a simpler approach serves the decision better.


What you'll bring


Senior-level influence. You've driven real business impact by shaping decisions at the leadership level and moving revenue outcomes.
High agency. Habit of driving decisions through tailored communication and data storytelling.
Sharp analytical instincts. You scope vague questions with vetted frameworks, state hypotheses up front, and reason to a defensible answer even with imperfect data.
Sound judgement about data. A solid grasp of statistics and the discernment to know when past data predicts the future and when to override it for where the business is heading.
Technical versatility. Proficiency across SQL and Python (notebooks in Hex), dbt (on BigQuery), plus experience with AI coding tools and techniques including context engineering and evals.
Platform curiosity. You don't need prior rights-management experience, but you're genuinely curious to learn how the music platforms actually work, and how that connects to our business.


Music to our ears


Experience combining data across platforms — e.g. joining first-party product data with external platform data — to explain shifts in user behaviour.
Data modelling know-how and experience with semantic layers.
A track record of validating and refining metrics in a data-mature environment, and of building business cases that turned into real product investment.


What we'll offer


A fast-paced environment with real room for personal growth and tangible impact on the business.
The chance to help write the new playbook for running Analytics in the AI era, combining timeless principles with fresh learnings from emerging tooling.
A diverse, collaborative team of Analysts and Engineers building together.


Equal opportunity employer

We believe that bringing people together from different backgrounds, experiences and perspectives makes for a healthy workplace, a more successful business and a better world. We value diversity and encourage everyone to come and soundtrack the world with us.

Application

Ready to make the world feel your work? Please apply, in English.

Title: Labor Demand Expert
Description:
Job Description



WHAT YOU WILL DO



As a Labor Demand Expert, you own the logic that translates store demand signals into labour hours. You build and continuously improve the global labour demand model, ensuring labour allocation is data‑driven, fair and aligned with both commercial and operational realities across store types and markets.



You will:



Own and develop the end‑to‑end labour demand model, translating demand signals into task frequencies and labour hours
Ensure the model reflects real store activity patterns and evolves with changes in customer behaviour, omnichannel flows and operating models
Define, document and maintain assumptions, scaling rules and calculation logic in a clear and structured way
Build logic for daily, weekly and seasonal variation, including store‑type and size scaling factors
Support scenario modelling to assess labour impacts of new store formats, omnichannel initiatives and process changes
Act as a subject matter expert on labour demand logic, providing guidance to both technical and non‑technical stakeholders



WHO YOU WILL WORK WITH



You will work closely with Controlling, Operations Development, Tech and Workforce Management teams, as well as global teams and Sales Markets. In this role, you partner cross‑functionally to ensure labour demand logic is well understood, consistently applied and fit for scale.





WHO YOU ARE



You are an analytical and structured problem‑solver who is comfortable working with complex models and translating them into clear, practical insights.



We are looking for people with:



Experience in retail forecasting, workforce management, labour planning or commercial analytics
Strong analytical and modelling skills, with the ability to work with complex data sets
Experience translating complex models and assumptions into clear, practical guidance
A solid understanding of retail operational drivers such as traffic, deliveries, transactions and omnichannel flows
Strong documentation skills and a high attention to detail
Fluency in English, both written and spoken



WHO WE ARE



H&M Group is a global company of strong fashion brands and ventures. Our goal is to prove that there is no compromise between exceptional design, affordable prices and sustainable solutions. We want to liberate fashion for the many and our customers are at the heart of every decision we make.



We are made up of thousands of passionate and talented colleagues united by our shared culture and values. Together we want to use our power, our scale and our knowledge to push the fashion industry towards a more inclusive and sustainable future. Read more about us here.





WHY YOU’LL LOVE WORKING HERE



At H&M Group we are proud to be a vibrant and welcoming company. We offer our employees attractive benefits with extensive development opportunities around the globe.



An example of our benefits:



25% Staff discount on all our H&M Group brands both in stores and online (H&M, COS, Weekday, Monki, H&M HOME, & Other Stories and ARKET).
H&M Incentive Programme – HIP. Learn more about the programme here.



JOIN US



Our uniqueness comes from a combination of many things – our inclusive and collaborative culture, our strong values, and opportunities for growth. But most of all, it’s our people who make us who we are.



Take the next step in your career together with us. The journey starts here.



This is a permanent position based in Stockholm, please send your CV in English as soon as possible.

*We are committed to a recruitment process that is fair, equitable, and based on competency. We therefore kindly ask you to not attach a cover letter in your application.

Title: SAP Data Engineer
Description:
For a Sustainable Future Together

Coop is Sweden's largest cooperative and a leading player in the grocery retail sector, with over 4 million members. We focus heavily on sustainability and work to create a greener and more just future by offering sustainable products and services.

We believe that a diversity of perspectives and experiences is key to creating a more inclusive and successful workplace. Therefore, we welcome everyone, regardless of gender, age, background, or ethnic origin.

Coop Sverige – Together we make food-Sweden sustainable

At Coop we are building the data backbone of a truly data-driven company. We run stores for our 4.2 million members, and our motto is “Relevance in every touchpoint.” Understanding our customers’ needs and behaviours is what fuels everything we do – from purchasing and logistics to personalised customer experiences.

We are now strengthening our data organisation with an SAP Data Engineer who will help connect our strong SAP estate to the next generation of AI and analytics at Coop.

About The Role

You will be part of our Data Platform team, which sits within Coop’s AI & Business Intelligence organisation – the team building the data foundation that powers analytics and AI across the company.

This is a role at the intersection of two worlds. You will build and manage data models, transformations and integrations in SAP Datasphere and SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) – and, just as importantly, make that SAP data flow into CAP, our Azure-based data platform, where it becomes available to data scientists, analysts and business users across the company.

In other words: you don’t just build inside SAP. You make SAP data usable everywhere. You will be central to integrating our SAP S/4HANA and analytical data into CAP, so that high-quality, well-modelled data powers AI/ML, self-service analytics and better decisions throughout Coop.

What You Will Do


Develop data models, transformations and integrations in SAP Datasphere and SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).
Integrate and deliver SAP data into CAP, our Azure data platform, ensuring it is reliable, well-structured and ready for downstream analytics and AI/ML use.
Refine data from SAP systems – ideally S/4HANA – into products and insights that drive real business value.
Design and build engaging reports and dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) that make data accessible and easy to understand.
Safeguard data quality, performance and usability across the full data flow.
Partner with the business to turn requirements into robust, scalable technical solutions, and contribute across the whole development lifecycle – testing, troubleshooting and deployment included.


Who are you?

You are someone who is energised by turning data into insight and opportunity. You see the potential in building something new – and you want to be part of it from the start. You enjoy close collaboration, sharing knowledge and solving problems together, and you move naturally between thinking strategically and rolling up your sleeves to deliver.

We believe that you


Have experience developing and optimising data solutions in SAP Datasphere, SAP HANA or SAP BW.
Have a solid command of SQL, data integration and data modelling, with an eye for quality and performance in data flows.
Understand ETL processes and how to extract, transform and load data efficiently and reliably.
Know CDS (Core Data Services) and how CDS views are designed for flexible, high-performance models.
Are comfortable refining data from SAP source systems (ideally S/4HANA) into insights that support the business.
Have experience building reports and dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud.
Can communicate and collaborate effectively in cross-functional teams, discussing solutions with both technical specialists and non-technical stakeholders.


It’s meritorious if you


Have hands-on experience with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC).
Have worked with cloud data platforms – preferably Azure (e.g. Databricks, data lake / medallion architecture) and understand how SAP and cloud estates connect.
Have familiarity with core SAP S/4 business processes (sales, purchasing, inventory, finance).
Have worked with DevOps and agile delivery.


As a Person, You Are


Engaged and humble – you help out where it’s needed.
Curious and solution-oriented – you see challenges as opportunities.
Structured and thorough – you build for long-term sustainability.
Communicative and collaborative – you create clarity and shared understanding.


About Us

The grocery trade is a stable, long-term industry, and Coop’s offering is essential to the transition toward a greener, more sustainable future. You’ll join a culture defined by humility, initiative and strong values, where we take joint responsibility and genuinely want to help each other succeed. Our office in Solna Business Park offers a modern work environment with great transport links, a fantastic staff restaurant, and the option of hybrid work in agreement with your manager.

If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to contact Hiring Manager Jakob Liljedahl (jakob.liljedahl@coop.se) or the recruiter Andreas Nilsson (Andreas.nilsson2@coop.se).

Welcome with your application!

The grocery trade is a stable, long-term industry, and Coop’s offering is essential to the transition toward a greener, more sustainable future. You’ll join a culture defined by humility, initiative and strong values, where we take joint responsibility and genuinely want to help each other succeed. Our office in Solna Business Park offers a modern work environment with great transport links, a fantastic staff restaurant, and the option of hybrid work in agreement with your manager.

If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to contact Hiring Manager Jakob Liljedahl (jakob.liljedahl@coop.se) or the recruiter Andreas Nilsson (Andreas.nilsson2@coop.se).

Welcome with your application!

Title: Quantitative Risk Analyst, Credit Risk Data Management [Assistant/Regular/Senior]
Description:
Job ID: 5814

We are looking for Quantitative Risk Analyst [Assistant/Regular/Senior] from all background and level to join our Risk Models Data and Strategy teams that is recognized for its professionalism, competence and capabilities.

Do you enjoy working with data analysis? Do you like solving problems and finding new and innovative solutions? Do you understand the principles of banking and are you able to apply this knowledge in your daily work?

If you have previous experience with Credit Risk models, then great! If you have a flair for data and a genuine interest in analysis, you are also welcome!

About Our Team

Meet the Credit Risk Data Management, a part of the Risk Models Data & Strategy organization in Risk Models. We have an ambitious development plan for development of IRB models and together with our colleagues in Nordea we’ll be part of creating the future of banking.

The Risk Models Data & Strategy department, is a department consisting of 6 team, each specialized in their own part of ensuring high quality data is supporting the bank capital foundation. We add value to Nordea and our customers by improving Nordea data quality, helping visualizing and create the understanding of our data so modelling teams can build great internal models used for Own Funds calculation and the for risk management.

Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us. 

What You’ll Be Doing

As a Quantitative Risk Analyst, the specific role may vary based on qualifications, but you could i.e. play a valuable role in Data analytics, optimizing data flows, product management or as a data governance specialist. Your role could also include coordinating data related activities and ensuring proper communication within the teams and across other Risk Models areas.

Main responsibilities in this role:


Root-cause analysis for data quality issues, where we meticulously investigate and identify the underlying reasons for any discrepancies in our data. This is closely followed by data reconciliation, ensuring that our data sets are consistent and accurate across different sources.
We focus on optimizing data flows and algorithms to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of our data processes.
Own and manage all data used in credit risk models across the end-to-end lifecycle, applying data product mindset to capture, enhance and realize value throughout credit risk models.
Identify and govern critical data elements though strong knowledge of credit risk models, regulatory expectation and capital.
Secure our Data Governance framework and processes with focus on effective Data Quality Controls, also ensuring documentation of Data Lineage toward all data providers and consumers for credit risk data.
Preparing reports and other documentation for senior management and external reporting is also an integral part of our role, ensuring transparency and informed decision-making.


You’ll join an international team with variety of backgrounds and skills. We believe that strong drive and passion for change are fundamental for delivering on our commitments. Bring your background and skills to our team, and you will have plenty of opportunities to collaborate, grow and develop.

Who You Are

This is the right role for you if you:


Have an adaptability to thrive in fast-paced and dynamic environment.
Are structured and able to coordinate several deliveries simultaneously.
Are self-driven and able to work independently but also enjoy collaboration with others.
Have strong analytical skills and keen attention to detail with the ability to analyse complex data and processes to derive meaningful insights.
Have good communication and presentations skills, are not afraid to speak up and can effectively convey business requirements to IT teams and facilitate collaboration between stakeholders.
Are curious and proactive, focus on solutions rather than problems and share our enthusiasm for developing and learning and constantly improving your skills and competences.


Your background and skills include:


Experience with Credit Risk modeling, capital reporting, and understanding regulatory requirements as BCBS239 and TRIM.
Knowledge of SQL, SAS, Snowflake, Python or similar.
Master’s degree in finance, economics, engineering, physics, mathematics or similar.
A strong focus on results, deliveries and deadlines.
Experience with Big Data would be an asset.
An interest in finance and technology.


What We Offer

People come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it’s to take their career to the next level. For others, it’s to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.

A culture that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.

Hybrid working model – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.

Diversity and inclusion are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.

If this sounds like you, get in touch!

Next steps

Submit your application no later than 09/08/2026.

We enable dreams and aspirations for a greater good.

We build relationships. We add a personal touch to everything we do – when advising our customers, collaborating with colleagues, and meeting our potential candidates.

We learn and develop. We take pride in being experts and thinking ahead. We use our expertise to meet our customers’ needs, from the simplest to the most complex. We bring a growth mindset to our work that enables us to focus on a broader perspective in our daily challenges.

We lead change. We are responsible and aware of the impact of our decisions, both for our customers and for our local and global communities. Mindful of our responsibility towards current and future generations, we have made sustainability an integrated part of our business strategy.

We are Nordea. We have a 200-year history of supporting and growing the Nordic economies and our values are deeply rooted in these open, progressive and collaborative societies. As one of the biggest employers in the Nordics, Poland and Estonia, you have excellent opportunities to evolve, develop and move forward with us.

Title: Total Rewards Analyst
Description:
About Us

Legora is redefining how legal work gets done. Not built for lawyers, built with them. We work alongside the world’s best legal teams, who expect excellence, precision, and speed, and we hold ourselves to the same bar.

Our AI-native workspace lets legal professionals move faster, think more clearly, and operate with sharper precision. By analysing thousands of documents in minutes and powering end-to-end workflows, we cut through complexity, teams can focus on what matters: judgment, strategy, and outcomes.

1,000+ customers across 50+ countries trust us, including Cleary Gottlieb, Goodwin, Linklaters, White & Case, Dentons, and Barclays. We’ve scaled to $100M+ in ARR, with teams across Europe, North America and APAC, and continue to expand through acquisitions including Qura, Walter AI and Graceview.

We partner with world-class performers: including Aaron Judge and the New York Yankees, Ludvig Åberg (and his caddie), and campaigns featuring Jude Law.

Joining Legora means three things.


We lean in: ownership over titles, outcomes over intentions.
We fight for excellence: high standards, direct, ego-free feedback.
We grow together: as a team and with our customers.


Mission before ego. Everyone contributes. No one coasts.

If you’re driven by impact, pace, and raising the bar. This is the place.

The role

As Total Rewards Analyst, you'll be the analytical backbone of Legora's reward function — turning data into insight across compensation, equity, benefits, and mobility. You'll work directly within the Total Rewards team, reporting to the VP of Total Rewards, and collaborate closely with the Total Rewards Manager.

This is a role for someone who is energised by making sense of complex data, building robust processes, and making sure every part of our reward engine runs smoothly and accurately.

What You Will Do


Build and maintain reward reporting and dashboards covering compensation, equity, benefits, and mobility — enabling the team and leadership to make data-informed decisions.
Support compensation benchmarking cycles: gather and clean market data, model scenarios, and present findings clearly.
Analyse equity data to track grant activity, vesting milestones, and programme performance across regions.
Build and improve People operations processes across the reward function — creating workflows and documentation that reduce manual effort and increase accuracy.
Support the administration of benefits programmes across markets, coordinating with external vendors and ensuring data accuracy.
Support the mobility programme operationally — coordinating documentation, timelines, and third-party providers.
Help prepare materials for employee and manager communications on compensation, benefits, and equity topics, and contribute to reward-related compliance activities as needed.


Who You Are

You love digging into data to find patterns and build clarity out of complexity. You're a natural problem-solver with a keen eye for process, accuracy, and making things run better.

We Think You'll Thrive Here If You Have


3–5 years of experience in a People, Reward, Finance, or Operations analyst role — ideally in a high-growth or international environment.
Strong analytical skills with experience handling complex datasets; proficiency in Excel or Google Sheets is essential, and experience with HRIS or reward tools is a plus.
Familiarity with compensation frameworks, equity programmes, or benefits administration — even if not a specialist in all areas.
High attention to detail and comfort working with sensitive, confidential data.
A process-builder mindset: you don't just do the work, you look for ways to make the work better.
Clear and confident communicator, able to present data and findings simply to a range of stakeholders.


Why Legora

This is an exceptional opportunity to grow within a reward function that's building from the ground up. You'll gain broad exposure across every dimension of total rewards — compensation, equity, benefits, and mobility — while working alongside experienced people leaders in a company with real momentum.

We value clarity, humility, and impact. Your work will directly support the team that shapes how every Legora employee is rewarded. We're just getting started.

Legora is an Equal Opportunity Employer

At Legora, we believe great teams are built on diversity of thought and experience. We’re proud to be an equal opportunity employer and committed to creating an inclusive, high-performance culture where everyone can do their best work. We welcome people of all backgrounds and don’t discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, disability, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
We are Sweden's leading app and marketplace for booking beauty and wellness services. As a SaaS company with industry-leading software, we empower more than 16,000 professionals across the country. Our platform brings together nearly every salon and clinic in Sweden, making it easy for consumers to book appointments with hairdressers, massage therapists, podiatrists, beauty salons, chiropractors, and many other specialists—all in one place.

On Bokadirekt.se, consumers can discover, book, and pay for treatments and services in just a few clicks. Every month, our platform is visited by 3 million consumers, and more than 2 million bookings are made through our website and app. We’re growing rapidly and are currently a team of more than 100 employees, with offices in Stockholm and Luleå.

About The Role

We are looking for a Data Engineer to join our Data & Analytics team and help shape the future of data and AI at Bokadirekt.

As a Data Engineer, you will be responsible for building, maintaining, and evolving the data platform that powers analytics, business intelligence, and AI across the company. You'll work at the intersection of data engineering and AI, ensuring our data infrastructure is reliable, scalable, and ready to support everything from reporting and experimentation to LLM-powered features and machine learning pipelines.

You will collaborate closely with product managers, software engineers, analysts, and other stakeholders to create high-quality data products and enable data-driven decision-making throughout the organization. This is an opportunity to have a significant impact on both our technical platform and how data and AI are used to create value for millions of users every month.

What You'll Do


Design, build, and optimise scalable ELT pipelines that power analytics, reporting, and AI initiatives across the company
Build a reliable, trusted and well-documented source of truth for company KPIs
Work on the integration of AI capabilities into our data infrastructure
Collaborate on the development and deployment of machine learning solutions and AI-powered features
Identify and carry out data platform cost optimization initiatives


Who you are


Degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Physics or related field
Proven experience (4-6 years) as a Data Engineer, Data Scientist, ML Engineer or AI Engineer, ideally in a technology-driven environment
Strong proficiency in SQL and Python
Solid data engineering fundamentals with hands-on AI experience
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders
Experience with GCP (BigQuery, GCS, Gemini Agent Platform, Looker), Airflow, dbt and Cube is a plus. Bonus points for prompt engineering, LLM APIs, NL querying and RAG
Professional fluency in English


What We Offer


The opportunity to build and scale the data platform behind one of Sweden's most widely used consumer apps
The chance to work with modern data and AI technologies, helping shape the foundations for analytics, machine learning, and LLM-powered solutions
A collaborative culture built on ownership, innovation, and knowledge sharing
A flexible hybrid work model with four days in the office each week, including Wednesdays as our "Inspiration Day," when the whole company comes together for breakfast and shared activities
A modern office in the heart of Stockholm
A competitive salary and benefits package


About The Position

Start date: As soon as possible / By agreement

Location: Our office at Kungsgatan 38 in central Stockholm (hybrid)

Employment type: Full-time, permanent position

Application deadline: August 14

Application

We review applications continuously, and the position may be filled before the application deadline. We therefore encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

Summer is here, and we are also taking some time to recharge. During the holiday period, our response times may be slightly longer than usual, and the recruitment process will resume from week 32.

Thank you for your patience. We wish you a wonderful summer and hope to hear from you soon!

We are a SaaS company offering industry leading software for companies and customers primarily within health and beauty.

We are proud to be Sweden's largest app and marketplace for bookings with over 16,000 affiliated specialists nationwide. We make almost all of Sweden's salons and clinics bookable in one place. For example, hairdressers, massage therapists, pedicurists, beauty salons, chiropractors, and much more.

On Bokadirekt, you as a consumer can search for, book, and pay for treatments and services with just a few clicks. Every month, 3 million consumers visit bokadirekt.se, and 2 million bookings are made via our website and app.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist
We're partnering with an established fintech that's using data science to solve complex business problems at scale. Operating in a highly digital environment, data sits at the centre of decision-making, giving the team the opportunity to test ideas quickly, measure outcomes, and create meaningful impact across the business.
As the company continues to invest in its Data & ML capabilities, they're looking for a Data Scientist to join a collaborative team where curiosity, analytical thinking and commercial impact are valued just as highly as technical expertise.
About the role:This is an opportunity to apply statistics, machine learning and advanced analytics to solve real business problems. You'll work closely with commercial teams to understand complex challenges, identify opportunities, and develop data-driven solutions that create measurable value.
The role has a particular focus on marketing and growth, but you'll be joining a wider Data Science function and working alongside experienced Data Scientists on a variety of business-critical initiatives.
This is a role for someone who enjoys asking the right questions, applying the appropriate analytical techniques, and taking ownership of problems from initial exploration through to models being deployed into production and delivering measurable impact.
What you'll do:Frame business challenges as data science problems and identify the most appropriate analytical approach.Develop statistical and machine learning models that improve decision making and business performance.Design and evaluate experiments, causal analyses and predictive models to generate actionable insights.Work closely with business stakeholders to understand priorities and translate data into commercial value.Partner with engineering teams to ensure models are successfully deployed, monitored and continue delivering value in production.Identify new opportunities where data science can improve efficiency and support business growth.
About you:We're looking for someone with a strong academic foundation who enjoys applying theory to real-world business challenges.
You'll likely have:3+ years' experience in a Data Science or advanced analytics role.A degree in Mathematics, Statistics or another highly quantitative discipline, with a Master's degree or above preferred.Strong Python skills for data analysis, statistical modelling and machine learning.Excellent understanding of statistical methods, modelling techniques and when to apply them appropriately.Experience building models that have been deployed into production and created measurable business value.Confidence translating ambiguous business questions into structured analytical solutions.Strong communication skills and the ability to work closely with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.A proactive mindset and the ability to take ownership from problem definition through to business impact.
Experience working with marketing analytics, experimentation, attribution or customer behaviour would be beneficial, but the strongest candidates will be those with excellent data science fundamentals and a passion for solving business problems.
Why join?This is a business where data genuinely drives decisions. With a highly digital product and rich datasets, you'll be able to form hypotheses, test them quickly and see the impact of your work in real time.
You'll be joining a collaborative Data & ML team within an established fintech that combines the stability of a successful business with the agility and autonomy of a smaller organisation. Rather than working on isolated research projects or predefined tickets, you'll have the opportunity to shape solutions, influence decision-making and see your work translate into measurable commercial outcomes.
The company offers a flexible hybrid working model (typically two to three days a week in their central Stockholm office), modern tooling, excellent benefits and the opportunity to work alongside experienced Data Scientists in an environment that values continuous learning, collaboration and technical excellence.
If you're a Data Scientist who enjoys combining strong statistical thinking with practical problem solving - and wants to see your work make a tangible business impact - we'd love to hear from you.
