Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About the Company

Our client is a global, technology-driven systematic investment manager operating across all major liquid asset classes. With a strong culture of innovation and a scientific approach to investing, they combine cutting-edge data, research, and engineering to deliver consistent, high-quality returns.




The Role

We are looking for an exceptional Data Scientist to join a high-performing Commodities desk. Sitting at the intersection of Research, Trading, and Engineering, you will play a critical role in ensuring the firm leverages its data effectively to drive investment decisions.




What You'll Be Doing

Collaborating with the trading desk to design and implement robust data pipelines
Working alongside data engineers to onboard relevant datasets into the firm's infrastructure
Implementing data transformations to produce high-value datasets used directly in investment decisions
Writing clean, well-structured Python code to extract, clean, and aggregate data from a wide range of raw sources and formats
Managing the full end-to-end process of onboarding new datasets
Proactively identifying and solving data-related problems to minimise time to production
Experimenting with novel data extraction methods to continuously improve the firm's data toolkit




What We're Looking For

3+ years of experience as a Data Scientist or in a similar quantitative role; buy-side experience is a strong advantage
Postgraduate degree (MSc or PhD) in a quantitative discipline - Mathematics, Physics, Engineering or similar
Advanced Python skills, including strong proficiency with data handling libraries such as Pandas and NumPy
Experience working with both traditional and alternative financial datasets
A genuine interest in financial markets and data-driven analysis
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively across Research, Trading, Engineering, and external vendors
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-performance environment




Why Apply?

This is a rare opportunity to join a world-class quantitative investment firm at the forefront of data-driven investing. The team offers a collaborative, intellectually rigorous environment where your work will have direct impact on the firm's investment performance.

  • Writing clean, well-structured Python code to extract, clean, and aggregate data from a wide range of raw sources and formats
  • Managing the full end-to-end process of onboarding new datasets
  • Proactively identifying and solving data-related problems to minimise time to production
  • Experimenting with novel data extraction methods to continuously improve the firm's data toolkit


What We're Looking For

  • 3+ years of experience as a Data Scientist or in a similar quantitative role; buy-side experience is a strong advantage
  • Postgraduate degree (MSc or PhD) in a quantitative discipline - Mathematics, Physics, Engineering or similar
  • Advanced Python skills, including strong proficiency with data handling libraries such as Pandas and NumPy
  • Experience working with both traditional and alternative financial datasets
  • A genuine interest in financial markets and data-driven analysis
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate effectively across Research, Trading, Engineering, and external vendors
  • Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-performance environment


Why Apply?

  • This is a rare opportunity to join a world-class quantitative investment firm at the forefront of data-driven investing. The team offers a collaborative, intellectually rigorous environment where your work will have direct impact on the firm's investment performance.

Title: Data Engineer - Supply Chain Advanced Analytics - Viby
Description:
Are you excited by building scalable data solutions that directly impact Supply Chain decisions across a global organization? Curious about how data can truly drive business decisions - and not afraid to challenge the status quo to get there?

You will be part of a collaborative Advanced Analytics team where we co-create data-driven solutions with the business and continuously improve our platforms and way of working. We value curiosity, dialogue and the courage to challenge ideas to create better outcomes.

How You Will Make an Impact?

As part of the Supply Chain Advanced Analytics product team, you will work across a broad analytics landscape supporting functions such as SC Finance, Logistics, Planning, QEHS and Procurement.

You will contribute to building and maintaining robust data solutions while collaborating closely with business stakeholders to deliver real value. You will not only implement solutions but also seek to understand business challenges, ask the right questions and help continuously improve how data is used across the organization.

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include


Build and maintain end-to-end data pipelines (ingestion, transformation and delivery)
Implement enhancements to improve functionality and performance of supported solutions
Provide technical feedback and drive improvements to operational practices
Lead and coordinate UAT and PAT with business stakeholders, ensuring solutions are challenged, validated and aligned with real business needs
Share and document knowledge to support team development and future scalability


What Will Make You Successful?

We are looking for a data-focused IT Developer with strong analytical and collaboration skills, who thrives in a self-managing, collaborative and business-driven environment. You are curious about both data and the business behind it and motivated to understand how solutions create value. You have strong communication skills and can translate between technical and business perspectives as well as an eagerness to learn and continuously develop your technical and business skills. Further, we are looking for below professional competencies


Hands-on experience with Azure data services (e.g., Databricks, Data Lake, Synapse, Azure SQL, Data Factory, Azure Data Explorer)
Good understanding of Azure as a platform
Solid experience with data systems, analytics and databases
SQL proficiency and working knowledge of relational databases
Basic to intermediate Python knowledge
Familiarity with data modelling and data pipeline technologies
Experience with Git and branching strategies
Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and deployments
Experience working closely with business stakeholders (requirements gathering, alignment, etc.)


What Do We Offer?

You will join a team of 21 specialists working across Data Foundation, Power BI, SAP BW, SAP Analytics Cloud, BODS and Power Apps. Together, we deliver business-driven analytics solutions that strengthen decision-making across the Supply Chain.

We offer


A collaborative and diverse product team working closely with the business
Exposure to a wide range of technologies and data platforms
Opportunities to shape future analytics capabilities
A strong culture of knowledge sharing and continuous improvement
A direct impact on global Supply Chain transformation


Our team delivers solutions such as BW reports, Power BI dashboards, Power Apps, curated datasets and machine learning models - with AI agents as our next step. All solutions are built in close partnership with stakeholders, ensuring real business value.

Would you like to join us?

If you are passionate about data engineering and want to make a real impact in a global Supply Chain organization, we encourage you to apply.

Please apply as soon as possible, as we process applications on a continuous basis.

For more information, please contact Magda Merecka at magmg@arlafoods.com

Shape the Future of Dairy

Arla is a global leader in the dairy industry, committed to enabling good food choices that make life better, providing people with natural, sustainable nourishment, while taking care at every step to ensure Arla is a choice they can feel good about. If you are looking to shape the future with an ambitious global cooperative that truly cares about your growth, where everyone feels valued and empowered, and collaboration is the core of culture, Arla is a choice you can feel good about.

Title: Bliv Data Analyst i EIFO – en 12-måneders tidsbegrænset stilling med fokus på data og beslutningskraft
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Har du lyst til at arbejde med Power BI, datamodellering og visuel formidling – og vil du være med til at gøre data til et endnu stærkere beslutningsgrundlag i EIFO? I Danmarks Eksport- og Investeringsfond søger vi en nysgerrig og motiveret Data Analyst til en 12-måneders tidsbegrænset ansættelse, som vil være med til at udvikle rapportering, self service-løsninger og datadrevet beslutningsstøtte på tværs af organisationen.




I EIFO er vi i fuld gang med at løfte dataområdet, så indsigt bliver til konkrete resultater i forretningen. Vores Data-afdeling er relativt ny og i hastig udvikling, og vi indgår dagligt i fire tværfaglige teams på tværs af Data & Digitalisering. I dag er vi seks dygtige kolleger med erfaring inden for bl.a. datamodellering, produktkendskab, data engineering og rapportering – og vi fungerer som ejere af EIFO’s BI-løsning.




Vi deler viden, har et højt fagligt ambitionsniveau og en kultur præget af åbenhed, engagement og lysten til at bygge noget stærkt sammen.




Vi leder efter dig, der brænder for data, har blik for forretningens behov og kan omsætte komplekse problemstillinger til enkle og værdiskabende dataløsninger.




Din nye rolle og ansvarsområder

Som Data Analyst bliver du en vigtig del af arbejdet med at udbrede datadrevet beslutningsstøtte i EIFO. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med både Data-teamet og forretningen om rapportering, dashboards, semantiske modeller og self service-løsninger, der gør data lettere at anvende i hverdagen.




Du får mulighed for at arbejde med hele værdikæden fra forretningsbehov til færdig dataløsning.




I praksis betyder det, at du blandt andet skal:




1) Forstå og samarbejde – Arbejde tæt sammen med forretningen for at forstå behov, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og omsætte ønsker til konkrete dataløsninger. Bidrage til at definere KPI’er, begreber og succeskriterier, så løsningerne skaber reel værdi.




2) Modellere og udvikle – Udvikle og vedligeholde Power BI-rapporter, dashboards, målinger og semantiske modeller. Arbejde med DAX, datamodellering og dokumentation, så vores rapporteringsløsninger er robuste, forståelige og nemme at anvende.




3) Enable og formidle – Understøtte forretningen i at anvende data bedre gennem dialog, sparring, træning og løbende feedback. Hjælpe brugerne med at forstå datagrundlag, rapporter og mulighederne i self service, så data bliver en naturlig del af beslutninger og arbejdsgange.




Du vil få ansvar fra start, men også støtte fra erfarne kolleger i teamet. Rollen giver mulighed for at udvikle dig fagligt og være med til at præge, hvordan vi arbejder med BI, self service og dataanvendelse i EIFO.




Hvem er du?

Vi ser for os en kollega, der har et godt analytisk fundament, interesse for forretningen og lyst til at udvikle sig sammen med os.




Vi forestiller os, at du kan se dig selv i følgende:

Har en relevant videregående uddannelse inden for fx økonomi, IT, data, business analytics eller lignende.
Har 2–4 års erfaring som Data Analyst, BI Analyst, Business Analyst eller i en beslægtet rolle.
Har erfaring med Power BI og interesse for at bygge brugervenlige rapporter og dashboards.
Har kendskab til DAX, datamodellering og semantiske modeller.
Har forståelse for datakvalitet, dokumentation og fælles definitioner.
Er stærk til at formidle data og komplekse problemstillinger på en enkel og forståelig måde.
Har lyst til at undervise, facilitere workshops eller træne brugere i anvendelsen af rapporter, dashboards og self service-løsninger.
Kan arbejde tæt sammen med forretningen og skabe tryghed, ejerskab og adoption omkring nye dataløsninger.
Erfaring med Microsoft Fabric eller øvrige produkter i Fabric-suiten er en fordel, men ikke et krav.




Du behøver ikke kunne det hele fra dag ét – vigtigst er, at du har drivkraft, læringslyst og et teknisk fundament, som vi kan bygge videre på sammen.




Et tydeligt formål og attraktive vilkår

I EIFO arbejder du for dansk erhvervslivs fremtid – og du får mulighed for at skabe reel værdi for virksomheder, der driver vækst, grøn omstilling og innovation.




Du får:

Et spændende og udfordrende job med et klart purpose og stor samfundsmæssig betydning
En arbejdsplads præget af forretningsfokus, enkelhed og tillid i en uhøjtidelig kontekst
Attraktive vilkår, fleksibilitet og plads til både ambition og balance i arbejdslivet
Engagerede og fagligt stærke kolleger i en mangfoldig og inkluderende kultur, hvor forskellighed ses som en styrke.




Er du klar til at tage næste skridt i din karriere?

Send din ansøgning og dit CV via ansøgningssystemet senest den 10. august 2026.




Som led i vores arbejde med inkluderende rekruttering beder vi dig venligst om ikke at inkludere billede, alder og civilstand i dit ansøgningsmateriale.




Vi behandler ansøgninger efter ansøgningsfristens udløb og forventer at gennemføre samtaler i forlængelse heraf.




Stillingen er en 12-måneders tidsbegrænset ansættelse med tiltrædelse hurtigst muligt.




Rekrutteringsforløbet består af to samtalerunder. I forbindelse med anden samtalerunde gennemføres personligheds- og logiske tests samt en mindre caseopgave. Referencer indhentes efter aftale i den afsluttende del af processen, og straffeattest skal fremvises ved ansættelse.




Vil du høre mere? Kontakt Product Owner, Rasmus Pallesen, på rasp@eifo.dk. Vi ønsker henvendelser pr. mail. Grundet sommerperioden kan der være længere svartid end normalt, og spørgsmål forventes besvaret i uge 32. Vi sætter pris på din forståelse og tålmodighed.




Hos EIFO tror vi på, at diversitet skaber bedre løsninger – derfor opfordrer vi alle kvalificerede kandidater til at søge uanset alder, køn, religion eller etnisk tilhørsforhold.




Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!




Om Danmarks Eksport- og Investeringsfond (EIFO)

Danmarks Eksport- og Investeringsfond (EIFO) er danske virksomheders fælles indgang til risikovillig statslig finansiering. Vi har et samlet forretningsomfang på omkring 150 mia. kr. og har knap 500 medarbejdere. Med professionalisme og internationalt udsyn arbejder vi hver dag for at understøtte de erhvervssucceser, der kan gøre Danmark rigere på det, der gør en forskel. EIFO er Danmarks nationale erhvervsfremmende bank, investeringsfond og Danmarks officielle eksportkreditinstitut i én og samme finansielle institution.

Title: Business Analytics Consultant
Description:
Twoday er den førende digitale transformationspartner i Nordeuropa med en global tilstedeværelse. Med cirka 3.000 teknologer samarbejder vi med de mest beundrede private og offentlige organisationer for at levere banebrydende digitale løsninger. Vores dybe brancheekspertise omfatter Data & AI, softwareudvikling, digitale oplevelser og forretningsapplikationer. Vores team, der opererer i hele Norden og Litauen, genererede en omsætning på 280 millioner euro i 2023. Vi betjener over 8.000 kunder og understøtter deres digitale transformationsrejser.

Vil du være med på Danmarks stærkeste team af Business Analytics-konsulenter? Vil du arbejde med nogle af Danmark største virksomheder? Tænder du på at gøre komplekse problemstillinger til simple løsninger for kunderne? Så er twoday Data & AI det rigtige sted for dig! Vi søger nye Business Analytics-konsulenter til et af vores kontorer i Danmark.

Om stillingen

Som Business Analytics-konsulent vil du indgå i et stærkt team med en varierende hverdag – du vil få ansvar, frihed og faglig udvikling. Vores konsulenter besidder hver især stærke kompetencer, og du vil opleve kolleger, der er specialister på deres område.

Du vil sammen med vores kunder afklare de forretningsmæssige behov og derefter designe, udvikle og implementere løsningen. Alt efter kompleksitet og størrelse af løsningen arbejder du enten i et team eller som primær konsulent på løsningerne.

Vi holder faste firmadage, hvor sparring, idéudveksling og kollegialt sammenhold er i højsædet. Du vil opleve at blive udfordret, men du vil også få mulighed for at udfordre dine kolleger.

Fristet, men stadig i tvivl?


Turbo på det faglige! Hos twoday Data & AI er vores medarbejdere kendt for at have et skyhøjt fagligt niveau. Samtidig med at vi har det godt socialt.
Lækre kontorer! twoday Data & AI har lækre og moderne kontorer centralt placeret i København, Aarhus, Aalborg og Kolding.
Tid til fordybelse! Vi har fire årlige fagdage, hvor idéudvikling, udvikling og sparring er i højsædet.
Uddannelse! Hos twoday Data & AI får du mulighed for efteruddannelse og certificeringer.
Attraktiv lønpakke! Vi tilbyder en attraktiv lønpakke med branchens bedste bonusordning.
Fantastisk kaffe! Hos twoday Data & AI har du adgang til fitness, frugt og fantastisk god kaffe.


Om dig

Du har flere års relevant erhvervserfaring og brænder for hele tiden at blive bedre. Du har en baggrund som BI- eller datakonsulent, controller i en større virksomhed eller noget lign. Du har erfaring med Azure Data Platform, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks og/eller SQL Server. Endvidere har du kendskab til én eller flere front-end værktøjer såsom Power BI, Excel eller lign.

Du ved, at livet som konsulent er udfordrende og til tider meget krævende. Vores vigtigste aktiv er vores kunder og medarbejdere, og du er klar til at give, hvad der skal til, for at skabe et godt forhold både internt og eksternt samt at udvikle Business Intelligence-løsninger i verdensklasse.

Din uddannelse kan være cand.merc., cand.merc.dat eller cand.oecon – eller for den sags skyld noget helt fjerde. Det vigtigste er, at du brænder for Business Analytics og ønsker at gøre en forskel.

Dine kommunikative evner er i top, og du skriver og taler flydende dansk og engelsk.

Vi begejstrer med data – måske vil du være med?

Så send din ansøgning i dag, de vil løbende blive læst.

Der vil løbende blive indkaldt til indledende samtaler. Du ansøger ved at klikke på knappen nedenfor. Alle ansøgninger behandles fortroligt.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er Ann Brahe-Orlandi, Talent Acquisition Manager, klar til at hjælpe. Hun glæder sig til at høre fra dig på ann.brahe-orlandi@twoday.com.

Hvad kan vi tilbyde?

Twoday er placeret i moderne lokaler på 30 steder i Norge, Sverige, Danmark, Finland og Litauen. Vi har et stærkt og unikt socialt miljø med højt engagerede medarbejdere. Hvis du er den kandidat, vi leder efter, vil du få en central rolle med spændende muligheder for at udvikle dig i en international og innovativ gruppe, med spændende produkter og tjenester inden for teknologi og software. Du vil også have fleksibiliteten til at "jumpe" og arbejde fra et af vores hovedkontorer i Oslo, Stockholm, København, Helsinki eller Vilnius til tider.

HTML:
Twoday er den førende digitale transformationspartner i Nordeuropa med en global tilstedeværelse. Med cirka 3.000 teknologer samarbejder vi med de mest beundrede private og offentlige organisationer for at levere banebrydende digitale løsninger. Vores dybe brancheekspertise omfatter Data & AI, softwareudvikling, digitale oplevelser og forretningsapplikationer. Vores team, der opererer i hele Norden og Litauen, genererede en omsætning på 280 millioner euro i 2023. Vi betjener over 8.000 kunder og understøtter deres digitale transformationsrejser.

Vil du være med på Danmarks stærkeste team af Business Analytics-konsulenter? Vil du arbejde med nogle af Danmark største virksomheder? Tænder du på at gøre komplekse problemstillinger til simple løsninger for kunderne? Så er twoday Data & AI det rigtige sted for dig! Vi søger nye Business Analytics-konsulenter til et af vores kontorer i Danmark.

Om stillingen

Som Business Analytics-konsulent vil du indgå i et stærkt team med en varierende hverdag – du vil få ansvar, frihed og faglig udvikling. Vores konsulenter besidder hver især stærke kompetencer, og du vil opleve kolleger, der er specialister på deres område.

Du vil sammen med vores kunder afklare de forretningsmæssige behov og derefter designe, udvikle og implementere løsningen. Alt efter kompleksitet og størrelse af løsningen arbejder du enten i et team eller som primær konsulent på løsningerne.

Vi holder faste firmadage, hvor sparring, idéudveksling og kollegialt sammenhold er i højsædet. Du vil opleve at blive udfordret, men du vil også få mulighed for at udfordre dine kolleger.

Fristet, men stadig i tvivl?

  • Turbo på det faglige! Hos twoday Data & AI er vores medarbejdere kendt for at have et skyhøjt fagligt niveau. Samtidig med at vi har det godt socialt.
  • Lækre kontorer! twoday Data & AI har lækre og moderne kontorer centralt placeret i København, Aarhus, Aalborg og Kolding.
  • Tid til fordybelse! Vi har fire årlige fagdage, hvor idéudvikling, udvikling og sparring er i højsædet.
  • Uddannelse! Hos twoday Data & AI får du mulighed for efteruddannelse og certificeringer.
  • Attraktiv lønpakke! Vi tilbyder en attraktiv lønpakke med branchens bedste bonusordning.
  • Fantastisk kaffe! Hos twoday Data & AI har du adgang til fitness, frugt og fantastisk god kaffe.

Om dig

Du har flere års relevant erhvervserfaring og brænder for hele tiden at blive bedre. Du har en baggrund som BI- eller datakonsulent, controller i en større virksomhed eller noget lign. Du har erfaring med Azure Data Platform, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks og/eller SQL Server. Endvidere har du kendskab til én eller flere front-end værktøjer såsom Power BI, Excel eller lign.

Du ved, at livet som konsulent er udfordrende og til tider meget krævende. Vores vigtigste aktiv er vores kunder og medarbejdere, og du er klar til at give, hvad der skal til, for at skabe et godt forhold både internt og eksternt samt at udvikle Business Intelligence-løsninger i verdensklasse.

Din uddannelse kan være cand.merc., cand.merc.dat eller cand.oecon – eller for den sags skyld noget helt fjerde. Det vigtigste er, at du brænder for Business Analytics og ønsker at gøre en forskel.

Dine kommunikative evner er i top, og du skriver og taler flydende dansk og engelsk.

Vi begejstrer med data – måske vil du være med?

Så send din ansøgning i dag, de vil løbende blive læst.

Der vil løbende blive indkaldt til indledende samtaler. Du ansøger ved at klikke på knappen nedenfor. Alle ansøgninger behandles fortroligt.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er Ann Brahe-Orlandi, Talent Acquisition Manager, klar til at hjælpe. Hun glæder sig til at høre fra dig på ann.brahe-orlandi@twoday.com.

Hvad kan vi tilbyde?

Twoday er placeret i moderne lokaler på 30 steder i Norge, Sverige, Danmark, Finland og Litauen. Vi har et stærkt og unikt socialt miljø med højt engagerede medarbejdere. Hvis du er den kandidat, vi leder efter, vil du få en central rolle med spændende muligheder for at udvikle dig i en international og innovativ gruppe, med spændende produkter og tjenester inden for teknologi og software. Du vil også have fleksibiliteten til at "jumpe" og arbejde fra et af vores hovedkontorer i Oslo, Stockholm, København, Helsinki eller Vilnius til tider.

Title: Business Intelligence Consultant
Company: SameSystem - Retail WFM | Part of ECIT
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
BI Consultant




Please note that fluency in Danish is a requirement for this position.

Due to the summer holidays, please expect a longer processing time.




SameSystem

Are you ready to turn data into real commercial value for some of Europe's largest retail and hospitality brands? At SameSystem, we empower companies like Bestseller, Nike, Normal, and Matas to optimize their operations daily with our cutting-edge AI-driven Workforce Management Solution. We're looking for a BI Consultant who is as comfortable building a clean dashboard as sitting across the table from a customer explaining what it means for their business.

This is a full-time role, and you'll join our Customer Success team in Copenhagen. You'll own the business intelligence and reporting side of our platform: building and maintaining reports in our BI tool, Sisense, and being the person our customers and colleagues turn to when a number needs to make sense. We've deliberately placed this role in Customer Success rather than in a back-office data team, because the reporting is only worth as much as the commercial insight it drives. This role suits someone who is happy behind the screen building things properly, but who does not want to only sit behind the screen.

This is a new role, so the right person will have real influence over how we build, package, and deliver the analytics side of our platform.




Key responsibilities

Building the reports

Design, build, and maintain BI reports and dashboards in Sisense, with strong attention to data integrity, performance, and usability
Translate customer and internal requirements into robust data models and reporting logic
Own reporting quality across accounts, including validation, troubleshooting, and iterative improvement
Develop new reporting capabilities and scope custom development work as our customers' needs grow




Working with customers

Join customer meetings and represent the BI and analytics part of the SameSystem platform
Talk specific development and scoping with customers, and translate their business questions into what we can actually build
Help customers see the value in their data, and support Customer Success and Sales in retention, upselling, and expansion through analytics
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and Support to feed customer needs back into the platform




What we're looking for

Proven experience building reports and dashboards in a BI tool. Experience with Sisense is a strong plus; solid experience with a comparable tool (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik) is very welcome
Enough experience under your belt to own your work end to end
Strong SQL and data modelling skills, and the instinct to build things properly rather than just quickly
Commercial acumen. You understand that a report exists to drive a decision, and you're comfortable talking value and scope with customers
Confidence in client-facing settings, presenting clearly to both operational and managerial stakeholders
The balance we keep coming back to: someone who can do focused, hands-on build work and also step into a customer meeting and hold their own
A self-starter who takes ownership and isn't afraid to share ideas. Execution matters
Nice to have: comfort scoping and pricing development work. If pricing is new to you, that's fine, we can teach it
Fluency in Danish and English; additional European languages are a plus
Familiarity with retail operations or workforce management is a plus




What you'll get

At SameSystem, we are committed to transforming retail and hospitality through cutting-edge technology. Joining our team means:

Working in a dynamic environment where innovation drives success.
Contributing to the success of over 185,000 employees across 12,500+ stores in 24 countries.
Access to professional growth opportunities in a company that values expertise and collaboration.
Being part of a supportive culture that prioritizes work-life balance and employee satisfaction.
Lots of social activities and fun parties as well as "clubs" run by employees where SameSystem sponsors going to a sauna, playing badminton, drinking port wine, or whatever else the team can think of.
Lunch and as much coffee, soda or energy drink as you can muster.




If you have any questions about this position, please reach out to Nikolai May Yde at nikolai.yde@samesystem.com.

Title: Data Scientists for Big Data, IoT and AI projects
Description:
Stillingsbeskrivelse

Do you like working creatively with data analytics, big data, AI, visualisation, NoSQL and databases? Would you like to join an international organisation where we already work with data analytics and machine learning projects? Join BEUMER Group and exploit the chance to innovate, develop and test concepts for products and tools and most importantly, develop your own skill.

You will team up with colleagues, so you can share and grow your knowledge within the data science field. It also means focusing on using data from an already existing infrastructure where we collect data from more than 350 sites worldwide.

Join our Customer Diagnostics Center

You become part of our Data Analytics department, which is part of our Customer Diagnostics Center (CDC). In the CDC, which is part of our Customer Support division, we are tasked with making sure our installed base of systems around the globe are healthy and running smoothly.

Here, you join a well-established but agile team of highly sought for data scientists. You have the freedom to develop solutions across the organisation focusing on creativity and innovation on the conceptual stages of possible new products, services and tools.

In the CDC, you will find a multitude of skills and competences, and we enjoy an informal atmosphere where we combine our strengths to create brand new solutions for colleagues and customers. Moreover, you become part of a data analytics forum where we work closely with universities to expand our knowledge.

Create value through data across the organisation

Through a continuous dialogue with our business and product development, you will be responsible for defining new products, services and tools based on data and machine learning. We are a large organisation with several specialist fields, which means that we can enjoy the value of data analysis across disciplines.

Your Tasks Will Include


Initiating mining of existing data from material handling systems running in production, looking for possible bottlenecks, optimizations etc.
Processing, cleansing, and verifying the integrity of data used for analysis.
Participating in the maintenance of the existing data pipeline.
Participate in defining and evaluating new techniques for data collection and analysis.
Drive the rollout and extension of our data collection to all sites in the BEUMER Group.


We have a strong preference for open-source software and are working with platforms and tools such as Kubernetes, ArgoCD, MLFlow, vLLM, Elastic stack, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, Numaflow, Python/Java/Golang, Jupyter notebooks and Opentelemetry. You will test and implement prototypes, e.g. for customer solutions or for your colleagues to provide them with the right tools to perfect their work. As such, you bridge the worlds of product development, subject specialists and technology to innovate the business.

Experience with data driven decision making, big data and concept development?

Role

Most important to this role is that you have a good all-round technical foundation, allowing you to implement software, configure tools, understand our domain and be a sounding board in the creative process. To do so, we also imagine that you:


Hold a master's degree or PhD as a computer scientist, engineer, physicist or similar.
Have a great understanding of and hands-on experience with using data analytics for system and concept development.
Speak and write English fluently and it is a big plus if you understand Danish as well.


Personality-wise, you are a strong communicator who can work across the organisation and with customers. Your strength is within innovation and creative thinking, which shows itself in added value in the concept development process and tests.

Interested?

If you have any questions regarding this position, please do not hesitate to contact Head of Global Data Analytics Anders Lildballe at +45 30 17 93 48. Please apply online via beumergroup.com/career.

We look forward to meeting you.

BEUMER Group

Conveying, loading, palletising, packing, sortation and distribution – BEUMER Group is a leader within the development and manufacture of high-technological intralogistics systems for global markets. Our employees stand out from others thanks to their ability to supply innovative solutions to our customers. They can do this because they know the industry and are passionate about continuously developing and expanding their knowledge. We support these high standards through teamwork, mutual respect and a working culture based on trust that fosters stability and security for all our employees. Our common goal is to implement outstanding and innovative projects worldwide.

Title: Data Management Consultant
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description



Are you passionate about data and driven by the opportunity to make a real impact by helping companies turn facts into actionable insights? Do you thrive in dynamic environments where you work with some of the most complex and modern data solutions? If so, you might be our next colleague.



Responsibilities & technologies



In Data Management, you will become part of a team that contributes to enabling our customers to develop and manage their business based on data and knowledge.



You will work in the intersection of technology and business. Information and data are at the core of our work, and we can see that our analyses, advice, and final solutions create value for our customers. The work involves developing technical solutions to collect, process, model, analyze, and visualize data.



To provide the best possible advice and support to our customers, we place great importance on staying up to date with the latest trends and technologies in data management. You will work with a broad range of market-leading technologies, including: Azure SQL Server, Snowflake, dbt, Microsoft SQL Server, and others.



Your tasks are highly varied and depend on your skills and the direction you wish to develop.



Your responsibilites will typically include:



Design, modeling, and building of data structures such as databases.
Structuring and collection of data from various sources.
Implementation of business logic on data for use in reporting.
Customer advisory, strategy work, and business analysis in collaboration with our customers.
Development of reports for business users with a focus on front-end.

About you



You are newly graduated or have up to a couple of years of relevant professional experience within data management. Through your education, you have gained a solid professional foundation and a logical approach to handling complex data and challenges. You thrive in a development-oriented environment with a focus on collaboration and continuous personal and professional development.



To succeed in this role, you have experience with or the interest to learn:



ETL and Database technologies.
Languages such as various versions of SQL, Python, and R.
Business Intelligence tools: Power BI, Tableau, and others.
Full professional proficiency in Danish and English (written and spoken) is a must.

Continue developing with us

At Netcompany, we focus on your personal and professional development.



When you join us, you will be assigned a personal mentor who will guide you through your development and help you achieve your goals. The close relationship with an experienced colleague helps ensure the well-being of each employee and provides you with concrete insights into the many different career opportunities as you build your experience.



Additionally, you will be enrolled in our internal training programme, Netcompany Academy, where you are offered courses and seminars on everything from technologies to customer management. All courses are taught by experienced Netcompany colleagues, ensuring that all content is highly relevant and builds upon your professional skills, preparing you for various tasks and continuously improving your expertise throughout your career with us.



Join an inclusive community



At Netcompany, we highly prioritise an inclusive community. We actively promote an open and welcoming work culture based on collaboration and mutual trust, where there is always an opportunity for sparring.



As part of our culture and values, all employees, regardless of background, identity, and experience, are met with understanding and respect and given equal opportunities for development and well-being.



As we are an international company with offices in many countries, you will have the opportunity to learn and collaborate internationally with colleagues from across the world. We offer a shared environment where we engage with each other across departments and projects. Additionally, we have a wide range of social and professional events that provide you with the best opportunities to expand and build a network early in your career.



Send us your application

Upload your resume and a motivational letter to our recruitment system via the link. If you have unanswered questions, feel free to write to hr.dk@netcompany.com.



All qualified candidates are encouraged to apply regardless of gender, gender expression, sexuality, disability, age, religion and belief, political views, nationality, ethnicity, or social background.



We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse environment. Our recruitment processes are based on individual merit. If you require any reasonable adjustments or additional support during the interview process, please email our recruiters at reasonableadjustments@netcompany.com for assistance.

Title: REVENUE & ANALYTICS MANAGER
Place: Kastrup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
CARGO REVENUE AND ANALYTICS MANAGER

Join SAS Cargo and help shape the future of our performance.

SAS Cargo is a premium multi-market airline driving sustainable profits on our way towards a consolidated future. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations. Besides expanding our presence in main hub Copenhagen, we are transforming how we make commercial decisions by using more data, acting faster, and serve better.

If you’re passionate about analytics, commercial optimization, and creating measurable business impact, this is your opportunity.

You will be part of Commercial’s RM and Pricing team, a small and diverse team responsible for optimizing SAS Cargo’s revenue and contribution, and ensuring that SAS Cargo is offering a competitive network, relevant products and competitive rates on SAS capacity.

What You’ll Do

In-depth analysis


Analyze performance across yields, load factors, revenue drivers, and flights to identify trends and improvement opportunities
Conduct scenario modelling and forecasting together with the Finance Business Controller and Partner and Strategic Pricing Manager to support strategic decisions and reporting outlooks
Contribute to our ongoing ML/AI initiatives, enhancing our predictive capabilities and enabling smarter, data‑driven decision‑making.
Support or lead development of business cases and business follow-up across SAS Cargo
Act as an internal ambassador for data-driven decision making within SAS Cargo.


Revenue Management


Optimize revenue contribution on SAS belly capacity on your own route portfolio through optimal rates, overbooking decisions and sales mix analysis
Work cross functional with Operations managers and planners, and Sales on day-to-day route optimization
Monitor and adjust steering (rates, capacity allocation) based on market changes and input from stakeholders
Evaluate and set up allotment allocations
Respond to special rate requests (spot market)
Work with Global Sales and Strategic Pricing Manager to set rates correctly and win deals
Work with our RM architect and rest of the RM-team to continuously develop best practice and adoption of our upcoming new RM system enhancements.


What You Bring


Required: 1-2 years of experience in an analytical or technical role. Minimum BSc degree or equivalent. Previous experience in revenue management, data/business analysis, or a related field within aviation, logistics, transportation, hospitality or other network industries is big plus.
Preferred: Experience with statistical modeling, RM systems, programming, or quantitative analytics will make you stand out.


Your strengths


Strategic & Analytical Thinking: You thrive on solving complex problems, making data-driven decisions, and turning insights into strategies that drive business growth.
Clear Communication: You excel at explaining complex data insights in a way that’s both clear and compelling, connecting with colleagues across departments.
Proactive & Results-Driven: You believe in making things happen! You take the initiative, move quickly from analysis to action, and consistently deliver impactful results.
Why You Should Apply: At our company, we believe in supporting each other and championing everyone’s potential. If you're passionate about what you do and want to work in a place that values personal development, well-being, and empowerment, this is the role for you.


Our Culture At SAS Cargo And What We Offer

At SAS Cargo, you will join a collaborative and supportive team where you can take initiative, influence key commercial processes, and contribute to creating value across the organization.

We Offer


A role with real responsibility and opportunity to drive improvements in revenue and decision-making
A strong team culture where we collaborate across SAS Cargo and the wider SAS organization, recognize each other’s efforts, and share successes
A structured onboarding program with support from experienced colleagues and your manager
Workplace flexibility, including the possibility of some remote work and option to access a gym
Social activities with colleagues in and outside of work
Access to staff tickets and discounts. You can read more at https://www.sasgroup.net/career/working-at-sas/benefits-and-compensations/


Additional Information 


Interviews will not take place before 2nd half of August. Application deadline 16 august 2026. Note that we cannot handle any applications that are sent by email, due to GDPR regulations.
Desired start date: As soon as possible.
Position: Fulltime (100%) starting with a 3-month probation period
This position is based at SAS Cargo’s new HQ facility at Copenhagen airport, and you will be expected to work onsite. Some remote work may also be an option, depending on the agreement made with your manager.
To be eligible for this position you need an EU-work permit. Please note that we ask you to inform us if you currently have a valid EU-work permit.

HTML:
CARGO REVENUE AND ANALYTICS MANAGER

Join SAS Cargo and help shape the future of our performance.

SAS Cargo is a premium multi-market airline driving sustainable profits on our way towards a consolidated future. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations. Besides expanding our presence in main hub Copenhagen, we are transforming how we make commercial decisions by using more data, acting faster, and serve better.

If you’re passionate about analytics, commercial optimization, and creating measurable business impact, this is your opportunity.

You will be part of Commercial’s RM and Pricing team, a small and diverse team responsible for optimizing SAS Cargo’s revenue and contribution, and ensuring that SAS Cargo is offering a competitive network, relevant products and competitive rates on SAS capacity.

What You’ll Do

In-depth analysis

  • Analyze performance across yields, load factors, revenue drivers, and flights to identify trends and improvement opportunities
  • Conduct scenario modelling and forecasting together with the Finance Business Controller and Partner and Strategic Pricing Manager to support strategic decisions and reporting outlooks
  • Contribute to our ongoing ML/AI initiatives, enhancing our predictive capabilities and enabling smarter, data‑driven decision‑making.
  • Support or lead development of business cases and business follow-up across SAS Cargo
  • Act as an internal ambassador for data-driven decision making within SAS Cargo.

Revenue Management

  • Optimize revenue contribution on SAS belly capacity on your own route portfolio through optimal rates, overbooking decisions and sales mix analysis
  • Work cross functional with Operations managers and planners, and Sales on day-to-day route optimization
  • Monitor and adjust steering (rates, capacity allocation) based on market changes and input from stakeholders
  • Evaluate and set up allotment allocations
  • Respond to special rate requests (spot market)
  • Work with Global Sales and Strategic Pricing Manager to set rates correctly and win deals
  • Work with our RM architect and rest of the RM-team to continuously develop best practice and adoption of our upcoming new RM system enhancements.

What You Bring

  • Required: 1-2 years of experience in an analytical or technical role. Minimum BSc degree or equivalent. Previous experience in revenue management, data/business analysis, or a related field within aviation, logistics, transportation, hospitality or other network industries is big plus.
  • Preferred: Experience with statistical modeling, RM systems, programming, or quantitative analytics will make you stand out.

Your strengths

  • Strategic & Analytical Thinking: You thrive on solving complex problems, making data-driven decisions, and turning insights into strategies that drive business growth.
  • Clear Communication: You excel at explaining complex data insights in a way that’s both clear and compelling, connecting with colleagues across departments.
  • Proactive & Results-Driven: You believe in making things happen! You take the initiative, move quickly from analysis to action, and consistently deliver impactful results.
  • Why You Should Apply: At our company, we believe in supporting each other and championing everyone’s potential. If you're passionate about what you do and want to work in a place that values personal development, well-being, and empowerment, this is the role for you.

Title: Audience Insights Specialist
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you want to be a valued part of the critical infrastructure that serves communities across Europe and beyond? Are you looking for a position in which you can set direction and help shape our company? If so, then you could be the newest addition to our team as Audience Insight Specialist.

Be part of the movement – and play your part

About The Role

We serve b2c Ferry passengers globally and we are seeking an analytical thinker with strong strategic vision. You should be capable of stakeholder management, with a strong background in turning data into insight and delivering innovative value-added analysis.

It is imperative you are someone that is proactive and passionate about insights. You will need to have a technical skill set, a thorough understanding of manipulating raw data and building this into actionable insight using analytical and visualisation tools.

You Will Be Responsible For


Support the Insights strategy through the building of reports that answer key business questions and track the performance of success metrics
Support the passenger audience segmentation strategy, definitions and tactics for activation, through incorporating audience segmentation splits within reporting
Support the Audience Insights Lead in mapping the passenger customer journeys and build reports to measure efficiency at specific touchpoints in the customer journey
Ensure insights are available across all markets and routes and aligned with business needs
Support the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team in identifying business opportunities through the identification of trends within reporting
Guide your colleagues in any research needs, aligned to the trends/ opportunities identified through Audience Insights reporting
Adopt cross-functional alignment: partner with revenue, marketing, sales and routes to ensure reporting and metrics are standardised
You will be data literate and have a strong ability to identify trends alongside an inquisitive nature to understand the root cause of specific trends identified.


Your Capabilities


Strong analytical capabilities with experience in visualisation tools such as Power BI and preferably also data connectivity tools such as SQL or similar
Logical thinker with the ability to understand business needs
Business acumen: tie insights to revenue, retention, product-market fit, and prioritization/ROI
Storytelling and influencing without authority, utilising data to provide actionable insights
Presentation and communication skills – complex is easy, simple is hard
Experience with surveys and survey design
Experience with brand tracking and/or online panel/communities is a plus


About You

You will work in the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team, utilising the data available to you to build a reporting suite that is aligned to business needs, generating the insights needed to guide strategic decisions. You will work alongside colleagues aligning reporting with research projects and programmes (e.g. The Voice of The Passenger – NPS and CSAT) to provide holistic actionable insights. You will be a very analytical individual that is excited by turning data into insights and capable to utilise tools to improve data connectivity and increase automation across reports. You will be an enthusiastic, curious, passionate and collaborative individual who continually looks for opportunities, with a solution focussed attitude.

Skills And Experience We Imagine You Bring


An analytical thinker with the ability to turn data into insights
2-3 years of insight and data & analytics experience (essential)
Preferably technical with experience in Power BI or similar tool and maybe also SQL, python, and/or other relevant tools
Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise own and others workload.
Ability to communicate complex data and ideas into digestible information.
Ability to be flexible, and adapt to change and leverage trends
Strong communication and collaboration skills


We think the strongest teams are built on diversity and inclusivity. Contrasting opinions, varied skills and strong characters bring out the best in us all. And we cannot wait for you to join.

Working at DFDS

Be a valued member of a diverse team at DFDS and help drive change together.

We promote a great workplace with a diverse and vibrant community and a healthy work–life balance. The position can be located in either Newcastle or Copenhagen.

While you’re with us, we want you to develop. At DFDS, we believe that development happens first and foremost through activities in the workplace. We will also offer training courses that can support you in your role, if needed.

Are we a match? Then what are you waiting for? Apply Now.

If you believe your experience, mindset, and motivation align with this role, we would be happy to hear from you. Please submit your CV at your earliest convenience, as we review applications on an ongoing basis.

Please note that due to the summer holiday period, there might be a delay in response time, but rest assured that we will get back to you as soon as possible.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Title: BI Consultant
Description:
Group BI & Analytics

Are you curious about how data can create real business value? Do you enjoy working with people, understanding business challenges, and turning insights into solutions?

At BORG Automotive, we are looking for a BI Consultant to join our Group BI & Analytics team. Whether you are early in your career or already have some experience within BI, analytics, or reporting, this role offers the opportunity to building broad expertise within BI & Analytics and developing deeper technical and non-technical competencies.

The Role

As a BI Consultant, you will work with BI and analytics solutions, from understanding business needs and gathering requirements to supporting development, testing, and implementation. You will act as the bridge between business stakeholders and the BI & Analytics team, helping translate opportunities into scalable solutions that support better decision-making across BORG Automotive.

This combination of requirement gathering, solution development, analytics, and domain specialization gives you the opportunity to specialize within specific domains such as supply chain, production, commercial, or finance, while deepening both your technical and non-technical competencies within BI & Analytics.

Key Responsibilities


Clarify business requirements and translate them into valuable BI and analytics solution designs in collaboration with technical specialists.
Support development, testing, and rollout of dashboards, reports, and analytics solutions.
Train and support key users, ensuring relevant knowledge, guidance, and documentation are available to end users.
Take part in the team’s support responsibility through weekly rotating shifts, helping ensure stable and reliable BI services.
Promote data-driven decision-making by identifying opportunities where BI and analytics can improve processes, transparency, and business performance.
Contribute to governance, standardization, and continuous improvement of BI solutions.


Who You Are

You are naturally curious and enjoy understanding how businesses operate. You are comfortable engaging with stakeholders and asking the right questions before jumping to solutions.

You Bring The Following Qualifications And Strengths


Have a relevant degree within Business, Economics, Engineering, IT, Data Analytics, or similar.
Enjoy combining business understanding with analytical thinking
Have strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Are structured and proactive in your approach.
Are motivated by learning and continuous development.
A problem solver who is persistent in turning needs into practical solutions
Help simplify data structures and processes through curiosity and active engagement with business stakeholders.


Experience with SQL, BI tools, reporting, analytics, or data modelling is an advantage, but the most important factor is a genuine interest in helping the business create value through data.

What We Offer


A role with significant interaction across functions, countries, and business areas.
The opportunity to build deep expertise within a selected business domain.
A strong learning environment focused on capability building and knowledge sharing.
Exposure to modern BI and analytics technologies and business-critical initiatives.
Clear opportunities to develop your skills, grow your expertise, and shape your future career path within BI & Analytics.


About Group BI & Analytics

Group BI & Analytics is a team of 8 employees responsible for enabling data-driven decision-making across BORG Automotive. We work closely with the business to transform data into trusted insights, scalable data products, and improved decision-making capabilities. Our mission is to help the organization turn data into business value through strong collaboration, governance, and analytics expertise.

We will process the applications ongoing, but due to summer holidays we will take the first look at candidates medio August.

Title: Business Developer
Description:
Commercial decisions are only as good as the insights behind them. At SELECTED, we're building a new Business Development team to help our markets make even better commercial decisions.

As one of the first members of the team, you'll work at the intersection of business and data. Together with commercial stakeholders across Europe, you'll turn insights into action and help shape how commercial decisions are made.

YOUR TEAM

You will join a newly established Business Development team at SELECTED. Together, we work to strengthen commercial decision-making by connecting business understanding, data and technology. Joining us from at an early stage, you'll have the opportunity to influence how we work, build strong relationships across the organisation and make an impact from day one.

Working closely with Country Sales Managers, Sales Representatives, Merchandise Managers and Partner Performance Managers across our European markets, you'll help shape how data creates value in the business. You'll also collaborate with colleagues in our buying organisation as well as BESTSELLER Tech to develop commercial data products that support our decision-making across the value chain.

YOUR ROLE

You'll help strengthen commercial decision-making across SELECTED by combining business understanding with data. Working closely with colleagues across our sales organisation and markets, you'll identify opportunities, develop tools and analyses and help colleagues make better and faster decisions across our markets.

More specifically, you will:


Analyse commercial performance across partners and markets to identify opportunities and recommend where actions will create the greatest value
Develop tools, analyses and AI-supported solutions that help our sales organisation make better and faster commercial decisions
Drive the adoption of data-driven ways of working through stakeholder engagement, collaboration and training
Support our buying organisation by bringing commercial insights into product-related decisions and processes
Collaborate with BESTSELLER Tech to shape and continuously improve commercial data products that create value across the business.


Your Benefits

This isn't a role where insights disappear into a report. You'll work closely with the people making commercial decisions, giving you the opportunity to challenge, advise and see the impact of your work first-hand. You'll also:


Help build a new function with room to influence priorities and ways of working
Work closely with commercial stakeholders across Europe, our buying organisation and BESTSELLER Tech
Benefit from flexible working hours and the opportunity to work from home, which is a natural part of how we work at SELECTED.


YOUR PROFILE

You're curious about what drives commercial performance and motivated by making a real impact through data. You enjoy navigating between business and analytics, and you're at your best when collaborating with others to solve complex commercial challenges.

In addition, we imagine that you:


Hold a relevant Master's degree, for example within Economics and Business Administration (cand.merc.), Business Intelligence or Data Science, or a similar field
Have experience working with commercial data and business insights, ideally within fashion, wholesale, retail or a comparable industry
Understand how commercial performance, partner dynamics and product decisions influence business results
Are comfortable working with analytical tools such as SQL, Python or similar, and enjoy using modern technologies, including AI, to develop analyses and practical solutions
Communicate complex insights in a simple and actionable way to stakeholders with different backgrounds
Communicate confidently in English, both written and spoken.


WE LOOK FORWARD TO HEARING FROM YOU

If you have questions about the position, you are welcome to contact Business Development Manager Alex Brink Poulsen. Otherwise, send us your application and CV as soon as possible and no later than 4 August 2026. We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the posting when we find the right candidate.

LOCATION: Brande, Denmark

About Selected

Based in Denmark, SELECTED is an international brand for men and women, providing modern consumers a unique offering of style and craftmanship at an attractive price point. We always strive to add an edgy touch to our progressive styles and aim to offer collections that can be the base to any wardrobe, always in sync with the current tendencies. We aim for quality over quantity, which is why sustainability is a main focus for us. Every collection should be more sustainable than the last. This underlines the responsibility we take for our future environmental footprint. With SELECTED you are never out of style but have a quality that is built to last. SELECTED is part of the worldwide fashion company BESTSELLER. For more information, please go to www.selected.com  or www.bestseller.com.

Title: Data and Invoicing Specialist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Data and Invoicing Specialist til UBsend A/S i København




Er du en nysgerrig og struktureret Data and Invoicing Specialist med flair for IT og en interesse for at skabe værdi gennem data og analyser? Hos UBsend A/S søger vi en engageret Data and Invoicing Specialist til at styrke vores team i København.




Om UBsend A/S




UBsend er specialiseret i transportløsninger og hjælper både store og små kunder med at finde de bedste løsninger til deres specifikke forsendelsesbehov. Vi arbejder med en bred vifte af B2B- og B2C-kunder og er i løbende vækst. Vores ambition er klar: Vi vil være den førende integrationsplatform på markedet.




Vi er en international virksomhed med ca. 80 medarbejdere fordelt på kontorer i Aarhus og København og er den del af Heartland/Bestseller-koncernen. Hos os bliver du en del af en dynamisk og ambitiøs virksomhed med et stærkt fokus på innovation og udvikling.




Dine arbejdsopgaver




Som Data and Invoicing Specialist hos UBsend vil du få en alsidig rolle med fokus på datahåndtering, kontrol samt fakturering, der understøtter vores forretning. Dine primære opgaver vil være:




Data processering
Fakturering
Sikring af datakvalitet gennem afstemning og kontrol
Opfølgning på data med kunder og transportører.
Udarbejdelse af ad hoc-analyser og rapporter.
Bidrag til udvikling og optimering af automatiserede løsninger.




Din profil




Vi leder efter en kandidat, der kan genkende sig selv i følgende:




Du er nysgerrig og har en struktureret tilgang til dit arbejde.
Du har flair for IT og gerne kendskab til data håndtering.
Du er analytisk stærk, detaljeorienteret og god til at arbejde med data.
Du har gode engelskkundskaber både mundtligt og skriftligt.
Du trives i en dynamisk og international virksomhed, hvor tingene udvikler sig hurtigt.







Vi tilbyder




En fuldtidsstilling i København.
En spændende rolle i en virksomhed i vækst, hvor du får mulighed for at præge din egen hverdag.
Et uformelt og internationalt arbejdsmiljø med engagerede kolleger.
Indgåelse i et team som har en faglighed stolthed og sætter stor ære i deres arbejde.
Fokus på faglig og personlig udvikling.




Ansøgning




Hvis dette lyder som jobbet for dig, vil vi meget gerne høre fra dig! Ansøg via LinkedIn hurtigst muligt eller senest den 14. august 2026. Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende, så tøv ikke med at sende din ansøgning.

Vores mission er på en nem, attraktiv og let tilgængelig måde og på tværs af alle leverandører at levere innovative, fleksible og sikre løsninger indenfor transport og betaling.

Data and Invoicing Specialist til UBsend A/S i København


Er du en nysgerrig og struktureret Data and Invoicing Specialist med flair for IT og en interesse for at skabe værdi gennem data og analyser? Hos UBsend A/S søger vi en engageret Data and Invoicing Specialist til at styrke vores team i København.


Om UBsend A/S


UBsend er specialiseret i transportløsninger og hjælper både store og små kunder med at finde de bedste løsninger til deres specifikke forsendelsesbehov. Vi arbejder med en bred vifte af B2B- og B2C-kunder og er i løbende vækst. Vores ambition er klar: Vi vil være den førende integrationsplatform på markedet.


Vi er en international virksomhed med ca. 80 medarbejdere fordelt på kontorer i Aarhus og København og er den del af Heartland/Bestseller-koncernen. Hos os bliver du en del af en dynamisk og ambitiøs virksomhed med et stærkt fokus på innovation og udvikling.


Dine arbejdsopgaver


Som Data and Invoicing Specialist hos UBsend vil du få en alsidig rolle med fokus på datahåndtering, kontrol samt fakturering, der understøtter vores forretning. Dine primære opgaver vil være:


  • Data processering
  • Fakturering
  • Sikring af datakvalitet gennem afstemning og kontrol
  • Opfølgning på data med kunder og transportører.
  • Udarbejdelse af ad hoc-analyser og rapporter.
  • Bidrag til udvikling og optimering af automatiserede løsninger.


Din profil


Vi leder efter en kandidat, der kan genkende sig selv i følgende:


  • Du er nysgerrig og har en struktureret tilgang til dit arbejde.
  • Du har flair for IT og gerne kendskab til data håndtering.
  • Du er analytisk stærk, detaljeorienteret og god til at arbejde med data.
  • Du har gode engelskkundskaber både mundtligt og skriftligt.
  • Du trives i en dynamisk og international virksomhed, hvor tingene udvikler sig hurtigt.



Vi tilbyder


  • En fuldtidsstilling i København.
  • En spændende rolle i en virksomhed i vækst, hvor du får mulighed for at præge din egen hverdag.
  • Et uformelt og internationalt arbejdsmiljø med engagerede kolleger.
  • Indgåelse i et team som har en faglighed stolthed og sætter stor ære i deres arbejde.
  • Fokus på faglig og personlig udvikling.


Ansøgning


Hvis dette lyder som jobbet for dig, vil vi meget gerne høre fra dig! Ansøg via LinkedIn hurtigst muligt eller senest den 14. august 2026. Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende, så tøv ikke med at sende din ansøgning.

Title: Dataanalytiker med stærke Python-kompetencer til Team Risikoanalyser
Company: Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø
Description:
Er du drevet af at omsætte komplekse datasæt til præcise risikovurderinger? Vil du være med til at sikre den grønne omstilling gennem avancerede statistiske modeller? Så er det dig, vi leder efter.

I Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø står vi over for en omfattende opgave: At sikre et rent vandmiljø og gennemføre en historisk omlægning af landbrugsarealer til natur og skov. En central del af denne opgave er at sikre en effektiv og korrekt implementering af den grønne trepart, og derfor ønsker vi nu at øge vores analysekapacitet.

Vi søger derfor en analytisk stærk profil til enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som kan bringe avancerede programmeringsteknikker og statistisk metodik i spil for at udvikle fremtidens analyse- og kontrolmodeller.

Din rolle: Fra rådata til intelligent risikoanalyse
Som vores nye dataanalytiker bliver du som noget af det første ansvarlig for at bidrage til løsningen af en specifik tværgående analyseopgave. Gennem vores onboarding-program bliver du introduceret til dine primære ansvarsområder, som vil omfatte:

Avanceret databehandling og automatisering: Du vil bruge Python til at automatisere komplekse arbejdsgange, håndtere store datamængder og transformere rådata til brugbare analyseresultater.
Statistisk modellering og analyse: Du vil udføre dybdegående analyser, der understøtter både den daglige kontrol og den overordnede politikudvikling i styrelsen.
Dokumentation og formidling: Du omsætter komplekse tekniske resultater til letforståelig dokumentation og informationsmateriale til både interne interessenter og eksterne partnere, herunder EU-kommissionen.


Din profil: Analytisk dybde og teknisk ekspertise
Vi forestiller os, at du har en relevant kandidatuddannelse inden for f.eks. statistik, data science, økonomi eller lignende kvantitative discipliner. Derudover lægger vi vægt på, at du har:

Stærke Python-kompetencer: Du er fortrolig med at bruge Python til datamanipulation, statistisk modellering og automatisering af analyseprocesser.
Metodisk fundering: Du har en dyb forståelse for statistisk analyse og erfaring med udvikling af prædiktive modeller.
Data-drevet mindset: Du er vant til at arbejde systematisk og struktureret med store, komplekse datasæt og kan navigere i både ustrukturerede og strukturerede data.
Programmeringskompetencer: Udover Python har du erfaring med SQL og herudover gerne kendskab til SAS Enterprise Guide.
Kommunikativ styrke: Du kan formidle tekniske konklusioner klart og præcist, både skriftligt og mundtligt, til kolleger med forskellige faglige baggrunde.


Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med projektledelse og kendskab til forvaltning af EU-regler på miljø- og landbrugsområdet.

Vi tilbyder
Du bliver en del af Team Risikoanalyser i enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som består af ca. 10 medarbejdere med forskellig baggrund. Vi arbejder i et miljø, hvor vidensdeling og gensidig sparring er i højsædet, og hvor du indgår i et tæt samarbejde med andre enheder i styrelsen. Som en del af Team Risikoanalyser vil du erfare, at du er med i opgaveløsningen fra start til slut, og det betyder, at du bidrager til hele processen og ikke kun en isoleret delmængde.

Vi tilbyder en fast stilling med stor faglig indflydelse, hvor dine analyser direkte bidrager til den grønne omstilling af Danmark.

Ansættelsesvilkår
Du vil blive ansat som fuldmægtig efter gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten.

Ansættelsesområdet er Ministeriet for Natur og Dyrevelfærd med tilhørende institutioner, og tjenestestedet er Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø, i Nyropsgade 30. 1780 København V.

Stillingen ønskes besat snarest muligt.

Kontakt
Vil du vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte kontorchef Henrik Berg på telefon 25 16 82 14.

Du kan læse mere om styrelsen på www.sgav.dk

Ansøgning
Søg stillingen ved at sende en ansøgning inkl. CV, eksamensbevis og evt. referencer online i vores rekrutteringssystem (tryk på ”Søg stilling”) senest mandag den 27. juli 2026.

Vi forventer at afholde ansættelsessamtaler i uge 32.

Skills
Risk Management
Data Analytics
Python (Programming Language)
Statistical Data Analysis
Programming
SQL
Administration
EU Law
Communication
Project Management

Title: Systems Developer, Business Intelligence, Lystrup
Description:
Would you like to play a key role in turning data into decisions that matter — building the dashboards and visual reporting layers that drive performance across a global defense and aerospace company? We are looking for a skilled and business-oriented BI developer to drive front-end BI delivery, serving stakeholders across the full value chain. In this hands-on position your output directly shapes how leaders and teams across Terma understand and act on business performance.




We are facing an exciting future with considerable growth and demanding changes, as we deliver advanced technologies and mission critical solutions for the defense and aerospace industry. Our systems provide security for people on land, at sea and in the air. Therefore, we are looking for a System Developer within Business Intelligence to become part of this journey and contribute to our overall purpose – securing people through advanced technology.




Your role in our team




As our new Systems Developer, you will design, build, and maintain Power BI solutions that translate complex business, operational, and financial data into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders across our functions and business areas.




You will be the bridge between the business and the data, collaborating closely with stakeholders across the value chain – including sales, business development, project delivery, R&D, executive leadership and finance to evolve KPI frameworks, performance metrics, and business narratives into visual formats. Understanding what decisions need to be made and ensuring the right information is available at the right time. Your solutions will include everything from project performance reviews and pipeline reporting to financial performance management and executive dashboards.




You will join Financial Systems & BI, part of our Performance & Financial Insights function — a team dedicated to bringing structure, insight, and analytical depth to performance management across Terma. You and the BI team will collaborate closely with our IT data infrastructure team on the underlying data models and structures, ensuring the data feeding your reports is fit for purpose.




How you will make a meaningful contribution




Your key responsibilities include:




Designing and developing Power BI dashboards and reports that meet the analytical and reporting needs of stakeholders across sales, supply chain, R&D, and finance
Translating business requirements into robust, well-structured BI solutions — working with stakeholders across the value chain from initial scoping and design through to deployment and user adoption
Developing DAX measures and calculation logic in close collaboration with IT, turning underlying data into clear, business-ready insight in Power BI
Maintaining and continuously improving our front-end BI reporting — covering report quality, consistency, visual standards, and performance optimization
Acting as the go-to BI expert within Financial Systems & BI — advising stakeholders on what is possible, guiding self-service capabilities, and enabling broader data literacy across the organisation
Contributing to BI standards and best practices within Financial Systems & BI, ensuring consistency and scalability across all reporting output




Bring your world class expertise into play




You are fluent in English both in writing and verbal at a professional level. Danish is an advantage but not a requirement. Moreover, we are also looking for a new colleague with the following qualifications:




A degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Business Intelligence, or a similar field of study
Preferably between 3–6 years of hands-on experience developing Power BI solutions in a professional, business-facing context
Strong proficiency in DAX and SQL, with a structured approach to building robust measures and calculation logic in collaboration with the teams who maintain the underlying data models
Experience working as an internal BI resource serving multiple functions or business areas, with the ability to balance competing priorities and manage stakeholder expectations
Familiarity with data infrastructure concepts (data warehouses, semantic models, pipelines, star schema) and comfortable working closely with IT on data structure and sourcing
A structured, quality-conscious approach to development — you document your work, build for maintainability, and care about the end-user experience




As a person, you are curious, positive, precise, and genuinely interested in what the data is trying to say. You are comfortable moving between the technical and the human side of BI work — equally at home refining a DAX expression and discussing with a business partner what they actually need to see on a page. You take ownership of your deliverables and follow through from design to adoption. Being part of a fast-paced environment, you are able to manage multiple tasks simultaneously and find the right balance between quality while at the same time delivering effective results and meeting deadlines as well as targets.




We are an international company representing world-class expertise, delivering complex and high-tech products. We value our colleagues and our meaningful contributions to securing people through advanced technology. You will also experience that we have a strong focus on your development, teaching you useful new competences and further strengthening current ones, both professionally and personally. Our company culture is autonomous and engaging, and we acknowledge the importance of flexibility in a great working environment.

When joining Terma, we promise that you will experience: Great people, challenging tasks, world-class expertise, and meaningful contribution in your daily work.




__________________________________________________________________________________




Benefits at Terma




At Terma, we prioritize the well-being of our employees, ensuring they are well taken care of. Joining our team comes with a multitude of perks designed to enhance your social life, cultural experiences, and health.

International Environment: Explore great teamwork and professional sparring with colleagues around the world

Flexible Working Hours: Enjoy the opportunity to work flexibly and the option to work from home up to two days per week. Internet connection at home is paid for by Terma

Comprehensive Benefits Package: Receive a 10% paid pension along with a beneficial health care and insurance package. Enjoy discounts from various business partners

Well-being Perks: Opportunity to enroll in our on-site massage-offer at our locations in Denmark

Social and Professional Events: We have an active staff association that arranges a variety of social activities and events to bring all of our colleagues together

Competence networking across professional experts in Terma: Professional presentations and knowledge sharing across the organization




__________________________________________________________________________________




We are dedicated to fostering a diverse, equitable, inclusive, and welcoming workplace. Our commitment to DEIB (Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Belonging) means we actively value and celebrate unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We strive to create an environment where all employees have equal opportunities to succeed, feel respected and valued, and experience a true sense of belonging.

To support an unbiased evaluation process, we kindly encourage applicants to omit personal details such as photos and age from their applications.




If you would like to take on the challenge of working in an innovative company, we look forward to receiving your application. A resume is required, while a cover letter is optional. To ensure that your application will reach us and is properly processed, we only accept applications via www.terma.com. Applications will be processed continuously.




__________________________________________________________________________________




Do you have any questions regarding the position?

Feel free to contact Hiring Manager on e-mail: KJEN@terma.com




Bear in mind, that we need you to confirm that you can obtain a security clearance by the Danish Defense Intelligence Service, and that all adults in your household are not posing any risk to the related risk-evaluation. This entails among other issues:

That you have as a minimum stayed within NATO/EU/MISWG countries for the last 7 years only interrupted by short stays abroad, and
That you have not committed serious criminal offences




Terma group has headquarters in Denmark and is a high-tech company developing products and systems for defense and non-defense security applications, including command and control systems, radar systems, self-protection systems for aircraft, space technology, and aerostructures for the aircraft industry. TERMA | Allies in innovation
