Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you a Data Engineer with 2-7 years of experience ready to take the next step? And would you like to work directly with clients while keeping up to date with the newest technologies? Then read on & apply to become part of a dynamic young team of 60 data experts who help companies gain new insights using a wide palette of modern tools.


Contract: Full-time, permanent
Location: Central Copenhagen, near Vesterport Station



Work Assignments

The role requires you to be both a hands-on builder and a point of contact for our clients' data challenges. Specifically, you will:


Build robust data pipelines and models that streamline the ingestion, transformation, and delivery of data.
Implement and develop modern data platforms through DevOps & CI/CD practices.
Collaborate with our clients’ engineers, analysts, & business users to solve business issues and ensure lasting value.



You will learn from our many data engineers with 7-20 years of experience, while taking on a mentoring role for our less experienced consultants.

What You Bring To The Team

Ideally, you have:


2-7 years of experience working as a data engineer.
Proficiency in Python, SQL, APIs, and/or software development.
An analytical approach to problem-solving, with a strong interest in how to drive business improvement using data.
Clear communication skills and the curiosity to engage with clients on projects.
Experience with some of these technologies: Snowflake, Databricks, Fabric, dbt, Azure, AWS, or GCP.
A relevant degree.
A current base near Copenhagen and fluency in Danish and/or English.



If you're excited about this role but you don’t tick all the boxes, we still encourage you to apply.

Why Devoteam Data Driven?

You will join a fantastic team of talented, curious data experts in a collaborative and dynamic workplace. Beyond working with nice colleagues on impactful projects, we offer comprehensive benefits to support your growth:


Continuous Growth & Community: We invest in your talent and dedicate time for learning to ensure you have up-to-date certifications in our technologies.
Work-Life Balance: We prioritize your personal time. In addition to your six weeks of paid vacation, we offer the flexibility of taking unlimited unpaid time off.
Social Events: We offer a variety of social events throughout the year. Recently we have done Laser tag, Pottery, Disc Golf, Book Club, Curling, Board Game Night, Foosball Tournament, Ice Hockey, among many other things.



Our Impact

Below are a few examples of our work:


Migrated Novo Nordisk's global supply chain analytics solution from Alteryx-based workflows to a governed, cloud-native architecture using dbt, Snowflake, and Azure DevOps to support enterprise-scale analytics and collaboration.
Developed a comprehensive end-to-end data warehouse solution, featuring automated pipelines that structured dimensional data from diverse operational systems for unified analytics for a client.
Assessed the current data landscape in Novonesis and implemented a new self-service framework to enable business users to utilise the power of the modern data stack.



Application Process

Apply by uploading your CV no later than August 9th.

We expect to invite selected candidates for interviews between mid-August and mid-September 2026. Note that our recruitment team is off 18/7-9/8.

We encourage all qualified candidates to apply, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or disability.

About Devoteam

Devoteam is a leading digital consultancy with a strong international presence, represented in 25 countries (primarily in Europe) with over 11,000 employees globally. In Denmark, our team comprises around 350 people and is spread across three business units - Data Driven (our unit), a ServiceNow unit, and a Management Consulting unit - each focused on different aspects of digital transformation.

Title: Intelligence Analyst
Description:
The R&D team at Chainalysis is inspired by solving the hardest technical challenges and building products that establish trust in cryptocurrencies. We're a global organization that thrives on challenging work and doing it alongside exceptionally talented teammates. Our industry evolves rapidly, and our mission is to build a flexible, AI-driven platform that automates entity resolution, optimizes data labeling pipelines, and creates predictive models that identify illicit patterns before they escalate.

Our data and solutions have been used to solve some of the world's most high-profile criminal cases and grow consumer access to cryptocurrency safely. Now, by pairing the industry's most trusted blockchain data with agents that reason, investigate, and act, we help our customers scale their workflows as the cryptocurrency economy becomes increasingly mainstream.

Chainalysis data has helped solve some of the world’s highest-profile criminal cases and made it safer for people everywhere to access crypto. The Global Intelligence Team produces the attribution at the heart of that data — connecting on-chain activity to the real-world entities behind it, across the global crypto ecosystem. It’s the intelligence data moat beneath every Chainalysis product, and what investigators and law enforcement rely on to follow the money and disrupt crime.

As an Intelligence Analyst, you’ll help build it across the crypto-services ecosystem — end to end: you collect the data, analyze it, and produce the attribution. And you won’t work one entity at a time; you’ll build and tune the AI and automation that turn your analysis into attribution at scale.

Who You’ll Work With

You’ll join a fast-moving, globally distributed team of analysts collaborating across time zones — async and in real time. We move fast, hold each other to a high bar, and back each other up across regions.

In This Role, You’ll


Investigate and attribute activity across the crypto-services ecosystem, connecting on-chain activity to real-world entities.
Proactively surface what’s worth investigating — and the automation worth building.
Build and tune AI workflows that scale your analysis — and handle the edge cases automation can’t.
Drive the data collection and research that keep our attribution comprehensive and fresh.
Hold a high bar for accuracy, analytic rigor, and documentation.
Become a certified cryptocurrency professional across all Chainalysis certifications.


We’re Looking For Candidates Who Have


Elite potential — high aptitude, sharp judgment, and a strong work ethic. This is a demanding role, and we hire only people who can clear a high bar and keep raising it.
2+ years of analytical or investigative experience (intelligence, law enforcement, cyber, fraud, compliance, or research).
Strong research instincts and analytic rigor.
Initiative — you spot what needs investigating and go after it without waiting to be told.
Adaptability — comfortable with ambiguity and energized by a fast-moving space; the threats, the tech, and our tooling are always evolving.
Real enthusiasm for AI — you want to build with it, not just use it.
Comfort working with data, and curiosity about crypto and blockchain.


AI at Chainalysis

AI is not a feature at Chainalysis - it is a new way of working. One that turns instructions into work done, and helps us move faster than the threats we're built to counter, and we expect our employees to take ownership of the output and ensure quality. As the world's most trusted blockchain analytics platform, Chainalysis sits at a rare intersection of proprietary data, regulatory relationships and crypto expertise that makes it uniquely placed to shape and lead the next era of AI-driven intelligence - and we expect everyone here, regardless of role, to be an active part of it.

AI fluency is tied directly to how we measure performance and how we plan to win. There is no substitute for your own curiosity. We provide the tools, workflows, and space to experiment - but the expectation is that you develop these capabilities yourself, bring ideas, and collaborate across teams to reinvent the way work gets done. We are not using AI to do less. We are using it to do what was never possible before.

About Chainalysis

Chainalysis is the blockchain data platform, making it easy to connect the movement of digital assets to real-world services. Powered by deep blockchain data and AI, organizations can investigate illicit activity, manage risk exposure, and develop innovative market solutions built on the industry's most trusted blockchain intelligence. Our mission is to build trust in blockchains, blending safety and security with an unwavering commitment to growth and innovation.

You belong here.

At Chainalysis, we believe that diversity of experience and thought makes us stronger. With both customers and employees around the world, we are committed to ensuring our team reflects the unique communities around us. We’re ensuring we keep learning by committing to continually revisit and reevaluate our diversity culture.

We encourage applicants across any race, ethnicity, gender/gender expression, age, spirituality, ability, experience and more. If you need any accommodations to make our interview process more accessible to you due to a disability, don't hesitate to let us know. You can learn more here. We can’t wait to meet you.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Fredericia, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you want to help build scalable and high-quality data solutions? Join us as a Data Engineer and contribute to the development of data products that support business decisions across DLG.

About The Position

As a Data Engineer, y ou will contribute to the development and maintenance of our data platform and data pipelines. Working closely with experienced engineers and stakeholders, you will help deliver reliable, scalable, and well-structured data solutions that support business needs.

You will be part of an environment where collaboration, continuous learning, and quality are highly valued. While working with an established architecture and engineering framework, you will have the opportunity to develop your technical skills and grow your expertise in data engineering and cloud-based data platforms.

In this role you will

As our Data Engineer, you will work with the design, development, and maintenance of data pipelines and data products on Databricks. You will contribute to building solutions that are reliable, maintainable, and aligned with industry-leading engineering standards.

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include


Develop and maintain data pipelines using Python, PySpark, and/or SQL
Support data ingestion processes across batch and streaming workloads
Implement data quality checks and validation processes within existing frameworks
Contribute to CI/CD processes and follow established data engineering development practices
Work with data governance principles, including data classification, access control, and compliance requirements
Collaborate with engineers and stakeholders to deliver high-quality data solutions and resolve technical challenges


About you

You are a data engineer with a focus on building reliable and well-structured data pipelines. You are comfortable working within an established architecture and contributing to the delivery of high-quality data solutions as part of a collaborative engineering team.

You have a curious mindset and take pride in delivering maintainable solutions. You communicate proactively, collaborate effectively with others, and are comfortable raising questions or challenges when needed.

We Imagine That You


Are proficient in Python and SQL
Have experience working with Lakehouse or medallion architecture principles
Have experience with data ingestion patterns, including batch and/or streaming workloads
Are comfortable using Git or similar version control tools
Have a solid understanding of CI/CD principles and data development practices
Have hands-on experience with Databricks and understand data quality and governance concepts
Communicate fluently in English, both written and spoken


Furthermore, It Is An Advantage If You


Have experience with Databricks Asset Bundles / Declarative Automation Bundles (DABs) or similar deployment and configuration tooling
Have worked with streaming technologies such as Auto Loader, Kafka, Event Hubs, or similar solutions
Have experience working with Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud Platform (GCP)


What we offer

At DLG, you will join a collaborative and ambitious organisation where data plays an increasingly important role in supporting business decisions and creating value across the business.

We offer a flexible and supportive work environment with excellent opportunities for professional development, knowledge sharing, and career growth. You will work closely with experienced colleagues and become part of a culture characterized by collaboration, trust, and continuous improvement.

Practical information

The position is full-time and based in either Fredericia or Flensburg. Start date is expected to be in October/November.

Please submit your CV - applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis. We expect to invite for the first round of interviews in the beginning of August.

If you have any questions that are not answered in the above, please contact Head of Data Engineering, Thomas Nielsen, at

What we offer

At DLG, you will join a collaborative and ambitious organisation where data plays an increasingly important role in supporting business decisions and creating value across the business.

We offer a flexible and supportive work environment with excellent opportunities for professional development, knowledge sharing, and career growth. You will work closely with experienced colleagues and become part of a culture characterized by collaboration, trust, and continuous improvement.

Practical information

The position is full-time and based in either Fredericia or Flensburg. Start date is expected to be in October/November.

Please submit your CV - applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis. We expect to invite for the first round of interviews in the beginning of August.

If you have any questions that are not answered in the above, please contact Head of Data Engineering, Thomas Nielsen, at

Title: Business Consultant – Data & Process
HTML:
(Senior) Business Consultant – Data & Process

med fokus på forretningsdata, processer og driftsnære kundeleverancer


Trives du i krydsfeltet mellem data, processer og forretning – og motiveres du af at skabe løsninger, der fungerer både på tegnebrættet og i den daglige drift?


Hos COMPA Mobility søger vi en erfaren profil, der kan forstå kundedata, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og omsætte komplekse input til praktiske løsninger, der fungerer i den daglige drift.


Rollen passer til dig, der ikke kun ser data som tal, filer eller systemfelter – men som vigtig forretningsinformation, der skal bruges korrekt for at sikre kvalitet, compliance og gode kundeoplevelser.


Det vigtige er, at du har stærk forretningsforståelse, arbejder struktureret og kan stå sikkert i dialogen med både kunder, kollegaer og samarbejdspartnere.


Vi forventer derudover, at du er stærk i Excel og har erfaring med blandt andet pivottabeller, avancerede formler, datamodeller, Power Query og analyse af større datamængder. Erfaring med VBA, SQL, BI-værktøjer eller automatisering er en stor fordel.

 


Hvad går jobbet ud på?

COMPA Mobility er i vækst, og vi søger en Business Consultant eller en Senior Business Consultant, der kan være med til at styrke vores arbejde med data, processer og driftsnære kundeleverancer inden for Global Mobility.


Du får en central rolle i at skabe overblik og struktur i komplekse opgaver, hvor data, mennesker, processer og myndighedskrav mødes. Ingen kunder er ens, og det kræver en profil, der kan tilpasse sig, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og få tingene til at fungere i praksis.


Rollen ligger i krydsfeltet mellem dataforståelse, drift, procesdesign og kundedialog. Du vil arbejde tæt sammen med vores specialister inden for skat, immigration, social sikring og international payroll.


Du får blandt andet ansvar for at:

–    forstå, strukturere, validere og kvalitetssikre kundedata fra forskellige kilder;

–    identificere, hvilke data der er nødvendige for en korrekt og effektiv leverance;

–    skabe overblik, når data er uensartede, ufuldstændige eller kommer fra flere systemer og interessenter;

–    sikre gode dataflows mellem HR, løn, økonomi, medarbejdere, udenlandske payrolls og eksterne rådgivere;

–    omsætte komplekse kundebehov til praktiske processer og arbejdsgange;

–    arbejde med processer inden for blandt andet international payroll, shadow payroll, skattepligtig løn og rapportering;

–    være i direkte dialog med kunder og sikre fremdrift, afklaring og kvalitet;

–    samarbejde med interne specialister og bidrage til løsninger, der er effektive, skalerbare og fungerer i praksis.


Hvem er du?

Vi leder ikke efter én bestemt uddannelse eller én bestemt tidligere titel. Vi leder efter en erfaren profil, der kan skabe struktur, stille de rigtige spørgsmål og tage ansvar for komplekse leverancer.


Du har sandsynligvis arbejdet med data, processer, økonomi, payroll, HR, systemer, implementering, controlling, projektledelse eller rådgivning – og du ved, hvad det vil sige at få tingene til at fungere i praksis.


Du behøver ikke komme fra Global Mobility-branchen. Det vigtigste er, at du har solid erfaring, arbejder struktureret og kan repræsentere COMPA professionelt over for kunder.


Din baggrund kan for eksempel være inden for:

–    finance, controlling eller økonomi;

–    payroll, HR operations eller HR-transformation;

–    projektledelse, procesoptimering eller implementering;

–    rådgivning, revision eller konsulentarbejde;

–    ERP, CRM, BI, systemer eller digital transformation;

–    data-, proces- eller forretningsanalyse.


Vi lægger især vægt på, at du:

–    har solid erfaring og professionel ballast;

–    arbejder struktureret og følger opgaver til dørs;

–    forstår data i en forretningsmæssig sammenhæng;

–    kan se, hvad data skal bruges til – ikke kun hvordan de kan behandles;

–    kan stille de rigtige spørgsmål til både kunder og kollegaer;

–    forstår samspillet mellem HR, løn, økonomi, systemer og forretning;

–    trives med kundedialog og kan skabe tillid i relationen;

–    kan navigere i detaljer uden at miste det store overblik;

–    har blik for kvalitet, effektivitet og økonomi;

–    taler og skriver flydende dansk og engelsk.


Det er en fordel, hvis du også har erfaring med:

–    payroll, shadow payroll, HR-data, finance-data eller rapportering;

–    Excel, BI, ERP, CRM, automatisering eller systemimplementering;

–    konsulentarbejde, projektledelse eller komplekse kundeprojekter;

–    internationale processer eller organisationer;

–    miljøer med mange interessenter og komplekse dataflows.


Hvad betyder data i denne rolle?

Data er en vigtig del af rollen, men ikke som en isoleret teknisk disciplin. Hos os handler data om at forstå kundens virkelighed – at vide, hvilke data der er nødvendige, hvad de bruges til, og hvordan de hænger sammen med processer, compliance og forretningsbehov.


Data kan for eksempel være løn- og HR-data, finance-data, kontraktoplysninger, bonusser, benefits, rejse- og arbejdsdage, opholdsdage, cost allocations og skatterelevante oplysninger – ofte i mange formater og fra mange systemer.


Shadow payroll betyder i denne sammenhæng processer, hvor løn- og skattedata opgøres og rapporteres i et arbejdsland, selvom lønnen udbetales fra et andet land.


Du må gerne være stærk teknisk, men det afgørende er, at du kan forstå sammenhængen mellem data, processer, compliance og kundens forretning – og omsætte det til løsninger, der virker i praksis.


Hvem er COMPA Mobility?

COMPA Mobility er en specialiseret Global Mobility-konsulentvirksomhed. Vi hjælper virksomheder med medarbejdere, der arbejder på tværs af landegrænser – til og fra Danmark og mellem andre lande.


Vi er et specialiseret team med høj faglighed, tæt samarbejde og en praktisk tilgang til opgaverne. Vi løser ofte opgaverne helt ud i driften på vegne af vores kunder.


Vi rådgiver og leverer ydelser inden for blandt andet immigration, arbejdstilladelser, registreringer, myndighedskrav, skat, social sikring, posted workers, destination services, international payroll og dataflows mellem HR, løn og økonomi.


Vi tilbyder

Vi tilbyder en erfaren rolle med stor mulighed for at præge, hvordan COMPA arbejder med data, processer og skalerbare kundeleverancer.


Du bliver en vigtig del af et område i vækst og får mulighed for at arbejde tæt sammen med dygtige specialister inden for Global Mobility.


Hos os får du:

–    en central rolle med indflydelse på processer, dataflows og kundeleverancer;

–    mulighed for at være med til at udvikle et vigtigt område i COMPA;

–    komplekse og varierende kundeopgaver;

–    tæt samarbejde med specialister inden for skat, immigration, social sikring og payroll;

–    et uformelt, ambitiøst og fagligt stærkt miljø;

–    mulighed for personlig og faglig udvikling i et specialiseret nicheområde;

–    fleksibilitet i hverdagen, herunder mulighed for hjemmearbejdsdage;

–    arbejdssted i enten Brædstrup eller Aarhus, med udgangspunkt i minimum tre dage om ugen på kontoret.


Interesseret?

Søg via LinkedIn eller send dit CV og gerne et par linjer om din motivation til lof@compamobility.com


Vi behandler ansøgninger løbende og ansætter, når vi har fundet den rette kandidat.


Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Louise Fangel, Director of Projects, Processes & Data: lof@compamobility.com

Title: Procurement Analyst - Procurement Intelligence & Finance Partnering
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Procurement Analyst – Procurement Intelligence & Finance Partnering

Are you someone who can move from a raw dataset to a decision-ready recommendation – and enjoys doing both? At Lundbeck, we’re looking for an analytically strong professional to join our Procurement Intelligence & Finance Partnering team at our global headquarters in Copenhagen. You could be the person who turns data, market intelligence and AI into better sourcing decisions.

Your new role

You will support fact-based decision-making across our procurement categories by combining data analysis, financial understanding and modern tooling – including AI – and translating the results into actions our category managers and stakeholders can execute on.

The role is deliberately broad. Depending on the priorities of the moment, you might be modelling the financial impact of a negotiation strategy one week and building an AI-assisted workflow that removes manual reporting work the next. What stays constant is the expectation: rigorous analysis, clear communication, and insights that lead to action.

It is your chance to:


Analyze spend, savings, contract and market data across categories, and translate findings into concrete, prioritized recommendations.
Build and maintain reporting and dashboards that track savings delivery, category performance and value creation – for both operational and executive audiences.
Help drive the practical adoption of AI in our procurement work: identifying use cases, building or configuring solutions, and helping the team work smarter.
Support the financial side of procurement, including savings validation, budget follow-up and variance analysis in collaboration with Finance.
Partner with category managers to prepare negotiations, business cases and sourcing decisions with solid analytical foundations.
Prepare high-quality, executive-ready materials that shape decisions beyond procurement.



Your future team

Corporate Procurement is a global team of approximately 50 colleagues across Copenhagen, Chicago, and Krakow. You will join the Procurement Business Intelligence & Finance Partnering team, a team of five that spans data analytics, project management and sustainability.

The team is characterized by high standards, strong ownership and a fact-based approach to decision-making. Responsibility is given early, and development is driven through real business challenges and close collaboration.

The position is based in Copenhagen, Denmark.

What You Bring To The Team

You combine strong analytical capabilities, and a good understanding of Procurement. You have the ability to combine analytical horsepower with commercial and financial understanding. We believe the following will set a candidate apart:


A Master’s degree in business, economics, finance, engineering, data science or a related field.
2–5 years of experience from management consulting, procurement, finance or a similar analytical role
Strong data analysis skills: advanced Excel and PowerPoint are a given; experience with Power BI, or other data visualization tools.
A working understanding of procurement and corporate finance fundamentals: savings methodology, total cost of ownership, budget and P&L impact.
Structured problem-solving and the ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable recommendations for senior audiences.
Strong communication skills – you can defend your numbers and land your message with stakeholders at all levels.



In addition, you thrive in a fast-paced but collaborative environment with high expectations and exposure to senior management.

Our promise to you

Lundbeck offers an inspiring workplace and innovative culture, where our curiosity, accountability and adaptability enable us to transform lives. We want to go faster and further on addressing the big unmet needs of people living with brain disorders. We offer rewarding careers with a mix of exciting tasks and development opportunities that are balanced with initiatives focused on your well-being.

We need every brain in the game, and at Lundbeck, we are committed to building a workforce that is as diverse as the people we serve. Read more about our commitment at www.lundbeck.com/global/about-us/our-commitment/diversity-and-inclusion.

Annual base salary range: 582,000 - 799,700 DKK gross.

At Lundbeck, we are committed to fair and transparent pay. Please note that the final base salary within the stated range will be based on relevant qualifications, skills, competencies, and level of proficiency as well as internal pay equity.

Apply now

Can you see yourself in this role? We want to hear from you. Does this sound like your dream job, but you’re not sure if you meet all the requirements? We still want to hear from you!

Upload your CV and include a few lines about your motivation for applying. We ask you not to include a photo or personal information that does not relate to your professional experience.

If you have questions, please contact Christian Pedersen, CNE@Lundbeck.com.

Applications must be received by 27 July 2026.

Learn more about us at www.lundbeck.com, LinkedIn or Instagram (@h_lundbeck). #EveryBrainInTheGame

This job ad is intended for individuals seeking a career opportunity with Lundbeck. We engage with recruitment and search firms where needed on the basis of a written agreement, and we do not accept unsolicited requests of any kind. If you work as a recruitment consultant, you are kindly instructed to refrain from contacting Lundbeck or the hiring manager directly with suggested candidates. If you violate this policy, you do so at your own risk and for your own account, and Lundbeck will not assume any liability nor pay any associated fees resulting from such violation.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Sønderborg, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Would you like to work for a company with solutions that contributes to Saab’s mission of keeping people and society safe?

Would you like to be a part of shaping the Future of Digital Engineering and building the Future of Engineering?

Engineering is evolving rapidly. Model Based Systems Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, connected engineering data, and digital collaboration are transforming how complex systems are designed and delivered.

We are looking for a Data Engineer to help build the data foundation that enables modern engineering. You will connect engineering data, improve digital workflows, and prepare the organization for AI enabled engineering.

Your role

You will:


Develop data pipelines and system integrations across engineering tools
Improve data quality, consistency, governance, and traceability
Support Digital Thread and MBSE initiatives
Prepare engineering data for AI and advanced analytics
Design scalable data models and dashboards
Identify opportunities to automate engineering processes
Collaborate with engineers, IT, and business stakeholders to deliver practical solutions



Your Profile

You have experience in one or more of the following areas:


Data Engineering
Software Engineering
Systems Engineering
Data Science
Computer Science
Information Technology



Experience with Python, SQL, APIs, cloud platforms, Power BI, graph databases, engineering lifecycle tools, or AI technologies is an advantage but not a requirement.

More important than your exact background is your ability to solve complex problems, connect information, and turn data into business value.

We Are Looking For Someone Who


Thinks analytically and works in a structured way
Enjoys collaborating across technical disciplines
Is curious about new technologies and continuous improvement
Can translate engineering challenges into digital solutions
Takes ownership and delivers pragmatic results



Why Join Us

This role offers the opportunity to work at the intersection of engineering, data, and emerging technologies.

Your work will help accelerate Digital Engineering, strengthen MBSE adoption, improve engineering efficiency, and establish the foundation for future AI capabilities.

If you want to build solutions that will influence how engineering is performed in the years ahead, we would like to hear from you.

What can you expect from us?

Saab Danmark is a company that is growing and you will therefore experience a company that is dynamic and able to adapt. In the Saab group we have a saying” Business is done between people”, and this is also the environment you can expect here. We offer:


Initial learning process with mentorship and support
Good career and development opportunities in a professional company
A vibrant and international atmosphere with colleagues from all over the world
Benefits that support our employees’ wellbeing
A variety of social activities, lunch scheme, pension as well as flexible work conditions
Focus on our teams, good cooperation and giving our employees the ability to take ownership of their tasks.



Candidates must be able to obtain a security clearance by the Danish Defence Intelligence Service. This entails that candidates, as a minimum, have had residency within NATO/EU for the last 7 years.

About Saab Danmark A/S

Saab in Denmark is part of the Saab Group with headquarters in Stockholm, Sweden. At the same time, Saab Danmark is an independent legal company with a flat organisational structure. Our main business area is delivering integrated communication solutions to military markets in Denmark and abroad.

You can find further information about our integrated communication solution TactiCall, at www.saab.com/naval.

Title: BI Consultant
Description:
Group BI & Analytics

Are you curious about how data can create real business value? Do you enjoy working with people, understanding business challenges, and turning insights into solutions?

At BORG Automotive, we are looking for a BI Consultant to join our Group BI & Analytics team. Whether you are early in your career or already have some experience within BI, analytics, or reporting, this role offers the opportunity to building broad expertise within BI & Analytics and developing deeper technical and non-technical competencies.

The Role

As a BI Consultant, you will work with BI and analytics solutions, from understanding business needs and gathering requirements to supporting development, testing, and implementation. You will act as the bridge between business stakeholders and the BI & Analytics team, helping translate opportunities into scalable solutions that support better decision-making across BORG Automotive.

This combination of requirement gathering, solution development, analytics, and domain specialization gives you the opportunity to specialize within specific domains such as supply chain, production, commercial, or finance, while deepening both your technical and non-technical competencies within BI & Analytics.

Key Responsibilities


Clarify business requirements and translate them into valuable BI and analytics solution designs in collaboration with technical specialists.
Support development, testing, and rollout of dashboards, reports, and analytics solutions.
Train and support key users, ensuring relevant knowledge, guidance, and documentation are available to end users.
Take part in the team’s support responsibility through weekly rotating shifts, helping ensure stable and reliable BI services.
Promote data-driven decision-making by identifying opportunities where BI and analytics can improve processes, transparency, and business performance.
Contribute to governance, standardization, and continuous improvement of BI solutions.


Who You Are

You are naturally curious and enjoy understanding how businesses operate. You are comfortable engaging with stakeholders and asking the right questions before jumping to solutions.

You Bring The Following Qualifications And Strengths


Have a relevant degree within Business, Economics, Engineering, IT, Data Analytics, or similar.
Enjoy combining business understanding with analytical thinking
Have strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Are structured and proactive in your approach.
Are motivated by learning and continuous development.
A problem solver who is persistent in turning needs into practical solutions
Help simplify data structures and processes through curiosity and active engagement with business stakeholders.


Experience with SQL, BI tools, reporting, analytics, or data modelling is an advantage, but the most important factor is a genuine interest in helping the business create value through data.

What We Offer


A role with significant interaction across functions, countries, and business areas.
The opportunity to build deep expertise within a selected business domain.
A strong learning environment focused on capability building and knowledge sharing.
Exposure to modern BI and analytics technologies and business-critical initiatives.
Clear opportunities to develop your skills, grow your expertise, and shape your future career path within BI & Analytics.


About Group BI & Analytics

Group BI & Analytics is a team of 8 employees responsible for enabling data-driven decision-making across BORG Automotive. We work closely with the business to transform data into trusted insights, scalable data products, and improved decision-making capabilities. Our mission is to help the organization turn data into business value through strong collaboration, governance, and analytics expertise.

We will process the applications ongoing, but due to summer holidays we will take the first look at candidates medio August.

Experience with SQL, BI tools, reporting, analytics, or data modelling is an advantage, but the most important factor is a genuine interest in helping the business create value through data.

What We Offer

  • A role with significant interaction across functions, countries, and business areas.
  • The opportunity to build deep expertise within a selected business domain.
  • A strong learning environment focused on capability building and knowledge sharing.
  • Exposure to modern BI and analytics technologies and business-critical initiatives.
  • Clear opportunities to develop your skills, grow your expertise, and shape your future career path within BI & Analytics.

About Group BI & Analytics

Group BI & Analytics is a team of 8 employees responsible for enabling data-driven decision-making across BORG Automotive. We work closely with the business to transform data into trusted insights, scalable data products, and improved decision-making capabilities. Our mission is to help the organization turn data into business value through strong collaboration, governance, and analytics expertise.

We will process the applications ongoing, but due to summer holidays we will take the first look at candidates medio August.

Title: REVENUE & ANALYTICS MANAGER
Place: Kastrup, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
CARGO REVENUE AND ANALYTICS MANAGER

Join SAS Cargo and help shape the future of our performance.

SAS Cargo is a premium multi-market airline driving sustainable profits on our way towards a consolidated future. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations. Besides expanding our presence in main hub Copenhagen, we are transforming how we make commercial decisions by using more data, acting faster, and serve better.

If you’re passionate about analytics, commercial optimization, and creating measurable business impact, this is your opportunity.

You will be part of Commercial’s RM and Pricing team, a small and diverse team responsible for optimizing SAS Cargo’s revenue and contribution, and ensuring that SAS Cargo is offering a competitive network, relevant products and competitive rates on SAS capacity.

What You’ll Do

In-depth analysis


Analyze performance across yields, load factors, revenue drivers, and flights to identify trends and improvement opportunities
Conduct scenario modelling and forecasting together with the Finance Business Controller and Partner and Strategic Pricing Manager to support strategic decisions and reporting outlooks
Contribute to our ongoing ML/AI initiatives, enhancing our predictive capabilities and enabling smarter, data‑driven decision‑making.
Support or lead development of business cases and business follow-up across SAS Cargo
Act as an internal ambassador for data-driven decision making within SAS Cargo.


Revenue Management


Optimize revenue contribution on SAS belly capacity on your own route portfolio through optimal rates, overbooking decisions and sales mix analysis
Work cross functional with Operations managers and planners, and Sales on day-to-day route optimization
Monitor and adjust steering (rates, capacity allocation) based on market changes and input from stakeholders
Evaluate and set up allotment allocations
Respond to special rate requests (spot market)
Work with Global Sales and Strategic Pricing Manager to set rates correctly and win deals
Work with our RM architect and rest of the RM-team to continuously develop best practice and adoption of our upcoming new RM system enhancements.


What You Bring


Required: 1-2 years of experience in an analytical or technical role. Minimum BSc degree or equivalent. Previous experience in revenue management, data/business analysis, or a related field within aviation, logistics, transportation, hospitality or other network industries is big plus.
Preferred: Experience with statistical modeling, RM systems, programming, or quantitative analytics will make you stand out.


Your strengths


Strategic & Analytical Thinking: You thrive on solving complex problems, making data-driven decisions, and turning insights into strategies that drive business growth.
Clear Communication: You excel at explaining complex data insights in a way that’s both clear and compelling, connecting with colleagues across departments.
Proactive & Results-Driven: You believe in making things happen! You take the initiative, move quickly from analysis to action, and consistently deliver impactful results.
Why You Should Apply: At our company, we believe in supporting each other and championing everyone’s potential. If you're passionate about what you do and want to work in a place that values personal development, well-being, and empowerment, this is the role for you.


Our Culture At SAS Cargo And What We Offer

At SAS Cargo, you will join a collaborative and supportive team where you can take initiative, influence key commercial processes, and contribute to creating value across the organization.

We Offer


A role with real responsibility and opportunity to drive improvements in revenue and decision-making
A strong team culture where we collaborate across SAS Cargo and the wider SAS organization, recognize each other’s efforts, and share successes
A structured onboarding program with support from experienced colleagues and your manager
Workplace flexibility, including the possibility of some remote work and option to access a gym
Social activities with colleagues in and outside of work
Access to staff tickets and discounts. You can read more at https://www.sasgroup.net/career/working-at-sas/benefits-and-compensations/


Additional Information 


Interviews will not take place before 2nd half of August. Application deadline 16 august 2026. Note that we cannot handle any applications that are sent by email, due to GDPR regulations.
Desired start date: As soon as possible.
Position: Fulltime (100%) starting with a 3-month probation period
This position is based at SAS Cargo’s new HQ facility at Copenhagen airport, and you will be expected to work onsite. Some remote work may also be an option, depending on the agreement made with your manager.
To be eligible for this position you need an EU-work permit. Please note that we ask you to inform us if you currently have a valid EU-work permit.


SAS Cargo is the leading provider of air freight solutions to, from and within Scandinavia, delivering world class quality and customer care. SAS Cargo’s services are based on the cargo capacity of SAS network, extended by dedicated truck operations.

Title: Business Intelligence Consultant
Company: SameSystem - Retail WFM | Part of ECIT
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
BI Consultant




Please note that fluency in Danish is a requirement for this position.

Due to the summer holidays, please expect a longer processing time.




SameSystem

Are you ready to turn data into real commercial value for some of Europe's largest retail and hospitality brands? At SameSystem, we empower companies like Bestseller, Nike, Normal, and Matas to optimize their operations daily with our cutting-edge AI-driven Workforce Management Solution. We're looking for a BI Consultant who is as comfortable building a clean dashboard as sitting across the table from a customer explaining what it means for their business.

This is a full-time role, and you'll join our Customer Success team in Copenhagen. You'll own the business intelligence and reporting side of our platform: building and maintaining reports in our BI tool, Sisense, and being the person our customers and colleagues turn to when a number needs to make sense. We've deliberately placed this role in Customer Success rather than in a back-office data team, because the reporting is only worth as much as the commercial insight it drives. This role suits someone who is happy behind the screen building things properly, but who does not want to only sit behind the screen.

This is a new role, so the right person will have real influence over how we build, package, and deliver the analytics side of our platform.




Key responsibilities

Building the reports

Design, build, and maintain BI reports and dashboards in Sisense, with strong attention to data integrity, performance, and usability
Translate customer and internal requirements into robust data models and reporting logic
Own reporting quality across accounts, including validation, troubleshooting, and iterative improvement
Develop new reporting capabilities and scope custom development work as our customers' needs grow




Working with customers

Join customer meetings and represent the BI and analytics part of the SameSystem platform
Talk specific development and scoping with customers, and translate their business questions into what we can actually build
Help customers see the value in their data, and support Customer Success and Sales in retention, upselling, and expansion through analytics
Collaborate cross-functionally with Product, Sales, and Support to feed customer needs back into the platform




What we're looking for

Proven experience building reports and dashboards in a BI tool. Experience with Sisense is a strong plus; solid experience with a comparable tool (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik) is very welcome
Enough experience under your belt to own your work end to end
Strong SQL and data modelling skills, and the instinct to build things properly rather than just quickly
Commercial acumen. You understand that a report exists to drive a decision, and you're comfortable talking value and scope with customers
Confidence in client-facing settings, presenting clearly to both operational and managerial stakeholders
The balance we keep coming back to: someone who can do focused, hands-on build work and also step into a customer meeting and hold their own
A self-starter who takes ownership and isn't afraid to share ideas. Execution matters
Nice to have: comfort scoping and pricing development work. If pricing is new to you, that's fine, we can teach it
Fluency in Danish and English; additional European languages are a plus
Familiarity with retail operations or workforce management is a plus




What you'll get

At SameSystem, we are committed to transforming retail and hospitality through cutting-edge technology. Joining our team means:

Working in a dynamic environment where innovation drives success.
Contributing to the success of over 185,000 employees across 12,500+ stores in 24 countries.
Access to professional growth opportunities in a company that values expertise and collaboration.
Being part of a supportive culture that prioritizes work-life balance and employee satisfaction.
Lots of social activities and fun parties as well as "clubs" run by employees where SameSystem sponsors going to a sauna, playing badminton, drinking port wine, or whatever else the team can think of.
Lunch and as much coffee, soda or energy drink as you can muster.




If you have any questions about this position, please reach out to Nikolai May Yde at nikolai.yde@samesystem.com.

Title: Quantitative analyst for Short-term Power Trading
Description:
Experienced in quantitative modeling for energy or finance? Skilled in programming? Join us as a quantitative analyst for Intraday Analysis and make a real impact.




Here’s what the job entails

In this role, you will help optimise our trading performance by developing quantitative models that impact the decision-making process across our trading teams.




More specifically, your responsibilities include:

developing and enhancing quantitative models to drive trading decisions
taking active part in coding algorithmic trading infrastructure and optimisation engines used for live trading
conducting market research and strategy analysis aimed at improving overall PnL and operational robustness
collaborating with team members to ensure the operational stability of algorithms and tools developed by the team




Meet the team

As our new quantitative analyst, you will join our Intraday Analysis team, where we support the trading and asset optimisation teams by building quantitative models and trading tools. Our primary area of responsibility is to develop strategies, trading tools, and quantitative models that enable decision-making for our proprietary trading and asset business.




We are a team of 6 specialists in energy trading with backgrounds in economics and mathematics. We are a curious, dedicated, and highly analytical team who always strive to improve processes, systems, and strategies to fine-tune the infrastructural engine of our power trading business.




But enough about us… over to you!

As you will be developing models and analyses used for decision-making, we are looking for someone who has a data-driven approach and an eye for market risks while also evaluating other factors in your analyses. Also, as we operate in an ever-changing market, we see you as someone who thrives in a dynamic environment and can quickly adapt to market conditions.




We also imagine that you:

have an educational background in a quantitative field, for example, economics, mathematics, physics, statistics, data science, or engineering
have experience in a similar position, either in the energy industry, the financial markets or similar
have solid coding skills with the ability to write production-level Python code
have great communication skills as you should be able to explain your analyses to stakeholders who do not have the same insights as our team
are proficient in English - both written and verbal




Please note that prior knowledge of the energy sector is not required for this role.




We’ll take good care of you

At Danske Commodities, we take great care of our people. Joining us means you’ll get a lot of great perks – including social events, cultural experiences, tasty food and benefits to better your health, your life and your future.




Here are some of the benefits we offer our employees:

Shape your development tailored growth plans, 100+ DC University courses & Harvard Learning access

100+ yearly events from office parties to DC Sports events, CS:GO team, student network & more

Global career paths as part of our parent company, Equinor’s 20,000-strong international network

Flexible work life adjustable hours, part-time options & up to 2 work from home days/week

Fuel Fridays one paid Friday off every month (July and December excluded)

Top-tier parental leave up to 26 weeks paid, pension during leave & post-leave support

Unlimited paid child's sick days because family comes first

Extra time off 5 additional days on top of your 5 weeks

Senior days extra time off if you’re 55+

Attractive package 10% pension, health insurance, bonus scheme & more

Share savings the option to join Equinor’s attractive share programme

Home setup covered paid internet, phone & IT gear

Free tickets & discounts culture, family fun, hotels, restaurants & more

Organic meals light breakfast, lunch, snacks, dinner & take-away

Barista café high-quality coffee, always on us

On-site wellness state-of-the-art gym, physio, chiro and massage therapy

New HQ in the heart of Aarhus with stunning views, inspiring architecture and close to public transport

…. and much more

Title: Digital Solution Engineer – Offshore Wind Industry
Place: Vejle, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you passionate about developing, maintaining, and analysing data? Do you want to work in the offshore wind industry where your skills and expertise will help secure a sustainable future based on renewable energy? Then keep reading – Cadeler is looking for a Digital Solution Engineer with your skills and interests!

Cadeler is a global partner in the offshore wind industry, specializing in wind farm construction and maintenance. We are part of an industry that is now, more than ever, both relevant and play a critical role in the energy transition with a strong long-term outlook. As our industry continues to develop, so do we! We are now looking for a Digital Solution Engineer to help optimise our operational performance within jacking and monopile installation.

What will you do?

As our new Digital Solution Engineer, you will act as a bridge between offshore operational teams and onshore engineering by translating operational data into actionable insights. Furthermore, you will work hand-in-hand with the BI team regarding the data platform, data validation, and the development of dashboards.

Your Main Tasks Include


Analyse operational data from vessel systems, jacking logs, geotechnical data, and installation records.
Develop dashboards and visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Python-based dashboards, or similar) based on Cadeler’s data platform.
Identify trends, deviations, and performance improvement opportunities based on installation and jacking data. Support root cause analysis of operational events using data-driven methodologies.
Identify, propose, and refine engineering performance metrics in collaboration with the Operational Excellence and BI teams
Identify opportunities for performance optimization, cost reduction, and risk mitigation using data analytics.
Engage with clients when data-driven reporting or performance analysis is required


To succeed in this role

Being part of the Cadeler community means you will have a natural interest and care for the environment and our shared mission to speed up the green energy transition. Your environmental considerations and the safety of our people and the planet will be a key priority in your daily work.

We are looking for someone with a strong analytical and structured mindset, who possesses strong communication skills and is driven by curiosity and motivation to improve operations through digital solutions.

We think you will be a good match if you:


2–5 years of relevant experience
Academic degree in engineering (civil, mechanical, geotechnical, marine) or data science with strong technical/engineering exposure.
Experience with data analysis tools such as Python, Power BI, SQL, or similar.
Hands-on experience working with cloud-based data platforms (e.g., Snowflake or equivalent technologies)
Experience in offshore wind, marine operations, heavy lift, or jack-up vessels is an advantage but not mandatory.
Experience working with large datasets and operational performance analysis.


Come work with us!

By becoming a Cadeler employee, you will be part of a rapidly growing company with a diverse and energetic team. We offer an exciting position focused on delivering excellence in the face of interesting new challenges, within a positive and rewarding work environment in an international company with great development possibilities.

Cadeler is headquartered in Copenhagen, but we also have offices in Vejle, UK, US, and Taiwan, as well as colleagues working offshore aboard our fleet of Wind Farm Installation Vessels (WFIV).

Meet some of our colleagues here: https://www.cadeler.com/career

Apply

Use the APPLY button to apply for this position. For data protection purposes, applications are only accepted via the link and are assessed continuously until we find the right candidate, so please don’t hesitate to send in your application as soon as possible and no later than the 10th of August. You can expect to hear back from us after that date, due to vacation.

If you have any questions regarding the position, please contact Geotechnical & Jacking Engineering Team Lead, Can Mollaibrahimoglu, on (+45) 2979 9623, and for practical matters please contact peopleandculture@cadeler.com.

Title: Business Analytics Consultant
Description:
Twoday er den førende digitale transformationspartner i Nordeuropa med en global tilstedeværelse. Med cirka 3.000 teknologer samarbejder vi med de mest beundrede private og offentlige organisationer for at levere banebrydende digitale løsninger. Vores dybe brancheekspertise omfatter Data & AI, softwareudvikling, digitale oplevelser og forretningsapplikationer. Vores team, der opererer i hele Norden og Litauen, genererede en omsætning på 280 millioner euro i 2023. Vi betjener over 8.000 kunder og understøtter deres digitale transformationsrejser.

Vil du være med på Danmarks stærkeste team af Business Analytics-konsulenter? Vil du arbejde med nogle af Danmark største virksomheder? Tænder du på at gøre komplekse problemstillinger til simple løsninger for kunderne? Så er twoday Data & AI det rigtige sted for dig! Vi søger nye Business Analytics-konsulenter til et af vores kontorer i Danmark.

Om stillingen

Som Business Analytics-konsulent vil du indgå i et stærkt team med en varierende hverdag – du vil få ansvar, frihed og faglig udvikling. Vores konsulenter besidder hver især stærke kompetencer, og du vil opleve kolleger, der er specialister på deres område.

Du vil sammen med vores kunder afklare de forretningsmæssige behov og derefter designe, udvikle og implementere løsningen. Alt efter kompleksitet og størrelse af løsningen arbejder du enten i et team eller som primær konsulent på løsningerne.

Vi holder faste firmadage, hvor sparring, idéudveksling og kollegialt sammenhold er i højsædet. Du vil opleve at blive udfordret, men du vil også få mulighed for at udfordre dine kolleger.

Fristet, men stadig i tvivl?


Turbo på det faglige! Hos twoday Data & AI er vores medarbejdere kendt for at have et skyhøjt fagligt niveau. Samtidig med at vi har det godt socialt.
Lækre kontorer! twoday Data & AI har lækre og moderne kontorer centralt placeret i København, Aarhus, Aalborg og Kolding.
Tid til fordybelse! Vi har fire årlige fagdage, hvor idéudvikling, udvikling og sparring er i højsædet.
Uddannelse! Hos twoday Data & AI får du mulighed for efteruddannelse og certificeringer.
Attraktiv lønpakke! Vi tilbyder en attraktiv lønpakke med branchens bedste bonusordning.
Fantastisk kaffe! Hos twoday Data & AI har du adgang til fitness, frugt og fantastisk god kaffe.


Om dig

Du har flere års relevant erhvervserfaring og brænder for hele tiden at blive bedre. Du har en baggrund som BI- eller datakonsulent, controller i en større virksomhed eller noget lign. Du har erfaring med Azure Data Platform, Microsoft Fabric, Databricks og/eller SQL Server. Endvidere har du kendskab til én eller flere front-end værktøjer såsom Power BI, Excel eller lign.

Du ved, at livet som konsulent er udfordrende og til tider meget krævende. Vores vigtigste aktiv er vores kunder og medarbejdere, og du er klar til at give, hvad der skal til, for at skabe et godt forhold både internt og eksternt samt at udvikle Business Intelligence-løsninger i verdensklasse.

Din uddannelse kan være cand.merc., cand.merc.dat eller cand.oecon – eller for den sags skyld noget helt fjerde. Det vigtigste er, at du brænder for Business Analytics og ønsker at gøre en forskel.

Dine kommunikative evner er i top, og du skriver og taler flydende dansk og engelsk.

Vi begejstrer med data – måske vil du være med?

Så send din ansøgning i dag, de vil løbende blive læst.

Der vil løbende blive indkaldt til indledende samtaler. Du ansøger ved at klikke på knappen nedenfor. Alle ansøgninger behandles fortroligt.

Hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen, er Ann Brahe-Orlandi, Talent Acquisition Manager, klar til at hjælpe. Hun glæder sig til at høre fra dig på ann.brahe-orlandi@twoday.com.

Hvad kan vi tilbyde?

Twoday er placeret i moderne lokaler på 30 steder i Norge, Sverige, Danmark, Finland og Litauen. Vi har et stærkt og unikt socialt miljø med højt engagerede medarbejdere. Hvis du er den kandidat, vi leder efter, vil du få en central rolle med spændende muligheder for at udvikle dig i en international og innovativ gruppe, med spændende produkter og tjenester inden for teknologi og software. Du vil også have fleksibiliteten til at "jumpe" og arbejde fra et af vores hovedkontorer i Oslo, Stockholm, København, Helsinki eller Vilnius til tider.

Title: Audience Insights Specialist
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you want to be a valued part of the critical infrastructure that serves communities across Europe and beyond? Are you looking for a position in which you can set direction and help shape our company? If so, then you could be the newest addition to our team as Audience Insight Specialist.

Be part of the movement – and play your part

About The Role

We serve b2c Ferry passengers globally and we are seeking an analytical thinker with strong strategic vision. You should be capable of stakeholder management, with a strong background in turning data into insight and delivering innovative value-added analysis.

It is imperative you are someone that is proactive and passionate about insights. You will need to have a technical skill set, a thorough understanding of manipulating raw data and building this into actionable insight using analytical and visualisation tools.

You Will Be Responsible For


Support the Insights strategy through the building of reports that answer key business questions and track the performance of success metrics
Support the passenger audience segmentation strategy, definitions and tactics for activation, through incorporating audience segmentation splits within reporting
Support the Audience Insights Lead in mapping the passenger customer journeys and build reports to measure efficiency at specific touchpoints in the customer journey
Ensure insights are available across all markets and routes and aligned with business needs
Support the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team in identifying business opportunities through the identification of trends within reporting
Guide your colleagues in any research needs, aligned to the trends/ opportunities identified through Audience Insights reporting
Adopt cross-functional alignment: partner with revenue, marketing, sales and routes to ensure reporting and metrics are standardised
You will be data literate and have a strong ability to identify trends alongside an inquisitive nature to understand the root cause of specific trends identified.


Your Capabilities


Strong analytical capabilities with experience in visualisation tools such as Power BI and preferably also data connectivity tools such as SQL or similar
Logical thinker with the ability to understand business needs
Business acumen: tie insights to revenue, retention, product-market fit, and prioritization/ROI
Storytelling and influencing without authority, utilising data to provide actionable insights
Presentation and communication skills – complex is easy, simple is hard
Experience with surveys and survey design
Experience with brand tracking and/or online panel/communities is a plus


About You

You will work in the Audience Insights and Strategic Development team, utilising the data available to you to build a reporting suite that is aligned to business needs, generating the insights needed to guide strategic decisions. You will work alongside colleagues aligning reporting with research projects and programmes (e.g. The Voice of The Passenger – NPS and CSAT) to provide holistic actionable insights. You will be a very analytical individual that is excited by turning data into insights and capable to utilise tools to improve data connectivity and increase automation across reports. You will be an enthusiastic, curious, passionate and collaborative individual who continually looks for opportunities, with a solution focussed attitude.

Skills And Experience We Imagine You Bring


An analytical thinker with the ability to turn data into insights
2-3 years of insight and data & analytics experience (essential)
Preferably technical with experience in Power BI or similar tool and maybe also SQL, python, and/or other relevant tools
Strong organisational skills with the ability to prioritise own and others workload.
Ability to communicate complex data and ideas into digestible information.
Ability to be flexible, and adapt to change and leverage trends
Strong communication and collaboration skills


We think the strongest teams are built on diversity and inclusivity. Contrasting opinions, varied skills and strong characters bring out the best in us all. And we cannot wait for you to join.

Working at DFDS

Be a valued member of a diverse team at DFDS and help drive change together.

We promote a great workplace with a diverse and vibrant community and a healthy work–life balance. The position can be located in either Newcastle or Copenhagen.

While you’re with us, we want you to develop. At DFDS, we believe that development happens first and foremost through activities in the workplace. We will also offer training courses that can support you in your role, if needed.

Are we a match? Then what are you waiting for? Apply Now.

If you believe your experience, mindset, and motivation align with this role, we would be happy to hear from you. Please submit your CV at your earliest convenience, as we review applications on an ongoing basis.

Please note that due to the summer holiday period, there might be a delay in response time, but rest assured that we will get back to you as soon as possible.

We look forward to hearing from you.

Title: Procurement Analyst - Procurement Intelligence & Finance Partnering
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Procurement Analyst – Procurement Intelligence & Finance Partnering

Are you someone who can move from a raw dataset to a decision-ready recommendation – and enjoys doing both? At Lundbeck, we’re looking for an analytically strong professional to join our Procurement Intelligence & Finance Partnering team at our global headquarters in Copenhagen. You could be the person who turns data, market intelligence and AI into better sourcing decisions.

Your new role

You will support fact-based decision-making across our procurement categories by combining data analysis, financial understanding and modern tooling – including AI – and translating the results into actions our category managers and stakeholders can execute on.

The role is deliberately broad. Depending on the priorities of the moment, you might be modelling the financial impact of a negotiation strategy one week and building an AI-assisted workflow that removes manual reporting work the next. What stays constant is the expectation: rigorous analysis, clear communication, and insights that lead to action.

It is your chance to:


Analyze spend, savings, contract and market data across categories, and translate findings into concrete, prioritized recommendations.
Build and maintain reporting and dashboards that track savings delivery, category performance and value creation – for both operational and executive audiences.
Help drive the practical adoption of AI in our procurement work: identifying use cases, building or configuring solutions, and helping the team work smarter.
Support the financial side of procurement, including savings validation, budget follow-up and variance analysis in collaboration with Finance.
Partner with category managers to prepare negotiations, business cases and sourcing decisions with solid analytical foundations.
Prepare high-quality, executive-ready materials that shape decisions beyond procurement.



Your future team

Corporate Procurement is a global team of approximately 50 colleagues across Copenhagen, Chicago, and Krakow. You will join the Procurement Business Intelligence & Finance Partnering team, a team of five that spans data analytics, project management and sustainability.

The team is characterized by high standards, strong ownership and a fact-based approach to decision-making. Responsibility is given early, and development is driven through real business challenges and close collaboration.

The position is based in Copenhagen, Denmark.

What You Bring To The Team

You combine strong analytical capabilities, and a good understanding of Procurement. You have the ability to combine analytical horsepower with commercial and financial understanding. We believe the following will set a candidate apart:


A Master’s degree in business, economics, finance, engineering, data science or a related field.
2–5 years of experience from management consulting, procurement, finance or a similar analytical role
Strong data analysis skills: advanced Excel and PowerPoint are a given; experience with Power BI, or other data visualization tools.
A working understanding of procurement and corporate finance fundamentals: savings methodology, total cost of ownership, budget and P&L impact.
Structured problem-solving and the ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable recommendations for senior audiences.
Strong communication skills – you can defend your numbers and land your message with stakeholders at all levels.



In addition, you thrive in a fast-paced but collaborative environment with high expectations and exposure to senior management.

Our promise to you

Lundbeck offers an inspiring workplace and innovative culture, where our curiosity, accountability and adaptability enable us to transform lives. We want to go faster and further on addressing the big unmet needs of people living with brain disorders. We offer rewarding careers with a mix of exciting tasks and development opportunities that are balanced with initiatives focused on your well-being.

We need every brain in the game, and at Lundbeck, we are committed to building a workforce that is as diverse as the people we serve. Read more about our commitment at www.lundbeck.com/global/about-us/our-commitment/diversity-and-inclusion.

Annual base salary range: 582,000 - 799,700 DKK gross.

At Lundbeck, we are committed to fair and transparent pay. Please note that the final base salary within the stated range will be based on relevant qualifications, skills, competencies, and level of proficiency as well as internal pay equity.

Apply now

Can you see yourself in this role? We want to hear from you. Does this sound like your dream job, but you’re not sure if you meet all the requirements? We still want to hear from you!

Upload your CV and include a few lines about your motivation for applying. We ask you not to include a photo or personal information that does not relate to your professional experience.

If you have questions, please contact Christian Pedersen, CNE@Lundbeck.com.

Applications must be received by 27 July 2026.

Learn more about us at www.lundbeck.com, LinkedIn or Instagram (@h_lundbeck). #EveryBrainInTheGame

This job ad is intended for individuals seeking a career opportunity with Lundbeck. We engage with recruitment and search firms where needed on the basis of a written agreement, and we do not accept unsolicited requests of any kind. If you work as a recruitment consultant, you are kindly instructed to refrain from contacting Lundbeck or the hiring manager directly with suggested candidates. If you violate this policy, you do so at your own risk and for your own account, and Lundbeck will not assume any liability nor pay any associated fees resulting from such violation.
