Title: Netanalytiker
Place: Horsens Municipality, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
Description:
Motiveres du af at omsætte data til konkrete indsigter med reel betydning? Vil du være med til at analysere og udvikle elnettet, så vi understøtter den grønne omstilling og træffer de bedste beslutninger på et solidt datagrundlag?




Hos KONSTANT i Horsens søger vi en netanalytiker, der vil være med til at udvikle og optimere fremtidens elforsyningsnet.




Hvem er KONSTANT?

KONSTANT er en del af NRGi-koncernen og sikrer stabil elforsyning til godt 255.000 kunder i Østjylland. Vi er omkring 100 engagerede medarbejdere, der overvåger, vedligeholder og udvikler elnettet med fokus på driftssikkerhed, effektivitet og grøn omstilling.




Som et af landets største netselskaber ligger vi i toppen af branchens benchmarking og arbejder målrettet med at optimere og automatisere infrastrukturen, så vi er klar til fremtidens behov.




Jobbet som netanalytiker

Som netanalytiker bliver du en nøgleperson i arbejdet med vores måledata og analyser. Du får en central rolle i at videreudvikle vores interne analyse- og visualiseringsværktøjer og sikre, at data bliver omsat til værdifuld indsigt.




Du vil blandt andet:

• Omsætte komplekse måledata til klare indsigter og beslutningsgrundlag

• Udvikle dashboards og visualiseringer, der skaber overblik

• Udarbejde og følge op på nøgletal (KPI’er)

• Sikre datakvalitet og sammenhæng i analyser

• Optimere hjemtagning og håndtering af data fra vores 255.000 fjernaflæste målere

• Understøtte forretningen med analyser, der gør en forskel i hverdagen




Du arbejder tæt sammen med både tekniske specialister og forretningen – og bliver bindeleddet mellem data og beslutninger.




Din profil

Vi forestiller os, at du har en relevant uddannelse inden for data, IT, ingeniørvidenskab, matematik, fysik eller lignende. Du kan være nyuddannet eller have erfaring – vigtigst er din analytiske tilgang og interesse for at gøre hverdagen nemmere for dine kollegaer.




Du arbejder struktureret, er løsningsorienteret og formår at bevare overblikket i komplekse opgaver.




I rollen får du en bred kontaktflade både internt og eksternt, og derfor er det vigtigt, at du kan kommunikere klart og skabe gode relationer. Din evne til at samarbejde og finde løsninger er med til at sikre, at vi når i mål med projekterne og skaber værdi for både kunder og organisation.




Hvad tilbyder vi?

Vi tilbyder en central rolle i den grønne omstilling og et fagligt miljø, hvor vi arbejder tæt sammen og bruger hinandens faglighed til at skabe de bedste løsninger. Vi ser forskellighed som en styrke, og det er netop samspillet mellem vores erfaringer og fagligheder, der gør os stærke som organisation.




Vi er stolte af, at mange af vores medarbejdere har været hos os i mange år – et tegn på, at vi trives og har en kultur, hvor man har lyst til at blive. Her er der plads til humor, fællesskab og respekt for hinanden, og vi lægger vægt på en god balance mellem arbejde og privatliv.




Vi tilbyder en lønpakke med pensionsordning, fritvalg og en attraktiv sundhedsordning. Som en del af KONSTANT Net/NRGi har du desuden adgang til et væld af rabatordninger bl.a. på el og ladeløsninger.




Interesseret

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte Afdelingschef for Netudvikling Chris Hedegaard Birch på mail chhb@konstant.dk.




Vi ønsker stillingen besat hurtigst muligt, men venter gerne på den rette kandidat.

Title: Bliv Process Mining Analyst til Process Performance teamet i en foreningsejet Nykredit-koncern
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Er du interesseret i processer, dataanalyse og Business Intelligence? Og vil du have et job, hvor du driver datadrevne procesforbedringer på tværs af Nykredit‑koncernen? Så er du måske vores nye analytiker i Process Performance‑teamet.

Nykredit-koncernen er ejet af en forening af vores kunder, Forenet Kredit. Det betyder, at vi kan dele vores fremgang med vores kunder, når det går os godt, og at vi påtager os et særligt ansvar over for det samfund, vi er en del af. Det vil du også opleve, når vi kan byde dig velkommen som Process Mining Analyst.

Dine vigtigste arbejdsopgaver bliver:


Analysere procesdata, generere indsigt, identificere udfordringer og beregne effekt- og gevinstcases i projekter.
Designe, teste, implementere og validere dashboards, visualiseringer og brugeroplevelser til forskellige proces- og forretningsinteressenter.
Samarbejde med forretnings- og proceskonsulenter for at forstå konteksten af forretningsprocesser, hovedmålepunkter og mål, og omsætte dem til Process Mining‑anvendelsescases.
Samarbejde med proces- og datakonsulenter og omsætte opgaver til tekniske og datamæssige krav.
Træne og understøtte proceskonsulenter i at analysere procesdata samt støtte og vejlede slutbrugere for at fremme anvendelsen af analyserne.



Er det dig, vi leder efter?

For at lykkes i rollen forventer vi, at du har erfaring som data‑, proces‑ eller businessanalytiker og er fortrolig med dataanalyse og Business Intelligence. Vi lægger vægt på selvstændigt arbejde og evnen til at tage initiativ.

Vi leder efter en kollega, der er nysgerrig og motiveret for at arbejde med nye værktøjer som vores Process Mining‑platform Celonis, og som har lyst til at udvikle sig til ekspert gennem træning, praksis og kontinuerlig læring – en udvikling, vi naturligvis støtter aktivt.

Da vi opererer i en kompleks og reguleret branche, er flydende dansk (både skriftligt og mundtligt) et krav. Derudover ser vi gerne, at du har:


erfaring som analytiker med fokus på dataanalyse, forretningsprocesser og procesforbedring,
erfaring med design, udvikling og implementering af datadrevne løsninger, kendskab til SQL og erfaring med databasehåndtering,
erfaring med Business Intelligence‑værktøjer som et solidt fundament for at arbejde med og adoptere Celonis‑platformen,
evne til at visualisere data og præsentere resultater tydeligt og brugervenligt,
interesse for Process Mining‑værktøjer som Celonis.



Til gengæld lover vi både udvikling og muligheder til dig, der vil og kan.

Dit nye team

Du bliver en del af et team på seks kolleger i Process Performance‑teamet, som driver brugen af Celonis Process Mining og arbejder med både projekt- og driftsopgaver for at sikre effektiv anvendelse og udbredelse i Nykredit‑koncernen.

Vores mål er at skabe visualiseringer af processer og ydeevne, så processejere og ledere kan træffe de bedste beslutninger om, hvordan processerne skal forbedres.

Process Performance‑teamet i Process Development er en del af Processer og Driftsoptimering – et stort fagfællesskab med fokus på procesforbedring, automatisering og styring. Vi har vores hverdag i Nykredit-koncernens hovedkvarter i Nordhavn, København, hvor du får adgang til frokostordning, gode fordele og masser af inspirerende og fagligt dygtige kolleger.

Vi glæder os til at tage godt imod dig som vores nye kollega.

Vil du vide mere?

Du er velkommen til at kontakte mig, funktionschef Matthias Dondrup (Gielkens) på tlf. 23 70 08 91, hvis du har spørgsmål til stillingen. Du kan også finde mig på LinkedIn her: Matthias Dondrup | LinkedIn.

Har du spørgsmål til rekrutteringsprocessen, kan du kontakte rekrutteringsteamet på nytjob@nykredit.dk

Vi inviterer løbende til samtaler og ser frem til at modtage din ansøgning og dit CV.

Title: Senior Analyst, In-Field Repair Quality
Description:
As a global leader in micromobility, Lime is on a mission to build a future where transportation is shared, affordable and carbon-free. A Time Magazine 100 Most Influential Company, Lime has powered more than one billion rides in close to 30 countries across five continents, spurring a new generation of clean alternatives to car ownership. Learn more at li.me.

Lime is hiring a Senior Analyst to join our Global Operations team. In this role, you'll be at the forefront of improving in-field repair quality performance— developing key metrics, building tools that drive real-time visibility, and delivering targeted analysis that informs operational decisions and strategic opportunities.

You'll serve as the functional bridge between Local Operations and our Product organization, translating operational needs into structured data requirements and tooling improvements. You'll collaborate closely with Local Operations, Product, Data Science, and Hardware teams to help shape the future of Lime's in-field repair quality across all of our operations as we continue to scale.

The ideal candidate is highly analytical, technically strong, and passionate about using data to solve complex problems. You're equally comfortable partnering with local operations teams and product or engineering stakeholders — and you know how to both structure a process and build a dashboard. If you have 3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations, strong data analysis and visualization skills, and a drive to influence outcomes through insight, we'd love to hear from you.

This is a hybrid role open to candidates based anywhere in Europe (London, Berlin, Paris, Munich, or Copenhagen preferred), within 120 minutes of a Lime warehouse. The role requires occasional travel (~10%) to Lime warehouses or other locations for team meetings.

What You’ll Do


Improve visibility into in-field repair quality: Define and maintain metrics to track repair performance, effectiveness, compliance, efficiency, and ROI — and build tooling to surface these insights in real time across operations
Build models and dashboards: Use SQL, Tableau, and spreadsheets to develop repair volume forecasting models, quality compliance dashboards, and performance reports for local operations teams — with robust logic, clearly defined assumptions, and intuitive interfaces
Monitor trends and diagnose performance gaps: Track repair efficiency and quality compliance over time, identify root causes behind variances or emerging trends, and recommend actionable solutions at both tactical and strategic levels
Turn operational complexity into structured clarity: Bridge Local Operations and Product/Tech by converting messy, real-world business needs into crisp specs, user stories, data requirements, and SOPs that teams can actually execute against
Formalize and improve operational processes: Map and document quality workflows, surface inefficiencies, and partner with Local Operations to define and roll out improved standard operating procedures
Collaborate with partner teams to improve efficiency: Provide insight and targeted analysis to troubleshoot issues or highlight opportunities. Partner with Operations leadership and local teams to land recommendations and drive action through to resolution
Drive continuous improvement in data analytics capabilities across global operations: Contribute to a culture of data-driven decision making and analytical excellence through peer collaboration, knowledge sharing, and support


About You


Extremely strong proficiency in data analytics, including advanced SQL, spreadsheet modeling, and data visualization (e.g. Tableau or equivalent).
Bachelor's degree in Business, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, or equivalent industry experience
3+ years of experience in business analytics or operations analytics, with demonstrated ability to build reports and dashboards used by operational stakeholders
Experience translating business needs into functional data and reporting requirements, ideally working closely with a Product or Engineering team
Ability to structure and document operational processes, comfortable with ambiguity and working from first principles when standard frameworks don't exist
Curious mindset to spot trends in data, deep-dive root causes, and generate proposals for both tactical and strategic solutions
Clear verbal and written communicator, with strong collaboration and influencing skills across both field teams and technical stakeholders


Preferred Experience


Python, Google Apps Script, or other automation/scripting capabilities are a strong plus
Previous experience in a fast-paced, scaling operations environment (gig economy, last-mile, field services, or similar)
Experience working closely with Product or Engineering teams on tooling/product improvements


If you want to make an impact, Lime is the place for you. Not sure if you meet all the qualifications? If this role excites you we encourage you to apply. Explore all opportunities on our career page.

Lime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe different perspectives help us grow and achieve more. That’s why we’re dedicated to building and developing a team that reflects a wider range of backgrounds, abilities, identities, and experiences. If you require a reasonable accommodation during the application or hiring process, please email recruiting-operations@li.me for assistance.

Title: Data Scientists for Big Data, IoT and AI projects
Description:
Stillingsbeskrivelse

Do you like working creatively with data analytics, big data, AI, visualisation, NoSQL and databases? Would you like to join an international organisation where we already work with data analytics and machine learning projects? Join BEUMER Group and exploit the chance to innovate, develop and test concepts for products and tools and most importantly, develop your own skill.

You will team up with colleagues, so you can share and grow your knowledge within the data science field. It also means focusing on using data from an already existing infrastructure where we collect data from more than 350 sites worldwide.

Join our Customer Diagnostics Center

You become part of our Data Analytics department, which is part of our Customer Diagnostics Center (CDC). In the CDC, which is part of our Customer Support division, we are tasked with making sure our installed base of systems around the globe are healthy and running smoothly.

Here, you join a well-established but agile team of highly sought for data scientists. You have the freedom to develop solutions across the organisation focusing on creativity and innovation on the conceptual stages of possible new products, services and tools.

In the CDC, you will find a multitude of skills and competences, and we enjoy an informal atmosphere where we combine our strengths to create brand new solutions for colleagues and customers. Moreover, you become part of a data analytics forum where we work closely with universities to expand our knowledge.

Create value through data across the organisation

Through a continuous dialogue with our business and product development, you will be responsible for defining new products, services and tools based on data and machine learning. We are a large organisation with several specialist fields, which means that we can enjoy the value of data analysis across disciplines.

Your Tasks Will Include


Initiating mining of existing data from material handling systems running in production, looking for possible bottlenecks, optimizations etc.
Processing, cleansing, and verifying the integrity of data used for analysis.
Participating in the maintenance of the existing data pipeline.
Participate in defining and evaluating new techniques for data collection and analysis.
Drive the rollout and extension of our data collection to all sites in the BEUMER Group.


We have a strong preference for open-source software and are working with platforms and tools such as Kubernetes, ArgoCD, MLFlow, vLLM, Elastic stack, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, Numaflow, Python/Java/Golang, Jupyter notebooks and Opentelemetry. You will test and implement prototypes, e.g. for customer solutions or for your colleagues to provide them with the right tools to perfect their work. As such, you bridge the worlds of product development, subject specialists and technology to innovate the business.

Experience with data driven decision making, big data and concept development?

Role

Most important to this role is that you have a good all-round technical foundation, allowing you to implement software, configure tools, understand our domain and be a sounding board in the creative process. To do so, we also imagine that you:


Hold a master's degree or PhD as a computer scientist, engineer, physicist or similar.
Have a great understanding of and hands-on experience with using data analytics for system and concept development.
Speak and write English fluently and it is a big plus if you understand Danish as well.


Personality-wise, you are a strong communicator who can work across the organisation and with customers. Your strength is within innovation and creative thinking, which shows itself in added value in the concept development process and tests.

Interested?

If you have any questions regarding this position, please do not hesitate to contact Head of Global Data Analytics Anders Lildballe at +45 30 17 93 48. Please apply online via beumergroup.com/career.

We look forward to meeting you.

BEUMER Group

Conveying, loading, palletising, packing, sortation and distribution – BEUMER Group is a leader within the development and manufacture of high-technological intralogistics systems for global markets. Our employees stand out from others thanks to their ability to supply innovative solutions to our customers. They can do this because they know the industry and are passionate about continuously developing and expanding their knowledge. We support these high standards through teamwork, mutual respect and a working culture based on trust that fosters stability and security for all our employees. Our common goal is to implement outstanding and innovative projects worldwide.

  • Drive the rollout and extension of our data collection to all sites in the BEUMER Group.

We have a strong preference for open-source software and are working with platforms and tools such as Kubernetes, ArgoCD, MLFlow, vLLM, Elastic stack, Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, Numaflow, Python/Java/Golang, Jupyter notebooks and Opentelemetry. You will test and implement prototypes, e.g. for customer solutions or for your colleagues to provide them with the right tools to perfect their work. As such, you bridge the worlds of product development, subject specialists and technology to innovate the business.

Experience with data driven decision making, big data and concept development?

Role

Most important to this role is that you have a good all-round technical foundation, allowing you to implement software, configure tools, understand our domain and be a sounding board in the creative process. To do so, we also imagine that you:

  • Hold a master's degree or PhD as a computer scientist, engineer, physicist or similar.
  • Have a great understanding of and hands-on experience with using data analytics for system and concept development.
  • Speak and write English fluently and it is a big plus if you understand Danish as well.

Personality-wise, you are a strong communicator who can work across the organisation and with customers. Your strength is within innovation and creative thinking, which shows itself in added value in the concept development process and tests.

Interested?

If you have any questions regarding this position, please do not hesitate to contact Head of Global Data Analytics Anders Lildballe at +45 30 17 93 48. Please apply online via beumergroup.com/career.

We look forward to meeting you.

BEUMER Group

Conveying, loading, palletising, packing, sortation and distribution – BEUMER Group is a leader within the development and manufacture of high-technological intralogistics systems for global markets. Our employees stand out from others thanks to their ability to supply innovative solutions to our customers. They can do this because they know the industry and are passionate about continuously developing and expanding their knowledge. We support these high standards through teamwork, mutual respect and a working culture based on trust that fosters stability and security for all our employees. Our common goal is to implement outstanding and innovative projects worldwide.

Title: Dataanalytiker med stærke Python-kompetencer til Team Risikoanalyser
Company: Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø
Description:
Er du drevet af at omsætte komplekse datasæt til præcise risikovurderinger? Vil du være med til at sikre den grønne omstilling gennem avancerede statistiske modeller? Så er det dig, vi leder efter.

I Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø står vi over for en omfattende opgave: At sikre et rent vandmiljø og gennemføre en historisk omlægning af landbrugsarealer til natur og skov. En central del af denne opgave er at sikre en effektiv og korrekt implementering af den grønne trepart, og derfor ønsker vi nu at øge vores analysekapacitet.

Vi søger derfor en analytisk stærk profil til enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som kan bringe avancerede programmeringsteknikker og statistisk metodik i spil for at udvikle fremtidens analyse- og kontrolmodeller.

Din rolle: Fra rådata til intelligent risikoanalyse
Som vores nye dataanalytiker bliver du som noget af det første ansvarlig for at bidrage til løsningen af en specifik tværgående analyseopgave. Gennem vores onboarding-program bliver du introduceret til dine primære ansvarsområder, som vil omfatte:

Avanceret databehandling og automatisering: Du vil bruge Python til at automatisere komplekse arbejdsgange, håndtere store datamængder og transformere rådata til brugbare analyseresultater.
Statistisk modellering og analyse: Du vil udføre dybdegående analyser, der understøtter både den daglige kontrol og den overordnede politikudvikling i styrelsen.
Dokumentation og formidling: Du omsætter komplekse tekniske resultater til letforståelig dokumentation og informationsmateriale til både interne interessenter og eksterne partnere, herunder EU-kommissionen.


Din profil: Analytisk dybde og teknisk ekspertise
Vi forestiller os, at du har en relevant kandidatuddannelse inden for f.eks. statistik, data science, økonomi eller lignende kvantitative discipliner. Derudover lægger vi vægt på, at du har:

Stærke Python-kompetencer: Du er fortrolig med at bruge Python til datamanipulation, statistisk modellering og automatisering af analyseprocesser.
Metodisk fundering: Du har en dyb forståelse for statistisk analyse og erfaring med udvikling af prædiktive modeller.
Data-drevet mindset: Du er vant til at arbejde systematisk og struktureret med store, komplekse datasæt og kan navigere i både ustrukturerede og strukturerede data.
Programmeringskompetencer: Udover Python har du erfaring med SQL og herudover gerne kendskab til SAS Enterprise Guide.
Kommunikativ styrke: Du kan formidle tekniske konklusioner klart og præcist, både skriftligt og mundtligt, til kolleger med forskellige faglige baggrunde.


Det er en fordel, hvis du har erfaring med projektledelse og kendskab til forvaltning af EU-regler på miljø- og landbrugsområdet.

Vi tilbyder
Du bliver en del af Team Risikoanalyser i enheden Kvalitet & Risikoanalyse, som består af ca. 10 medarbejdere med forskellig baggrund. Vi arbejder i et miljø, hvor vidensdeling og gensidig sparring er i højsædet, og hvor du indgår i et tæt samarbejde med andre enheder i styrelsen. Som en del af Team Risikoanalyser vil du erfare, at du er med i opgaveløsningen fra start til slut, og det betyder, at du bidrager til hele processen og ikke kun en isoleret delmængde.

Vi tilbyder en fast stilling med stor faglig indflydelse, hvor dine analyser direkte bidrager til den grønne omstilling af Danmark.

Ansættelsesvilkår
Du vil blive ansat som fuldmægtig efter gældende overenskomst for akademikere i staten.

Ansættelsesområdet er Ministeriet for Natur og Dyrevelfærd med tilhørende institutioner, og tjenestestedet er Styrelsen for Grøn Arealomlægning og Vandmiljø, i Nyropsgade 30. 1780 København V.

Stillingen ønskes besat snarest muligt.

Kontakt
Vil du vide mere om stillingen, er du velkommen til at kontakte kontorchef Henrik Berg på telefon 25 16 82 14.

Du kan læse mere om styrelsen på www.sgav.dk

Ansøgning
Søg stillingen ved at sende en ansøgning inkl. CV, eksamensbevis og evt. referencer online i vores rekrutteringssystem (tryk på ”Søg stilling”) senest mandag den 27. juli 2026.

Vi forventer at afholde ansættelsessamtaler i uge 32.

Skills
Risk Management
Data Analytics
Python (Programming Language)
Statistical Data Analysis
Programming
SQL
Administration
EU Law
Communication
Project Management

Title: Retail Performance Analyst
Description:
Retail Performance Analyst

Location:

Aarhus C, DK, 8000

Category: Retail

Job Type: Headquarter

At Søstrene Grene, we believe that great retail experiences are created when commercial insight, operational excellence, and a passion for people come together. We are now looking for a Retail Performance Analyst who will play a central role in supporting and developing the performance of our retail business.

As a trusted partner to Retail, you will help transform data and insights into actions that strengthen both business results and customer experiences. You will work closely with colleagues across the organization and serve as an important link between Retail and key support functions.

If you are driven by turning complexity into clarity, enjoy working with performance data, and thrive in a collaborative environment, this could be the role for you.

Key Responsibilities

You will be responsible for driving performance management across Retail and ensuring that strategic priorities are translated into tangible results.

More specifically, you will:


Drive retail performance
Analyse key operational and commercial KPIs to identify opportunities for growth and improvement.
Translate data and insights into clear recommendations and actions.
Support Retail leaders with performance insights and decision-making.
Ensure strong performance follow-up
Monitor and follow up on sales performance and key business metrics on a daily and weekly basis.
Conduct monthly KPI reviews and performance analyses.
Provide insights into store performance and financial results to support continuous improvement.
Support financial planning processes
Support forecasting, profitability analyses, and ongoing financial follow-up.
Help ensure a strong commercial focus across the organization.
Improve tools, processes, and ways of working
Develop and optimize performance management tools and frameworks.
Drive initiatives that improve operational efficiency and support an excellent customer experience.
Contribute to a culture of continuous improvement and knowledge sharing.
Collaborate across Søstrene Grene
Act as a trusted sparring partner for Retail leadership.
Build strong relationships across functions such as Real Estate, Store Design, Franchise, and other business areas.
Support strategic initiatives that strengthen our retail organization and future growth.

What We Hope You Bring

We imagine that you are analytical, commercially minded, and motivated by creating value through collaboration.

You likely have:


Experience working with business performance, financial follow-up, or commercial analysis.
Experience from a retail organization, either from headquarters or operations.
Strong analytical capabilities and experience translating data into meaningful actions.
Experience with budgeting, forecasting, and financial performance management.
A structured and proactive approach to driving projects and improvements.
Strong stakeholder management skills and the ability to build relationships across different functions and levels.
Professional proficiency in Danish and English, both written and spoken, with the ability to communicate effectively across all levels of the organization.


Most importantly, you are curious, collaborative, and motivated by making a positive difference for both colleagues and customers.

What Success Looks Like

In this role, you will help create transparency around retail performance and enable better decisions across the business. Through strong partnerships, clear insights, and a focus on execution, you will contribute to the continued growth and development of Søstrene Grene's retail organization.

About Søstrene Grene's Culture

At Søstrene Grene, we strive to touch people’s hearts through a unique universe that embraces diversity and inclusion and creates a welcoming community for everyone, regardless of gender, ethnicity, religion, disability, or background.

We have a workplace where everyone is called “Sister,” regardless of gender. We share joys, strengthen one another, and laugh together. We call this the “Sister spirit,” and we warmly encourage everyone to apply and help create a vibrant and inclusive workplace.

Practical information

Location: Åboulevarden 21, 8000 Aarhus C

Working hours: 37 hours per week

Recruitment Process

Please upload your CV and application as soon as possible. We review applications on an ongoing basis and reserve the right to close the position once we have found the right candidate.

If you have questions regarding the role, feel free to contact Niklas Holz, Head of Retail Performance & Experience, at niklas.holz@sostrenegrene.com.

We will be enjoying a summer break during weeks 29–31. We kindly ask for your patience, as responses can be expected again from week 32.

Please note that the email address is for job-related questions only, and all application documents must be uploaded via our recruitment system.

Title: Intelligence Analyst
Description:
The R&D team at Chainalysis is inspired by solving the hardest technical challenges and building products that establish trust in cryptocurrencies. We're a global organization that thrives on challenging work and doing it alongside exceptionally talented teammates. Our industry evolves rapidly, and our mission is to build a flexible, AI-driven platform that automates entity resolution, optimizes data labeling pipelines, and creates predictive models that identify illicit patterns before they escalate.

Our data and solutions have been used to solve some of the world's most high-profile criminal cases and grow consumer access to cryptocurrency safely. Now, by pairing the industry's most trusted blockchain data with agents that reason, investigate, and act, we help our customers scale their workflows as the cryptocurrency economy becomes increasingly mainstream.

Chainalysis data has helped solve some of the world’s highest-profile criminal cases and made it safer for people everywhere to access crypto. The Global Intelligence Team produces the attribution at the heart of that data — connecting on-chain activity to the real-world entities behind it, across the global crypto ecosystem. It’s the intelligence data moat beneath every Chainalysis product, and what investigators and law enforcement rely on to follow the money and disrupt crime.

As an Intelligence Analyst, you’ll help build it across the crypto-services ecosystem — end to end: you collect the data, analyze it, and produce the attribution. And you won’t work one entity at a time; you’ll build and tune the AI and automation that turn your analysis into attribution at scale.

Who You’ll Work With

You’ll join a fast-moving, globally distributed team of analysts collaborating across time zones — async and in real time. We move fast, hold each other to a high bar, and back each other up across regions.

In This Role, You’ll


Investigate and attribute activity across the crypto-services ecosystem, connecting on-chain activity to real-world entities.
Proactively surface what’s worth investigating — and the automation worth building.
Build and tune AI workflows that scale your analysis — and handle the edge cases automation can’t.
Drive the data collection and research that keep our attribution comprehensive and fresh.
Hold a high bar for accuracy, analytic rigor, and documentation.
Become a certified cryptocurrency professional across all Chainalysis certifications.


We’re Looking For Candidates Who Have


Elite potential — high aptitude, sharp judgment, and a strong work ethic. This is a demanding role, and we hire only people who can clear a high bar and keep raising it.
2+ years of analytical or investigative experience (intelligence, law enforcement, cyber, fraud, compliance, or research).
Strong research instincts and analytic rigor.
Initiative — you spot what needs investigating and go after it without waiting to be told.
Adaptability — comfortable with ambiguity and energized by a fast-moving space; the threats, the tech, and our tooling are always evolving.
Real enthusiasm for AI — you want to build with it, not just use it.
Comfort working with data, and curiosity about crypto and blockchain.


AI at Chainalysis

AI is not a feature at Chainalysis - it is a new way of working. One that turns instructions into work done, and helps us move faster than the threats we're built to counter, and we expect our employees to take ownership of the output and ensure quality. As the world's most trusted blockchain analytics platform, Chainalysis sits at a rare intersection of proprietary data, regulatory relationships and crypto expertise that makes it uniquely placed to shape and lead the next era of AI-driven intelligence - and we expect everyone here, regardless of role, to be an active part of it.

AI fluency is tied directly to how we measure performance and how we plan to win. There is no substitute for your own curiosity. We provide the tools, workflows, and space to experiment - but the expectation is that you develop these capabilities yourself, bring ideas, and collaborate across teams to reinvent the way work gets done. We are not using AI to do less. We are using it to do what was never possible before.

About Chainalysis

Chainalysis is the blockchain data platform, making it easy to connect the movement of digital assets to real-world services. Powered by deep blockchain data and AI, organizations can investigate illicit activity, manage risk exposure, and develop innovative market solutions built on the industry's most trusted blockchain intelligence. Our mission is to build trust in blockchains, blending safety and security with an unwavering commitment to growth and innovation.

You belong here.

At Chainalysis, we believe that diversity of experience and thought makes us stronger. With both customers and employees around the world, we are committed to ensuring our team reflects the unique communities around us. We’re ensuring we keep learning by committing to continually revisit and reevaluate our diversity culture.

We encourage applicants across any race, ethnicity, gender/gender expression, age, spirituality, ability, experience and more. If you need any accommodations to make our interview process more accessible to you due to a disability, don't hesitate to let us know. You can learn more here. We can’t wait to meet you.

Title: Ansvarlig for data science og analyse (Data scientist)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Lige nu leder vi efter dig, der kan omsætte kvantitative data til meningsfulde indsigter, som understøtter Clevers beslutninger og udvikling af løsninger.

Din rolle

Du vil være med til at drive den grønne omstilling til gavn for hele samfundet. Her spiller virksomheder en vigtig rolle gennem elektrificering af deres bilflåder. For at kunne understøtte denne udvikling er det vigtigt, at vi driver omstillingen baseret på datadrevne beslutninger funderet i kundernes adfærd. Netop dét har vi brug for dig til at hjælpe med. 

Du vil være ansvarlig for at omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Hvad fyldes din dag med?

Rollen er hverken en ren Power BI-rolle eller en rolle, hvor du sidder og bygger tunge ML-modeller. Det er en rolle for dig, der vil være tæt på forretningen og være med til at løfte vores data modenheden i Erhverv, så vores beslutninger bliver skarpere, hurtigere og mindre afhængige af enkeltpersoner. I din hverdag vil du omsætte kvantitative indsigter fra kundernes forbrugs- og adfærdsdata til beslutninger, der skaber reel forretningsværdi. Du analyserer og visualiserer forbrugs- og tilfredshedsdata, og fungerer som datarådgiver for vores erhvervsforretningen. Fokus er ikke kun på, hvad der er sket, men i høj grad på, hvad der sandsynligvis vil ske, så vi kan skabe værdi på tværs af udvikling, salg og support.

Konkret betyder det at du:


Driver overgangen fra manuelle Excel-processer til robuste, skalerbare og datadrevne beslutningsgrundlag – f.eks. inden for forecasting, segmentindsigter og prissætning.
Samarbejder med vores Data Enablement-team om at udvikle dataløsninger og omsætte forretningens behov til konkrete data- og analyseleverancer.
Udvikler og videreudvikler Power BI-appen “Clever Erhverv” samt andre rapporterings- og analyseværktøjer, så de bidrager til bedre beslutninger for forretningen.
Sparrer og rådgiver om data for vores produkt-, salgs- og supportteams.
Aarbejder sammen med vores kvalitative indsigtsrolle for at skabe et stærkt beslutningsgrundlag på tværs af kvantitative og kvalitative indsigter.



Dit team

Du bliver en del af teamet Forretningsudvikling for Erhverv. Vi er et udviklingsteam på ni personer med kompetencer inden for økonomiske og kvalitative indsigter, produktudvikling samt salgs- og erhvervsstrategi. Du kommer til at arbejde tæt sammen med den kvalitative indsigtsrolle, produktudviklerne og resten af erhvervsforretningen.

Du vil have berøringsflader med mange forskellige interessenter på tværs af erhvervsforretningen og navigere i et udviklende og tempofyldt område, hvor dine kompetencer vil være efterspurgte. Samtidig bliver du en del af et stærkt fagligt fællesskab med Clevers øvrige specialister inden for kvalitative indsigter, hvor vi sparrer og vidensdeler.

Hvem er du?

Når vi forestiller os vores kommende kollega, ser vi dig, der har:


Fokus på beslutninger frem for rapporter og motiveres af at hjælpe forretningen med at forudse udviklingen og handle på den – ikke blot at visualisere historiske data.
Tekniske dybde for at indgå i en kvalificeret dialog med vores Data Enablement-team.
Kendskab til statistiske og datavidenskabelige metoder, kan arbejde i SQL og Databricks og kan vurdere kvaliteten af dataløsninger, selvom du ikke selv bygger dem.
Erfaring med Power BI og Excel (DAX er en fordel), og ser værktøjerne som middel til at skabe bedre beslutninger.
Stærke formidlingsevner til at oversætte mellem data og forretning og skabe struktur og klarhed for både tekniske og ikke-tekniske interessenter.
Lyst til at drive udvikling og være med til at løfte, hvordan vi arbejder datadrevet i Erhverv.



Vi er ekstra begejstrede, hvis du lytter aktivt, arbejder analytisk og kan skabe struktur og klarhed i komplekse dialoger.

Medledelseskompetencer


Du holder dit ego i skak og ved, at succes handler om at løfte i flok mod fælles mål  
Du tager naturligt ansvar for både dig selv og dine kollegaer, med omsorg og nærvær  
Du er en holdspiller, der ser arbejde som et samarbejde, ikke en konkurrence  



Har du sat mentale flueben hele vejen igennem?

Send gerne din ansøgning afsted allerede i dag – for vi kan godt finde på at lukke opslaget ned før tid, hvis vi møder det rigtige match. Vi håber, at vi gennem din ansøgning kan lære dig at kende og se, hvad du kan bidrage med. Hvis du bruger AI-værktøjer til inspiration, er det fint, men husk at personliggøre den for at skille dig ud.

Vi holder en lang velfortjent sommerferie væk fra computeren og er tilbage i uge 33, hvor du kan forvente at høre fra os. Hvis du har nogle spørgsmål i mellem tiden, så kontakt os på rekruttering@clever.dk .

Du kan læse mere om, hvordan det er at arbejde hos os på: www.clever.dk/velkommen eller vores Instagramkonto @liveticlever.

Er du nysgerrig på vores rekrutteringsproces kan du læse mere om den her: Rekrutteringsprocessen i Clever | Clever .

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig!

Title: ESG-analytiker til Kredit
Place: Aabenraa, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Vil du arbejde i krydsfeltet mellem data, regulering og bæredygtighed og være med til at forme, hvordan vi arbejder med ESG i praksis i bankens Kreditområde?

Om stillingen

Som ESG-analytiker får du en central rolle i arbejdet med bankens ESG-data og rapportering. Stillingen indebærer arbejde med SQL og Excel samt udvikling af værktøjer og processer, der understøtter bankens rådgivere og styrker bankens ESG-indsats.

Nogle af dine primære opgaver vil være:


Udarbejde intern og ekstern ESG-rapportering i samarbejde med relevante interne områder samt eksterne aktører, herunder revision
Bidrage til bankens rapportering i henhold til EU-taksonomien og CSRD
Bearbejde og strukturere ESG-data, herunder datagrundlaget for bankens grønne obligationer
Bidrage til at rapportering er korrekt, rettidig og med den højeste datakvalitet
Udarbejde analyser af ESG-forhold og deres betydning for bankens kunder
Fungere som aktivt bindeled mellem analytikere og ESG-fagspecialister
For at kunne lykkes i rollen er det nødvendigt, at du kan kommunikere flydende på dansk, både mundtligt og skriftligt, da arbejdet foregår i en dansksproget kontekst.


Du bliver en del af et stærkt fagligt miljø, hvor du samarbejder tæt med ESG-specialister, kredit-analytikere samt kolleger inden for regnskab, risiko og intern revision.

Stillingen giver dig stor indflydelse på, hvordan vi arbejder med ESG-data, processer og rapportering, og hvordan dette skal struktureres og udvikles fremadrettet. Det giver derfor mulighed for at præge udviklingen i et felt under konstant forandring og med stigende regulatoriske krav

Om dig

Du trives med at arbejde datadrevet og analytisk, og motiveres af at omsætte komplekse krav til konkrete løsninger.

Vi forestiller os, at du:


Har en relevant videregående uddannelse (fx økonomi, erhvervsjura, ingeniør eller lignende)
Har erfaring med datadrevet rapportering og databehandling i Excel og/eller SQL
Har kendskab til eller interesse for ESG-forhold, f.eks. GHG-protokollen, EU Taksonomien og/eller CSRD
Har stærke analytiske kompetencer, hvor du arbejder struktureret og metodisk med fokus på datakvalitet og validering
Motiveres af at arbejde på tværs af fagligheder og indgå i samarbejder på tværs af organisationen


Derudover er det vigtigt, at du:


Er nysgerrig og løsningsorienteret
Tager ansvar for dine opgaver og motiveres af at følge dem til dørs
Har lyst til at arbejde i et område under udvikling, hvor rammerne ikke altid er fastlagte
Kan håndtere opgaver med korte deadlines i et felt med stor bevågenhed


Hvis du vil være med til at udvikle bankens ESG-arbejde og arbejde tæt på både data, forretning og regulering, så er dette en spændende mulighed for dig.

Om virksomheden

AL Sydbank er blandt Danmarks største banker med hovedsæde i Aabenraa. Vores mål er at skabe økonomisk tryghed og udvikling – for kunder, lokalsamfund og hinanden. Hos os bliver du en del af et fællesskab med mere end 4.000 kolleger i Danmark og Nordtyskland. Vi tror på, at stærke relationer skaber de bedste resultater, og derfor bygger vi vores kultur på samarbejde, korte beslutningsveje, høj faglighed og et fælles fokus på at gøre tingene effektivt og ordentligt.

Spørgsmål

Ansøgningsfristen er 9. august og vi forventer at afholde samtaler i uge 34 og 35.

Arbejdspladsen kan være bankens hovedsæde i Aabenraa eller i AL Sydbank huset på Sluseholmen i København. Man skal som ansøger være indstillet på, at der forventes at være rejseaktivitet mellem Aabenraa og kontoret på Sluseholmen.

Har du spørgsmål til stillingen, er du i uge 28-29 velkommen til at kontakte senior ESG-konsulent Stine Quorning på tlf. 21245496. I uge 30-32 kan du kontakte afdelingschef Kenneth Breinholt Sejling på tlf. 20318417.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Fredericia, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Do you want to help build scalable and high-quality data solutions? Join us as a Data Engineer and contribute to the development of data products that support business decisions across DLG.

About The Position

As a Data Engineer, y ou will contribute to the development and maintenance of our data platform and data pipelines. Working closely with experienced engineers and stakeholders, you will help deliver reliable, scalable, and well-structured data solutions that support business needs.

You will be part of an environment where collaboration, continuous learning, and quality are highly valued. While working with an established architecture and engineering framework, you will have the opportunity to develop your technical skills and grow your expertise in data engineering and cloud-based data platforms.

In this role you will

As our Data Engineer, you will work with the design, development, and maintenance of data pipelines and data products on Databricks. You will contribute to building solutions that are reliable, maintainable, and aligned with industry-leading engineering standards.

Your Key Responsibilities Will Include


Develop and maintain data pipelines using Python, PySpark, and/or SQL
Support data ingestion processes across batch and streaming workloads
Implement data quality checks and validation processes within existing frameworks
Contribute to CI/CD processes and follow established data engineering development practices
Work with data governance principles, including data classification, access control, and compliance requirements
Collaborate with engineers and stakeholders to deliver high-quality data solutions and resolve technical challenges


About you

You are a data engineer with a focus on building reliable and well-structured data pipelines. You are comfortable working within an established architecture and contributing to the delivery of high-quality data solutions as part of a collaborative engineering team.

You have a curious mindset and take pride in delivering maintainable solutions. You communicate proactively, collaborate effectively with others, and are comfortable raising questions or challenges when needed.

We Imagine That You


Are proficient in Python and SQL
Have experience working with Lakehouse or medallion architecture principles
Have experience with data ingestion patterns, including batch and/or streaming workloads
Are comfortable using Git or similar version control tools
Have a solid understanding of CI/CD principles and data development practices
Have hands-on experience with Databricks and understand data quality and governance concepts
Communicate fluently in English, both written and spoken


Furthermore, It Is An Advantage If You


Have experience with Databricks Asset Bundles / Declarative Automation Bundles (DABs) or similar deployment and configuration tooling
Have worked with streaming technologies such as Auto Loader, Kafka, Event Hubs, or similar solutions
Have experience working with Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud Platform (GCP)


What we offer

At DLG, you will join a collaborative and ambitious organisation where data plays an increasingly important role in supporting business decisions and creating value across the business.

We offer a flexible and supportive work environment with excellent opportunities for professional development, knowledge sharing, and career growth. You will work closely with experienced colleagues and become part of a culture characterized by collaboration, trust, and continuous improvement.

Practical information

The position is full-time and based in either Fredericia or Flensburg. Start date is expected to be in October/November.

Please submit your CV - applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis. We expect to invite for the first round of interviews in the beginning of August.

If you have any questions that are not answered in the above, please contact Head of Data Engineering, Thomas Nielsen, at

Title: Student Worker within Business Intelligence/Enabling
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Could you see yourself take part in delivering automated digital solutions that enable leaders to have easy overview of program management, project progress and how projects support the strategy? Combined with building KPI structure for progress and follow up on daily, weekly and monthly basis?

You will get a fantastic opportunity to help visualize how management brings strategy to life.

Buckle up and get yourself energized for a student worker journey, full of development opportunities within Portfolio Management and Business Enabling. Bring out your sharpest analytical instruments, find your inner data driven spirit, pack-up your best communication skills to take the role as student worker.


Core Responsibilities





Design and develop Project Portfolio reports in Power app and/or BI.
Support the Project Portfolio and data tracking on our projects
Create KPI structure in Power app and/or BI
Support on data analysis and visualizations
Develop and maintain Project Portfolio Management System
Drive collaboration and development with internal stakeholders




Play your part in our team succeeding

Through the use of knowledge, processes, data, statistics and standards we validate technologies and platforms for the global industrialized production set-up of the business.

The overall focus of the Mould Qualification and Assembly organisation is to deliver best-in-class mould solutions—driving excellence in both development and industrialization. Our scope includes





1. Mould assembly and disassembly, with a focus on sustainability
2. Mould and process qualification, including new technologies and methods
3. Continuous improvement through data-driven decision-making




We are proud to be a trusted partner across the value chain, with a strong focus on delivering productivity, stability, and high-quality solutions to support global manufacturing.

We want to enhance our digital cabilities to develop our program and KPI structure for the future.

The objective of our Development function is to provide a structured program management and KPI overviews for our leaders showing how single projects are progressing (KPI structure included) and support the strategy.

The purpose of this position is to enable leader in Mould Qualficitation Development to have easy access to KPI’s, project data, progress and how projects support our strategy.

Do you have what it takes?





Bachelor’s or Master student in an IT or business related field.
Advanced Excel incl. python.
Knowledge of Power BI, PowerApps and Power Automate.
Experienced with AI.
Knowledge about Lean Six Sigma, incl. Data foundation and Data analysis.
Knowledge about Machine Learning.
Knowledge within quality systems.
Good understanding of SharePoint.
Solid process development skills enabled by strong structure and organization skills.
Ability to communicate complex/detailed information in a simple and visual way – fluent English.
Customer driven and service minded.
Person must thrive juggling multiple tasks in a dynamic environment with many stakeholders, have a good sense of humor and an accommodating personality.
Knowledge on portfolio management and portfolio analysis/reporting.
Ability to combine and reuse knowledge into new ideas.
Ability to take initiative and act on opportunities.
Cross-functional collaboration.




Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Welwyn Garden City, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Join Our Team as a Data Analyst at PayPoint

Are you ready to embark on an exciting journey where you'll turn data into actionable insights? PayPoint is seeking a talented individual to join our team and play a crucial role in understanding customer behavior, product performance, and retailer trends across all our products and business areas.




The team work in a Hybrid model, 3 days per week onsite at our Welwyn Garden City office.




Why choose PayPoint?

At PayPoint, we're committed to fostering an inclusive culture where everyone can thrive and contribute to our success. As a Data Analyst, you'll have the opportunity to make a real impact by translating data into meaningful insights that drive decision-making processes and support business growth.




What will you be doing?

Your role will involve a variety of responsibilities, including:

Building fully automated reports to provide valuable insights to stakeholders and support decision-making.
Tracking key performance indicators (KPIs) across the business to ensure effective monitoring and analysis.
Collaborating with stakeholders and the Business Intelligence team to identify improvement opportunities and propose data-driven solutions.
Developing dynamic reporting that effectively communicates how our customers or clients interact with our business.
Evaluating the effectiveness of data sources and data-gathering techniques to enhance analysis and reporting.
Staying up-to-date with the latest technology, techniques, and methods in data analysis.




What would we like from you?

Hands on experience dedicated to Power BI report development
Deep, practical understanding of DAX evaluation context, variables and performance-tuning techniques
Proficiency in data manipulation and programming in SQL.
Excellent communication and presentation skills.
Analytical mindset with excellent problem-solving skills.
Ability to understand business requirements and effectively communicate results.
Strong teamworking skills and a collaborative approach.
Attention to detail and a passion for prioritising customer needs.
A positive can-do attitude.




It would be great if you already have…

Salesforce reporting experience.
Understanding of ETL processes.
Knowledge of customer segmentation techniques and predictive analytics.
Experience in campaign and impact analysis.
Knowledge of best practices within SQL and Statistical Programming Languages (Python/R).




What can we do for you

We offer a range of benefits, including:

Holiday purchase scheme, with 25 days holiday plus bank holidays.
On-site gym (free) and nationwide corporate rate gym membership.
Online benefits portal offering discounts on shopping and holidays.
Love2Shop Everyday Benefits Card.
Contributory company pension scheme.
Progression and Development.
Private medical insurance and life assurance (with option purchase additional cover
Additional benefits available at a discounted rate.
Cycle2Work scheme
Electric Car Scheme




At PayPoint, we're committed to creating an inclusive culture where everyone can thrive and feel a sense of belonging. PayPoint is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation, or age.

Join us in shaping the future of technology at PayPoint. Apply today and embark on an exciting journey with us!

Title: Data & Analytics Analyst
Place: Kingston Upon Thames, England, United Kingdom
Description:
Manpower are currently seeking an interim Data & Analytics Analyst to work with our global FMCG client The Magnum Ice Cream Company, renowned for brands such as Magnum, Ben & Jerry's, Cornetto and Wall's, and become an integral part of their fast-paced FMCG environment.




The position is based in Kingston upon Thames, Surrey. This is a full-time temporary role to run for 6 months, requiring 36.25 hours per week, Monday to Friday. Compensation for this role is competitive paying up to £52,500 per annum, pro rata, depending on experience.




The role currently offers a mix of remote and onsite working, subject to adjustment based on business requirements.




Data & Analytics Analyst – Wholesaler Sales

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is on a mission to create the ultimate snacking company. A place where growth, innovation, founder and ownership mentality, and performance are cultivated and rewarded. And where we craft extraordinary ice cream experiences - transforming ordinary moments into lasting memories.

Because we know, life tastes better with ice cream.




ABOUT THE MAGNUM ICE CREAM COMPANY

The Magnum Ice Cream Company is all about growth. Growing our business. Growing our customers’ businesses. Growing our people’s careers. Growth begins with empowerment. So, we free our people to be innovative, responsible entrepreneurs, driven and equipped to give our consumers more amazing products and unforgettable moments – and having fun doing it.

With 19,000 expert ice cream colleagues and iconic brands like Wall’s, Cornetto and Ben & Jerry’s, loved in 76 countries, we are the world’s largest Ice Cream company leading the industry.




Position Overview

We’re looking for a Data & Analytics Analyst to unlock the value of wholesaler and customer sales data across Magnum’s UK business.

You will play a critical role in connecting multiple wholesaler data sources, working with Databricks and pipelines and transforming raw sales data into clear, actionable insights. This includes enabling visibility on sell-in, sell-out, distribution, range, and performance across wholesale partners.

You will work closely with data engineers to ensure data is ingested, structured, and ready for analysis, while acting as a Power BI super user to deliver dashboards that drive commercial decisions across sales, account management, and operations.




Key Responsibilities

Connect & Integrate Wholesaler Data

Connect and visualise multiple wholesaler sales data sources (including but not limited to EPOS, shipment/sell-in, CRM, partner data)
Work with Databricks to query, review and report on large-scale sales datasets
Connect to and leverage data pipelines to access reliable, up-to-date wholesaler data
Partner with data engineers to onboard new wholesale partners and data feeds
Ensure consistent, accurate mapping of SKU, customer and channel data




Data Ingestion & Pipeline Collaboration

Support and advise on data ingestion processes for wholesaler and partner data to ensure you have the information required for reporting usage
Validate and sense-check incoming data to ensure accuracy and completeness
Help ensure data pipelines deliver timely, structured, analysis-ready datasets




Data Visualisation & Commercial Insights

Act as a Power BI super user, building dashboards focused on wholesale performance and sales performance
Deliver reporting on key commercial metrics (e.g. sales trends, distribution, range compliance, customer performance)
Create intuitive data visualisations for sales and account teams
Translate complex sales data into clear insights and actions
Maintain and improve existing dashboards to meet business needs
Identify opportunities to utilise new technology to present data in the best way
Strong commercial experience designing and implementing Power BI solutions.
Proven expertise in data modelling, data transformation and report development.
Experience creating dashboards, data visualisations and management information reporting.
Strong understanding of KPI and OKR reporting frameworks
Enable self-serve analytics for commercial teams




Set Us Up for Success

Support scalable solutions that accommodate new wholesalers and growing data complexity
Contribute to data documentation, definitions and data dictionaries
Ensure high standards of data quality & governance
Identify opportunities to improve data coverage & accuracy




Business Partnering & Opportunity Identification

Work closely with sales, account managers and CRM teams to answer key business questions
Identify trends and opportunities within wholesaler performance and distribution
Proactively highlight risks and opportunities (e.g. gaps in distribution, declining accounts, growth pockets)
Translate business needs into analytical solutions




Required Skills

Advanced Power BI expertise (data modelling, DAX, dashboard delivery)
Experience working with Databricks (or similar) (data querying, transformation of large data sets)
Strong SQL skills and ability to work with large, complex sales datasets
Experience with data pipelines and ingestion processes
Proven ability to connect and combine multiple data sources
Experience working with sales, wholesaler, or FMCG data is highly desirable
Strong understanding of commercial metrics and reporting
Experience collaborating with data engineers and non-technical stakeholders




Key Attributes

Commercially minded with strong curiosity for sales performance drivers
Detail-oriented with a strong focus on data accuracy and consistency
Proactive and solutions-driven
Ability to manage conflicting priorities and short deadlines
Strong communicator, able to translate data into actionable business insight
Experience within large-scale transformation programmes, change initiatives or application modernisation projects would be advantageous.
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills.
Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities.

Title: Data Analyst
Place: Newcastle Upon Tyne, England, United Kingdom
Description:
BMC Recruitment Group are delighted to be supporting a leading financial services and pensions organisation in the search for a Data Solutions Analyst to join its growing Pension Operations team.




The successful candidate will play a key role in providing technical expertise across pension administration functions while driving efficiencies, maintaining regulatory compliance, and supporting continuous improvement initiatives.




Key Benefits they offer:

Salary £32,492
Full-time permanent position
Working Hours - Monday to Friday 8:30am - 5:30pm (1hr Lunch)
Hybrid Working following training - 2 days from home - 3 days office based
Free on-site parking
33 days annual leave, including bank holidays (increases with length of service)
Company pension contribution (increases with length of service)
Company/team events throughout the year




The Role




As a Data Solutions Analyst, you will act as a technical specialist within Pension Operations, providing support across administration teams and working closely with colleagues to deliver exceptional service standards.




The role combines pensions expertise, operational support, process improvement, data analysis, and stakeholder liaison, making it ideal for someone who enjoys both technical and operational responsibilities.




Key Responsibilities

Provide technical and operational support across pension administration functions.
Act as a subject matter expert for pension-related processes and complex casework.
Support the completion of pension administration activities, ensuring accuracy and efficiency.
Monitor regulatory and procedural changes, implementing updates where required.
Assist with compliance and regulatory reporting requirements.
Act as a key point of contact for technical pension queries from internal stakeholders.
Identify opportunities for process improvements and increased efficiencies.
Investigate and resolve complex pension enquiries through to completion.
Assist with pension calculations, reviews, system updates, and operational improvements.
Ensure service level agreements and quality standards are consistently achieved.

About You

To be successful in this role, you will ideally have:

Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills.
Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy.
Experience reviewing processes and implementing operational improvements.
Ability to work effectively with stakeholders across different teams.
Excellent communication and organisational skills.
A proactive and solution-focused approach.
Experience mentoring or supporting junior colleagues would be advantageous.

Title: Director, Business Intelligence
Description:
Who We Are



Cint is a pioneer in research technology (ResTech). Our customers use the Cint platform to post questions and get answers from real people to build business strategies, confidently publish research, accurately measure the impact of digital advertising, and more. The Cint platform is built on a programmatic marketplace, which is the world’s largest, with nearly 300 million respondents in over 150 countries who consent to sharing their opinions, motivations, and behaviours.







We are feeding the world’s curiosity!





Job Description



Cint is looking for a strategic Director of Business Intelligence to lead the next phase of its data evolution. This leader will be responsible for empowering Cint’s global organization with the tools and real-time insights that drive platform performance, commercial strategy and operational efficiency.



Our vision is to move beyond reporting into a world of intelligent automation, AI-driven insights, and strategic value creation. You will lead the transition to a scalable, self-service ecosystem that empowers every department to make impactful, real-time decisions.



Responsibilities



Define and execute the BI strategy, shifting the organization from reactive reporting to a proactive, "intelligence-first" mindset that drives faster and better business decisions.



Partner with senior stakeholders across Finance, Sales, Customer Experience, Trust & Safely, Marketing and Product to align the BI roadmap with global goals.



Define and operationalize enterprise KPIs and executive-level dashboards to ensure consistent measurement of business performance and accountability.



Oversee the evolution of our modern cloud data stack, ensuring it is optimized for high-performance querying, cost-efficiency, scalability and reliability.



Unify enterprise data models across the organization to eliminate fragmented silos and ensure a single, governed, and trusted "Source of Truth."



Maintain ownership of data cataloging, quality, and governance standards while ensuring BI platforms and data assets comply with security, privacy, and regulatory requirements across regions.



Establish rigorous data quality, observability, and reliability standards to build sustained trust in analytics and reporting.



Own vendor relationships and the BI budget, focusing on Total Cost of Ownership and Databricks compute efficiency.



Oversee and ensure timely delivery of month-end data while implementing automation to streamline processes and improve long-term efficiency.



Champion the use of AI and advanced analytics to automate insights, enhance forecasting, and surface proactive recommendations for the business.



Lead the transition to modern BI tools, empowering teams to explore data independently while maintaining governance.



Lead organizational change management and data literacy initiatives to drive adoption of self-service BI and analytics across the business.



Lead and mentor a global team of engineer and analyst professionals, transitioning the function from manual data processing to high-value strategic innovation.





Qualifications



Qualifications Required:



Skilled at leading distributed, high-performing teams through organizational change, platform migrations, and tool modernizations, while fostering a culture of collaboration, data literacy, and innovation.



Proven track record of leading global BI functions within technology companies.



Deep understanding of modern data stacks, specifically Databricks.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Overall Ambition




Nando’s is on a journey to Create Lasting Happiness, making a difference to the lives of our people, our customers, and the communities we work in. Originally started in Johannesburg, South Africa, we pride ourselves on creating memorable experiences for everyone involved in Nando’s: from our employees, communities, founders, and suppliers, but most importantly, our customers.

Whilst we always look back on our history and story with pride, the story is still evolving. We have recently been growing our digital capabilities to ensure the Nando’s experience remains relevant to modern-day customers.




You will be joining a tech team that has grown rapidly in the past five years and is determined to enable change within the restaurant industry by bringing innovative thinking and the latest technology tools and practices to solve some of its most complex problems.







The Data and Analysis Team

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our Data and Analysis team. Our remit is to ensure everyone has access to the right information, at the right time, and that Nando’s gets the best value out of our data to achieve our business goals.




Our current focus is extracting insights from the data and creating a single source of accurate, timely data as we continue to increase our data maturity and advance our predictive analytic capability and explore the role of AI in the business.




You will be part of our work to support our strategic goals and build a data-informed attitude for addressing our opportunities and challenges.







Our Values

At Nando’s, our values guide how we work, make decisions, and show up for each other every day:

Pride – We take pride in everything we do, from the quality of our food to the impact of our work.
Passion – We bring energy, enthusiasm, and a love for what we do.
Courage – We speak up, challenge the status quo, and are not afraid to try new things.
Integrity – We do the right thing, act honestly, and build trust with each other.
Family – We care for and support one another, creating a sense of belonging where everyone can thrive.







What we're looking for:

As a Data Analyst you will work across a wide range of domains from financial and operational data, to kitchen tech and stock, helping our teams make better decisions grounded in sound analysis. As a curious problem-solver, you will be able to move between the technical and the commercial; and be comfortable building data models as well as presenting findings to senior stakeholders.




In this role you’ll work independently across the full data stack, understanding the ‘why’ behind requests before jumping to the ‘what’, and contributing to the translation of business problems into well-defined, actionable work. You’ll be supported by a collaborative team and won’t be expected to deliver everything solo from day one, but you’ll be expected to grow into doing so.

You will…







Write and maintain SQL models (in Dataform) to transform and serve reliable data across our pipelines
Support data engineers in the design, building and testing of data pipelines, able to contribute meaningfully, not just consume the output
Build and iterate on cloud-based reports and dashboards in Looker (or similar BI tool), giving our support teams and restaurants clear, actionable insight into performance
Translate business questions into well-scoped analysis and understand the problem and domain before reaching for the answer
Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, choosing the right medium for the audience
Peer review colleagues’ work and contribute to data quality standards and documentation
Identify opportunities to automate manual processes and data tasks, and take the initiative to implement them







Skills required

You will have proven experience in the following:

Writing complex SQL to query, transform and model data across our cloud data platform (window functions, CTEs, complex datatypes)
Building and maintaining data models in Dataform, or a similar data transformation tool such as Snowflake or DBT, with an understanding of how they fit into the broader pipeline
Building reports and dashboards in Looker or a similar BI tool, serving reliable, self-serve data to the business
Using Git as standard practice — branching, pull requests and code review
Contributing to the translation of business requests into clearly scoped, actionable work items
Applying data quality techniques — writing tests, identifying issues proactively and communicating data limitations clearly
Documenting data models, definitions and lineage to a production standard
Understanding data governance principles and championing good data management practice within the team
Applying and understanding when to use relevant statistical methods to support analysis
Supporting data engineers in designing, coding and testing data processing pipelines
Using AI-assisted coding tools (e.g. Claude) to improve the speed and quality of your work — critically, not blindly — with an awareness of responsible AI use and data governance implications







You will also display

Clear communication with both technical and non-technical stakeholders, able to explain complex findings in plain language and tell the story behind the data
Genuine intellectual curiosity, actively exploring new tools and methods, including AI, and sharing what you learn with the team
Comfort with ambiguity, able to define the right question and find the “so what” before jumping to answer
A product mindset, understanding the business objective behind every piece of analysis, not just the request on the surface
A proactive approach to improving processes, data quality and ways of working, with the initiative to make it happen, not just flag it
A collaborative team attitude, peer reviewing others’ work, sharing knowledge and contributing to a stronger team




Inclusion

At Nando’s, everyone is welcome. Inspired by our Southern African heritage we know and value the richness that diversity brings to Nando’s. We’re committed to creating an inclusive and respectful culture for all; including our existing and future Nandocas, partners and suppliers, customers and the communities we are a part of. We deliberately work hard to create an environment where every individual is valued, respected and can flourish regardless of who they are, their background or outlook on life.




We welcome applications from a diverse range of individuals and will consider any reasonable adjustments in order to enable candidates to perform as well as possible during the recruitment process.

Title: Data Analyst, Business Intelligence
Description:
As a Data Analyst in our Business Intelligence team at AgileOne, you will be responsible for designing and delivering data-driven insights that support internal teams and global MSP clients.




Working closely with stakeholders across the business, you will build impactful dashboards, develop data models, and translate complex workforce data into clear, actionable insights that drive decision-making and performance







Our Requirement

Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards and reports for internal and client ue
Develop interactive visualizations to highlight trends and key workforce metrics
Build and maintain data models (e.g. star schemas, fact and dimension tables)
Create complex DAX measures and calculations for reporting and analytics
Extract, transform, and load data using SQL and Power Query (M)
Ensure data accuracy and consistency across multiple systems
Optimize report performance and usability (UX, drill-throughs, automation, etc.)
Collaborate with data engineering and infrastructure teams
Support automation of reporting processes (e.g. Power Automate)
Document data sources, logic, and reporting standards




Your Profile:

Experience working as a Data Analyst / BI Analyst (preferably within MSP, staffing, or workforce solutions environment
Good understanding of workforce data, including metrics such as spend, bill rates, headcount, tenure, compliance, and supplier performance
Familiarity with VMS tools and data structures (e.g. Fieldglass, Beeline, VNDLY) is highly valued
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI (Desktop & Service)
Advanced knowledge of DAX, Power Query (M), andSQL
Solid understanding of data modeling and analytics best practices
Experience working with large and complex datasets
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
High attention to detail and data accuracy
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Strong communication skills and ability to present data clearly
Comfortable working independently and collaboratively




What We Offer

A collaborative, international work environment with a global leader in MSP/VMS services
Remote work
Mental Wellbeing support, Nectar points, Medical coverage
Pension scheme and Employee referral awards
Supportive leadership and opportunity for long-term career growth

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst | £60k + Bonus | West London - 3 days a week in the office




This is a Data Analyst role in an exciting, fast paced FTSE company, which will focus on helping the business make critical decisions using cutting-edge data analytics.




You’ll partner with core business areas to deliver insights, dashboards, reporting and analytical projects that directly influence business performance and strategy.




Its paying £45-50k and it would suit someone who has a minimum of 1.5 years experience in a Data Analyst role.




Main Responsibilities:




Partner with a core business area to understand data, reporting, visualisation, and analytics needs.
Assess and prioritise analytics project requests.
Work with stakeholders to define project requirements and objectives.
Deliver analysis and insights to support data-driven decisions.
Support the management of dashboards, visualisations, and data sources.
Design and develop analytics solutions for business needs.
Recommend effective approaches to solve analytical challenges.
Break projects into tasks, estimate timelines, and identify risks.
Support stakeholder engagement and communicate analytical approaches clearly.




Qualifications & Experience

Degree qualified
Minimum 1.5 years’ experience in a Data Analyst role
SQL experience
Tableau and/or Power BI experience nice to have but not essential
Hands-on programming/coding experience (education or industry)
Experience manipulating datasets and writing technical code
Strong communication skills with the ability to partner with stakeholders across the business
