Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Location: Manchester, UK (Hybrid – Tuesday, Wednesday & Thursday Onsite)

Day Rate: £425 per day (Inside IR35)

Contract Duration: 12 Months




Overview

We are supporting a leading global organisation in their search for an experienced Data Analyst to join their European data function on a 12-month contract.




This role will play a key part in supporting strategic business initiatives through data-driven insights, reporting, and analytics. Working closely with business and technology stakeholders, you will help deliver new data capabilities, enhance reporting platforms, and provide analytical support for both strategic programmes and day-to-day business operations.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, validate, and analyse large datasets from multiple sources.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, Excel, and other BI tools.
Translate business requirements into analytical solutions and actionable insights.
Perform exploratory data analysis (EDA) to identify trends, opportunities, and process improvements.
Deliver ad hoc analysis to support business and operational decision-making.
Monitor KPIs and provide regular reporting to key stakeholders.
Develop and optimise SQL queries, data models, and reporting solutions.
Identify and resolve data quality issues in collaboration with relevant teams.
Support forecasting, trend analysis, and predictive modelling initiatives.
Ensure compliance with data governance, security, and privacy standards.
Document analytical methodologies, data definitions, and reporting processes.
Present findings and recommendations to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Drive continuous improvements through reporting automation and process optimisation.




Essential Skills & Experience

Proven commercial experience as a Data Analyst.
Strong SQL and Python skills for data querying and analysis.
Experience building dashboards using Power BI and/or Tableau.
Strong analytical and quantitative skills with the ability to interpret complex datasets.
Experience translating business requirements into meaningful data solutions.
Excellent problem-solving and critical thinking abilities.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
Experience working with cross-functional business and technology teams.
Proactive, self-motivated, and able to work independently in a fast-paced environment.




Desirable Skills

Experience supporting enterprise data platform initiatives.
Exposure to forecasting and predictive analytics.
Understanding of data governance and data privacy principles.
Experience automating reporting processes and workflows.
Previous experience working within large, complex enterprise environments.

About The Role

As a Data Analyst at Sporty, you’ll be mining through petabytes of data to provide key insights that drive core business decisions. You’ll create dashboards that are used on a daily basis by product managers, traders, and executives, as well as provide in-depth reports on various areas of the business with complete freedom on your methodology.

Furthermore, our analysts play a key role in ensuring the stability of our reporting infrastructure. They are vital in maintaining our ability to deliver accurate and consistent reporting to the various stakeholders.

Our Stack


MySQL
Python
AirFlow
AWS
Metabase
Redshift
Linux


What You'll Be Doing


Analysing large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and actionable insights that support business decision-making.
Designing, defining, and monitoring core business KPIs to evaluate product and operational performance.
Partnering with product, marketing, and business teams to translate business questions into structured data analysis.
Delivering clear, concise, and data-driven insights to stakeholders and shareholders through presentations and reports.
Developing and maintaining dashboards and reporting frameworks that enable self-service analytics.
Conducting ad hoc analysis to support strategic initiatives and emerging business needs.
Ensuring data quality and integrity by validating datasets and collaborating with data engineering teams when needed.


What You'll Bring


3+ years experience in a similar data-driven role
Excellent working knowledge of SQL
Familiarity with Python and a range of BI and data visualization tools
Strong knowledge with ETL data pipelines
Strong knowledge of A/B Testing
Familiarity with Airflow
Understanding of principles of relational databases and data warehouse
Strong understanding of sports and gaming will be beneficial
Proactive and self-driven with excellent analytical skills
Ability to thrive in a fast paced and dynamic environment
Great communication skills


Even better if you have


Hands-on experience with machine learning
Relevant knowledge or experience in the gaming industry
Working knowledge of Python
Experience with Airflow and Metabase


What’s In It For You


Sporty is a remote first company in pursuit of sustainability
A competitive salary + individual performance based bonuses every quarter

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Senior Data Analyst – Tax Technology




Base Location: Canary Wharf, London OR Glasgow based (Hybrid – 2 days per week in office)




KPMG UK offices | KPMG UK




The KPMG Tax Technology & Innovation are made up of Technology Consultants, Business Analysts, Product Owners, Data Architects & Engineers, Multi-disciplinary Engineering teams and IT Service delivery professionals working on a large portfolio of systems used to improve productivity and solve key business problems across Tax & Legal.




This business has 2500+ tax & legal professionals covering multiple disciplines. We serve many thousands of clients and private individuals by providing a modern, market-leading suite of technology solutions that provide an efficient digital experience from routine to complex tax advice.




KPMG is one of the world's largest and most respected consultancy businesses, we've supported the UK through times of war and peace, prosperity and recession, political and regulatory upheaval. We've proudly stood beside the institutions and businesses which make the UK what it is.




Why Join the KPMG Tax Technology & Innovation team as a Senior Data Analyst?




Currently KPMG Tax and Legal provide services to some of the world’s largest companies.




KPMG is investing $5bn over 5 years in technology and our goal is to achieve the highest standards of quality and to deliver excellent solutions for our clients.




We are a team of 300+ technologists providing technology enablement services and solutions across the Tax and Legal business. This business has 2,500 tax & legal professionals covering multiple disciplines. We serve many thousands of clients and private individuals by providing a modern, market-leading suite of technology solutions that provide an efficient digital experience from routine to complex tax advice.




Tax is highlighted frequently as an industry where generative AI will have a significant impact on how services are provided. You will be working as a team at the forefront of navigating that change and redefining the way in which tax is determined in the UK and globally, making sure people and companies pay the right amount of tax with the minimum amount of effort.




What will you be doing?




As a Senior Data Analyst in the Tax Technology team, you will be an integral part of a team delivering analytics solutions to clients across KPMG’s Tax & Legal practice. You will be working with a mixture of UK-based and India-based data engineers and data analysts and in this role you will:




Play a key role in the design and implement high quality analytics solutions for internal and external clients.
Work with teams of analysts using Alteryx and related tools to automate a variety of ETL and reporting processes for internal and external clients.
Work with teams of BI Developers to develop visualisations, providing insights to our clients.
Coach and mentor others to help them achieving their potential.
Engage with a range of stakeholders, both internally and clients.




The role can be based anywhere in the UK, though preference will be given to candidates in London. Regular travel to London will be required.




Whilst there is no expectation of existing knowledge of tax, we would expect you to develop a degree of domain knowledge over time.




What will you need to do it?




Previous experience in a client facing / consulting role as a Data Analyst or Analytics Engineer.
Recent experience using advanced analytics to solve complex business problems.
Excellent knowledge of Alteryx, ideally with the Alteryx Designer certification.
Experience manipulating large data sets.
Awareness of data visualisation tools eg. Power BI, Tableau etc.
Experience of SQL Server and/or Azure SQL and an understanding of SQL programming.
Expert problem-solving skills with the ability to logically analyse complex requirements, processes and systems.
Demonstrable experience of being able to engage with a wide range of external client stakeholders, including scoping of requirements, organising workshops and giving client presentations.




Skills we’d love to see/Amazing Extras:




Experience using the Azure data platform; Synapse Analytics, Data Lake, SQL Data Warehouse & Fabric.
Programming experience using R or Python.
Worked with Agile teams using Scrum or the Scaled Agile framework.
Alteryx Advanced or Expert certifications.




To discuss this or wider Technology roles with our recruitment team, all you need to do is apply, create a profile, upload your CV and begin to make your mark with KPMG.




Our Locations:




We are open to talk to talent across the country but preference will be given to candidates who are willing and able to do 2 days per week in one of our core Tech hubs for this role, which are:




Canary Wharf, London




We can potentially facilitate flexible hours, and part-time options. If you have a need for flexibility, please register and discuss this with our team.




Find out more:




Within Tech and Engineering we have a range of divisions and specialisms. Click the links to find out more below:




Technology and Engineering at KPMG: www.kpmgcareers.co.uk/experienced-professional/technology-engineering/

ITs Her Future Women in Tech programme: www.kpmgcareers.co.uk/people-culture/it-s-her-future/

KPMG Workability and Disability confidence: www.kpmgcareers.co.uk/experienced-professional/applying-to-kpmg/need-support-let-us-know

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Hey! We're Huel, and we're pleased to meet you 👋

Our culture thrives on high performance, and we set the bar high for new Hueligans who join us.

We invite you to read the advert below for our new Data Analyst position to understand the role, and explore our 7 Huel values before applying to ensure we have the right working environment for you!




The role

Huel is looking for a Data Analyst to turn raw behavioural and commercial data into clear, actionable insights for Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail teams. You’ll build measurement frameworks, run causal analyses, and create self-serve dashboards, helping the business move fast and make confident, data-driven decisions.




Key responsibilities include:

Owning end-to-end analysis – translate open-ended questions from Marketing, Trading, Product, and Retail into clear analytical briefs, run the analysis, and land unambiguous recommendations.
Designing & evaluating experiments – design A/B tests and causal-inference studies (lift analysis, hold-out tests) and communicate results in plain language.
Defining measurement frameworks – set the right KPIs and establish how new initiatives will be measured, ensuring every launch has a clear success metric and data plan from day one.
Scale our data foundations – team up with Engineering to design efficient, accurate data sets and schemas, define requirements for new data feeds, and automate the checks that keep everything running smoothly.
Championing self-serve analytics – develop and maintain live dashboards, document datasets, and run training sessions so non-analysts can answer routine questions without waiting in a queue.
AI & Automation - Champion the adoption of AI-powered analytics, using AI tools to enhance analysis, automate routine activities, uncover deeper insights, and improve productivity, while enabling colleagues to self-serve trusted data and answer business questions independently.
Spotting growth opportunities – combine behavioural and marketing data with commercial metrics to size new products, markets, and campaigns, prioritising by impact and effort.




This role is based at our HQ in Tring, Hertfordshire. We operate a hybrid working model, with office days on Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays, and the flexibility to work from home on Wednesdays and Fridays.




Not yet sure if this is the right role for you? Click HERE to read a more detailed job description.




What we're looking for in you

We’re looking for a confident, curious analyst who’s comfortable working with statistics, behavioural data, and experiment design. You can move seamlessly between SQL, product analytics tools, and storytelling through slides. You’re proactive, independent, and always looking for high-impact opportunities to raise the bar on how we use data. In a fast-paced environment, you adapt quickly and communicate clearly - collaborating with both technical and non-technical teams to get things done.

SQL – you can write quick exploratory queries or optimise a multi-table join on Snowflake without breaking a sweat.
Product analytics – comfortable in platforms such as Heap, Amplitude or Mixpanel and happy to set up events, funnels and cohorts from scratch.
Statistical – solid on A/B testing, experiment design and the basics of causal inference; you know when a result is worth acting on.
Measurement mindset – you’ve built tracking plans and KPI frameworks that let teams see what “good” looks like before they ship.
Data - experience modelling data in dbt (or similar), automating checks and keeping feeds clean and accurate as they scale.




And as always, we’re on the lookout for new Hueligans who love a fast-paced environment and are always striving to be their best. We want people who aren’t afraid to think differently and push boundaries. If you’re all about setting big goals and believe that amazing things happen through creativity and teamwork, you’ll fit right in at Huel!




What do we offer in return?

We know that at times, our teams face demanding pressures, and exceptional effort deserves meaningful rewards. That's why we've created a world-class perks and benefits program designed to support our Hueligans in achieving their best, both professionally and personally, while celebrating the global impact they're making!




🕖 Hybrid working - We spend Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays in the office together. The remaining two days have the option to be worked from home.

🌴 30 days annual leave PLUS bank holidays

🥤 Free Huel to keep you going

🏖️ 2 weeks a year to work remotely from anywhere!

🐾 Dog friendly

🙋 Paid Volunteering Days

🏋️ Free on-site gym with free classes, and we will give you your own nutrition plan

🧘 Free on-site wellness area offering infrared saunas and an ice bath🧘

🧠 Free 1-on-1 therapy provided by Self-Space.

🏥 Private Medical and Health insurance for you and your loved ones, including free life insurance covering up to 4x your salary

⚡ Electric Car Scheme with onsite charging

🚴Cycle to Work Scheme

🤰 Enhanced Family Leave

👪 Workplace Nursery Scheme

🎓 Huel Academy

💸 Paid Employee Referral Scheme

🎉 Biannual events to celebrate success - Have you heard about Huelchella?




We are Hueligans

We know that diversity isn't just important; it’s essential, and it makes us stronger. We're all about embracing our differences, celebrating what makes us unique, and bringing together Hueligans from all walks of life.

Whilst we all share the 7 values of Huel, it’s our individual differences that truly enhance our culture of belonging. We seek out Hueligans from around the world, encouraging authenticity, diverse views, and fresh ideas to create products that our global customers love.

Meet our teams here.

  • SQL – you can write quick exploratory queries or optimise a multi-table join on Snowflake without breaking a sweat.
  • Product analytics – comfortable in platforms such as Heap, Amplitude or Mixpanel and happy to set up events, funnels and cohorts from scratch.
  • Statistical – solid on A/B testing, experiment design and the basics of causal inference; you know when a result is worth acting on.
  • Measurement mindset – you’ve built tracking plans and KPI frameworks that let teams see what “good” looks like before they ship.
  • Data - experience modelling data in dbt (or similar), automating checks and keeping feeds clean and accurate as they scale.


And as always, we’re on the lookout for new Hueligans who love a fast-paced environment and are always striving to be their best. We want people who aren’t afraid to think differently and push boundaries. If you’re all about setting big goals and believe that amazing things happen through creativity and teamwork, you’ll fit right in at Huel!


What do we offer in return?

We know that at times, our teams face demanding pressures, and exceptional effort deserves meaningful rewards. That's why we've created a world-class perks and benefits program designed to support our Hueligans in achieving their best, both professionally and personally, while celebrating the global impact they're making!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Summary




The Senior Data Analyst supports the organisation's objective of enhancing supervisory effectiveness through better use of entity and regulatory data. The role helps transform how Supervision teams identify risks, prioritise interventions and monitor firms, delivered through data-driven insights, dashboards, analytical tools and supervisory intelligence. It sits within a live testing programme for a regulatory data reporting regime. The analyst contributes to systems acceptance, firm feasibility and supervisory usability of the data being collected. The outcome is greater confidence in the regulatory data build through faster, more thorough testing.




Key Responsibilities




Supporting Supervisory Outcomes: Analyse risk indicators, alerts and supervisory insights to enable risk-based supervision and decision making.
Regulatory Data Analysis: Analyse authorisation, holdings, transactions, legal entity and regulatory reporting data. Create integrated views across firms and entities so supervisors see a coherent picture rather than fragmented sources.
Supervisory Intelligence and Risk Analytics: Identify trends, anomalies, concentrations and emerging risks. Support thematic reviews, quantitative investigations and synthesis of alerts.
Data Quality and Improvement: Assess data quality, implement validation controls and validate legal entity identifiers (LEIs). Enrich core datasets using external sources such as GLEIF, Companies House and market data proxies.
Reporting and Decision Support: Develop Tableau dashboards, management information and self-service analytics for Supervision teams, moving towards reusable, automated reporting.
Business Analysis and Stakeholder Engagement: Gather requirements, facilitate workshops and translate supervisory challenges into analytical solutions. Cohere disparate and conflicting business needs into workable plans through engagement and governance forums.
Future State Capability: Support the move towards automated reporting, advanced analytics, continuous monitoring and early-warning indicators, building a mature supervisory intelligence capability.




Essential Skills Technical




SQL and large-scale data analysis across complex, multi-source datasets.
AWS services such as S3, DynamoDB, Athena, etc.
Data visualisation and dashboarding in Tableau or Power BI.
Data quality assessment, validation and improvement.
Stakeholder management across business and technical audiences.
Regulatory or financial services experience.







Behavioral (must-have)




Highly proactive and self-driven — sets direction and momentum without close supervision.
Strategically astute — operates across different business areas, building knowledge and relationships rapidly.
Adaptable — pivots to address new challenges at short notice as contexts change.
Data-fluent — experience driving data-focused projects with a solid grasp of common data challenges in large organisations.
Coheres competing needs — reconciles disparate business requirements into deliverable plans via engagement, workshops and governance.




Desirable Skills




Familiarity with regulatory or financial data reporting regimes.




Eligibility Criteria




UK Citizen & SC Cleared
3 days mandatory work from London Client Site.










Why Join Us?

We’re a purpose‑driven organisation committed to creating positive impact and championing inclusion.
You’ll join a culture that encourages innovation, collaboration, and continuous growth.
We celebrate difference and believe diverse perspectives lead to stronger solutions.
Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply — great talent comes from a variety of backgrounds.
Your lived experience, unique strengths, and personal story matter — they help us design better outcomes for the communities we serve.
We celebrate diversity of experience, knowledge, backgrounds, and perspectives, and believe these differences enable us to create meaningful impact. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and are committed to fairness and inclusion for all, regardless of sex, race, religion or belief, ethnic or national origin, disability, age, citizenship, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity, pregnancy or related conditions, or any other protected characteristic. If you require any reasonable adjustments or additional support during the recruitment process, please let us know.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Meet the Team

Cisco ThousandEyes is a Digital Assurance platform that empowers organizations to deliver flawless digital experiences across every network – even the ones they don’t own. Powered by AI and an unmatched set of cloud, internet and enterprise network telemetry data, ThousandEyes enables IT teams to proactively detect, diagnose, and remediate issues – before they impact end- user experiences.

ThousandEyes is deeply integrated across the entire Cisco technology portfolio and beyond, helping customers deploy at scale while also delivering AI-powered assurance insights within Cisco’s leading Networking, Security, Collaboration, and Observability portfolios.

Your Impact

You will employ analytical techniques to solve key questions that make the business of Cisco ThousandEyes successful. You will be primarily responsibly to produce statistical reports that covers analyses reporting internet performance for Government customers.


Lead key, cross-functional analytical projects, identifying and bringing together stakeholders/ partners, developing joint analytical approaches, and completing the project.
Collaborate with internal and external partners, including Data Scientists within the company and across clients, on methodology for reporting that performance of ISPs in specific geographies and to explain trends and patterns in the data.
Develop and maintains data pipelines and dashboards to monitor and track trends and changes in the customer data.
Use data to find opportunities and patterns that Government customers may be interested in.
Display deep data wrangling techniques, strong visualization skills to depict/ present narratives that identify growth opportunities.


Minimum Qualifications


Strong R for production reporting pipelines: tidyverse, multi-file project navigation, debugging across pipeline stages, and package/version management with renv.
SQL for analytical querying against BigQuery and MariaDB via bigrquery, DBI, and RMariaDB.
Literate programming and reporting: R Markdown and Quarto rendering to Word, chart/table formatting with ggplot2/flextable/officedown, and reproducible quarterly output.
Statistical judgment: interpreting confidence intervals, weighted means, trimmed means, and sample size limitations - knowing when a result is technically accurate but misleading.
Report writing for a regulatory audience: clear, detailed, defensible prose with consistent terminology, informative tone, and editorial judgment that AI drafting alone can't replace.
Attention to detail in published outputs: catching mislabelled figures, wrong baselines, numbers that don't sum, and chart axes that obscure trends.
Data management across multiple systems: BigQuery, GCS, SharePoint/OneDrive, and local R sessions with no automated orchestration -- manual, script-by-script execution requiring care and rigor.
Comfortable using AI coding tools (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) as a force-multiplier for pipeline maintenance, debugging, drafting, and routine code changes.


Preferred Qualifications


Experience with broadband measurement, telecom/ISP data, or network performance concepts.
Spatial data basics in R.
Awareness of PII and data confidentiality constraints.
Shiny for small internal tools.


Why Cisco?

At Cisco, we’re revolutionizing how data and infrastructure connect and protect organizations in the AI era – and beyond. We’ve been innovating fearlessly for 40 years to create solutions that power how humans and technology work together across the physical and digital worlds. These solutions provide customers with unparalleled security, visibility, and insights across the entire digital footprint.

Fueled by the depth and breadth of our technology, we experiment and create meaningful solutions. Add to that our worldwide network of doers and experts, and you’ll see that the opportunities to grow and build are limitless. We work as a team, collaborating with empathy to make really big things happen on a global scale. Because our solutions are everywhere, our impact is everywhere.

We are Cisco, and our power starts with you.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analyst (Customer Insights) | Banking

Location: Remote with occasional travel to Milton Keynes

Rate: £600 per day (Inside IR35)

Duration: Initial 10-month contract




We're looking for an experienced Senior Data Analyst to join a leading UK financial services organisation on an initial 10-month contract.




This role is ideal for an analyst who thrives on turning complex customer data into meaningful insights that influence business decisions. You'll work closely with stakeholders to identify customer opportunities, build insightful reporting, and provide data-driven recommendations that shape strategy and improve business outcomes.




This is an insight-led analytical role rather than a data engineering or data modelling position.

The Role




You'll be responsible for analysing customer and business data to uncover trends, identify opportunities and deliver clear, actionable recommendations. Working with business stakeholders across the organisation, you'll translate analytical findings into insights that drive customer engagement, commercial performance and strategic decision-making.




Key Responsibilities

Analyse customer and business data to identify trends, opportunities and actionable insights.
Produce high-quality reports, dashboards and presentations that support business decision-making.
Identify and develop customer segmentation strategies to support targeted campaigns and business initiatives.
Partner with business stakeholders to understand challenges and translate requirements into analytical solutions.
Present complex analytical findings in a clear, engaging and commercially meaningful way.
Support continuous improvement of reporting and analytical processes using modern data tools.
Use AI tools to improve analytical productivity and streamline day-to-day workflows where appropriate.




Essential Skills & Experience

Proven experience as a Data Analyst or Insight Analyst delivering business-focused analytical solutions.
Excellent SQL skills for data extraction, manipulation and analysis.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
Strong experience producing business reports, dashboards and analytical insight.
Experience translating data into meaningful recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
Demonstrable experience identifying customer trends, behaviours or segmentation opportunities.




Desirable Experience

Experience within banking or financial services.
Customer analytics, CRM analytics, marketing analytics or customer insight experience.
Power BI or Tableau dashboard development.
Experience working with cloud-based data platforms such as Snowflake or AWS Athena.
Exposure to organisations transitioning from legacy reporting environments to modern cloud-based analytics platforms.




AI & Modern Ways of Working

Duration: Initial 10-month contract


We're looking for an experienced Senior Data Analyst to join a leading UK financial services organisation on an initial 10-month contract.


This role is ideal for an analyst who thrives on turning complex customer data into meaningful insights that influence business decisions. You'll work closely with stakeholders to identify customer opportunities, build insightful reporting, and provide data-driven recommendations that shape strategy and improve business outcomes.


This is an insight-led analytical role rather than a data engineering or data modelling position.

The Role


You'll be responsible for analysing customer and business data to uncover trends, identify opportunities and deliver clear, actionable recommendations. Working with business stakeholders across the organisation, you'll translate analytical findings into insights that drive customer engagement, commercial performance and strategic decision-making.


Key Responsibilities

  • Analyse customer and business data to identify trends, opportunities and actionable insights.
  • Produce high-quality reports, dashboards and presentations that support business decision-making.
  • Identify and develop customer segmentation strategies to support targeted campaigns and business initiatives.
  • Partner with business stakeholders to understand challenges and translate requirements into analytical solutions.
  • Present complex analytical findings in a clear, engaging and commercially meaningful way.
  • Support continuous improvement of reporting and analytical processes using modern data tools.
  • Use AI tools to improve analytical productivity and streamline day-to-day workflows where appropriate.


Essential Skills & Experience

  • Proven experience as a Data Analyst or Insight Analyst delivering business-focused analytical solutions.
  • Excellent SQL skills for data extraction, manipulation and analysis.
  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
  • Strong experience producing business reports, dashboards and analytical insight.
  • Experience translating data into meaningful recommendations for non-technical stakeholders.
  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.
  • Demonstrable experience identifying customer trends, behaviours or segmentation opportunities.


Desirable Experience

  • Experience within banking or financial services.
  • Customer analytics, CRM analytics, marketing analytics or customer insight experience.
  • Power BI or Tableau dashboard development.
  • Experience working with cloud-based data platforms such as Snowflake or AWS Athena.
  • Exposure to organisations transitioning from legacy reporting environments to modern cloud-based analytics platforms.


AI & Modern Ways of Working

We're particularly interested in candidates who actively use AI tools such as ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot or Claude to enhance their day-to-day analytical work. Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Company Description

Here at Gousto, we are on a mission to become the UK's most loved way to eat dinner, and for every meal to leave the planet better off. Gousto is changing how people shop, cook and eat food at home. It’s an incredibly exciting time to join our team - and we’re a friendly bunch!

We’re proud to be one of the fastest-growing companies in the UK. Powered by data and a love of food, we’re a recipe box company that’s disrupting the sector, and we’re passionate about our diverse team and our customers.

All of our people are responsible for the success of Gousto, and we’re passionate about creating an inclusive environment for all to thrive. Our guiding values of, Dream, Deliver and Care - show our commitment to innovation, our ambition to hit goals at speed, and our deep respect for the people we work with.

Job Description

Welcome to the Analytics team at Gousto! Based at our London HQ, you will join us as an individual contributor reporting directly to our Analytics Manager. You will be embedded within a cross-functional tribe, partnering closely with product managers, engineers, and fellow analysts to influence key business choices and drive operational performance.

This is a fantastic opportunity to build the technical foundations of your data craft within a supportive, collaborative environment. In this role, you will work across the full analysis lifecycle, from data extraction and manipulation to advanced visualisation and communication, delivering clear insights that help expand our customer base and optimise day-to-day operations.

Core Responsibilities


Deliver clear, accurate insights to defined requests from your tribe, working with your manager and senior peers to shape the approach
Communicate findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, tailoring the level of detail to the audience
Contribute to the design and maintenance of dashboards that scale insight and enable proactive performance monitoring
Investigate anomalies and trends in day-to-day performance, forming hypotheses and testing them against the data
Build reusable, well-documented data models and code that other analysts can pick up and extend
Actively seek and act on feedback to improve the quality of your work
Understand how your work ladders up to your tribe's OKRs and align your priorities accordingly
Contribute to Analytics knowledge sharing and improving ways of working



Who You Are


Brings a structured problem-solving approach and strong attention to detail
Demonstrates clear written and verbal communication, including the ability to explain analysis to non-technical stakeholders
Shows a genuine interest in the commercial context behind the data — understanding what a metric means for the business, not just how to calculate it
Feels comfortable writing SQL to extract, transform and interrogate data in a modern cloud data warehouse such as Databricks, Snowflake, BigQuery or similar
Experience presenting data through a BI tool such as Looker or Tableau as a plus
Maintains familiarity with web or product analytics platforms like GA4, Mixpanel, or Amplitude, and understands how event data supports decision-making as a plus
Possesses a curious, learning mindset, actively seeking feedback and motivated to grow both technical and stakeholder skills
Enjoys collaborating across cross-functional teams of product managers, engineers and other analysts



Additional Information

Interview Process


Video Call with a Talent Acquisition Partner
Video Interview with Hiring Manager
Case Study/Task Interview



Gousto is for everyone:

Whether it’s creating diversity in our recipes or building new teams, we care about our people and the opportunities they have at Gousto. Across our business we lead with inclusivity and strive for equality in all we do; working hard to ensure Gousto is an environment where you can be totally yourself.

Everyone is welcome and we’re looking for applications from people of all backgrounds and experiences.

Excited but wondering if you tick every box? We recommend applying anyway so that we can review your profile. And, if you’re in a job share, why not just apply as a pair.

For our roles outside of Operations, most of our people spend 1 or 2 days in our offices every week, combining the benefits of flexibility and time together with colleagues. We want to enable you to do your best work, and if you require additional flexibility, please talk to us about it.

If you have a disability that you’re worried will affect you during the interview process, please let us know, and we will do our best to help you feel comfortable.

We’d love it if you could submit your application online. If you require an alternative method of applying, please let us know.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About the Role

We are seeking a curious and analytical Junior Data Analyst to join our team. You will turn raw data into actionable insights that drive business decisions. You will work closely with senior analysts and stakeholders to collect, clean, and visualize data. This role is ideal for a proactive problem solver eager to develop advanced analytical skills.

Key Responsibilities

Clean, prepare, and validate raw datasets for analysis.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards using BI tools.
Analyze trends to help teams optimize business performance.
Extract data from relational databases using SQL queries.
Deliver weekly, monthly, and ad-hoc performance reports.

Qualifications

Degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or Computer Science.
Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel (VLOOKUPs, Pivot Tables).
Foundational knowledge of SQL for querying databases.
Familiarity with visualization software like Tableau or Power BI.
Strong communication skills to explain findings to stakeholders.

What We Offer

Direct mentorship from seasoned data professionals.
Access to modern data stacks and corporate tools.
Clear pathways to advance into senior analytical roles.
Competitive compensation, health benefits, and flexible hours.

Title: Analyst
Description:
We are looking for a strong self-starter with a genuine passion for data and a drive to produce results. The right candidate will thrive in a fast-paced environment, where priorities can shift quickly, and will demonstrate the self-motivation and intellectual curiosity needed to continuously learn and develop within a specialist, high-performing team.




Above all, the successful candidate must embody Lockton's core principles:




Client first
Results and impact driven
Self-motivated
Continuous improvement




Role Purpose

The LPS Analyst will report to the LPS Data Lead, providing hands-on analytical and data support across LPS's monoline facilities and wider Lockton portfolio solutions initiatives. The primary focus of the role will be to:




Continue building and maintaining the data infrastructure underpinning Lockton Portfolio Solutions monoline facilities.
Support opportunity scoping through the interrogation and modelling of premium and claims data.
Support the Data Lead in the preparation of high-quality data packs and submissions for insurance markets.
Build and maintain reporting and dashboards to monitor the performance of LPS facilities.
Provide analytical support across a range of LPS strategic initiatives.




Key Responsibilities

Monoline Facilities – Data & Analytics

Extract, interrogate and analyse data to support the Portfolio solutions Lead in internal scoping discussions with relevant stakeholders, using BI and analytics to identify opportunities and support the PS Lead.
Produce Meaningful BI and reporting to assess the profitability and scope of monoline facility opportunities.
Continue to develop and build on the data infrastructure for the monoline facility initiative




Market Submissions & Engagement

Work with Data Lead to produce professional data packs for submission to market in Excel, PowerPoint and PDF, including a range of analytical exhibits.
Respond to ad-hoc data requests from markets in a timely and accurate manner.
Support data lead on maintaining robust reporting on key facility metrics, ideally delivered via interactive dashboards, including:
Indexation: percentage of in-scope business placed versus not placed.
Premium and claims performance by team and risk code.
Aggregate accumulations versus limits and modelled loss estimates versus limits (using RMS / AIR outputs).
Ensure reporting remains current and reliable as facilities grow and data sources evolve.




Must-Have Skills & Experience

End-to-end development of Power BI reports, including ETL, data modelling, DAX expression writing and report creation.
Proficiency in SQL development and good practices in query writing and optimisation.
Good understanding of relational databases and data architecture.
Advanced Excel, including Power Query and Power Pivot.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear insights.
Strong communication skills — able to present findings to both technical and non-technical audiences.




Desirable Skills & Experience

Experience working in the insurance or financial services sector.
Familiarity with London Market data processes, including BDX, Xchanging or Vipr.
Experience working with large, multi-source datasets and building data pipelines.
Knowledge of Python for data analysis and automation.
Experience building automated reporting solutions or data workflows.
Hands-on experience using Databricks for analytics and reporting workflows

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job description

The University of Portsmouth is a global employer of choice where exceptional people create, share and apply knowledge that makes a difference.




Experience the pride of being part of a select group – one of only four universities in the south-east of England to achieve a prestigious Gold rating in the Teaching Excellence Framework. Additionally, we proudly rank among the top 140 universities globally in the Times Higher Young University World Rankings.




Ambition, Responsibility and Openness drive our every endeavour. Join our esteemed institution with a proven track record of success, and where dedication to excellence is key. We want people to make their mark in a professional community that truly values people, innovation, and achievement.




The Role:

This role sits within the University’s Strategic Planning team, supporting the delivery of strategic planning, performance monitoring and data-driven insight across the University. The postholder will provide high-quality data analysis and insight to inform strategic decision-making. This includes sourcing, preparing and analysing complex internal and external datasets, and translating findings into clear, actionable outputs aligned to University priorities. The role requires the ability to interpret a wide range of strategic and operational information, identifying trends, risks and opportunities.




The Data Analyst where required will develop and maintain robust, user-focused management information solutions, including automated reports and interactive dashboards, ensuring data is accurate, timely and accessible to stakeholders at all levels. A strong focus on data quality, governance and continuous improvement is essential. Working collaboratively across professional services and academic areas, the postholder will support performance tracking against strategic objectives, contributing to planning cycles, regulatory returns and benchmarking activity. The role requires strong analytical capability, attention to detail, and the ability to communicate complex information clearly to non-technical audiences. An understanding of the higher education environment (or a similarly complex organisation) would be advantageous, though not essential. Experience in delivering data analysis would also be beneficial.




We would prefer candidates to be able to work full-time, but this is negotiable with the right candidate. Whilst there is a need to be in Portsmouth a few days each week, hybrid working is available for this role, with a minimum of two days per week in the office.




Interviews are currently anticipated to be held on 6 August 2026.




If you have any queries regarding this position, please contact Sam Bowden at samuel.bowden@port.ac.uk




Please note: candidates must submit both a CV and a cover letter as part of their application.




Employer, External Only: University of Portsmouth Academic Services Ltd (UASL)




Discover the advantages of joining our university. We offer a range of attractive benefits and opportunities to enhance your work experience:

Competitive salary, including incremental progression within your scale.
Generous pension scheme.
Generous leave entitlement of 32–35 days a year, plus bank holidays, and an additional Christmas closure.
Family-friendly policies supporting flexible working.
Staff discounts and loyalty schemes.
Staff car parking and discounted public travel.
Excellent training and development opportunities.
Staff wellbeing programmes.
Recreation facilities, including discounted gym membership, food on campus schemes, use of the Library and staff social activities.
Discounted learning.
Start-up business mentoring from expert entrepreneurs via our Entrepreneurs in Residence programme.
The perks don’t stop there - click ‘apply’ for further information on My Reward and Benefits




Salary Information:

New appointments will routinely be made to the entry spine point (minimum) for the advertised grade, from where the individual will (in addition to receiving annual cost-of-living-related increases) progress through the pay range to the maximum for the grade, within a reasonable timeframe. This ensures fairness, consistency and transparency in our pay structures. A higher starting salary or market supplement may be available for a truly exceptional candidate when circumstances fully justify a departure from our normal policy.




UKVI Statement

Prior to submitting your application, kindly ensure that you can either demonstrate or acquire the necessary right to work in the UK. If you currently do not possess the right to work in the UK, please be aware that our offer of employment is conditional upon you obtaining it.




ED&I Statement:

We are committed to upholding the University’s values of being ambitious, open and responsible, in an inclusive working environment that promotes dignity and respect.




Please note that this vacancy may be closed earlier than advertised, so early applications are advised.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Graduate / Early Career Data Analyst




📍Guildford (Hybrid – office & home working)




You’ll be joining a European team focused on improving how we understand and manage supply chain performance. This is a hands-on data role where your work directly supports decision-making across Finance, Technology, and Supply Chain.




In this role, you’ll take ownership of sourcing, validating, and structuring data from multiple international partners. You’ll play a key part in turning complex, fragmented information into clean, reliable datasets that support reporting, forecasting, and business performance insight.




This role would suit someone who enjoys detail, problem-solving, and working with data at scale, particularly someone looking to build their experience and develop a career in data within a global business environment.




✅ What you’ll be doing

Collecting and extracting data from multiple internal and external sources
Loading and transforming datasets into structured formats for analysis
Validating data quality and working with stakeholders to resolve inconsistencies
Maintaining and setting up master data (products, brands, suppliers, locations)
Researching and enriching missing data attributes (e.g. pack size, manufacturer)
Performing data cleansing and quality assurance to ensure accuracy
Preparing datasets for reporting and financial analysis
Building and running reports using SQL to support business insights
Collaborating with European teams across supply chain, finance, and technology




🔎 What we’re looking for

Experience working with data in an analytical or operational environment (academic or professional)
Strong Excel skills and confidence handling large datasets
Working knowledge of SQL for reporting and data extraction
Strong attention to detail and a methodical approach to problem-solving
Clear communication skills in English and ability to work across teams




⭐ Nice to have

Additional European language skills (Spanish is particularly valuable)
Experience working with supply chain or procurement data




🤝 What we offer

A role with real ownership and visibility across European operations
A supportive environment to develop and grow your data skills
Exposure to international stakeholders and large-scale supply chain data
Flexible hybrid working and a collaborative team environment




📩 Ready to apply?

If you’re excited by working with data, solving problems, and contributing to a fast-moving international business, we’d love to hear from you.




We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels respected and valued. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and ensure fair and equal opportunities regardless of gender, age, disability, religion, belief, sexual orientation, marital status, or race.

Title: Senior Data Analyst
Description:
About our Team: LexisNexis is a data and analytics company with 10,500 colleagues serving customers in more than 150 countries. We’re one of the largest information and analytics companies on the planet. We design solutions that help our customers increase productivity, improve decision-making and outcomes, and be more successful.




About the role: The senior data analyst consults with internal stakeholders to clarify business problems, gather requirements, and collect, analyze, and interpret data to support data-driven decisions. This role develops advanced insights and recommendations within their domain and uses analytics tools to curate data, build models, create visualizations, and communicate findings to business audiences. The Sr. Data Analyst I independently leads high-complexity analytics projects and mentors junior analysts.




Responsibilities

Partner with stakeholders to understand business needs and identify the most relevant analyses, KPIs, and success measures.
Support stakeholders in setting appropriate goals and defining measurable outcomes aligned to business objectives.
Prepare and transform data using advanced blending, refinement, and quality techniques (including large/complex datasets).
Apply statistical methods and intermediate data models/scenarios to evaluate trends, drivers, and potential outcomes.
Create clear, compelling data visualizations and narratives tailored to the target audience.
Lead and execute analytics projects independently, including scoping, planning, and managing deliverables to deadlines.
Serve as a subject matter expert within an assigned domain and coach/mentor junior team members.
Develop working knowledge of adjacent disciplines and how they influence analytics needs and interpretation.
Leverage approved AI tools to accelerate analysis and deliverables (e.g., drafting analyses, summarizing findings, generating code/queries, documenting work) while maintaining accountability for accuracy and quality.
Develops data products that meets the data consumer where they are in their data literacy journey (e.g. chatbot/genie for metrics questions, reusable dashboards).




Requirements:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Analytics, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related field; or equivalent practical experience.
Significant experience in analytics, reporting, or data science-adjacent roles.
Ability to understand complex data structures and apply advanced data preparation, blending, refinement, and quality techniques (including big data).
Experience applying intermediate statistics to business problems.
Significant experience leveraging SQL and Python for data querying, collection, transformation, and analysis.
Experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau and/or Power BI; ability to design dashboards and narratives for business audiences.
Familiarity with advanced analytics/data platforms (e.g., Databricks or equivalent).
Strong project execution skills: able to structure work into modular tasks, manage multiple projects in parallel, communicate progress, and resolve blockers.
Ability to craft effective prompts, iterate toward higher-quality outputs, and incorporate domain context and constraints.







Work in a way that works for you

We promote a healthy work/life balance across the organisation. We offer an appealing working prospect for our people. With numerous wellbeing initiatives, shared parental leave, study assistance and sabbaticals, we will help you meet your immediate responsibilities and your long-term goals.

Working flexible hours - flexing the times when you work in the day to help you fit everything in and work when you are the most productive




Working for you

We know that your wellbeing and happiness are key to a long and successful career. These are some of the benefits we are delighted to offer:

Generous holiday allowance with the option to buy additional days
Health screening, eye care vouchers and private medical benefits
Wellbeing programs
Life assurance
Access to a competitive contributory pension scheme
Save As You Earn share option scheme
Travel Season ticket loan
Electric Vehicle Scheme
Optional Dental Insurance
Maternity, paternity and shared parental leave
Employee Assistance Programme
Access to emergency care for both the elderly and children
RECARES days, giving you time to support the charities and causes that matter to you
Access to employee resource groups with dedicated time to volunteer
Access to extensive learning and development resources
Access to employee discounts scheme via Perks at Work




About the Business

LexisNexis Legal & Professional are a leading global provider of legal and regulatory intelligence. We give organisations the business information and analytics they need to make better, more impactful decisions. We are building powerful new decision tools that use machine learning, natural language processing, visualisation and artificial intelligence that aid and enhance decision making.

Title: Data Analyst - Power BI (Hospitality)
Description:
Data Analyst - Hospitality

Based London




Our client is seeking a highly capable Data Analyst to take ownership of their internal Business Intelligence platform and automation capabilities within a fast-paced hotel operations environment. This is a key role within the Technology team, responsible for delivering trusted data, scalable reporting, and efficient automation solutions that support decision-making across the organisation.




The Role

You will lead the development and optimisation of the Power BI reporting estate, ensuring high-quality, secure, and well-governed analytics. Working closely with business stakeholders, you will turn complex data into actionable insights, while continuously improving data processes and controls.

Key Responsibilities

Own and enhance the internal BI platform and reporting catalogue, ensuring accuracy, usability, and performance
Develop Power BI dashboards and datasets, delivering a single source of truth for the business
Build and maintain automated workflows using Power Automate to reduce manual effort and improve data quality
Partner with stakeholders to translate business requirements into scalable data and automation solutions
Manage BI services within an Azure environment, including deployments, monitoring, and access controls
Ensure robust data governance, security, and quality controls
Deliver meaningful insights across core hospitality KPIs such as occupancy, ADR, RevPAR, and booking trends
Support business users with training, adoption, and self-service analytics best practices




Key Requirements

Strong Power BI expertise coupled with strong experience with Tableau
Experience with Azure data platforms and Microsoft tools
Understanding of Power Automate workflows
Strong grasp of data governance
Experience in hospitality, hotels or similar sectors such as retail is essential
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills




This is an exciting opportunity to play a key role in shaping a growing data and automation capability working with modern technologies across Power Platform and Azure. You will have the ability to contribute directly to business-critical decisions and performance, collaborating with cross-functional teams in a dynamic, supportive environment

Description:
Data Analyst

North West or London, Hybrid

Up to £60,000




Looking for a role where your analysis drives business decisions? Want to join a business where you'll build reporting from the ground up, work with senior stakeholders, and influence how data is used across the organisation? You'll help shape the company's reporting capability, build new dashboards, uncover valuable insights and become a trusted partner to teams across the business. This isn't a role where you'll spend your days refreshing the same reports. You'll help build the function.

Salary up to 60K, Hybrid working (1-2 days onsite per week), Clear career progression as the team grows, Flexible hybrid working with offices in London and the North West, and much more!


This is an ideal opportunity for someone who wants more responsibility, more ownership and the chance to build something from the beginning instead of maintaining existing reports. Apply today!

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Leafe is a fast-growing hospitality technology company helping operators manage compliance, operations, workforce activity, and business performance through one platform.

We are entering an important stage of growth and are preparing to improve our use of product and customer data, and strengthen the way we make strategic decisions. To support this, we are hiring our first dedicated data role to help build the foundations of a data-driven culture across the business.




About the role




You will be responsible for bringing together data from multiple platforms, building reporting and insight capability, and helping the leadership team make better decisions across marketing, product, revenue, retention, and customer growth.




You will work closely with the CTO and engineering team to access, model, analyse, and communicate business-critical data. This is a hands-on role suited to someone who can operate independently, turn broad business questions into clear analysis, and present findings in a way that drives action.




Key responsibilities




Design, build, and maintain Leafe’s data analytics infrastructure and business intelligence dashboards.
Bring together data from multiple sources, including product, marketing, sales, Firebase / Firestore, Segment, Amplitude, Google Analytics, and advertising platforms.
Write clean and efficient SQL queries to extract, join, manipulate, and analyse data.
Translate strategic business questions into clear analytical projects.
Analyse customer behaviour, product usage, retention, MRR, conversion, and marketing performance.
Identify trends, risks, opportunities, and performance insights across the business.
Support decision-making around marketing spend, customer acquisition, pricing, retention, and product adoption.
Improve the quality, consistency, and accessibility of data across the company.
Build clear dashboards and reports for leadership and operational teams.
Communicate insights clearly to non-technical stakeholders, including senior leadership.
Help champion data literacy and a data-driven culture across the business.




Essential skills and experience




Strong SQL skills, with the ability to write clean, efficient queries.
Experience working with data from multiple systems, platforms, or APIs.
Ability to clean, structure, and model data for reporting and analysis.
Experience creating dashboards, reports, or data visualisations.
Ability to turn broad business questions into clear analysis and recommendations.
Confident communicating complex data clearly to non-technical stakeholders.
Commercial awareness and the ability to connect data insights to business outcomes.




Desirable experience




Experience working in a SaaS, subscription, marketplace, hospitality, or start-up environment.
Experience with Firebase / Firestore, Segment, Amplitude, Google Analytics, or advertising platforms.
Experience analysing MRR, retention, conversion, customer cohorts, product usage, or marketing performance.
Familiarity with BI tools such as Looker Studio, Power BI, Tableau, Metabase, or similar.




What success looks like




Clear dashboards and reporting across core business metrics.
Faster access to reliable product, revenue, and marketing insights.
Improved visibility of customer behaviour, retention, and conversion.
Better understanding of marketing effectiveness and return on spend.
Stronger evidence to support leadership decisions and future funding conversations.
A more data-driven culture across the business.




Pay, Holiday and Additional Benefits




£40k - £50k, based on experience
28 days annual leave plus bank holidays.
Option to work from home on Wednesdays.
Private counseling and mental health sessions provided.
Gym or sports membership of your choosing.
Regularly explore new and exciting restaurants and culinary experiences with the team.
Learning and personal development allowance.
Tech and hardware accessories budget.
Employee stock options scheme.
Cycle to work scheme.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
The Company




We’re Octopus Legacy: one of the fastest growing tech companies in the country, part of the UK’s most exciting and best-trusted group (you may have heard of some of our sister companies…) and we have a mission.




We’re on a path to scale, aiming to become a household name while transforming a long-established industry and helping our customers prepare for, and find support after, death.




But we can’t do it alone, and that’s why we’re assembling a team of top performers to build with us.




Ready to be part of something big?




The Role

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our growing data team at Octopus Legacy. You’ll play a central role in helping teams across the business make smarter, faster, more informed decisions.




While you’ll sit within the data team, your day-to-day will involve working directly with stakeholders from across the organisation such as Growth, Product and Operations. You’ll turn messy real-world data into actionable insights, bring structure to ambiguity, and help the business better understand what drives performance.




This is a hands-on, high-impact role in a fast-moving environment. You’ll need to be comfortable navigating shifting priorities, championing a data culture, and bringing rigour to complexity.




We’re still early in our data journey - right now, the focus is on building the foundations: modelling key metrics, creating dashboards, and getting reliable data into the hands of the people who need it. As our data maturity grows, there’ll be opportunities to go deeper into analysis, experimentation, and more advanced analytics.




Key Responsibilities:

Act as a thought partner to teams across the business - helping them define problems, clarify questions, and uncover the metrics that matter
Translate stakeholder needs into end-to-end data solutions: from raw data to dbt models to dashboards in Metabase
Define and document clear, consistent metrics to support better decision-making across departments
Build intuitive, impactful dashboards that make data accessible and actionable for non-technical users
Deliver ad-hoc and project-based analysis to evaluate initiatives in growth, product, operations, and beyond
Collaborate with the data team to improve the reliability, clarity, and accessibility of our data models and pipelines
Operate with a high degree of autonomy - scoping projects, pushing for clarity, and challenging assumptions when needed
Contribute to a culture of curiosity and rigour, laying the groundwork for more advanced analytics and data science over time




Who Are You?

You have 2+ years of experience in a data analytics role, ideally in a startup or a company building out its data capability
You're proficient in SQL and have worked with BI tools (like Metabase, Looker, or Tableau) to build dashboards that help teams make better decisions
You have hands-on experience with dbt or similar tools, and a solid understanding of data modelling best practices
You’re comfortable working with messy or incomplete data, and know how to turn it into something clean, structured, and useful
You enjoy working with non-technical stakeholders - you ask thoughtful questions, clarify ambiguous requests, and help teams think through what they really need
You take ownership of your work, are comfortable scoping projects with limited direction, and aren’t afraid to challenge assumptions
You’re curious, pragmatic, and excited by the idea of shaping a modern data function from the ground up
Bonus: you have an interest in experimentation or data science, and are keen to grow into more advanced analytics over time




Skills and Knowledge




Must haves:

Strong curiosity and proactive mindset, with the ability to independently explore data, ask the right questions, identify opportunities, and drive projects forward without requiring constant guidance.
Experience in data analytics and the ability to extract actionable insights from complex datasets.
Proficiency with SQL, DBT, python is desirable.
Proven ability to work closely with business stakeholders and translate their needs into data-driven solutions.
Experience in developing reports and dashboards to monitor key metrics.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to present complex data in a way that is understandable and actionable for non-technical stakeholders across departments.
Proficiency with data visualisation tools (e.g., Tableau, Looker, Metabase).




Nice to haves/we can work on these:

Basic programming skills in languages such as Python.
Understanding of statistical methods and their application to business problems.
Experience working in a startup or fast-paced environment.




Bonus if you:

You have a basic understanding of Data Science concepts and are interested in learning more.
You have experience in training or educating non-technical teams on data literacy.




Our Mission

When people think about planning for death they think about wills, life insurance and funerals. We take these cold processes and turn them into something more human. Share more than money: leave voice notes, music, recipes. Shape a legacy that connects you while you're here. And after you're gone.




Founded by Sam after his mum died suddenly, we’re a group of people who work in death because we’ve been affected by it. We know the difference a good plan makes, and what it’s like when there isn’t one.




Death can come between us, leave mess, legal fees, frustration. But it can also make us stronger. We see a world where people talk openly about death, and work out the real meaning of legacy, one that connects to them. We’re here to make that world happen.




Benefits:

Octopus share incentive scheme.
Hybrid working (London Bridge).
Flexible holiday + extra day off for your Birthday.
Work from anywhere in the world for up to 4 weeks per year.
Vitality Health & Life Insurance.
Pension scheme.
Enhanced parental leave.
Free Will & LPAs + discounts on other Octopus services.
Cycle to Work Scheme and access to the Electric Vehicle (EV) Salary Sacrifice Scheme.
Octopus Giving: we match any charitable fundraising that you do up to £500.
Octopus Springboard: where we help our employees become fully-fledged entrepreneurs.
Dog friendly office.
Breakfast every day, snacks and wellness activities




We know that to be truly innovative, we need to have a diverse team around us. That is why Octopus Legacy is committed to creating an inclusive environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst | £45k-£50k + Bonus | West London - 3 days a week in the office




This is a Data Analyst role in an exciting, fast paced FTSE company, which will focus on helping the business make critical decisions using cutting-edge data analytics.




You’ll partner with core business areas to deliver insights, dashboards, reporting and analytical projects that directly influence business performance and strategy.




Its paying £45-50k and it would suit someone who has a minimum of 1.5 years experience in a Data Analyst role.




Main Responsibilities:




Partner with a core business area to understand data, reporting, visualisation, and analytics needs.
Assess and prioritise analytics project requests.
Work with stakeholders to define project requirements and objectives.
Deliver analysis and insights to support data-driven decisions.
Support the management of dashboards, visualisations, and data sources.
Design and develop analytics solutions for business needs.
Recommend effective approaches to solve analytical challenges.
Break projects into tasks, estimate timelines, and identify risks.
Support stakeholder engagement and communicate analytical approaches clearly.




Qualifications & Experience

Degree qualified
Minimum 1.5 years’ experience in a Data Analyst role
SQL experience
Tableau and/or Power BI experience nice to have but not essential
Hands-on programming/coding experience (education or industry)
Experience manipulating datasets and writing technical code
Strong communication skills with the ability to partner with stakeholders across the business

Title: Data Business Analyst
Description:
Agency

Credera

Job Function

Consulting

Job Subfunction

Business and Change Consulting

Job Description

We know great talent comes in many forms - including people returning from a career break. If you’re looking for part-time hours, flexible working, or a supportive transition back into your career, we’d love to explore how we can make this role work for you. Would you like to be part of a consulting organisation that nurtures an open, collaborative learning environment? Are you passionate about technology, data and people? Whatever your aspirations, we’re trying to create the best boutique consultancy globally and we're looking for talented consultants to be a part of that. Credera helps organisations solve their biggest, most exciting problems. We’ve created banks from scratch on Kubernetes and AWS, helped retail organisations become more agile, built streaming analytics solutions that protect the country and everything in between. We do all this in a work environment where regular social events, inclusivity and an ego-free culture mean we’ve been officially voted a “Great Place to Work”. We’re not interested in cutting corners and believe in helping our clients to make the right choice for the long-term. We draw on our reputation for outstanding delivery to allow our consultants to do the right thing for our clients, and not necessarily the easy thing. Innovation is in our DNA, and we encourage our engineers and consultants to work together to rethink conventional wisdom on how problems should be solved. Here’s what you will do As a Technical Business Analyst you will work with Credera clients and colleagues to translate customer requirements and business intent into effective change.  You’ll help clients achieve their goals by gathering and analysing requirements, understanding and mapping processes and analysing data.  Typically, you will be involved in improving clients working practices, products and software through business and data analysis.  As well as working on large-scale IT and business transformation programmes, our consultants get involved with a broad range of rewarding activities; from the development of new products and tools, to sponsored sporting events and cake baking in support of our annual chosen charity.  Based out of any of our UK offices, this is a permanent role within our Management Consulting Function. At Credera, we operate a flexible hybrid working model. This requires 3 days per week working onsite - either at client site (as required) or in your local Credera office (London, Manchester, Leeds or Newcastle). We value collaboration and client impact, so our hybrid model is designed to strike a balance between flexibility, in-person connection, and delivering exceptional outcomes for our clients. Responsibilities Gather and validate business and technical requirements using business analyst and design thinking techniques  Articulate user experiences through developing user persona, storyboards, customer journey maps  Assist with the development of and prioritisation of product user story / requirements and maintain the overall product backlog  Support the facilitation of workshops using different techniques   Design and document processes  Support the design of operating models  Support the implementation of business change  We are looking for ambitious and intellectually curious consultants to join our high performing team. You will be perceptive, personable, culturally-sensitive and demonstrate a high degree of emotional intelligence. Being able to work collaboratively is essential.  Benefits Along with a great company culture, Credera provides an exceptional compensation package including a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits plan. Our consistent growth and entrepreneurial environment provide an excellent platform to embark upon an exciting career path, where your contribution really counts. Along with a great company culture, Credera provides an exceptional compensation package including a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefit plan. Our consistent growth over the years and entrepreneurial environment, provides an excellent platform to embark upon an exciting career path, where your contribution really counts, and we will recognise it.  With personalised development opportunities, experienced colleagues around the globe and exciting client assignments, progression can be extremely rapid for high performers.  We are a social bunch of people and connect as a team on a regular basis. You can also expect:   Learn more Credera is Omnicom’s global consulting firm that combines transformational consulting capabilities, deep industry knowledge, AI and technology expertise to deliver valuable customer experiences and accelerated growth across a broad range of industries worldwide. Our one-of-a-kind global boutique approach means we provide our clients with tailored solutions unique to their organisation that can scale due to our extensive footprint. As a values-led organisation, our mission is to make an extraordinary impact on our clients, our people, and our community. We believe it is this approach that has allowed us to work with and transform the most influential brands and organisations in the world, from strategy through to execution. More information is available at credera.co.uk. We are part of the OPM Group of Companies, within Omnicom Group Inc. Credera will never ask for money up front and will not use apps such as Facebook Messenger, WhatsApp or Google Hangouts for communicating with you. You should be very wary of, and carefully scrutinize, any job opportunity that asks for money prior to starting and/or one where all communications take place exclusively via chat."

Omnicom’s policy requires employees to work in the office for a minimum of three days a week, unless additional in-office days are directed by their agency or manager. Our objective is to increase this requirement over time, and many of our agencies as well as Omnicom’s corporate group already require five days of in-office attendance.

Omnicom is committed to hiring and developing exceptional talent. We agree that talent is uniquely distributed, and we’re focused on developing inclusive teams that can bring the best solutions to everything we do. We strongly believe that celebrating what makes us different makes us better together. Join us—we look forward to getting to know you. We will process your personal data in accordance with our Recruitment Privacy Notice.

Link to Recruitment Privacy Notice: https://www.omc.com/privacy-notice/

We agree that talent is uniquely distributed, and we’re focused on developing inclusive teams that can bring the best solutions to everything we do. We strongly believe that celebrating what makes us different makes us better together. Join us—we look forward to getting to know you. We will process your personal data in accordance with our Recruitment Privacy Notice.

Title: IT Administrator
Description:
IT Administrator – Nottingham / Hybrid - 6 Months FTC

£28,000 - £32,000 pro rata + great benefits

This multi-site and fast-growing organisation require an experienced IT Administrator to maintain the seamless functionality of IT services for more than a thousand users. You will be given the opportunity to develop your technical abilities whilst working with a variety of stakeholders and clients.

One of the key responsibilities for this IT Fulfilment Specialist is to ensure a smooth on-boarding process for new starters and that the IT department is effectively resourced. You will be responsible for the creation and removal of all user accounts and devices as well as the fulfilment and accurate asset management of end-user IT equipment.

You will be an advocate for continuous service improvement, identifying areas for improvement to optimise, automate and enhance service delivery. This will be a hybrid position, with the expectation of at least three days per week required in their Nottingham office. It will be a 6-month contract initially.

Package


Bonus opportunities

25 days holiday + 5 days buy/sell + bank holidays.
Professional development opportunities
5% employer pension, rising with service + many more.


Experience


Ability to effectively prioritise and execute tasks in a fast-paced setting.
Excellent communication and problem-solving skills
Experience of user account administration within Active Directory and Exchange Online.
IT asset / equipment management
Knowledge of Windows 11 Enterprise environments
Excellent knowledge of Microsoft Office 365 products.


The company have an excellent reputation within their sector, and have experienced 14 consecutive years of growth, posting record revenues for the last financial year. They promote a healthy work-life balance and will give you the opportunity to develop your technical knowledge.
