Title: Client Manager
Description:
Client Manager | Boots Media Group (SMG)




Lead high-impact retail media campaigns for some of the UK's biggest brands and help shape the future of retail media.




📍 Hybrid – 2 days a week in either the Boots Support Office or with suppliers

💰 Salary: From £39,000 + 10% discretionary bonus




Ready to step into a role where you'll make a real commercial impact?




At Boots Media Group (BMG), powered by SMG, we're transforming how brands connect with millions of Boots customers. As a Client Manager, you'll take ownership of exciting retail media campaigns from strategy through to execution, building strong client relationships, driving commercial growth, and helping develop the next generation of talent.




If you're passionate about marketing, love working with clients, and thrive in a fast-paced environment where no two days are the same, we'd love to hear from you.




Who are we?

SMG are the pioneers of connected commerce marketing. Since 2008, we've been helping the world's biggest retailers and brands create smarter, data-driven advertising that reaches shoppers at the moments that matter most.




At Boots Media Group, you'll work at the heart of one of the UK's most recognised retailers, delivering innovative retail media campaigns that genuinely influence customer behaviour and drive measurable results.




What you'll be doing:




You'll be responsible for delivering best-in-class campaigns while supporting the commercial success of Boots Media Group.




Your day-to-day will include:

Owning end-to-end retail media campaigns from client agreement through to delivery.
Building trusted relationships with Boots teams, suppliers and leading FMCG brands.
Driving campaign performance through optimisation, reporting and data-driven insights.
Managing and growing your own media channels, identifying opportunities to increase commercial value.
Creating strategic marketing plans that deliver against both client and business objectives.
Leading campaign planning across key supplier categories.
Coaching and developing members of the Client Management team.
Working closely with senior stakeholders to help deliver ambitious commercial targets.
Continuously improving processes, campaign performance and client experience.




What we're looking for:




You'll probably have experience in a client-facing marketing, media or retail environment and be ready to take the next step in your career.




We're particularly interested in people who have:

Experience managing and developing at least one team member.
A background in marketing, retail media, media agencies or client services.
Strong campaign management and project management skills.
Experience working with multiple clients or stakeholders simultaneously.
Confidence analysing campaign performance, budgets and commercial data (Excel is essential).
A proactive mindset with a passion for delivering exceptional client experiences.




Most importantly, we're looking for someone who's curious, collaborative and excited by the opportunity to help shape one of the UK's fastest-growing retail media businesses.




Don't tick every box? Apply anyway. We know great people don't always meet every requirement, and we'd love to hear from you if you think you'd be a great fit.




Why join SMG?

At SMG, you'll join a business that's growing fast, investing in its people and constantly pushing the boundaries of retail media.




Alongside exciting career opportunities, you'll enjoy:

✨ 10% discretionary bonus

💙 £1,800 annual Wellbeing Fund (on top of your salary)

🧠 Free Headspace subscription

📚 £500 annual Learning ("Uni") Fund

🌴 4 additional Wellbeing Days each year

🎉 Summer & Winter conferences plus regular team celebrations

🕓 4pm finish every Friday

🏡 Flexible hybrid working




If you're looking for a role where you'll work with household-name brands, influence commercial success, develop your leadership skills and grow your career in one of the UK's most exciting retail media businesses, we'd love to hear from you.

Title: Data Business Partner
Description:
Data Business Partner – Central Nottingham / Hybrid

Please read the following job description thoroughly to ensure you are the right fit for this role before applying.

£50,000 - £56,000 + bonus, 35-hour work week and great benefits

This profitable and growing business are looking to add an experienced Data Business Partner to their collaborative team. The ideal candidate will have good knowledge of the principles and best practice in MI development. This role leans itself to be more of a ‘Business Data Analyst role’, where you will act as the bridge between the data team and wider business stakeholders.

The Data Business Partner will still undertake hands on data analysis, but you will work closely with clients and central marketing teams to provide insight and guidance on how data and MI can add value. A technical understanding is expected, but the key to this role is having the personality to develop strong links with leadership teams and become their trusted advisor within the data team.

You will develop the data strategy for clients to align with business priorities and their needs whilst providing value through data and MI.

The role will require 1-2 days per week in the office, with the ideal location being their head office in Nottingham. Birmingham or London could also be considered.

Package

Bonus opportunities

35-hour work week with flexible working

25 days holiday + 5 days buy/sell + bank holidays.

Professional development opportunities

5% employer pension, rising with service + many more.

Key Requirements

Excellent communication skills and the ability to communicate complex data to non-technical audiences.

Experience working with internal stakeholders / clients to understand their data journey and how value can be provided through data and MI

Experience designing, developing and maintaining MI Solutions e.g. reporting, dashboards and analytics tools

Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills

Experience of working in an analytical and data intensive environment

Tech Stack – SSRS, SSMS, PowerBI, T-SQL, SQL Server, Visual Studio

The company have an excellent reputation within their sector, and have experienced 14 consecutive years of growth, posting record revenues for the last financial year. They promote a healthy work-life balance and will give you the opportunity to develop your technical knowledge.

Title: Head of Commercial- Nottingham
Description:
If you're an experienced commercial leader looking for the opportunity to shape the commercial strategy of a growing national healthcare business, drive product and service innovation, and make a real impact on business performance, this could be your next career move.

Join Boots Hearingcare at an exciting stage of our growth as we continue to strengthen our position as one of the UK's leading hearingcare providers. Reporting directly to the Senior Director of Marketing & Commercial, you'll lead our commercial strategy across products, services, pricing and promotions, helping to maximise customer value, commercial performance and long-term business growth.

If you're looking for a role where you can influence strategic decision-making, work collaboratively across the business and lead commercial excellence, we'd love to hear from you.

The role

As Head of Commercial, you'll be responsible for leading the commercial direction of Boots Hearingcare's product and service portfolio, ensuring we continue to deliver compelling customer propositions while driving profitable growth.

You'll:


Develop and deliver Boots Hearingcare's commercial strategy across products, services, pricing and promotions.
Lead category planning to maximise customer acquisition, conversion, retention and lifetime value.
Identify new commercial opportunities using customer insights, market trends and competitor analysis.
Own pricing strategies, promotional planning and commercial performance across the portfolio.
Lead supplier relationships and commercial negotiations to secure best value and maximise profitability.
Drive the development and launch of new products, services and customer propositions.
Work closely with Marketing, Sales & Operations, Finance and Sonova colleagues to deliver commercial objectives.
Lead and develop a high-performing commercial team while fostering collaboration across the business.



Progression & development

This is a highly visible leadership role offering the opportunity to shape Boots Hearingcare's commercial future while working alongside senior leaders across the organisation.

You'll also benefit from:


Working within a growing healthcare business backed by Sonova.
The opportunity to influence commercial strategy at executive level.
Exposure to major product launches, innovation projects and strategic initiatives.
Ongoing leadership and professional development.



What’s In It For You


Competitive salary DOE
Hybrid working, 3 days from our Norttingham HO- 2 days from home
25–30 days annual leave plus bank holidays
Boots Discount Card
Free hearing aids (if required as an employee)
Free flu vaccination (discounted for family members)
Pension scheme & life assurance
Flexible Benefits Box – tailor your package
Telus wellbeing support – confidential support inside and outside work
Long service awards recognising your contribution



What We’re Looking For


Proven experience leading commercial, category, product or commercial strategy functions within a multi-site retail, healthcare or consumer business.
Strong commercial acumen with a track record of delivering revenue growth, profitability and market share improvements.
Experience developing pricing strategies, promotional plans and product portfolios.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate data into commercial decisions.
Experience managing supplier relationships and leading commercial negotiations.
Excellent stakeholder management and leadership skills with the ability to influence at all levels.
A collaborative, customer-focused approach with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.



We care. We drive innovation. We strive for excellence. We build the best team.

#BootsHearingcare

Sonova is an equal opportunity employer.

We team up. We grow talent. We collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds to win with the best team in the market place. We guarantee every person equal treatment in regard to employment and opportunity for employment, regardless of a candidate’s ethnic or national origin, religion, sexual orientation or marital status, gender, genetic identity, age, disability or any other legally protected status.

Title: Data Modeller (SC Cleared)
Description:
Job Title: Data Modeller (UAF)

Location: near Nottingham (Hybrid, 2 to 3 office days per week)

Start Date: August 2026

Contract Length: 5 Months (with scope to extend)

IR35: Inside

Interview Process: 2 Stage, MS Teams

Clearance: SC

Nationality Required: UK




We are supporting a public sector client in hiring a Data Modeller with specialist knowledge of the Unified Architecture Framework to join a complex engineering and enterprise architecture programme.




This is a hands-on data modelling role requiring the ability to work across enterprise and solution architecture teams, translating business, operational and technical requirements into well-structured conceptual, logical and physical data models. The successful candidate will apply UAF principles within a complex, regulated environment and contribute to the ongoing development and governance of the data architecture estate.




This role suits an experienced Data Modeller with proven UAF expertise, comfortable working alongside architects, engineers and business stakeholders in a complex, security-conscious environment.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain conceptual, logical and physical data models aligned to programme and enterprise architecture standards
Apply Unified Architecture Framework principles to data modelling activity, ensuring models are consistent with the broader architectural approach
Work closely with enterprise and solution architects to translate business, operational and technical requirements into structured data models
Contribute to data architecture governance, standards and documentation across the programme
Engage with technical and non-technical stakeholders to validate data models and ensure alignment with business needs
Use architecture modelling tools and frameworks to produce clear, well-structured artefacts for review and approval
Support the integration of data models into wider system and enterprise architecture deliverables




Essential Skills

Strong data modelling expertise across conceptual, logical and physical model design
Practical knowledge and application of the Unified Architecture Framework (UAF)
Experience working with enterprise and solution architecture teams in complex delivery environments
Ability to translate business, operational and technical requirements into structured data models
Proficiency with architecture modelling tools and frameworks
Strong communication skills with the ability to engage stakeholders across both technical and non-technical audiences




Desirable Skills

Experience within defence, government, aerospace or highly regulated industry environments
Familiarity with related architecture frameworks such as TOGAF, MODAF or NAF
Experience contributing to data governance and architecture assurance processes
Exposure to complex engineering or systems integration programmes

Title: Partnering Support Officer
Description:
£30,639 - £32,251 p/a

Permanent - Full Time

Closing date - 27 July 2026

The role

We have an exciting opportunity for an experienced individual to work in our East Midlands Regional Property Team.

The role will be working in our Nottingham Office to provide support to the Managers and Surveyors, helping contact customers, liaise with contractors, and ensure great record keeping is in place.

This is a busy, fast paced operation and the ideal candidate will be used to working in a similar environment where organisation is key.

What You'll Need To Succeed


Expectation of our residents is high and budgets are tight, so this role requires a candidate that and has an aptitude for detail working under pressure.
We would anticipate you would have a commercial awareness and be able to follow and input into businesses processes.
Alongside excellent communication skills, your experience and a relevant track record are an absolute requirement with demonstrable use of spreadsheets, IT systems and other generic software. We would encourage candidates that have a natural curiosity for resolving problems and genuine passion for helping our customers.


Duties will include but will not be limited to the following


Working with contractors and residents on continuous improvement of projects, ensuring clear communication with all parties when booking and confirming appointments
Leading on keeping regional trackers up to date and to control the data input to ensure accuracy
Supporting the Repairs Manager in effectively managing the tracker to input legal, contractor and Surveyor updates
Proactively assisting the team with general administration or telephone duties, ensuring all internal processes are followed through to completion and an excellent customer service is provided to all customers and stakeholders


What You'll Need To Succeed


Experience of dealing with customers from different backgrounds, and delivering a positive and easy service to customers
Experience of analysing customer feedback to achieve service improvements
Attention to detail and accuracy in reporting
Experience of working with external stakeholders
Experience of dealing with customers and responding to customer enquiries on the phone and in writing
An experienced user of Microsoft Office and the ability to easily learn other IT packages
Strong administrative skills and the ability to manage a varied and demanding workload
Ability to work independently and use initiative to problem solve


What You Need To Do Now

If you're interested in this role, take a look at the attached Job Description for more details.

  • We would anticipate you would have a commercial awareness and be able to follow and input into businesses processes.
  • Alongside excellent communication skills, your experience and a relevant track record are an absolute requirement with demonstrable use of spreadsheets, IT systems and other generic software. We would encourage candidates that have a natural curiosity for resolving problems and genuine passion for helping our customers.

Duties will include but will not be limited to the following

  • Working with contractors and residents on continuous improvement of projects, ensuring clear communication with all parties when booking and confirming appointments
  • Leading on keeping regional trackers up to date and to control the data input to ensure accuracy
  • Supporting the Repairs Manager in effectively managing the tracker to input legal, contractor and Surveyor updates
  • Proactively assisting the team with general administration or telephone duties, ensuring all internal processes are followed through to completion and an excellent customer service is provided to all customers and stakeholders

What You'll Need To Succeed

  • Experience of dealing with customers from different backgrounds, and delivering a positive and easy service to customers
  • Experience of analysing customer feedback to achieve service improvements
  • Attention to detail and accuracy in reporting
  • Experience of working with external stakeholders
  • Experience of dealing with customers and responding to customer enquiries on the phone and in writing
  • An experienced user of Microsoft Office and the ability to easily learn other IT packages
  • Strong administrative skills and the ability to manage a varied and demanding workload
  • Ability to work independently and use initiative to problem solve

What You Need To Do Now

Title: Finance Partner – Go To Market
Description:
Company Description

Experian is a global data and technology company, powering opportunities for people and businesses around the world. We operate across a range of markets, from financial services to healthcare, automotive, agribusiness, insurance, and many more. Experian invests in people and new advanced technologies to unlock the power of data. We have an amazing team of 25,200 people in 32 countries.

Job Description

Grade: EB8

Location: London or Nottingham

Shape commercial success through insight, innovation, and partnership.

We're looking for a commercially minded Finance Business Partner to join our Go-to-Market (GTM) Finance team. Reporting to the GTM Finance Director, you will work in partnership with Sales, Commercial Operations, Senior Leadership and Finance Leaders to improve business growth through forecasting, performance analysis, and strategic financial insight.

This is a fantastic opportunity for an finance professional looking to develop their career within a global, market-leading organisation while helping transform finance through data, AI, and automation.

What You Will Do


Lead forecasting and budgeting for new business revenue.
Analyse sales pipeline performance, identifying risks and opportunities.
Partner with commercial teams to support growth and improve decision-making.
Produce business performance reports for senior leadership.
Manage GTM costs, monitor forecasts, and support budget owners in achieving targets.
Develop Tableau dashboards and deliver meaningful business insights.
Improve process improvements using AI, automation, and digital technologies.
Collaborate with Finance Systems and Data teams to enhance reporting and data quality.


Qualifications

We are looking for future talent, and so you must be someone who thrives from adding value to the business and to your own development.

We Are Looking For


Qualified accountant (ACA, ACCA, CIMA, or equivalent).
Experience in Finance Business Partnering, FP&A, or Commercial Finance.
Forecasting, analytical, and commercial skills.
Experience with Tableau (or similar BI tools), Excel, and finance systems.
Experience of process improvement and applying AI or automation.
Excellent stakeholder management experience, communicating analytical findings to a range of different audiences.
Proactive, collaborative, and committed to continuous improvement.


Additional Information

Our benefits package includes:


Hybrid working - 40% office based
Great compensation package and discretionary bonus plan
Core benefits include Pension, BUPA healthcare and more
25 days annual leave plus bank holidays


Our uniqueness is that we celebrate yours. Experian's people first, inclusive and purpose driven culture is multi award-winning; World's Best Workplaces™ 2025 (Fortune Global Top 25), Great Place To Work™ in 26 countries to name a few. Check out Experian Life on social or explore our Careers Site to understand why. Experian is also proud to be an Equal Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know at the earliest opportunity.

This is a hybrid remote/in-office role.

Title: Senior Technician - Forensic Science
Description:
Diverse community. Inclusive Research. Rewarding Careers.

Doing things differently.

Senior Technician - Forensic Science

Grade F (£28,608 - £32,089 p.a. pro rata)

Full Time/ Permanent

About The Role

Join our Technical Team and help shape the next generation of industry professionals.

You'll demonstrate specialist equipment and techniques, support students and academic staff in laboratories, workshops, and studios, and help create a positive, industry-focused learning environment. Working collaboratively, you'll promote safe working practices, stay up to date with emerging technologies and trends, and build valuable internal and external networks to enhance learning and employability outcomes.

For more details, please take a look at the role profile. We'll still consider applications even if you don't meet every single one of the requirements, so don't be put off if you don't match them perfectly.

About Us

The School of Science and Technology at Nottingham Trent University (NTU) is an exciting multidisciplinary environment for learning, teaching and research, with some of the best facilities in the UK.

We pride ourselves on delivering high-quality teaching and diverse, real-world research. We specialise in biosciences, chemistry, computing and technology, as well as engineering, forensic science, mathematics, physics and sport science. This mix of traditional and modern subjects encourages and inspires future innovators.

Our industry-standard Forensic Science facilities and equipment support our teaching and research and they are also used regularly by external organisations. Our crime scene training facility is one of the largest in the UK and is regularly used for teaching, training and research.

For any informal queries about the role or the team, please contact Diego Lo Destro at diego.lodestro@ntu.ac.uk

Join Us


Flexibility - take ownership over how you get your work done. We're open to different working patterns and approaches.
25 - 30 days annual leave, plus statutory bank holidays and 5 university closure days pro rata.
Salary Sacrifice Retirement Savings Plan with life assurance and income protection. Available to colleagues who choose to opt out of the contractual pension scheme. Minimum colleague contributions of 0% matched with minimum NTU contributions of 8%.
Range of health and wellbeing services, including a Health Cash Plan, voluntary benefits, discounts, and savings for all colleagues.
Access to a wealth of formal and informal professional development opportunities to develop your skills and advance your career.
We are committed to advancing Equality, Diversity and Inclusion across all areas of our work. We actively promote a culture of respect and belonging and we welcome applications from people of all backgrounds. For more information please click here - Equality, Diversity and Inclusion | Nottingham Trent University
And a whole lot more…Find out more about the range of benefits we offer.


Come and be part of our success. Apply today.

Safe and Inclusive

At NTU, we continue to build an inclusive culture that encourages, supports and celebrates the diverse voices and experiences of our students and colleagues. By championing positive wellbeing, we promote an environment where all can thrive and reach their full potential. We welcome the unique contributions that you can bring and we encourage people from underrepresented communities and backgrounds to apply to join our team.

Please note that unfortunately, this role has been assessed as ineligible for sponsorship under the UK Visas & Immigration points-based immigration system however, we recommend that you assess your eligibility before applying for this position. For more information visit the Government Skilled Worker visa support page. However, applications are welcome from candidates who do not currently have the right to work in the UK, but who would be eligible to obtain a valid visa via another route. Please consult the Home Office website for further information.

Please note that this role is covered by the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act (1974) and successful applicants will be asked to declare any unspent criminal convictions.

Title: Admin Officer
Description:
If you need support in completing the application or if you require a different format of this document, please get in touch with at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com or call TCS London Office number 02031552100 with the subject line: “Application Support Request”.







Role: Admin Officer

Job Type: Permanent

Location: Nottingham, UK

Number of hours: 40 hours per week – full time




Ready to utilize your experience and expertise in Office Administration.




We have an exciting role for you –Admin Officer.




Careers at TCS: It means more




TCS is a purpose-led transformation company, built on belief. We do not just help businesses to transform through technology. We support them in making a meaningful difference to the people and communities they serve - our clients include some of the biggest brands in the UK and worldwide. For you, it means more to make an impact that matters, through challenging projects which demand ambitious innovation and thought leadership.




Build strong relationships with a diverse range of stakeholders.
Gain access to endless learning opportunities.
Work closely with the range of teams within the business to bring products to life.




The Role




As an Admin Officer, you will be the backbone of our daily operations, ensuring a safe, efficient, and welcoming workplace. You will act as the primary liaison between the office, vendors, and building management, overseeing everything from facility maintenance and health and safety compliance to procurement and event coordination. You have to manage essential office floor services, support financial administration through purchase order tracking, and serve as a key point of contact for staff health and safety needs.




Key responsibilities:




Line of communication between the office and the vendors and the landlord/ building management
Arranging of the required monthly and annual maintenance checks and any ad-hoc maintenance repairs
Ordering supplies for the office including general office supplies and items for the coffee machine/ canteen
Maintenance of the office floor including ensuring meeting rooms are kept tidy ready for use, storerooms are kept organized and the coffee machine stays stocked with coffee beans and chai/ chocolate powder etc.
Communicating with the finance team to raise and track required purchase orders for the maintenance work and any orders placed
Tracking and reporting of company card spends and raising purchase order requests and processing receipts and invoices
As the Health and Safety Executive for the office floor, responsible for maintaining the health and safety of the office, ensuring services are up to date to remain compliant and completing the monthly reports and risk assessments
Assisting the team with any ad-hoc concerns or issues, Organising events.
Arrangement and monitoring of the keycard systems for the floor including creating new keycards for new staff, visitors, any required contractors, removing access for staff who leave, and producing monthly reports to track employee and visitor access.




Your Profile




Essential skills/knowledge/experience:




Familiarity using MS Office, O365 (Outlook, Teams, Word, Excel, PowerPoint)
Strong verbal/written communication; high level of organization; procurement and budget tracking; ability to prioritize multiple ad-hoc tasks.
Proven experience in office administration, facilities management, or a similar operational support role.




Desirable skills/knowledge/experience:




Proficiency in Health & Safety (HSE) regulations; understanding of DSE (Display Screen Equipment) assessment protocols.
Event planning experience; proficiency in managing electronic access/keycard systems; advanced skills in reporting and data tracking.







Rewards & Benefits




TCS is consistently voted a Top Employer in the UK and globally. Our competitive salary packages feature pension, health care, life assurance, laptop, phone, access to extensive training resources and discounts within the larger Tata network.




Diversity, Inclusion and Wellbeing




Tata Consultancy Services UK&I is committed to meeting the accessibility needs of all individuals in accordance with the UK Equality Act 2010 and the UK Human Rights Act 1998.




We believe in building and sustaining a culture of equity and belonging where everyone can thrive. Our diversity and inclusion motto is ‘Inclusion without Exception’. Our continued commitment to Culture and Diversity is reflected across our workforce implemented through equitable workplace policies and processes.




You’ll find a welcoming culture and many internal volunteering and social networks to join (these are optional). Our diversity, inclusion and social activities include 12 employee networks such as gender diversity, LGBTQIA+ & Allies, mental health, disability & neurodiversity inclusion and many more, as well as health & wellness initiatives and sports events and we sponsor the London Marathon.




We welcome and embrace diversity in race, nationality, ethnicity, disability, neurodiversity, gender identity, age, physical ability, gender reassignment, sexual orientation. We are a disability inclusive employer and encourage disabled people to apply for this role.




If you are an applicant who needs any adjustments to the application process or interview, please contact us at UKI.recruitment@tcs.com with the subject line: “Adjustment Request” or call TCS London Office 02031552100 to request an adjustment. We welcome requests prior to you completing the application and at any stage of the recruitment process.




Next Steps




Due to a high volume of applications, we will be unable to contact each applicant individually on the status of their application. If you have not received a direct response within 30 days, then it should be deemed unsuccessful on this occasion.




Join us and do more of what matters. Apply online now.

Title: Graduate Land Surveyor
Description:
The role represents a chance for an individual to develop a professional career within mapping and consultancy. You will be joining a well-established team undertaking a variety of surveys across the UK, you will have the full support and training from the team allowing you to develop and learn new skills whilst making a positive contribution to the group.

SLR and Our Team

SLR has a busy, well-established Land Survey team which provides Geomatic surveying throughout the country, offering our experience in a wide range of industries for topographical surveying services. The team manages various surveying contracts in specific detail for a range of clients including minerals/waste operators, residential/commercial developers, civil/petrochemical/power engineering firms, and more. The team prides itself on understanding the importance of the client’s requirements for site surveys, setting out, and design, providing consistency with its professional digital capability.

Our Land Survey team sits within a wider Geospatial and Data Solutions team. This team comprises CAD specialists, GIS analysts, Data Scientists, and Developers. Being part of this wider structure gives the opportunity to work with fellow professionals and develop wider skills in the Geospatial field.

The Role

We are looking for a talented individual to join the team based at either our Nottingham or Newcastle office. You will be resourceful, creative, and capable of working under your own initiative as part of multi-disciplinary teams delivering projects to a wide variety of clients. The work will be partly office-based but will also require regular site work throughout the UK for up to a week at a time. You must hold a full UK driving license and be familiar with Ordnance Survey and other sources of online mapping. You will be either studying for a degree or hold a degree in Surveying/Geomatics.

Responsibilities


Assisting the surveyor(s) across a variety of fieldwork projects throughout the UK concerning briefs and specifications.
As experience and skills develop, the successful candidate will be required to work both independently and within a team on projects from start to finish showing initiative and responsibility to deliver surveys to a high standard.
Processing and producing survey plans in LSS and AutoCAD, volumetric calculations, and reports.


About You


0-2 years of surveying experience using GNSS and total Stations
Knowledge of using AutoCAD and LSS, and possibly n4ce.
Knowledge and experience of using Word and Excel and other Microsoft products.
Hardworking and able to self-motivate.
Good written and verbal communication skills.
Full UK driving license
An adaptable and flexible approach to work, to meet demands at peak times;
The ability to work outdoors over difficult terrain, UK wide;
A positive, adaptable, and challenge-ready personality.
Desirable but not essential skills include Laser Scan processing with Register360, Drone data processing with DJI Terra or Pix4D, QGIS, experience with RD8000 Radio Detection equipment, and drone piloting experience.


About Us

SLR are global leaders in Sustainability Solutions, helping our clients achieve their sustainability goals. We are a consultancy with 4000+ employees across 6 regions in over 125 countries. Our ‘one team’ culture is at the heart of our business, providing a collaborative and supportive environment for professional development.

Along with competitive salaries, our staff enjoy a comprehensive benefits package with a company pension plus excellent healthcare, travel, and life insurance, and a structured career framework with regular reviews offering outstanding opportunities for progression. Alongside 25 days’ annual leave, with additional flexible bank holidays, we offer flexible, agile, and hybrid working which enables staff to tailor hours worked around core hours, with family-friendly policies to help balance the needs of professional and home life.

Culture & Inclusion at SLR

We are committed to being an equal opportunities employer, and our expectations go beyond purely complying with legislation. Through our Regional C&I Committees, Global Council, and Employee Networks, we foster an inclusive environment where all people regardless of their background can thrive.

We welcome applications from all individuals, regardless of their background, including those from groups that are currently under-represented at SLR, such as people identifying as 2SLGBTQI+, Indigenous peoples, ethnic minority groups, women, and people with visible or invisible disabilities, including those who are neurodivergent.

Should you require any special adjustments throughout the recruitment process with us, please let us know. As a Disability Confident Committed employer, we seek to ensure all applicants have equitable support when applying to SLR.

Should you require any special adjustments throughout the recruitment process with us, please let us know. As a Disability Confident Committed employer, we seek to ensure all applicants have equitable support when applying to SLR.

Title: Digital Content Manager
Description:
Digital Content Manager﻿




Location: HQ based in Nottingham, UK (Hybrid)

Reports to: Director of Marketing & Communications




Why Gill?




For more than 50 years, Gill has been engineering technical apparel and equipment trusted by sailors and boaters around the world. From Olympic campaigns and offshore races to weekend cruising and everyday adventures, our products are built to perform when it matters most.




Founded by sailors and driven by innovation, Gill has earned its reputation by creating products that are tested in the toughest conditions and trusted on the water. Today, we're a global brand, distributed in more than 35 countries, with an ambitious vision for continued growth.




As we continue to evolve, we're looking for talented people who share our passion for life on the water and want to help shape the next chapter of the Gill story.




Purpose of the Role




We're looking for a Digital Content Manager to lead the planning, creation and delivery of Gill's digital content across our global channels.




Working across Marketing and Ecommerce, you'll shape how the Gill brand comes to life through seasonal campaigns, product launches, ambassador activations and compelling storytelling that strengthens our brand, engages our community and supports commercial growth.




This is a hands-on role for someone who combines creativity with commercial awareness. You'll lead content strategy and planning, oversee content production, collaborate with photographers, videographers and ambassadors, and ensure every digital touchpoint delivers a premium, consistent brand experience.




Key Responsibilities




Content & Storytelling




Own Gill's digital content calendar, planning seasonal campaigns, product launches and brand storytelling across all digital channels.
Lead the development of creative concepts and content ideas, working closely with photographers, videographers and the wider marketing team to produce engaging, high-quality content across photography, video and digital.
Manage the planning and delivery of content creation, supporting campaign shoots and ensuring assets are captured to maximise value across multiple channels.
Develop authentic content that showcases Gill's products, ambassadors, partners and community, bringing the brand to life through meaningful storytelling.
Act as the guardian of Gill's digital brand standards, ensuring all content maintains a consistent premium look, feel and tone of voice across every platform.




Digital Channels




Manage Gill's global social media channels, driving audience growth, engagement and community management.
Work closely with the Ecommerce team to ensure the website accurately reflects seasonal campaigns, product launches and brand priorities through engaging homepage content, landing pages and supporting digital assets.
Collaborate closely with the Ecommerce team on email campaigns, providing campaign messaging, copy and creative assets while ensuring alignment with wider marketing activity.
Support key retail partners by delivering digital assets that strengthen Gill's online brand presence and create a consistent customer experience.
Maintain and continually develop Gill's digital asset library, ensuring photography, video and campaign assets are organised, accessible and effectively managed for global teams and partners.




Campaign Delivery & Collaboration




Lead the digital delivery of seasonal campaigns, coordinating content across social media, website, retail partners and other owned channels.
Work closely with Gill ambassadors, athletes and partners to plan content activations that support product launches, campaigns and brand storytelling.
Coordinate ambassador content requirements, ensuring assets are delivered on time and aligned with campaign objectives.
Work closely with Product, Ecommerce, Trade Marketing, Creative and regional teams to ensure campaigns launch successfully and on time.
Manage relationships with photographers, videographers, content creators and external agencies where required.
Identify opportunities to maximise the value of campaign assets across multiple digital platforms and audiences.




Performance & Insight




Monitor and report on digital performance across social media, website and campaign activity, providing clear recommendations to improve future performance.
Analyse customer engagement and digital trends to optimise content and campaign effectiveness.
Stay up to date with emerging digital platforms, technologies and best practice, identifying opportunities to keep Gill at the forefront of digital communications.
Share performance updates and key insights with stakeholders across the business to support informed decision making.




Skills & Experience




Proven experience managing digital content and channels for a consumer-facing brand, ideally within the sailing, marine or outdoor industry.
A strong understanding of sailing, boating and life on the water, or demonstrable experience creating content for these audiences.
Experience planning and delivering integrated digital campaigns across social media, website and other owned digital channels.
Strong understanding of social media strategy, content planning and digital storytelling.
Experience managing content production, working with photographers, videographers and creative partners to deliver high-quality creative assets.
Experience working with ambassadors, athletes, influencers or brand partners to develop engaging content and campaign activations.
Experience working with website content management systems and maintaining digital asset libraries.
Excellent copywriting skills with a strong eye for visual storytelling, design and brand consistency.
Experience collaborating with Ecommerce teams to deliver commercially successful campaigns.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to translate performance data into actionable insight.
Highly organised, proactive and able to manage multiple projects and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills with the confidence to work across multiple teams and external partners.




About You




You're equally comfortable developing a creative campaign idea as you are analysing performance data. You have excellent judgement, a strong eye for quality and understand what great content looks like.




Most importantly, you have a genuine passion for life on the water and understand the audiences we're speaking to. Whether through direct experience in sailing, boating or the outdoor industry, you'll be able to create authentic content that resonates with our global community and reflects the credibility of the Gill brand.




Benefits

Competitive salary (DOE)
24 days holiday plus bank holidays
Company pension scheme
Annual profit share
Salary sacrifice scheme
Generous staff discount on Gill products

Title: Human Resources Advisor
Description:
Nottingham High School is delighted to invite applications for the position of HR Advisor to join our dedicated and supportive team. This is an exciting opportunity for an experienced and proactive HR professional to play a key role in delivering a high quality HR service within one of the UK's leading independent schools. Working closely with colleagues across the school, the successful candidate will contribute to fostering a positive, inclusive, and collaborative working environment while supporting a wide range of HR activities. We welcome applications from individuals who are passionate about people, committed to excellence, and eager to make a meaningful contribution to our vibrant school community.




This role is integral to the success of the HR team, ensuring the delivery of a consistent, high-quality HR service across the School. The postholder will provide expert support and guidance to staff and managers on a wide range of HR matters, contributing to a positive employee experience and effective people management.




The role will also be responsible for ensuring compliance with employment legislation and best practice, maintaining the highest standards of confidentiality, and delivering an efficient and professional service at all times.




Key Responsibilities

Manage employee relations casework proactively and effectively, providing timely and consistent advice in line with employment legislation and organisational policies, including absence management, flexible working requests, disciplinary matters, grievances, and probation reviews.
Coach, support, and advise line managers to build their confidence and capability in managing people-related matters.
Review, maintain, and update HR policies and procedures, ensuring that legislative changes and best practice developments are reflected promptly.
Support employee engagement, wellbeing, and recognition initiatives, including the administration and analysis of the annual employee survey.
Provide guidance and support throughout recruitment and selection processes as required.
Ensure internal HR communications are clear, timely, accurate, and tailored appropriately to different audiences.
Maintain accurate and up-to-date HR records and employee data, ensuring compliance with data protection requirements.
Produce and analyse HR management information and reports to support decision-making, identify trends, and highlight potential areas of concern.
Ensure all employee-related changes are processed accurately and communicated effectively to payroll.
Keep abreast of developments in employment law, HR best practice, and sector trends, applying this knowledge to support continuous improvement.

General Responsibilities

Deliver a professional, responsive, customer-focused HR, recruitment, and compliance service to all stakeholders.
Undertake any other duties commensurate with the level of the role, as reasonably requested by the Line Manager.

Essential Criteria

CIPD Level 5 qualification (or equivalent professional qualification).
Significant experience managing a broad range of employee relations casework.
Experience operating in a similar role.
Sound knowledge of UK employment legislation and HR best practice.
Strong interpersonal, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to handle sensitive and confidential information with discretion and professionalism.




Apply

Please apply directly through LinkedIn or our website: https://www.nottinghamhigh.co.uk/contact-us/current-vacancies/item/1114/hr-advisor-1

Title: VDPS Doctor - Hybrid
Description:
VDPS Doctor - Hybrid

£72,000 Per Annum

Nottingham




Job Introduction

Be part of something great Maximus is a global organisation that specialises in providing health and employment services to millions of people every year. Here in the UK we employ around 5,000 people across the country to deliver services that have a profound impact on people’s lives. From assessments and health services to employability programmes and specialist support, we do work that matters with people who care. The role is necessary to carry out clinical reviews and provide case managers with advice in relation to complaints and enquiries in connection with PIP referrals. Complaints and enquiries can come from customers, representatives (including welfare rights groups & MPs), as well as DWP. Part of the role is also to provide outcomes and feedback on all complaints about healthcare professionals that is used as part of professional development and improvement in addition to being utilised to make overall business improvements.




Do good. Be great as a doctor.

Are you a Registered Medical Doctor, General Practitioner or Junior Doctor seeking flexible hours, a convenient location and a friendly working environment?




About the role

As a Medical Assessor, you’ll carry out thorough assessments to understand how a person’s disability or health condition affects their daily life. You’ll produce clear, factual reports to support benefit entitlement decisions across a range of services, including:




Personal Independence Payment (PIP)
Work Capability Assessments (WCA)
Industrial Injuries Benefit (IIB)
Vaccine Damage Payment Scheme (VDPS)




Every assessment is different. You may work with individuals who have complex physical or mental health conditions, neurological disorders, respiratory diseases, industrial injuries, or vaccine-related cases. You’ll be given sufficient time and support to complete high-quality, respectful assessments focused on the individual.




What we’re looking for

Valid GMC registration number
A registered healthcare professional with at least 5 year’s post-registration clinical experience in the UK
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce accurate, detailed reports
Confidence using computer systems and digital tools
A professional and empathetic approach, with the ability to assess a wide range of conditions, including complex and mental health cases
A proactive learner who is open to developing new skills outside of traditional clinical environments
You MUST have the right to work in the UK - we cannot offer sponsorships
Excellent oral and written communication skills
Comfortable using computer software to type and produce detailed reports




What we offer

£72,000 per annum
Competitive annual bonus, paid quarterly
£1,000 bonus for achieving accreditation within four months
Professional indemnity cover
No bank holidays, evenings or weekends
Leading maternity and paternity paid leave
Bank holidays plus 25 days’ holiday with the option to buy or sell 5 days
Ongoing CPD, clinical development and reimbursed validation fees
£2,000 for referring a friend
Life insurance and Private Bupa Healthcare




Join us and become part of a team that's making a real difference to people’s lives.

Title: Tiler
Description:
United Infrastructure delivers essential infrastructure services across utilities and social infrastructure, supporting some of the UK’s largest network operators and housing providers.

PiLON, part of the United Infrastructure Group, specialises in planned maintenance, refurbishment, and repairs within the social housing sector. With over 20 years of experience, we are known for delivering quality work with care and respect for the communities we serve.

Job Description


Isolate kitchen power (switch off consumer unit)
Lock off kitchen circuit with padlock while working
Remove sockets, then reinstall after work is complete
Inform resident that kitchen sockets cannot be used for approx. 2 hours (risk of short circuit)
Install tiles around the bath only
If bath is between 3 walls: tile all 3 walls along full bath length
If bath is in an L‑shape (2 walls): tile only along the bath length
No full tiling at WC area
Tile behind sink only, up to 3 rows high


Qualifications


Asbestos awareness training (can be provided in-house by Pilon)
CSCS Card


Additional Information

In addition to the above qualifications, we are looking for:


Right to work in the UK
Own tools (110v or battery-operated)
Full UK driving licence and access to your own vehicle (van preferred)
Good conversational English
Reliable, organised, and able to work to deadlines
Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality

Title: Vice-Chair of Trustees
Description:
The opportunity

We are seeking a Vice-Chair to join our Board of Trustees and bring their skills, experience and enthusiasm to the Board as we move into an exciting new phase of development for the charity. This is a fantastic opportunity for an aspiring Chair to further develop their experience of charity governance working alongside committed and experienced trustees. 

We are also interested to hear from candidates with commercial experience (current or recent experience of working within the business community) - either for the Vice-Chair role or joining us separately as a Trustee.




About us

Ignite! has a long and distinguished history as a small, but mighty Nottingham-based charity that supports children and young people to develop their curiosity and creativity to equip them for an unknown, but exciting future. We facilitate creative partnerships between professional sectors and communities of young people, including schools, youth groups and community groups, particularly those at risk of social exclusion. We link young people with a variety of professionals to share learning, develop understanding, and celebrate new ideas and ways of thinking. Find out more about us: www.ignitefutures.org.uk

 

Main responsibilities of the Vice-Chair:

Deputise for and support the Chair when necessary and assume any or all of the duties in the absence of the chair or as delegated by the Chair. 
Liaise closely with the Chair and assist them in overseeing the conduct of Ignite!’s business.
With the Chair, ensure that trustees understand the scope of their responsibilities.   
Assist the Chair to facilitate the effective direction of Ignite! by seeking an appropriate combination of skills and experience among trustees and, if appropriate, by developing the use of appropriate committees and sub-groups of the board.
Act as an informal mediator in situations where there are difficult board dynamics, including any tensions between the Chair and other board members, or the Chair and management team.
The Vice-Chair may wish to lead on time-limited actions/subgroups aligned with their interests.




Person Specification

We are seeking individuals with: 

commitment to our vision, mission and values; 
excellent teamwork and leadership skills and effective decision-making; 
willingness to commit time and effort to the role; 
good, independent judgement, strategic vision and ability to communicate ideas; 
history of impartiality, fairness and commitment to promoting equality and diversity. 
*recent experience of working within the business community  (*if applying to the board as a trustee with commercial expertise)




Remuneration: The role of Trustee/Vice-Chair is voluntary (with travel expenses covered).

Location: Ignite!’s main office is in Nottingham. Board meetings are predominantly held online with at least one in-person meeting per year.

Time commitment: Five Board meetings per year, which includes the AGM (usual board meeting times are 5-7pm). Additional meetings including sub-groups may be held between board meetings. Time commitment for the role is 0.5 to 1 day per month




To find out more and apply: Opportunities | ignitefutures

Title: Network Engineer
Description:
Salary £49,000 p/a

Hybrid role with occasional travel to our Nottingham Head Office, clubs which are UK wide and data centres.

Ready to take on a role where you can shape, support, and evolve the technology behind one of the UK’s biggest entertainment brands?

We're looking for a passionate, proactive Network Engineer to join our IT team and help power the systems our customers rely on every day, both in club and online.

Why You’ll Love This Role

You’ll be joining a collaborative, forward‑thinking team that looks after the entire retail and online IT estate including data centres, offices, and bingo clubs across the UK.

You’ll take ownership of our network infrastructure and play a key part in both BAU operations and exciting, business‑wide projects. From day one, you’ll have real impact and real responsibility.

What You’ll Be Doing


Owning 3rd‑line network support and responding to escalations
Installing, configuring, and optimising network hardware and systems
Supporting large-scale deployments across the group
Leading and contributing to projects that deliver business change
Monitoring production systems and participating in the BAU/on‑call rota
Working closely with DevOps, Infrastructure, and Project teams
Championing standards, best practice, and continuous improvement


What You’ll Get In Return


Help@Hand - 24/7 access to GPs, mental health support, and more for you and your family
Company performance annual bonus
Thrive App - NHS-approved mental wellbeing support
Buzz Brights Apprenticeships & Buzz Learning - access to 100s of online courses
Buzz Brilliance Awards - employee recognition scheme
5 weeks annual leave plus public holidays (pro-rated for part-time roles)
Holiday Buy Scheme - purchase an extra week of holiday (eligibility applies)
50% staff discount on bingo tickets, food, and soft drinks

Strong technical skills in:

Palo Alto / FortiGate technologies
Cisco Meraki

Plus the mindset to match:


Excellent communication able to translate technical issues for any audience

A positive “can‑do” attitude and a collaborative nature

Experience with ITIL, or industry‑specific systems (bingo/gambling), is a bonus.

Title: Data Protection Administrator
Description:
About The Job

Games Workshop has an exciting opportunity for a Data Protection Administrator. We’re looking for someone who has the skills to work in a supportive administrative capacity to a team of Data Protection Advisors, who work to ensure Games Workshop meets its compliance requirements.

This role is a varied one, we’re looking for someone who has a genuine interest in Data Protection, can understand the importance of accuracy and be tenacious in adhering to a streamlined process for Data Protection activities.

Conscientious and diligent in your approach, we’ll support your development as you learn how to respond to Data Subjects Rights Requests, including requesting, chasing, and tracking data across the business informing relevant parties of any search activity undertaken. You’ll need to be thorough and use your people skills when communicating with Data Subjects as you’ll need to keep them updated on any progress or notify them of any extension requests.

Collaboration and working closely with the Privacy team will be key as they’ll rely on your natural skills in organisation to assist in the Privacy Team’s Global Audit process. You’ll develop your knowledge as you collate supporting documents, redact, take notes from sessions, and assist with audit reports. You’ll be able to take direction from the team as you update department records and privacy notices. They’ll support your development as you assist them in handling data deletion requests, data breaches, and track progress on Privacy by Design documents.

We’re looking for someone methodical, process driven who can respond quickly and take challenges on board readily. You’ll also have the initiative to hold others to account when chasing information that has not been received in a timely manner knowing that handling this in the right way can achieve effective solutions.

Ideally, You’ll Have Some Knowledge Of Data Protection Combined With a Genuine Passion And Strong Commitment To Consistency. You’ll Understand The Importance Of Professionalism In Handling Sensitive Business And Personal Data. If You Want To Be Part Of a Supportive Team Apply Today

Working at Games Workshop

At Games Workshop we are looking for people who will do their best to understand the needs of the company and to put those needs first when they are at work. Because of this we believe that what you are like, hence the attitude you show to work and the way you choose to behave is even more important than your skills and experience.

The Warhammer World site is based just outside of Nottingham city centre and benefits from a nearby tram stop and free onsite car parking.

In return for your hard work, you will receive 33 days holiday per year (including public holidays), matching contributory pension scheme up to 7.5%, profit share bonus (subject to GW meeting profit targets) and option to join our Share Save scheme. Oh and let’s not forget, a staff discount of between 25% and 50% on our products!

Other Essential Information

Location: This position is granted on a hybrid working basis where you will be expected to work out of our head office in Lenton, Nottingham, twice a week

Closing date for applications is midnight, UK time, Sunday 19th July 2026

How to Apply

To apply, please send us a letter or a video telling us why you want this job. This is a great opportunity for you to let us know that you understand what we are looking for.

Please include an up-to-date CV to support your application. When you’re ready, please click apply, below.

Title: Monitoring Engineer
Description:
As a Monitoring Surveyor at Survey Solutions, you will work independently completing small to large scale monitoring projects, both manual and automated sensor-based solutions. You will have acquired previous practical experience and technical knowledge of land surveying through a technician role on small and medium projects.

You will work independently on varied monitoring projects, installing, maintaining and decommissioning monitoring programmes on site using innovative technology and processing data in the office to a high working standard.

On larger projects, Monitoring Surveyors will take the lead with approval from the Monitoring Manager and deliver reliable and robust monitoring solutions.

Monitoring Surveyors will be motivated to progress their career with Survey Solutions, developing expertise in automated solutions, IoT monitoring systems, advanced scanning and heat mapping technologies where applicable, with future development into geotechnical monitoring solutions.

Requirements

Main Duties / Key Responsibilities

Technical


Complete all sizes of manual monitoring projects independently and to a high standard, offering robust and repeatable solutions with backup stations and full understanding of manual measurement procedures.
Conduct manual monitoring installations and routine monitoring readings ensuring accuracy, repeatability and consistency of recorded data.
Apply knowledge to contribute to justification of appropriate monitoring methodologies and project specifications regardless of project size.
Work independently under guidance from the Monitoring Manager and operations coordinators on a catalogue of varying projects
Process monitoring results to a consistently high and uniform standard.
Produce reports showcasing monitoring data, including environmental elements including noise, dust and vibration
Contribute effectively to the successful delivery of monitoring projects with minimal supervision.
Understand project scopes of work and ensure delivery to exact client specifications.
Maintain monitoring equipment to a high working standard ensuring all equipment remains fit for purpose.
Set up monitoring instruments, record data and provide logistical support to other monitoring surveyors where required.
Carry out levelling surveys and maintain high levels of precision for settlement and movement monitoring programmes.
Installation, maintenance, recommendation and decommissioning of contact sensors including:
Noise monitoring
Dust monitoring
Vibration monitoring
Tilt monitoring
Deck monitoring
Automated crack monitoring
Accelerometers
Perform physical installation and maintenance of monitoring systems requiring practical hands-on experience using drills, fixings and installation tools.
Conduct repeat verticality checks on structures and masts, and produce accurate monitoring records.
Support installation, operation and maintenance of Automated Total Station (ATS) monitoring solutions.
Support automated monitoring systems and assist with maintenance and troubleshooting of automated equipment.
Assist with deployment, maintenance and exploration of IoT-enabled monitoring systems where required.
Interpret monitoring data and issue formal reports on:
Structural movement
Noise monitoring values
Dust monitoring values
Vibration monitoring values
Tilt movement trends
Manage monitoring datasets using Excel, ensuring accurate data entry, graph production and reporting output.
Use CADsoftware to support monitoring layouts, technical drawings and project deliverables where required.
Provide education and mentoring on basic manual monitoring solutions to Monitoring Technicians.


Professional Development


Continue enhancing technical surveying skills by seeking advice from experienced surveyors and utilising internal training resources.
Attend training and development days to improve knowledge of monitoring techniques, emerging technologies, automated monitoring systems and data processing solutions.
Develop technical capability across advanced monitoring technologies including automated monitoring systems, sensor-based monitoring and IoT monitoring solutions.
Provide feedback to line manager during regular progression reviews.


Health & Safety


Follow Survey Solutions health and safety policies and procedures outlined within the H&S Manual.
Promote high standards of health and safety and public welfare.
Undertake site inspections and complete dynamic risk assessments when attending site.
Ensure required safety measures are in place before commencing work.
Conduct monthly vehicle checks ensuring company vehicles remain in safe working condition.
Operate handheld installation tools safely and responsibly in accordance with site safety requirements.

Title: Consultant Business Analyst
Description:
Who is this great for?

Our Consultant Business Analysts are pragmatic problem-solvers who know how to make change happen in the real world, not just on paper. We’re looking for experienced BAs who can build trusted relationships, work collaboratively with clients and teams, and deliver meaningful outcomes without hiding behind process or consultancy theatre. You’ll bring strong analysis skills, commercial awareness, adaptability, and a people-first approach that aligns with Herd’s “non-consultancy vibes”.

We hire experienced adults and trust people to get the job done. As a growing scale-up, you’ll be trusted with responsibility early and expected to get stuck in where needed.

As a Consultant Business Analyst You’ll Be


A passionate business analyst with experience beyond just process modelling and requirements engineering; able to draw upon your know-how to get stuff done for clients in a pragmatic and impactful way.
Experienced in business analysis across different contexts, from small improvement initiatives through to user-centred, product-led delivery and large-scale transformation.
A relationship builder who can create and foster long-term client relationships, helping build Herd’s brand as a trusted partner whilst embracing “non-consultancy vibes”.
Obsessed with outcomes: delivering what’s needed for the client, when it’s needed.
A team player who embraces the “Power of Herd”, sharing knowledge and expertise to enhance the overall experience clients get from working with us.
Committed to continuous learning, proactively seeking ways to improve your knowledge, toolkit, and refine your craft.
Commercially aware and able to deliver value for money for clients in line with our company values.
An adaptable all-rounder who enjoys taking on challenges and can work across different industries, sectors, and delivery environments.
A positive presence within the team, contributing to an inclusive environment for Herd folk and clients alike, and helping keep Herd a great place to work.
Able to tell a compelling story through your work in order to influence stakeholders on the best way forward.
Pragmatic enough to know that the “right” approach depends on the client, the context, and the outcome needed.


Key elements of the role


Own defined business analysis delivery within projects, products, or programmes.
Take ownership of your work, manage stakeholder relationships, and escalate issues appropriately, proposing practical options and solutions where needed.
Operate primarily in a billable consulting role, either independently within blended client teams or as part of a wider Herd engagement.
Work across small and medium-sized initiatives, whilst supporting larger transformation programmes where required.
Collaborate closely with customers throughout delivery — building relationships, managing your own work, supporting collaboration, and helping teams reach informed decisions.
Join engagements at different stages of the lifecycle, from early exploration and discovery through to live delivery and implementation.
Contribute to wider professional communities and share your experiences internally across Herd and externally through blogs, podcasts, and events.
Support the growth of Herd through contributions to bids, tenders, pre-sales activities, Statements of Work, case studies, and thought leadership content.
Embrace the reality of a fast-growing scale-up environment and be willing to get stuck in where needed.we’re looking for folk who’ll embrace that and get stuck in where needed.


Core skills and characteristics


Enjoy getting things done and driving tangible outcomes.
Build strong working relationships with people at all levels, across teams, functions, and cultures, and can influence where needed.
Able to synthesise complex information and communicate it in a simple, clear, and effective way.
Accountable, proactive, and comfortable owning your work and deliverables.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with excellent attention to detail.
A self-starter who can identify priorities, make decisions, and work independently when needed.
Confident influencing stakeholders and constructively challenging ways of working where there may be a better approach.
Comfortable working at pace within fast-moving delivery environments.
Curious and adaptable, with the ability to learn new concepts, domains, and tools quickly.
Comfortable presenting and defending your thinking, whilst remaining open-minded to better ideas and alternative perspectives.
Able to apply pragmatism and judgement to choose the right approach depending on the situation and desired outcome.
Comfortable working within multidisciplinary teams alongside product, delivery, design, engineering, and operational colleagues.
Someone who takes pride in producing high-quality, engaging, and visually clear deliverables
Comfortable constructively challenging customers and colleagues when there may be a better way of doing things, balancing confidence with empathy and professionalism.
Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills.


Desirable experience


Experience working within central government, arms-length bodies, local authorities, higher education, or charities.
First-hand experience working within the GOV.UK Service Standard.
Experience using data analysis tools such as Power BI, SQL, or Python.


Right to work

You must have the right to work in the UK. Applicants without this will not be progressed. Herd Consulting does not currently offer sponsorship.

Security clearance

Due to the nature of the clients we work with, you may be required to undergo security vetting up to and including Security Check (SC) or Developed Vetting (DV) clearance levels.

If you have any reason to believe you may not successfully obtain the required clearance, this must be declared as early as possible and before accepting any offer of employment.

Location

You can be based anywhere across mainland UK. There will be some expectation to travel to client sites as and when needed.

You’ll also be expected to visit our Sheffield office at least once per month for team meetings. For those living within a 25-mile radius of Sheffield, we encourage regular office time where possible, typically around once per week. Outside of that, we’re completely comfortable with folk working remotely from within the UK. Due to the nature of the work we do, remote working from outside of the UK is not permitted.

Benefits

Package: £42,000-£52,000 (depending upon experience) plus the following benefits and perks:


25 days annual leave, plus Bank Holidays
An additional day off to celebrate your birthday
£1,000 annual personal development fund to spend as you wish
Employee perks programme with exclusive discounts and savings across hundreds of retailers
Electric Vehicle (EV) and Cycle to Work salary sacrifice schemes (available after passing probation)
Life assurance cover at 3x salary
24/7 Virtual GP and Advanced Nurse Practitioner access
Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) available 24/7, 365 days a year
Child Mental Health Support
Cancer Awareness and Nurse Support, provided by Perci Health, the UK’s leading virtual cancer clinic.
Online physiotherapy service
Second Medical Opinion — offers access to a UK based specialist consultant who can provide a second medical opinion on a diagnosis or treatment plan.
Dog-friendly employer — we love seeing dogs in the office!
Paid pet bereavement leave from Day 1
Discounts with retailers in Leah’s Yard
Regular Herd socials, both virtual and in-person, including lunches, beer tasting, award nights, crazy golf, and more


We're also proud to be named one of the Sunday Times Best Places to Work for three years running, 2024, 2025, and 2026.

Closing Date

We review applications as they come in and may close the advert early if we receive a high volume of suitable applications or fill the roles ahead of schedule. If it sounds like your kind of thing, we’d encourage you to apply sooner rather than later.
